| **Reason** | `nullable`, string, max 2000 chars ŌĆö but the UI marks the field `required` so the form won't submit without one | `leave.request` | The model is permissive (so a manager pre-filling a leave for someone over the phone isn't blocked) but the standard UI insists, because a leave history with empty reasons is operationally useless. |
| **Supporting Document** | optional; PDF / DOC / DOCX / JPG / JPEG / PNG; Ōēż 5 MB | `leave.request` | The MVP captures the upload widget but the storage hookup for leave attachments is delivered in the same Phase 2 wave as the leave-balance seeding work ŌĆö until then the file is dropped after submit. The field is in the UI now to lock the user-facing contract. |
| **Submit Request** (cobalt CTA, panel footer) | POSTs to `/leave-requests`. On success the panel closes, the form resets, a "Leave request submitted successfully." toast fires, and the page reloads with the new row at the top. | `leave.request` | Spinner state on `leaveForm.processing` so double-clicks don't double-submit. |
| **Cancel** | Closes the panel without firing anything. | Anyone | Standard escape. |

> *Notes:* `StoreLeaveRequest::authorize()` is the gate ŌĆö anyone without `leave.request` gets a 403 before any field is checked. The controller calls `LeaveService::request()` which inserts the row in `LeaveRequest::create($validated)` and immediately dispatches `LeaveRequested(leave, actor)` ŌĆö three listeners react (see "How it talks to other modules" below). The request lands in `pending` status by default because the migration sets `status('default', 'pending')`.

### Approvals Desk ŌĆö Pending Approvals tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Tab ŌĆö Pending Approvals** | Filters the table to `status === 'pending'`. The tab label carries a count badge fed by `LeaveRequest::pending()->count()` on the controller. | `leave.approve` or `leave.manage` (HR / dept heads) | Default tab on landing ŌĆö pending work is what an approver opened the page for. |
| **Approve** (green button per row) | Opens the decision modal pre-set to `approved`. | `leave.approve` on this request (i.e. HR with `leave.manage`, **or** the manager of the requesting employee's department) | Two-step confirmation prevents accidental click-throughs; the optional comment becomes the audit reason. |
| **Reject** (red button per row) | Opens the decision modal pre-set to `rejected`. | Same RBAC pair | Same confirmation flow; the comment placeholder switches to "Reason for rejectionŌĆ”". |
| **Waiting cell** | Days since the request was submitted, in monospaced text. Goes amber at two days, red at five. | Anyone viewing | A visual nudge to clear the backlog ŌĆö backed only by `created_at`, so it costs nothing. |
| **Avatar** | Initials in a cool-family gradient seeded by a stable hash of the name, plus an outline ring. Hover scales it 5%. | n/a | The same avatar treatment used on the Employees directory, so faces are recognisable across modules. |
| **Empty state** | "No pending requests ┬Ę All leave requests have been actioned." with a `task_alt` glyph. | Anyone viewing | Honest empty state ŌĆö celebrates "inbox zero" without inventing data. |

### Approvals Desk ŌĆö All Requests tab

| Field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Employee filter** | Drop-down populated only when the viewer has `leave.approve` or `leave.manage`. Self-service users get an empty array and so the picker disappears. | `leave.approve` / `leave.manage` | Department heads and HR need to find one person's history fast ŌĆö search-by-name is the most-used filter in this module. |
| **Leave Type filter** | Drop-down of the seven `LeaveType` enum values. | Same | "Show me every maternity leave this year" is a real recurring question. |
| **Status filter** | `pending` / `approved` / `rejected` (the `LeaveStatus` enum is three-valued in the MVP ŌĆö see "What's planned next" for the cancelled/expired states that arrive with bidirectional approval routing). | Same | Status is the second most-used filter. |
| **From / To** | ISO date inputs. From ŌåÆ `start_date >= v`; To ŌåÆ `end_date <= v`. | Same | Lets HR scope the table to a quarter without paginating through history. |
| **Apply** | Re-issues the Inertia GET with the chosen filters as query string; `preserveState: true` so scroll position is kept. | Same | One round-trip, server-side filtering ŌĆö the JS never trims a list it's been handed. |
| **Clear** | Resets every filter and re-queries with no params. | Same | One-click undo. |
| **Approve / Reject** (per row, conditional) | Same flow as the pending tab ŌĆö but only renders on rows with `status === 'pending'`. Approved or rejected rows show only the "View" button. | `leave.approve` on that row | Keeps the action density low on the historical view. |
| **View** | Opens the Request Detail slide panel; same panel as the row-click on the employee view. | Anyone with view-permission on that row | Quick read without leaving the table. |
| **Pagination** | 20 per page; hidden when there is only one page. | Anyone viewing | Same as everywhere else. |

### Approvals Desk ŌĆö Leave Calendar tab

| Control | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Month chevrons** | Move the calendar one month back / forward. The header `MMMM YYYY` updates in step. | Anyone with calendar access | A 12-month picker is overkill for the day-to-day question of "who is out this month?". |
| **Day cell click** | Opens the Day Detail slide panel ŌĆö list of every employee on approved leave on that day, plus the holiday name if `GH_HOLIDAYS` matches. | Anyone with calendar access | Faster than scanning the chips inside the cell when the day is busy. |
| **Today indicator** | The day number on `new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` renders as a filled cobalt pip. | n/a | Standard "you are here" affordance ŌĆö keeps the eye on the right cell when you land on the tab mid-month. |
| **Holiday pip + name** | The 8 seeded Ghanaian statutory holidays (New Year, Constitution Day, Independence, Workers', Founders', Kwame Nkrumah, Christmas, Boxing) render with a slate-grey pip and the holiday name beneath. | n/a | Sets expectation that no leave should be approved on these days ŌĆö and helps the requester see at-a-glance why a chosen date might not need a leave entry at all. |
| **Leave chips** | Up to three first-name chips per cell, colour-coded by leave type. `+N more` chip appears when the day has more than three approved leaves. | Anyone with calendar access | High signal at low cost ŌĆö a glance is enough to spot a department-wide collision. |
| **Legend** | Bottom of the calendar ŌĆö five type chips and the public-holiday pip. | n/a | The chips are colour-only on small screens; the legend is the accessibility floor. |

> *Notes:* The calendar pulls its data from the same paginated `leaves` collection the table uses ŌĆö it does not make a second request. That means it shows leaves from the **current page only**, which on a well-populated institute can hide entries that span across page boundaries. The fix (a dedicated lightweight calendar endpoint) is on the Phase 2 list ŌĆö see "What's planned next". The Ghana holiday map is hard-coded in the MVP; live integration with the official Public Holidays Calendar arrives with the Year-End Closeout pack in Chapter 31.

### Decision modal (Approve / Reject)

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Status** | `required`, one of the `LeaveStatus` enum values (`pending`, `approved`, `rejected`) | `leave.approve` on this row | The modal sends `approved` or `rejected` only ŌĆö `pending` is the initial state, not a destination. |
| **Comment** | optional, free string | Same | The placeholder text switches between "Any notes for the employeeŌĆ”" and "Reason for rejectionŌĆ”" depending on `actionType`. The MVP forwards the comment in the PATCH body but does not yet persist it (a `leave_request_history` table arrives with the bidirectional routing ŌĆö see "What's planned next"). |
| **Confirm Approval / Confirm Rejection** (gradient CTA) | PATCHes `/leave-requests/{id}` with `{ status, comment }`. On success: refreshes the page, fires the toast, decrements the pending badge. | `leave.approve` on this row | Spinner state on `actionLoading`; the button gradient flips green / red to match the action. |
| **Cancel** | Closes the modal without firing anything. | Anyone | Standard escape. |

> *Notes:* Approval is wrapped in a database transaction (`LeaveService::updateStatus`). Inside that transaction the service writes back `status` and `approved_by` on the request, then row-locks (`lockForUpdate`) the `LeaveBalance` for `(employee_id, type, year)` and increments `used_days` by the request's `durationInDays()`. A new balance row seeds at 21.0 days if one doesn't exist ŌĆö that figure is a holding default that will be replaced by a per-grade, per-type entitlement schedule when Positions/Grades go live (see Chapter 19's establishment column on the Phase 1 work). The lock prevents two managers approving overlapping requests from oversubscribing the balance.

### Request Detail page (`/leave-requests/{id}`)

| Element | What it does | Who can see it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Breadcrumb** ("Leave Management ŌåÆ Request #N") | Navigates back to the index. | Anyone with view on this request | Standard wayfinding. |
| **Type chip + icon well** | Read-only. The chip carries the type label and the icon glow tinted from `TYPE_META`. | Anyone with view | Identity at a glance ŌĆö the same visual language used in the table and the calendar. |
| **Status badge** | The `LeaveStatus` enum rendered through `StatusBadge` ŌĆö amber for pending, green for approved, red for rejected. | Anyone with view | Single source of truth for the workflow state, reused from the directory. |
| **Period card** | Start and end dates rendered twice ŌĆö short (`08 Jun 2026`) and long (`Monday, 08 June 2026`) ŌĆö plus a coloured Working Days tile keyed off the request type. | Anyone with view | A leave conversation is mostly about dates ŌĆö they deserve a full card. |
| **Reason card** | The request reason with whitespace preserved (`whitespace-pre-line`). Empty-state copy is "No reason provided." in italics. | Anyone with view | Faithful to the submission ŌĆö line breaks the requester typed survive the round-trip. |
| **Approver decision card** | Renders only when `status !== 'pending'` and an `approver` is loaded. Green-tinted for Approved, red-tinted for Rejected. Shows the approver's name and the decision timestamp to the minute. | Anyone with view | A single, unambiguous "who decided, when" ŌĆö the auditor's question. |
| **Requester card** (right column) | Avatar (same gradient palette), name, employee number, position. | Anyone with view | Identity of the requester without leaving the page. |
| **Activity timeline** | Two-step vertical timeline ŌĆö "Leave Requested" (cobalt pin, requester + submission time) ŌåÆ either "Approved" (green) / "Rejected" (red) / dashed "Awaiting Decision" (amber). | Anyone with view | Approval as a story, not as a status string. |
| **Quick Links card** | Two anchors ŌĆö "All leave requests" (back to `/leave-requests`) and "View employee profile" (to `/employees/{id}`). | Anyone with view | The two most common follow-up questions, one click away. |
| **Approve / Reject buttons** (top-right, conditional) | Same decision modal as the Approvals Desk. Visible only when `isHR` and `status === 'pending'`. | `leave.approve` on this row | Lets the decision be made from either the queue or the detail page ŌĆö both flows land the same PATCH. |

## The data behind it

CIHRMS keeps two tables under this module, both pinned to the `employees` row:

- **`leave_requests`** ŌĆö one row per application. The columns: `employee_id` (the requester), `approved_by` (the deciding user, nullable until the request is approved), `start_date`, `end_date`, `type` (cast to `LeaveType`), `reason` (text, nullable, up to 2000 chars), `status` (cast to `LeaveStatus`, defaults to `pending`), the standard `timestamps()`, and `softDeletes()` for withdrawals. The `employee_id` is a cascade-on-delete foreign key, so removing an employee removes their requests ŌĆö the audit trail relies on soft delete plus the analytics events, not on the row itself.
- **`leave_balances`** ŌĆö one row per `(employee_id, type, year)` triple, with a unique compound index that enforces that constraint at the database level. The columns: `total_days` (decimal 5,1, defaults to 0) and `used_days` (decimal 5,1, defaults to 0). The model exposes `remainingDays()` as a computed accessor ŌĆö it does not store the remaining figure, so it can never disagree with itself.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **`durationInDays()`** is the inclusive day count between `start_date` and `end_date` ŌĆö a Monday-to-Friday range counts as five days, not four. The Apply panel's "Working days requested" pill, on the other hand, **subtracts weekends client-side** before the request is sent. That gap is intentional in the MVP ŌĆö the server stores the calendar-day count so the simplest possible joins with payroll work; the UI shows the working-day count because that is the number the employee actually cares about. The Phase 2 Attendance integration (see Chapter 5) reconciles the two.
- **Public holidays are recognised by the calendar but not by the engine.** The hard-coded `GH_HOLIDAYS` map gives the calendar its holiday pips, but neither `durationInDays()` nor the "Working days requested" pill subtracts them. The fix is in the same Phase 2 wave that introduces the official Public Holidays Calendar.
- **Balance seeding is a 21-day placeholder.** Until a balance row exists, the system invents one at 21.0 total days on first approval. The Positions/Grades work in Phase 1 (Chapter 31's establishment schedule) is what turns that into a per-grade, per-type entitlement read from the establishment table ŌĆö see "What's planned next".
- **Approval is transactional.** `LeaveService::updateStatus` opens a `DB::transaction`, writes the request, row-locks the balance with `lockForUpdate()`, and increments `used_days`. Two managers approving overlapping requests at the same instant cannot oversubscribe the balance ŌĆö the second one waits at the lock.
- **Soft delete means "withdrawn".** A row removed via `DELETE /leave-requests/{id}` is `deleted_at`-stamped, not erased. Restoring is a console action. Approved / rejected requests cannot be withdrawn at all ŌĆö `LeaveRequestPolicy::cancel` returns `false` for any non-pending status.

## How it talks to other modules

- **`LeaveRequested` event** ŌåÆ fired after every successful submission. Two listeners pick it up: `RecordAnalyticsEvent` writes a `leave.requested` analytics row (consumed by Chapter 31); `NotifyManagerOfLeaveRequest` (queued on the `integrations` queue) DMs the requester's line manager across whichever channels they have configured ŌĆö Slack DM, Teams card, WhatsApp template ŌĆö and broadcasts to the configured HR Slack/Teams channel when the `slack_leave_approvals` feature flag is on.
- **`LeaveStatusUpdated` event** ŌåÆ fired after every approval or rejection. Three listeners react: `RecordAnalyticsEvent` writes `leave.status_updated` with the new status; `SendNotifications` notifies the requesting employee via Laravel's notification system (`LeaveStatusChanged` mail / database notification); the same messaging dispatcher used on submission can be wired for cross-channel decision broadcasts in Phase 2.
- **Employee (Ch 3)** is the parent ŌĆö every leave request belongs to exactly one employee (`cascadeOnDelete`), and the employee detail page's Leave History tab loads the latest eight requests plus the four configured balance tiles via the same `LeaveBalance::where('employee_id', $id)->where('year', now()->year)` query the index uses.
- **Attendance (Ch 5)** consumes approved leave as the explanation for an otherwise-flagged absence ŌĆö the unapproved-absence rule looks at whether the day is inside an approved `LeaveRequest` before raising a flag.
- **Payroll engine (Ch 19)** reads approved leave per pay period ŌĆö Annual / Sick / Maternity / Paternity / Emergency / Study are all paid (the engine simply counts attendance days minus paid leave); `Unpaid` is converted into a per-day deduction at the employee's daily rate.
- **Notifications (Ch 16)** is the channel ŌĆö the `MessagingDispatcher` and Laravel's built-in notification stack route every leave-status change and leave-request submission through the user's preferred mix (in-app, email, Slack DM, Teams card, WhatsApp template).
- **Reports & Analytics (Ch 31)** consumes the two events to compute the ISO 30414 absenteeism KPIs, the average days-pending-approval, the approval-rate-by-manager, and the top-of-funnel leave-mix-by-type donut on the HR dashboard.

## Standards touchpoints

- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.5 (Productivity ŌĆö Absenteeism)** ŌĆö the absenteeism rate (╬Ż leave days / ╬Ż scheduled days), broken down by leave type and by reason category, is one of the disclosures named by the standard. CIHRMS captures every leave application with its type and dates, which is what makes that calculation possible. The institute-wide rollup happens in Chapter 31's analytics export. See Chapter 44.
- **Ghana Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦20 ŌĆö Annual Leave** ŌĆö the Apply panel pre-states "15 working days per year" against the Annual Leave option; this is the statutory floor for any worker who has completed twelve months of continuous service. The 21-day balance seed in `LeaveService::updateStatus` is a temporary placeholder used until Positions/Grades go live with per-grade entitlements (which can be higher than the statutory floor for senior grades). See Chapter 44.
- **Ghana Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦24 ŌĆö Sick Leave** ŌĆö the panel reflects "Up to 14 days with medical certificate". The Supporting Document upload exists specifically so a medical certificate can be attached; the controller accepts it but the MVP's storage hookup ships with the same Phase 2 balance-seeding wave. See Chapter 44.
- **Ghana Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦57 ŌĆö Maternity Leave** ŌĆö the panel pre-states "12 weeks (84 days); 14 weeks for multiple births". The `Maternity` leave type maps to 84 calendar days in the policy hint; the multiple-births uplift is captured manually on the Reason field today and becomes a structured input in Phase 2. See Chapter 44.
- **Ghana Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651) ŌĆö Paternity Leave** ŌĆö paternity leave is not a statutory entitlement under Act 651 itself but is a common collective-bargaining-agreement add-on; CIHRMS recognises it as a discrete leave type and caps it at 5 working days in the policy hint, which is the institute's bargained standard. See Chapter 44.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦18 ŌĆö Data Minimisation** ŌĆö sick-leave reasons and any uploaded medical certificate are sensitive personal data. The MVP keeps the Reason field optional at the DB layer (so the requester can choose how much to disclose) and surfaces the request only to the requester, their manager, and HR ŌĆö never on a department-wide list. The Supporting Document, when stored, will live under the same `storage/app/public/employee-documents/` tree as the Employees module and inherit the same access rules. See Chapter 44.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦17 ŌĆö Lawful Basis** ŌĆö processing of leave-related personal data sits on the employment-contract basis; CIHRMS does not require additional consent for an employee to log a sick day. The right-to-erasure (Act 843 ┬¦40) path is the same soft-delete + redaction-job pattern used on the Employees module ŌĆö withdrawing a leave request soft-deletes the row; full erasure for a departed employee is handled by the dedicated redaction job, not by clicking Withdraw. See Chapter 44.
- **National Pensions Act, 2008 (Act 766)** ŌĆö Maternity leave is paid leave under Act 651, and remains pensionable; the Payroll engine (Chapter 19) treats Maternity days the same as worked days for SSNIT and Tier-2 contribution purposes. Unpaid Leave days do not accrue contributions. See Chapter 44.

## What's planned next

Phase 2 of the government-grade roadmap lands four things directly on this screen: (1) per-grade, per-type entitlement schedules sourced from Positions/Grades replace the 21-day placeholder in `LeaveService::updateStatus`, so a Grade 12 officer sees their bargained 22 days where a probationer sees the statutory 15; (2) a dedicated `leave_request_history` table records every decision with the comment string captured by the modal today, so the audit pack can reconstruct the full timeline; (3) the calendar moves to a dedicated lightweight endpoint and starts honouring the official Ghana Public Holidays Calendar, with both the engine and the "Working days requested" pill subtracting public holidays from the count; and (4) the Attendance integration in Chapter 5 begins reconciling biometric clock data with approved leaves so the unapproved-absence flag fires reliably across the institute ŌĆö closing the last gap between "leave applied" and "leave actually taken".

<!-- ===== 05_attendance.md ===== -->

# Chapter 5 ŌĆö Attendance

> *In one paragraph.* Attendance is the institute's time-and-attendance ledger ŌĆö every clock-in, clock-out, late arrival, and overtime hour for every employee, computed from raw punches into a day-by-day summary that payroll trusts as its source of truth. The same ledger is fed from four channels (biometric devices, the public web kiosk, the employee's own browser with GPS, and HR-typed manual entries), reconciled against shifts and approved leave, and exposed in three views ŌĆö an org-wide register for HR, a personal monthly card for each employee, and a kanban for managers who decide on correction requests. It's the module the gap analysis (Chapter 44) flagged as Phase-2 work for Ghana public-sector compliance with the Labour Act ┬¦35; it shipped early and is in use today.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Workforce** group ŌåÆ **Attendance** (clock-face icon). The module tile is also pinned in the app switcher (the 3├Ś3 grid in the top bar) so any role with `attendance.view` lands on the org-wide register in one click. Employees without `attendance.view` see a "My Attendance" entry instead, which goes to `/attendance/me`.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every page, every employee, every device.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö every page, plus the only role that can record manual entries (`attendance.manage`) and define new shifts (`attendance.shift_manage`).
    - **dept_head / manager** ŌĆö the org-wide register, plus the Corrections kanban for requests from their reports. They can approve corrections (`attendance.approve`) but not change shift definitions.
    - **employee** ŌĆö only the personal "My Attendance" page at `/attendance/me`. They can clock themselves in/out (`attendance.clock_self`) and raise correction requests (`attendance.correct`); they never reach the org-wide register.
    - **finance_officer, it_support** ŌĆö `attendance.correct` only (they can raise their own corrections); they don't see the register.
    - **auditor** ŌĆö read-only on the register and the ability to raise their own corrections; no approvals, no shift edits.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö every attendance row belongs to one; Leave (Ch 4) ŌĆö approved leave days show up as "On Leave" status in the daily summary and are excluded from the absent denominator; Payroll (Ch 19) ŌĆö `PayrollService` reads `attendance_summaries.overtime_hours` straight off this module to compute overtime pay; Kiosk (Ch 29) ŌĆö the unauthenticated `/kiosk` device hands events off to the same `AttendanceService::record()`; Identity Verification (Ch 25) ŌĆö biometric devices and the kiosk's future face-recognition path tie into the same Ghana Card verification stack; Notifications (Ch 14) ŌĆö correction-request events fire analytics rows that drive the manager-side review badges; Standards Benchmark (Ch 44) ŌĆö this module is the Labour Act ┬¦35 and ISO 30414 ┬¦4.5 absenteeism touchpoint.

## The screens

![Attendance org-wide register ŌĆö masthead, trend chart, composition donut, records table](../assets/screenshots/05_attendance/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Editorial masthead ŌĆö month label, "ATTENDANCE REGISTER" eyebrow, a sub-nav strip (Daily / Corrections / Shifts) that hides Corrections and Shifts from users without the matching permission. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Visual band ŌĆö a daily LiveBars chart of "showed-up" counts across every day of the month (peak day highlighted gold) and a composition donut showing the present/late/absent/on-leave mix with the percentage of staff who showed up at the centre. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Filter strip ŌĆö month picker, status dropdown, free-text employee/staff-ID search. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Records table ŌĆö one row per (employee, day) with avatar, name, staff ID, status pill, first-in time, last-out time, hours worked, overtime premium hours, and the source icon (fingerprint / web / edit-note). ┬Ę ŌØ║ "Mark Clock-in / out" cobalt CTA in the top right ŌĆö opens a slide panel for HR to file a manual punch on someone's behalf.*

![My Attendance ŌĆö live clock hero, calendar heatmap, monthly KPIs, daily table](../assets/screenshots/05_attendance/me.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero clock card ŌĆö a cobalt ŌåÆ magenta gradient with a live-updating HH:MM:SS clock, today's date, and a CLOCKED IN / CLOCKED OUT pulsing pill that reflects the employee's current state. The big white "Clock In" / "Clock Out" button next to it captures GPS first (with a visible "Capturing GPSŌĆ”" ŌåÆ "GPS captured" / "GPS unavailable" status) before submitting. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Four StatCards ŌĆö Days Worked, Total Hours (gold accent), Late Arrivals, Overtime Hours ŌĆö for the selected month. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Month calendar grid ŌĆö a 7-column heatmap colouring each day cell by status (emerald for present, amber for late, rose for absent, violet for on-leave/holiday, slate for weekend), with today ringed in cobalt. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Daily records table ŌĆö same shape as the org-wide table but scoped to this person. ┬Ę ŌØ║ "Request Correction" button at the top-right opens the correction slide panel.*

![Attendance Corrections ŌĆö hero banner with KPIs, filter strip, three-column kanban, review modal](../assets/screenshots/05_attendance/corrections.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero banner ŌĆö the pending count in gold, the approval rate next to it, and a contextual sentence that escalates ("Oldest pending: 4 days ŌĆö consider reviewing soon to keep payroll on track") once anything has been waiting more than three days. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Four KPI tiles ŌĆö Pending Review, Approved Last 7 Days, Rejected Last 7 Days, Approval Rate. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Filter strip ŌĆö debounced free-text search (employee name or staff ID) plus a status dropdown; "Clear" appears only when a filter is set. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Three-column kanban ŌĆö Pending / Approved / Rejected. Each card shows the employee, the requested timestamp, the direction (clock-in / clock-out), the reason in a clipped preview, and ŌĆö once decided ŌĆö the reviewer's name and decision notes. Pending cards carry inline Approve / Reject buttons. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Review modal ŌĆö a centred dialog with the request summary at the top and a decision-notes textarea below. Notes are optional for an approval but required for a rejection (the form blocks submission until the field is filled).*

![Shift Schedules ŌĆö hero with KPIs, 24-hour coverage ribbon, shift cards, assignments table](../assets/screenshots/05_attendance/shifts.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero banner ŌĆö total active shifts in cyan, custom assignments in gold, "on shift now" live count (computed client-side by intersecting today's working days, shift start/end times against the local clock). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Today's coverage ribbon ŌĆö a 24-hour timeline with one bar per shift; bars currently running are highlighted in a cyanŌåÆcobalt gradient and a gold vertical "now" marker tracks the wall-clock time. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Shift cards ŌĆö code, name, time window (visualised as a proportional bar across a 24h track with the "now" marker layered on top), the 7 day-of-week chips with active days highlighted, grace-period minutes, optional department, and a "live" pill on any card that's currently running. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Assignments table ŌĆö employee, shift, effective from/to (open-ended assignments show a green pill; otherwise a "Nd left" or "Expired" badge driven by the end date). ┬Ę ŌØ║ "New Shift" and "Assign Shift" CTAs at the top-right open the two slide panels (described under "Create form" and "Edit form" below).*

> The four screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.21). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Org-wide register (`/attendance`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Mark Clock-in / out** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for filing a manual punch on any employee's behalf. Records Employee ID, event timestamp, direction (in/out), and a required reason (5-500 chars, audit-logged). | `attendance.manage` (hr_admin, super_admin, ceo) | Biometric devices and the kiosk fail occasionally ŌĆö HR needs a hand-typed safety valve, with every entry stamped with the operator's identity so the audit trail stays clean. |
| **Sub-nav: Daily / Corrections / Shifts** | Three pill links sitting under the masthead. "Daily" is the current page; "Corrections" and "Shifts" are hidden unless the viewer has `attendance.approve` and `attendance.shift_manage` respectively. | All viewers; the conditional links honour the same RBAC as the routes they go to | Avoids dead links and surprise 403s ŌĆö a manager never sees a Shifts tab they can't open. |
| **Month picker** | Filters every chart and the table to a single calendar month. Re-fires the Inertia request immediately on change. | Anyone with `attendance.view` | Most operational lookups are "this month so far" or "last month for payroll"; a month at a time is the right grain. |
| **Status dropdown** | All / Present / Late / Absent / On leave / Holiday. Re-queries on change. | Same | Cuts the table to the exception you're chasing. |
| **Search box** ("Search employee or staff ID") | Free-text filter that fires on Enter. Matches `LOWER(employee_no)` and the joined `users.name` with a case-insensitive LIKE ŌĆö the SQL is written cross-driver so it works the same on SQLite and MySQL. | Same | Fast-find for a single person; pressing Enter (rather than debouncing) keeps the query off the database on every keystroke. |
| **Trend chart** (LiveBars, left column) | Daily count of "showed up" employees (present + late) across the month. Peak day is auto-highlighted in gold; the median line is drawn. Hovering any bar shows "N staff" via the chart's tooltip. | Same | Spots the institute-wide patterns ŌĆö drops on Fridays, peaks at month-end. |
| **Composition donut** (right column) | Four-segment donut (present / late / absent / on leave) with the showed-up percentage as the big centre number. Total rows recorded shown below the title. | Same | Single-glance "how's this month going?" headline. |
| **Records table row** | Read-only ŌĆö clicking does not navigate. Avatar + name + staff ID, formatted date, status pill, monospace HH:MM first-in / last-out, two-decimal hours worked, overtime premium hours (greyed at zero, gold-bold when > 0), and a source icon (fingerprint / edit-note / web). | Same | The table is the working surface; row clicks are reserved for future detail drill-down. |
| **Pagination** (bottom) | 50 rows per page, with `withQueryString()` so month / status / search persist through page changes. | Same | Institutes with thousands of employees produce tens of thousands of summary rows per month; pagination keeps the page snappy. |
| **Manual entry slide panel** | Four fields: Employee ID (numeric), event timestamp (datetime-local picker), direction (radio: Clock-in / Clock-out), reason (required textarea). Hits `POST /attendance/manual` ŌåÆ `AttendanceService::record()` with `source = Manual` and the reason captured on the audit trail. | `attendance.manage` | The `Manual` source value in the `AttendanceSource` enum has its `requiresReason()` flag set to true ŌĆö the service throws a `DomainException` if the reason is missing, so the UI's required field mirrors a real backend guard, not just decoration. |

> *Notes:* `AttendanceController::index()` runs six pre-computed numbers in one render (today's present / absent / late / on-leave counts, the month-average hours-worked, and the active workforce size) alongside the daily-trend group-by, the status-distribution group-by, and the paginated table. Even with a thousand-row month every query uses an index ŌĆö the `(employee_id, summary_date)` unique constraint covers the table reads, and the `summary_date` standalone index covers the day-grouped charts. Authorisation routes through `AttendancePolicy::viewAny()`, which honours both `attendance.view` and `attendance.manage`.

### My Attendance (`/attendance/me`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Live clock hero** | A JavaScript `setInterval` ticks the displayed HH:MM:SS every second. Today's date is in long format below it. The card's status pill flips between **CLOCKED IN** (emerald, with a pulsing dot) and **CLOCKED OUT** (white-grey) based on whether today's summary row has a `first_in` but no `last_out`. | Any authenticated employee with an Employee record | Visual confirmation that the session is alive and that the clock-in button you're about to press will register at the time you see. |
| **Clock In / Clock Out** (big white button on the hero) | Calls `navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition()` with a 4-second timeout. If GPS resolves, lat/lng go on the POST; if denied or unavailable, the request goes through anyway. The button posts to `/attendance/clock` with `direction` set to whichever state the employee isn't currently in. | `attendance.clock_self` (every employee role) | Self-service is the primary attendance path for desk workers without a biometric device nearby. The fallback-on-failure approach matches Ghana office reality, where browsers often refuse geolocation but the punch is still the source of truth. |
| **GPS status line** (above the button) | Four states in sequence ŌĆö `idle` ("GPS available"), `resolving` ("Capturing GPSŌĆ”", pulsing), `captured` ("GPS captured", emerald), `denied` ("GPS unavailable", grey). | Same | Honest about whether location is being recorded ŌĆö DPA-friendly transparency. |
| **Request Correction** (top-right button) | Opens a slide panel asking for the requested timestamp, direction, and an 8-500 character reason. Posts to `/attendance/corrections` and closes on success with a toast. | `attendance.correct` (every role except super-admin-only ones) | The escape hatch for missed punches, biometric outages, or HR data-entry typos. Without it the employee would have to email their manager and wait. |
| **Stat cards** (4-tile band) | Days Worked, Total Hours (gold accent), Late Arrivals, Overtime Hours ŌĆö all computed by `AttendanceService::aggregatePeriod()` for the selected month. | Same | The institutional headline view of the employee's month, sitting one scroll below the hero. |
| **Month picker** (top-right of the calendar tile) | Re-fetches the page for a different month. Inertia `preserveState: true` keeps the live clock running. | Same | Backwards review for payroll-period reconciliation. |
| **Calendar heatmap** | A 7-column grid (Mon-first) of the selected month. Each day cell is coloured by status ŌĆö emerald (present), amber (late / half day), rose (absent), violet (on-leave / holiday), slate (weekend). Today is ringed in cobalt. The cell's tooltip shows the human-readable status label. | Same | The familiar pattern: a single glance tells you how the month is shaping up. |
| **Daily records table** (right column) | Date ┬Ę Status pill ┬Ę In ┬Ę Out ┬Ę Hours ┬Ę OT, scoped to this employee. The "Today" row is tinted cobalt. | Same | Auditable, copyable backup for the visual calendar. |
| **Pagination** | Only renders when there are more than three link-pages (most months never hit it; long-range views do). | Same | Quiet by default; available when needed. |

> *Notes:* `AttendanceController::myAttendance()` calls `AttendanceService::aggregatePeriod()` on render, which **materializes a summary row for every day in the range** (using `ensureSummaryForDay()`) before computing the totals. That's deliberate ŌĆö absences need to be explicit rows, not implicit holes, so payroll can see "this employee was absent on the 15th" rather than guessing from missing data. The clock-self endpoint also runs through a `throttle:10,1` rate limiter so a stuck repeat button can't carpet-bomb the table.

### Attendance Corrections (`/attendance/corrections`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Filter strip** | Debounced (380 ms) free-text search across the requester's name and staff ID. Status dropdown (Pending / Approved / Rejected) re-queries immediately. "Clear" button appears only when a filter is set. | `attendance.approve` (manager, dept_head, hr_admin, super_admin, ceo) | The reviewer's filter ŌĆö most weeks the queue is mostly approved; the filter lets the reviewer focus on pending. |
| **Kanban columns** | Three columns (Pending / Approved / Rejected) populated from the same paginated dataset. Cards are coloured on the left edge by their column. Empty columns show an italic "No pending review" placeholder. | Same | A queue, a recent decisions log, and a recent rejections log all in one screen ŌĆö no tabs to flip through. |
| **Card body** | Avatar + employee name + staff ID, age badge ("3d ago"), the requested clock-event (direction pill + monospace timestamp), the reason text (2-line clamp; full text in tooltip), and the reviewer attribution panel (name + decision date + optional notes) once decided. | Same | Everything the reviewer needs to decide without opening anything. |
| **Approve** (green inline button, pending cards only) | Opens the Review modal pre-set to "Approve". On submit, `AttendanceService::approveCorrection()` runs in a database transaction: it (1) creates a new `AttendanceRecord` with `source = Manual` carrying the reviewer's name and the approved correction's reason, (2) updates the correction row to `approved` / `reviewer_id` / `reviewed_at` / `decision_notes`, (3) recomputes the affected day's `AttendanceSummary` so the dashboards reflect the change immediately. | `attendance.approve` | The whole point of a correction system is that approval actually changes the record ŌĆö not just the request's status. |
| **Reject** (red inline button, pending cards only) | Opens the Review modal pre-set to "Reject". The decision-notes textarea becomes required (5+ chars). On submit, the correction is marked rejected and an `AttendanceCorrectionDecided` event fires. No `AttendanceRecord` is created ŌĆö the original day stands. | Same | Required notes on rejection because the employee will see them; you can't reject without explaining why. |
| **Review modal** | Centred dialog with the request summary panel at the top (employee, direction, requested timestamp, reason), a notes textarea below, and Cancel / Approve|Reject submit buttons. The submit button is colour-coded green for approve / red for reject; both have the spinner-on-submit pattern. | Same | A focused dialog beats a slide panel for a yes/no decision; the eye stays on the one thing being decided. |
| **Hero banner** | Big "N awaiting review" headline in gold, the approval rate next to it, and an SLA sentence that switches to amber once the oldest pending request is 3+ days old ("Oldest pending: 4 days ŌĆö consider reviewing soon to keep payroll on track"). | Same | Soft escalation ŌĆö the queue scolds you when it's stale rather than waiting for a payroll deadline to surface the backlog. |

> *Notes:* `AttendanceCorrection::approveCorrection()` enforces a one-way transition ŌĆö only Pending corrections can be approved or rejected (a `DomainException` blocks anything else). Both decisions fire the `AttendanceCorrectionDecided` event, which is wired in `AppServiceProvider::boot()` to `RecordAnalyticsEvent` so the audit trail and the manager-side analytics counter stay in lock-step with the queue. The Pending column always loads first when the user lands cold so reviewers can act without filtering.

### Shift Schedules (`/attendance/shifts`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **New Shift** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens a large slide panel for defining a shift pattern. | `attendance.shift_manage` (hr_admin, super_admin, ceo) | Shift catalogue lives separately from assignments ŌĆö define once, assign many. |
| **Assign Shift** (border button, top right) | Opens a smaller slide panel for binding an existing shift to one employee with an effective-from date and an optional effective-to. | Same | Default schedule (Mon-Fri 08:00-17:00 with 15 min grace) covers most office staff; this overrides for night-watch, weekend rota, or four-day-week pilots. |
| **24-hour coverage ribbon** | Hour-of-day timeline (00:00 ŌåÆ 24:00) with one bar per shift sized and positioned proportionally. Bars running right now light up in a cyanŌåÆcobalt gradient; the gold vertical line tracks the wall clock. | Same | "Who's covered right now?" answered without reading any numbers. |
| **Shift card** | Code (monospaced), name, time-window bar with the now-marker overlaid, 7 day-of-week chips with active days highlighted, grace-period minutes, optional department chip, count of employees on this shift, status pill (Active / Archived; Live when currently running). | Same | The single shift's at-a-glance reference card ŌĆö useful as a printout for ops teams. |
| **Active assignments table** | Employee row + shift code/name + effective-from + effective-to with a derived status ŌĆö Open-ended (no end date), "Nd left" with amber tint if Ōēż 7 days remain, "Expired" if the end date has passed. | Same | The audit lens ŌĆö who is on a non-default schedule, when does it expire, and is anything about to lapse? |
| **Live coverage pill** (top right, above the buttons) | A cyan "N on shift" count that intersects today's working days, shift start/end times, and the wall clock against assignment effective dates. | Same | Mirrors the visual ribbon as a single number for the header strip. |

> *Notes:* When `AttendanceService::recomputeDailySummary()` runs, it calls `ShiftService::scheduleFor(employee, date)` to find the assignment in effect (most recent `effective_from` Ōēż date, no `effective_to` or `effective_to` Ōēź date). If no assignment matches or the shift has been archived (`is_active = false`), the service falls back to the institute default ŌĆö Mon-Fri 08:00-17:00, 15 minute grace, 8 hours full day, 4 hours half day. That's why the org dashboard works on day one with no shifts defined.

### Create form (New Shift slide panel)

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Code** | required, max 20, unique on `shifts.code` | `attendance.shift_manage` | Free-text code; HR tradition is uppercase mnemonics (`MORNING`, `NIGHT_WATCH`, `WEEKEND_OPS`). The input forces uppercase via CSS. |
| **Name** | required, max 80 | Same | Human-readable label that surfaces in dropdowns and the assignment table. |
| **Start time / End time** | required, `H:i` format | Same | 24-hour clock; the UI uses `<input type="time">` so the browser enforces a valid wall-clock pair. Overnight shifts (end < start) are accepted at validation but the current overtime calculator treats each calendar day separately ŌĆö see "What's planned next". |
| **Working days** (button-grid) | optional array; values must be in `mon,tue,wed,thu,fri,sat,sun` | Same | Defaults to Mon-Fri if left empty. Click to toggle; the chosen days are persisted as a JSON array on the `shifts.working_days` column. |
| **Grace (minutes)** | optional, integer, 0-120; defaults to 15 | Same | The window after the official start time before a clock-in is marked Late. A first-in at 08:14 with a 15 min grace is Present; 08:16 is Late. |
| **Full day (hours)** | optional, numeric, 1-24; defaults to 8.0 | Same | The threshold above which overtime starts accruing on a weekday. |
| **Half day (hours)** | optional, numeric, 0.5-12; defaults to 4.0 | Same | Hours-worked below this threshold mark the day as Half-Day, not Present ŌĆö visible on the org register and excluded from the full-day rate in payroll. |
| **Department** | optional; must exist on `departments` | Same | Optional pinning of the shift to a department (informational; assignment is still per employee). |
| **Active shift** (checkbox) | optional, boolean; defaults to true | Same | Inactive shifts are archived and ignored by `ShiftService::scheduleFor()` ŌĆö useful for retiring a shift without breaking past assignments. |

> *Notes:* The whole create posts to `attendance.shifts.store` ŌåÆ `StoreShiftRequest`. The `working_days.*` rule means each element of the array is validated against the allowed set; an injected `"funday"` value returns a 422 with a useful message. The slide panel pre-fills sensible defaults (08:00-17:00, Mon-Fri, 15 min grace, 8h full, 4h half) so creating the institute's first shift takes about 20 seconds.

### Edit forms (Assign Shift slide panel)

| Field | Validation | Who can change it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Employee** | required, must exist on `employees` | `attendance.shift_manage` | The dropdown lists all active employees with their `employee_no` and name (joined via `User`). |
| **Shift** | required, must exist on `shifts` | Same | The dropdown lists every shift in the catalogue regardless of active status ŌĆö operators sometimes want to extend an "archived" historical pattern. |
| **Effective from** | required, valid date | Same | The first date the new schedule applies ŌĆö typically today or the start of next month. |
| **Effective to** | optional, valid date, Ōēź effective_from | Same | Leave blank for an open-ended assignment (most assignments are open-ended; rota-style fixed-term ones have an end date). Multiple historical assignments per employee are fine ŌĆö `ShiftService` picks the most recent matching one. |
| **Save assignment** (cobalt CTA) | POST to `/attendance/shifts/assignments` | Same | The new row goes into `shift_assignments`; next time `recomputeDailySummary()` runs on a date in the effective window it sees the new schedule. |

> *Notes:* There's no UI for editing or deleting an assignment in the current build ŌĆö to remove an override you create a new one with the original shift or end-date the active one through the API. That's the conscious MVP boundary (see "What's planned next"). The Shifts page itself does support editing and archiving shifts via `PATCH /attendance/shifts/{shift}` and `DELETE /attendance/shifts/{shift}`, but they're plumbed at the route layer and not yet surfaced as UI buttons.

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores attendance across four tables, with two more supporting catalogue entities:

- **`attendance_records`** ŌĆö the raw clock event ledger. One row per punch, never edited, only inserted. Carries `employee_id`, `device_id` (nullable ŌĆö manual entries don't have one), `source` (`biometric` / `gps_mobile` / `web_kiosk` / `manual` / `webhook`), `direction` (`in` / `out`), the device-side `event_at` timestamp, the server-side `recorded_at` timestamp (so an offline device that batches up a day and replays at 6 pm doesn't lie about when the punch happened), optional `geo_lat`/`geo_lng`, the device's confidence score (string, raw from the vendor), the `recorded_by` user id for manual entries, the required reason for manual entries, and the full vendor payload as JSON in `raw_payload`. Indexed on `(employee_id, event_at)` and `(device_id, event_at)` so the daily recompute is O(records-for-this-day) and not a table scan.
- **`attendance_summaries`** ŌĆö the derived per-day record that everything else reads. One row per (employee, day) pair, enforced by a unique index. Carries the computed `status` (`present` / `late` / `half_day` / `absent` / `on_leave` / `holiday` / `weekend`), `first_in` / `last_out` clock times, `hours_worked` and `overtime_hours` (both decimal(5,2)), boolean `is_weekend` / `is_holiday` flags so dashboards can colour cells without re-deriving the calendar, and the "dominant" `source` for the day. This table is the payroll contract ŌĆö `PayrollService` reads `overtime_hours` straight from here.
- **`attendance_corrections`** ŌĆö the request/decision lifecycle. Carries the optional FK to the `attendance_record` being corrected, the requester user id, the requested timestamp + direction, the reason text, the `status` (`pending` / `approved` / `rejected`), the reviewer's user id, the decision timestamp, and the decision notes. Soft-deleted (so dismissed requests stay in the audit log). Indexed on `(employee_id, status)` and on `status` alone ŌĆö the manager queue sorts by status and the employee history filters on employee.
- **`shifts`** ŌĆö the catalogue of named working-time patterns. `code` (unique short identifier), `name` (display label), `start_time` / `end_time`, `grace_period_minutes`, `full_day_hours` / `half_day_hours`, the array of allowed `working_days`, an optional `department_id`, an `is_active` flag, and soft-delete.
- **`shift_assignments`** ŌĆö the bridge between an employee and a shift, with an effective-from date and an optional effective-to. Soft-deleted. The "current" assignment is found by `effective_from Ōēż today` and (`effective_to` is null OR `effective_to Ōēź today`), ordered by latest effective-from.
- **`biometric_devices`** ŌĆö registered hardware. `code` (unique, e.g. `DEV-ACCRA-MAIN-01`), `name`, `vendor` (`zkteco` / `hikvision` / `suprema` / `other`), `location`, optional `department_id`, the per-device HMAC `shared_secret` (encrypted via Laravel's `encrypted` cast and hidden from JSON serialization), optional `geo_lat` / `geo_lng` / `geo_radius_m` for geofencing, an `is_active` flag, and a `last_seen_at` timestamp that the ingestion service updates every time it accepts a payload from this device.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **Summaries are derived, not authoritative.** Every time a new `AttendanceRecord` lands (from any source ŌĆö biometric, kiosk, self-service, manual, or a webhook), `AttendanceService::recomputeDailySummary()` runs synchronously for the affected day. If you ever need to rebuild the summary you can just call the service again; the source of truth is always the raw records.
- **The OvertimeCalculator implements Ghana Labour Act ┬¦35.** Beyond 8 hours on a weekday accrues at 1.5├Ś (`weekday_15x`); any hour worked on a weekend or public holiday accrues at 2├Ś (`premium_2x`); beyond 12 hours on any day flips into the 2├Ś bucket regardless of weekday/weekend. The `overtime_hours` column stores **premium-weighted equivalents** ŌĆö payroll multiplies them by the basic hourly rate and adds the result to gross pay, no further multiplier needed.
- **Public holidays come from the `PublicHoliday` model.** Any day matching a `PublicHoliday` row is automatically flagged `is_holiday = true` in the summary and statused `holiday`, regardless of whether anyone clocked in or out. This honours statutory holidays (Independence Day, AU Day, Eid, Christmas) without HR having to remember.
- **Approved leave wins over absence.** `AttendanceService::isOnApprovedLeave()` checks for any `LeaveRequest` with `status = approved` covering the day; if it matches, the day is statused `on_leave` and excluded from the working-days denominator in `aggregatePeriod()`. That's why the absenteeism rate stays honest ŌĆö leave isn't punished as absence.
- **Geofence is enforced server-side.** When a clock event has both a `device_id` and a GPS pair, the service computes the haversine distance from the device's stored coordinates; anything outside `geo_radius_m` throws a `DomainException` ("Clock event 412m outside 50m geofence for device DEV-ACCRA-MAIN-01") and the punch is rejected before insert. For self-service clocking through `/attendance/clock`, the service auto-pairs the punch with the nearest registered device within a half-degree latitude/longitude box (~55 km) before applying the same geofence.
- **Manual entries must carry a reason.** The `Manual` value of the `AttendanceSource` enum has `requiresReason() = true`. The service throws `DomainException` if the reason is missing, the FormRequest validates 5-500 chars, and the UI marks the field required. Three layers of the same guard.
- **Biometric secrets never leave the database in cleartext.** The `shared_secret` column is cast `encrypted` and listed under `$hidden` on the model. Even a `dd($device)` in tinker won't print it.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Leave (Ch 4)** is read by `AttendanceService::isOnApprovedLeave()` on every summary recompute. A day covered by an `approved` leave request is automatically statused `on_leave` and excluded from the working-days denominator. There is no double-counting ŌĆö a day can be either `on_leave` or `absent`, never both.
- **Payroll (Ch 19)** reads `attendance_summaries.overtime_hours` directly inside `PayrollService::calculateLine()`. The sum across the pay-run period multiplied by the basic hourly rate (`basic / 173.33` ŌĆö the standard 52w ├Ś 40h / 12 monthly hours) becomes the `overtime_pay` line on the payslip. There is no separate "overtime approval" step in the MVP ŌĆö once a punch lands and the summary recomputes, those overtime hours are billable.
- **Kiosk (Ch 29)** is a thin shell over the same `AttendanceService::record()`. `KioskController` matches the employee by `employee_no` + name, then calls the same service with `source = WebKiosk`. The kiosk has no auth; the matching pair is the "proof you're you" until face recognition lands.
- **Public biometric webhook** is exposed at `POST /webhooks/biometric`, signed with HMAC-SHA256 over `{X-Biometric-Timestamp}.{body}` keyed by the device's `shared_secret`. The `VerifyWebhookSignature` middleware (`webhook.signature:biometric`) verifies the signature, the device code, and a 5-minute timestamp window to prevent replay attacks. The `BiometricIngestionService` then translates the vendor-neutral payload (`device_code` + `events[]` with `employee_no`, `direction`, `event_at`, optional `score`/`geo_lat`/`geo_lng`) into one `AttendanceRecord` per event, returning `{accepted, skipped, errors[]}` to the device for its own retry logic. The device's `last_seen_at` is bumped on every accepted batch.
- **Public v1 API** (`GET /api/v1/attendance/summaries`) gives integrators a read-only paginated view, scoped by `api.scope:attendance:read`. Filters: `employee_id`, `from`, `to`, `status`, `per_page` (max 500). This is what the IPPD/GIFMIS integration bus reads from, and what an external BI tool would hit.
- **Analytics module** picks up `AttendanceCorrectionRequested` and `AttendanceCorrectionDecided` events via `RecordAnalyticsEvent`, writing `attendance.correction.requested` and `attendance.correction.decided` rows so the manager-side activity stream and the executive dashboards can count them.
- **Identity Verification (Ch 25)** is the future tie-in: biometric devices already carry an HMAC secret and a confidence score on every event; the next phase plugs that into the Ghana Card biometric match so a clock-in can be one-and-the-same with an identity check.
- **Notifications (Ch 14)** is wired for correction lifecycle events through the same dispatchable but does not currently mail anyone on a correction request ŌĆö the kanban itself is the inbox. Mail is roadmapped for the SLA-breach case (a request older than 3 days).

## Standards touchpoints

- **Ghana Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦33-39 ŌĆö Hours of work, overtime, and rest periods.** The default schedule (Mon-Fri, 08:00-17:00, 8h full day, 1h unpaid lunch, Ōēź 15 min grace) honours the ┬¦33 maximum 40-hour week. The `OvertimeCalculator` implements ┬¦35's three-tier rule (weekday OT at 1.5├Ś, weekend/holiday at 2├Ś, deep-OT beyond 12 hours at 2├Ś). The calculator file (`app/Services/Attendance/OvertimeCalculator.php`) explicitly cites ┬¦35 in its docblock. The institute's public-holiday register feeds `PublicHoliday::isHoliday()` so every gazetted holiday automatically pays at the ┬¦36 rate. See Chapter 44.
- **ISO 30414:2018 ┬¦4.5 (workforce productivity / absenteeism).** `AttendanceService::aggregatePeriod()` returns an `attendance_ratio` (`days_worked / working_days` excluding excused days) on every render of the My Attendance page; the org-wide register aggregates the same number across the workforce. The Standards Benchmark in Chapter 44 lists this as the institute's ISO 30414 absenteeism touchpoint.
- **ISO 30414:2018 ┬¦4.7 (workforce availability)** ŌĆö the live "on shift now" coverage count on the Shift Schedules page surfaces real-time availability against the rota, the metric the standard recommends for operational-readiness disclosures.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17 ŌĆö Lawful basis for processing personal data.** Biometric punches are processed on the employment-contract basis; the device-side `shared_secret` is encrypted at rest and never exposed via the API. The geofence pre-check rejects out-of-area punches before they enter the audit trail, which constrains the personal-data footprint to what the employer needs (the ┬¦18 data-minimisation principle). DPA ┬¦40 (right to erasure) requests are handled by the redaction job described in Chapter 27 ŌĆö soft-delete is the operational default for attendance corrections, not for raw records.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦20 ŌĆö Special personal data (biometric).** The biometric ingestion path stores only the `biometric_score` (a confidence string from the device) and the `raw_payload` JSON; the underlying fingerprint / face / iris template never leaves the device. This is the consent-required category under ┬¦20; the Phase 1 work (Chapter 25) wires the consent flow per employee. See Chapter 27 for the full Special-Category mapping.
- **National Identification Authority Act, 2006 (Act 707) ŌĆö Ghana Card integration** is the planned downstream join: once a device's confidence score can be matched against a NIA biometric record, a single clock-in becomes both an attendance event and an identity verification.

## What's planned next

Phase 2 of the government-grade roadmap (Chapter 46) lands four things directly on this module: (1) **Mobile app with offline buffer** ŌĆö a thin React Native client around `/attendance/clock` that caches events in IndexedDB when offline and replays them on reconnection, so field staff on patchy networks don't lose punches; (2) **Per-shift overtime policies** ŌĆö the `OvertimeCalculator` is currently hard-coded to the Labour Act ┬¦35 default rates, but for shifts (e.g. NIGHT_WATCH) that need a richer rule (night-premium percentages, double-time after midnight, etc.) the policy will move onto the `shifts` table as a configurable JSON column; (3) **Roster-style shift planning** ŌĆö today's `shift_assignments` table is one-shift-per-employee-per-window; the planned roster module sits above it as a weekly view that bulk-assigns shifts in a single drag-and-drop pass, with conflict detection against approved leave; (4) **Manager-side bulk approve** for the corrections kanban so end-of-pay-period clean-up doesn't require clicking each card individually. The screen-level gaps ŌĆö UI for editing and archiving shifts, UI for editing assignments, and a face-recognition clock path on the kiosk ŌĆö are tracked in the same wave; the routes and services are already in place.

<!-- ===== 06_performance.md ===== -->

# Chapter 6 ŌĆö Performance Management

> *In one paragraph.* Performance Management is the institute's annual rhythm of expectation, observation, evaluation, and correction ŌĆö turned into software. Every employee in CIHRMS has, or can have, a written performance contract for the cycle with weighted KPIs, an ongoing set of goals with periodic check-ins, one or more reviews (self, manager, peer, skip-level, upward) feeding a 1-to-5 rating, a calibration round-table that normalises those ratings against an agreed distribution, a 9-box that places every rated employee on a performance-vs-potential grid, and ŌĆö when things have not gone well ŌĆö a Performance Improvement Plan that the system insists on before any non-disciplinary termination can proceed. The gap analysis (Chapter 44) classified Performance Management as Phase-2 work; the foundation (cycles, goals, reviews, 9-box) shipped in Wave 13 and the PSC-style contracts, calibration sessions, and PIPs were completed and merged the week of 30 May 2026.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Performance** group (magenta colour code, "monitoring" icon). Expanding the group reveals seven entries ŌĆö **Analytics** (the landing page at `/modules/performance`), **Goals**, **Reviews**, **Contracts**, **Calibration**, **PIPs**, and **9-Box**. The group is gated behind `performance.view`; the four sensitive entries (Calibration, PIPs, 9-Box, and the analytics page itself) carry their own per-permission `visible:` flags so a user without the relevant grant never sees a dead link.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every page, every employee, every signature, every adjustment.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö Analytics, Goals, Reviews, Contracts (read-only on contracts unless also granted `performance.manage`), Calibration (`performance.calibrate`), and PIPs (`performance.pip_manage`). HR cannot apply a locked calibration session unless they also hold `performance.calibrate_apply` ŌĆö that is the dual-control gate.
    - **dept_head / manager** ŌĆö Goals and Reviews for the people in their reporting line, plus their own row. Contracts they own (as supervisor) for sign-off. 9-Box scoped to their department in Phase 1 (today it requires `performance.manage`, so most line managers cannot reach it).
    - **employee** ŌĆö their own Goals page, the Reviews page filtered to "Reviewee" so they only see what's about them, the Acknowledge button on a submitted manager review, and the Contracts page filtered to their own contract for signing. They do not see Calibration, PIPs, or the 9-box.
    - **auditor** ŌĆö read-only on every page in the group via `performance.view`, but no `manage` / `calibrate` / `pip_manage` powers.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö every contract, goal, review, calibration adjustment, and PIP hangs off exactly one `employee_id`; Leave (Ch 4) ŌĆö approved leave during a cycle is visible to the reviewer through the employee detail page and is one of the inputs a manager weighs at end-cycle; Learning & Development (Ch 7) ŌĆö a low rating on a competency-tagged KPI is the trigger the Phase-1 learning recommender will use; Establishment (Ch 8) ŌĆö the annual step-increment job (`StepIncrementService`) reads the latest closed-cycle manager review and gates the increment on a rating Ōēź 3.0, the one place in the MVP where Performance is wired to compensation; Off-boarding (Ch 9) ŌĆö closing a PIP with the `failed_terminated` outcome is the documented hand-off into the off-boarding case; Reports & Analytics (Ch 31) ŌĆö the 9-box matrix, rating distribution, and PIP success/termination counts feed the institute-wide leadership disclosures.

## The screens

![Performance Analytics ŌĆö masthead, KPI strip, hires/headcount velocity chart, department efficiency bars, top resolvers panel](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/analytics.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Editorial-Sovereign masthead ŌĆö "PERFORMANCE LEDGER" eyebrow, "Performance Management" title, a one-line summary of the four sub-modules (contracts, mid-cycle check-ins, calibration, PIPs), and two outline buttons that deep-link to Reviews and Goals. ┬Ę ŌØĘ A two-row analytics canvas ŌĆö hires-and-headcount-velocity line + area (cobalt fill) over twelve months, a tenure-distribution vertical bar set (active staff by years of service), a horizontal department-efficiency chart (ticket resolution rate per department, with the average resolution-hours number in the corner), and a four-segment leave-mix donut. ┬Ę ŌØĖ A self-driven live sync: every 15-20 seconds (jitter to avoid herd) the page issues a partial Inertia reload of just the `analytics` prop, so the numbers refresh without a full repaint and without trampling the user's scroll. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ The Top Resolvers panel ŌĆö six avatars with gold/silver/bronze rank dots for the top three, plus the closed-ticket count beside each name. The first card carries the institutional 5 % gold accent because "average achievement" is the metric the page exists for. ┬Ę ŌØ║ A live ticket-volume trend (mini line chart) and an approved-leave-volume LiveBars chart sit side-by-side at the bottom of the page.*

![Goals ŌĆö hero cards, "My / My Team / Org" segments, goal list with check-in entry](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/goals.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero strip ŌĆö four StatCards (Active Goals in cobalt, At Risk in red, Completed in green, Avg Achievement in gold). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Three-segment ribbon ("My / My Team / Org") that switches scope without leaving the page; for an `employee` only "My" is enabled. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Filter strip ŌĆö debounced free-text search, employee picker, cycle picker, status dropdown, "Clear" link that appears only when at least one filter is set. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Goal cards ŌĆö title, cycle pill, cadence pill, weight, the derived progress bar (computed live as `current_value / target_value ├Ś 100`), the status pill (Draft / Active / At Risk / Completed / Cancelled, each with its own colour from the `GoalStatus` enum), and a "Check in" inline action that opens a small panel for narrative + mood (green / amber / red). Reporting amber or red on an active goal auto-flips the goal's status to **At Risk** ŌĆö the system reads the signal the employee gave it rather than forcing them to file two updates.*

![Reviews ŌĆö hero KPIs, rating distribution chart, reviewer/reviewee tabs, review cards with submit/ack actions](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/reviews.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero ŌĆö four KPI cards (In Progress, Awaiting Feedback, Pending Acknowledgement in magenta as the "people-side action" colour, Completed) plus a five-bin rating histogram on the right computed client-side from the loaded reviews. ┬Ę ŌØĘ "Reviewee / Reviewer / All" tabs ŌĆö employees see only "Reviewee" by default and the other tabs are hidden; HR with `performance.manage` sees all three. ┬Ę ŌØĖ "New Review" slide panel ŌĆö cycle dropdown (defaults to active cycle), employee picker, reviewer picker (auto-populates with the signed-in user when type = self), type dropdown (`self`, `manager`, `peer`, `skip_level`, `upward`), three 1-5 rating sliders (overall, performance, potential), three large textareas (strengths, opportunities, comments). ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Per-review row actions ŌĆö "Submit" turns a draft into Submitted (and fires the `ReviewSubmitted` event); "Acknowledge" is shown only to the subject employee on a Submitted review and is the one signal the system has that the conversation took place.*

![Contracts ŌĆö index with filters, "Avg Achievement" gold KPI, contract rows with status pills](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/contracts_index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Four StatCards across the top ŌĆö Total Contracts (navy), In Evaluation (amber), Closed This Cycle (green), Avg Achievement (gold institutional accent). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Filter strip ŌĆö search (matches employee name and `employee_no` via cross-driver LOWER LIKE), cycle dropdown, status dropdown over the full `PerformanceContractStatus` set (Draft / Pending Signature / Active / Under Evaluation / Achieved / Missed / Cancelled). ┬Ę ŌØĖ "New Contract" slide panel ŌĆö cycle, employee, supervisor, and a JSON KPI textarea (the MVP shape ŌĆö Phase 1 swaps this for an editable KPI table). ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Each row links to the contract detail page; the status pill is rendered through the shared StatusBadge component so the colour palette matches every other status pill in the system.*

![Contract detail ŌĆö KPI table, signature workflow card, end-cycle evaluation form](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/contracts_show.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ KPI table ŌĆö one row per KPI with name, scorecard quadrant pill (financial / customer / process / learning, when set), weight (%), target + unit, actual (filled in at evaluation), and per-KPI score (capped at 100 % per KPI). Total weight is shown next to the heading; the service refuses to send a contract whose weights don't sum to exactly 100. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Workflow card ŌĆö "Send for signature" (Draft only), "Revoke to draft" (Pending Signature with no signatures yet), "Sign contract" (Pending Signature, signer must be the employee or the supervisor on the row), and the dual-signature status line that turns green segment by segment as each party signs. ┬Ę ŌØĖ End-cycle evaluation form (Active contracts only, gated on `performance.manage` + a fresh 2FA challenge) ŌĆö one numeric input per KPI for "actual", plus an end-year-note textarea. On save the service computes `╬Ż(weight ├Ś score) / ╬Ż(weight)` and transitions the contract to **Achieved** (Ōēź 60 %) or **Missed** (< 60 %).*

![Calibration ŌĆö sessions index with stats, distribution-vs-target chart, adjustment table](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/calibration.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Three StatCards ŌĆö Open Sessions (amber), Closed This Cycle (green), Total Adjustments (gold ŌĆö the institutional outcome the page produces). ┬Ę ŌØĘ "New session" slide panel ŌĆö cycle, optional department (leave blank for an org-wide session), and (in Phase 1) a target-distribution editor; today the system seeds the Ghana public-service default 10/25/40/20/5 split (5Ōśģ ŌåÆ 1Ōśģ) automatically when none is supplied. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Distribution-vs-target chart on the session detail page ŌĆö five rows (5Ōśģ ŌåÆ 1Ōśģ), each with the target percentage in dimmed navy and the actual percentage in either amber (over target) or emerald (at or under target) so the facilitator can see at a glance whether the cohort is rated too generously. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Reviews-in-scope table ŌĆö every manager-type review with a non-null rating in the selected cycle (and optionally department), each row showing the employee's name, original rating, and an inline "Adjust" form that captures the new 1-to-5 rating and a reason. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Three buttons drive the workflow: **Lock session** (facilitator only, freezes adjustments), **Reopen** (facilitator on a locked session), **Apply** (a different user with `performance.calibrate_apply` + a fresh 2FA challenge ŌĆö the service refuses if applier and facilitator are the same person).*

![PIPs ŌĆö open PIPs index with stat band, PIP detail with metrics, check-ins, extend/close actions](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/pips.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Four StatCards ŌĆö Open PIPs (amber), In Progress (cobalt), Succeeded YTD (gold ŌĆö institutional improvement success), Terminated YTD (red). ┬Ę ŌØĘ "Open PIP" slide panel ŌĆö employee picker, optional mentor (an Employee, not a User ŌĆö mentors are colleagues, not just account holders), duration in days (30-180, default 90), and a JSON textarea for target metrics. The service refuses a second open PIP for the same employee ŌĆö there can only be one in flight at a time. ┬Ę ŌØĖ PIP detail page ŌĆö masthead with the editorial volume / day-of-year label, the employee, mentor, opened-on and target-end dates, a live "days remaining" computation, and a coloured stage label that walks Open ŌåÆ In Progress ŌåÆ Extended ŌåÆ (Succeeded / Demoted / Terminated / Cancelled). ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Check-in form (required note 5-2000 chars, "met target" boolean) appends to the JSON `checkins` array on every save and flips the status from Open to In Progress on the first one. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Extend form ŌĆö additional days (14-90, capped at one extension by default), required reason. ┬Ę ŌØ╗ Close form ŌĆö outcome (Succeeded / Demoted / Terminated / Cancelled), required summary (10-5000 chars), 2FA-fresh challenge before commit, and the outcome label drives the next step in Off-boarding (Ch 9) when termination is chosen.*

![9-Box ŌĆö performance ├Ś potential grid with employee chips](../assets/screenshots/06_performance/nine_box.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Cycle picker at the top (defaults to the active cycle). ┬Ę ŌØĘ A 3├Ś3 grid ŌĆö rows are potential (high ŌåÆ medium ŌåÆ low), columns are performance (low ŌåÆ medium ŌåÆ high). Each cell carries the canonical talent-management label (Future Leader, Growth Employee, Enigma, High Performer, Core Player, Inconsistent, Trusted Professional, Effective, Risk), a one-sentence prescriptive description ("Stars: highest potential AND highest performance. Plan succession.") and a tinted background that signals zone (star / invest / develop / core / watch / risk). ┬Ę ŌØĖ Employees appear as small chips inside their cell, with name, position, department, and their average performance / potential scores. The placement is deterministic: every submitted review for the cycle is averaged per employee on both axes and bucketed (Low 1.00-2.33, Medium 2.34-3.66, High 3.67-5.00).*

> The eight screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.22). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Goals (`/performance/goals`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **My / My Team / Org segments** | Three-pill ribbon. "My" is loaded by default; the other two re-fire the Inertia request with a different `employee_id` filter (or no filter for Org). For an employee without `performance.manage`, only "My" is active. | Anyone with `performance.view` | Goals are the most-visited Performance page and almost every visit starts with "what am I committed to?". The default segment answers it in one paint. |
| **Search box** ("Search goalsŌĆ”") | Free-text filter that fires 380 ms after you stop typing. Matches `goals.title` with a LIKE scan. | Same | Most goal lookups are by title ŌĆö debounced search beats refresh-on-every-keystroke for feel and DB load. |
| **Employee picker** | Filters the list to one person. Hidden when the active segment is "My". | `performance.manage` or the dept_head viewing their reports | A manager landing on the page wants to flick through their team one person at a time. |
| **Cycle dropdown** | Filters the list to one cycle. The current active cycle is pre-selected if `?cycle_id=` isn't already set. | Anyone | Cycle is the natural slice for performance work ŌĆö H1 2026, H2 2026, probation cycles, etc. |
| **Status dropdown** | Filters by `Draft`, `Active`, `At Risk`, `Completed`, or `Cancelled`. | Same | "At Risk only" is the most-asked-for view for HR and dept heads. |
| **Clear** (appears only when a filter is set) | Resets all four filters and re-queries. | Same | One-click undo. |
| **New Goal** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel. Fields: employee (auto-set to self), cycle (auto-set to active cycle), title (required, Ōēż 200 chars), description (Ōēż 5000), cadence (`annual / half_year / quarterly / monthly / weekly`), target value, current value, unit, weight (0-100), starts_at, due_at. | `performance.manage`, or the employee for their own goal (the FormRequest's `authorize()` enforces this) | Goal-setting is the entry point to the rest of the module ŌĆö anything else (reviews, contracts, PIPs) builds on the goals an employee committed to at the start of the cycle. |
| **Goal card ŌåÆ Check-in** | Inline form, posts to `POST /performance/goals/{goal}/checkins`. Fields: progress %, current value, narrative (5-5000 chars), mood (green / amber / red). The service writes a `GoalCheckin` row, mirrors `current_value` back onto the parent goal so cards refresh, and **auto-flips the goal status to At Risk** if mood is amber or red on an Active goal. | `performance.manage` or the goal's own employee | Mid-cycle check-ins are how the system catches a slipping goal early ŌĆö the mood field is intentionally three colours, not five, so the choice is unambiguous. |
| **Goal card ŌåÆ Edit** | Re-opens the slide panel with the row pre-filled; PATCHes `/performance/goals/{goal}`. Setting status to Completed also stamps `completed_at = now()` if it wasn't already set. | `performance.manage` or the goal's own employee | Targets shift mid-cycle; the panel handles that without an extra page. |
| **Goal card ŌåÆ Delete** | Confirm dialog ŌåÆ `DELETE /performance/goals/{goal}`. Soft-delete via the `SoftDeletes` trait. | Same | Same one-way safeguard as employee delete; the row is recoverable from the console. |
| **Pagination** (bottom) | Standard 20-per-page; `withQueryString()` so filters persist through page changes. | Anyone | Keeps the page snappy on institutes with thousands of goals per cycle. |

> *Notes:* The controller's `goals` action eager-loads `employee.user`, `cycle`, and the single most-recent check-in per goal, so the card grid renders without an N+1. The "progress %" you see on each card is the model's derived `progressPct()` attribute ŌĆö it is **not** stored, it is computed from `current_value / target_value` on every render so a stale `progress_pct` column can never lie to you. Ownership of the check-in and update endpoints is enforced twice: at the `authorize()` layer of the FormRequest and again as a defensive `abort_unless()` inside `PerformanceController` (added in the audit-v2 tier-3 supplement, item 27) because the parent route is gated on `performance.view`, which is broader than "I am the subject of this goal".

### Reviews (`/performance/reviews`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Reviewee / Reviewer / All tabs** | Three-pill tab strip. "Reviewee" filters to reviews about me; "Reviewer" filters to reviews I wrote; "All" removes both filters. Non-managers only see "Reviewee". | Anyone with `performance.view` | The natural two perspectives an employee has on the page ŌĆö and the third is HR's view. |
| **Cycle / Type / Status dropdowns** | Filter the list to one cycle, one review type (`self / manager / peer / skip_level / upward`), or one status (`draft / submitted / acknowledged`). | Same | "All submitted manager reviews for the active cycle" is the dashboard a calibration facilitator opens straight to. |
| **Rating distribution histogram** | A five-bin client-side histogram of `overall_rating` (rounded) across the loaded rows. | Anyone | Visual cue that the cohort's rating mix is normal ŌĆö a healthy distribution looks like a small bulge at 3-4; rating inflation looks like a wall at 5. |
| **New Review** (cobalt CTA) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for capturing a new review.
