Fields: cycle (defaults to active), employee, reviewer (auto-fills with the current user when type = `self`), type, three 1-5 sliders (overall, performance, potential ŌĆö performance and potential drive the 9-box, overall is the headline number that calibration acts on), three large textareas (strengths, opportunities, comments). | `performance.manage`, or the signed-in employee for their own self-review (the `StoreReviewRequest::authorize()` checks both `employee_id` and `reviewer_id` match the user) | Self-review is the first half of the conversation; manager / peer / skip-level / upward fill in the other voices. |
| **Submit** | PATCH `/performance/reviews/{review}/submit` ŌĆö flips status to Submitted, stamps `submitted_at`, fires `ReviewSubmitted` event. The reviewer must be the user submitting, or have `performance.manage`. | Reviewer or `performance.manage` | Two-stage save (Draft ŌåÆ Submitted) means a reviewer can park a half-finished review and come back to it. |
| **Acknowledge** | PATCH `/performance/reviews/{review}/ack` ŌĆö flips status to Acknowledged, stamps `acknowledged_at`. Only the subject employee can press it. | The subject employee only | The acknowledgement is the one piece of evidence the system has that the review conversation actually happened ŌĆö required for the rating to count toward calibration. |
| **New Cycle** | Opens a small panel for creating a new `ReviewCycle`. Fields: name ("H1 2026"), cadence (annual / half_year / quarterly / probation), starts_at, ends_at (must be after starts_at), three optional due dates (self / peer / manager), status (defaults Draft). | `performance.manage` | Cycles are the calendar spine the rest of the module hangs off. |
| **Close Cycle** | PATCH `/performance/cycles/{cycle}/close` ŌĆö flips status to Closed, stamps `closed_at`. | `performance.manage` | Closing the cycle is what makes its reviews eligible for the annual step-increment job (see "How it talks to other modules"). |

> *Notes:* `(cycle_id, employee_id, reviewer_id, type)` is unique on the `reviews` table ŌĆö the same reviewer cannot file two separate manager reviews on the same employee in the same cycle, which prevents accidental double-rating during calibration. The 9-box page uses **only** submitted/acknowledged reviews; drafts are invisible to it. `ReviewSubmitted` is dispatched on submission so downstream listeners (e.g. notifications, analytics) can react without the controller knowing about them ŌĆö today no listener is registered for the event, but the wiring is in place.

### Performance Contracts (`/performance/contracts`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Filter strip** (search, cycle, status) | Same shape as the goals filters. Search is a cross-driver LOWER LIKE against `employee_no` and the joined `users.name`. | Anyone with `performance.view` | The contracts list is the cycle-management surface ŌĆö HR uses the cycle + status filter to find every "Active" contract due for evaluation. |
| **New Contract** (cobalt CTA) | Opens a slide panel for drafting a contract. Fields: cycle, employee, optional supervisor (when blank, the employee's manager from the org chart is used at sign-time), and a KPI list. Each KPI carries `id`, `name`, `weight` (0-100), `target` (Ōēź 0), optional `unit`, optional `scorecard` quadrant (one of `financial / customer / process / learning`). The service refuses a contract whose weights do not sum to exactly 100. | `performance.manage` only | The PSC framework (Public Service Commission, Performance Management Policy Framework, Cabinet April 2015) requires a written, signed contract with weighted KPIs at the start of every cycle. This is the panel where it gets written. |
| **Send for signature** | Workflow button shown only on Draft contracts. Refuses if KPIs are empty. Flips status to **Pending Signature** so the employee and supervisor can sign. | `performance.manage` | One-click hand-off to the two signatories. |
| **Revoke to draft** | Shown only on Pending-Signature contracts with no signatures yet. Returns the contract to Draft so KPIs can be amended; once any signature is recorded the button disappears and the contract has to be Cancelled instead. | `performance.manage` | The "I sent it too early" undo ŌĆö without this, a typo in the KPI weights would force HR to cancel and recreate, losing the linkage. |
| **Sign contract** | Workflow button shown to the employee and supervisor whose names are on the contract. Stamps `employee_signed_at` or `supervisor_signed_at` (whichever applies to the signer), and once **both** are stamped, auto-transitions the contract to **Active** and stamps `finalised_at`/`finalised_by`. | The named employee or the named supervisor only | The two-signature gate is the audit-defensible record that both sides agreed to the KPIs before the cycle started. |
| **End-cycle evaluation form** | Shown only on Active contracts. One numeric "actual" input per KPI (any value Ōēź 0). On submit the service walks every KPI, computes `score_i = min(100, max(0, actual_i / target_i ├Ś 100))`, then a weighted overall `╬Ż(weight ├Ś score) / ╬Ż(weight)`, writes the score and overall back to the row, and transitions the contract to **Achieved** (Ōēź 60 %) or **Missed** (< 60 %). | `performance.manage` plus a fresh 2FA challenge | End-cycle evaluation is the moment the cycle "closes" for that employee; the dual gate (permission + 2FA) raises the bar because the resulting number drives step increments, calibration adjustments, and (Phase 1) compensation decisions. |
| **End-year note** | Optional 5000-char textarea on the evaluation form. Saved to `end_year_note` on the contract. | `performance.manage` | A narrative companion to the number, for the staff file. |
| **Mid-year note** | Same shape, on the `mid_year_note` column. The column exists; the editing UI for it is Phase 1 work. | `performance.manage` | The PSC cycle includes a mid-year review; the data model is ready for the form. |

> *Notes:* `(cycle_id, employee_id)` is unique on the `performance_contracts` table ŌĆö the `draft()` service uses `updateOrCreate()` keyed on that pair, so re-drafting for the same cycle replaces the working copy rather than producing duplicates. Weighted-achievement scoring is deterministic ŌĆö the same `actuals` array always produces the same overall, with no randomness, no rounding drift, and no manager override on the formula. Manager override on the final rating is what **Calibration** is for, and it happens on the `Review` row, not on the `PerformanceContract`.

### Calibration (`/performance/calibration`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Sessions list** | Paginated list of calibration sessions across all cycles. Each row shows the cycle name, department (or "Org-wide"), facilitator, status (Draft / In Progress / Locked / Applied / Cancelled), and the count of adjustments captured. | `performance.calibrate` or `performance.calibrate_apply` | A facilitator landing on the page wants to pick up where they left off. |
| **New Session** | Opens a slide panel. Fields: cycle, optional department (blank = org-wide), optional target distribution (defaults to the Ghana public-service 10/25/40/20/5 split ŌĆö 10 % at 5Ōśģ, 25 % at 4Ōśģ, 40 % at 3Ōśģ, 20 % at 2Ōśģ, 5 % at 1Ōśģ). | `performance.calibrate` | Calibration sessions are scheduled, not ad-hoc ŌĆö opening one starts a clock. |
| **Distribution-vs-target chart** | Five-row chart on the session detail page. For each band (5Ōśģ ŌåÆ 1Ōśģ) it draws the target percentage in dimmed navy and the actual percentage in either amber (over target) or emerald (at or under target). | Anyone with view rights on the session | The visual answer to "is this manager rating their team too generously?" ŌĆö the most important question the room needs to ask. |
| **Reviews-in-scope table** | Lists every manager-type review with a non-null `overall_rating` in the selected cycle (filtered by department when the session is scoped). Each row shows the employee, the original rating, the adjusted rating (when one has been entered), the reason, and an "Adjust" form. | Anyone with view rights | The working surface ŌĆö every review the facilitator might want to nudge. |
| **Adjust** (inline form, per row) | Captures `adjusted_rating` (1.00-5.00, the service clamps it) and an optional reason (Ōēż 1000 chars). Posts to `POST /performance/calibration/{session}/adjust`. The service writes a `CalibrationAdjustment` row keyed by `(session_id, review_id)` (using `updateOrCreate` so re-adjusting overwrites in place), stamping the original rating, the adjusted rating, the reason, and the adjuster. Refuses if the session is not In Progress. | `performance.calibrate` | The whole point of the session is the adjustment record; original-side-by-side-with-adjusted is the audit trail. |
| **Lock session** | Workflow button shown on In Progress sessions. Stamps `locked_at`, flips status to Locked, freezes adjustments. | The facilitator | Lock is the "we've debated enough, freeze the decisions" signal ŌĆö and the gate for the dual-control apply. |
| **Reopen** | Workflow button shown on Locked sessions only (refuses on Applied). Returns the status to In Progress and clears `locked_at` so adjustments can be edited again. | The facilitator | One more "we noticed an error in the locked sheet" undo ŌĆö but only before Apply, after which Review rows have already been written. |
| **Apply** | Workflow button shown on Locked sessions. Walks every adjustment in the session and writes `adjusted_rating` back into `reviews.overall_rating` in a single transaction. Stamps `applied_at`, `applied_by`, flips status to Applied. | `performance.calibrate_apply` + a fresh 2FA challenge, **and** the applier MUST be a different user from the facilitator (the service throws a DomainException if they are the same). | Dual control. The facilitator (typically an HR business partner) sets the adjustments; a second user (typically the HR director or CEO) carries them through. This is the most consequential write in Performance Management ŌĆö every rating that drives a step-increment or a PIP trigger has been through this gate. |

> *Notes:* `CalibrationService::actualDistribution()` is a pure read ŌĆö it always computes the live distribution from the underlying reviews, so the chart never drifts from the data. The default 10/25/40/20/5 split is a constant on the service (`DEFAULT_DISTRIBUTION`); Phase 1 surfaces a per-cycle override in the cycle edit form.

### PIPs (`/performance/pips`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **PIPs list** | Paginated list with status filter and three stat tiles at the top ŌĆö Open PIPs (open + in_progress + extended count), Succeeded YTD, Terminated YTD. | `performance.pip_manage` for the list, plus the subject employee for their own | PIPs are the most-sensitive workflow in Performance Management ŌĆö keeping the list scoped to the people who need to act on it is intentional. |
| **Open PIP** (cobalt CTA) | Opens a slide panel. Fields: employee, optional triggered-by review (links the PIP back to the rating that caused it), optional mentor (an Employee ŌĆö typically a senior colleague who agrees to coach), duration in days (30-180, default 90), target metrics array (each metric needs a `metric` name and a `target`; up to 10 metrics). | `performance.pip_manage` only | The PSC framework and Ghana Labour Act ┬¦63 both require a documented improvement opportunity before any non-disciplinary termination; this is the panel that produces the record. The service refuses a second open PIP on the same employee. |
| **Check-in** | Required note (5-2000 chars), required "met target" boolean. Appends to the JSON `checkins` array. The first check-in transitions the PIP from **Open** to **In Progress**. | `performance.pip_manage` | The check-in cadence is what differentiates a real PIP from a paper one; the system is built to capture every conversation. |
| **Extend** | Additional days (14-90), required reason. Refuses once `extensions_used >= max_extensions` (default 1) or once the PIP is in a terminal state. Bumps `extensions_used`, recalculates `target_end_date`, appends an `[EXTENSION] reason` entry to the check-ins, and flips status to **Extended**. | `performance.pip_manage` | One-time extension is the typical Ghana practice ŌĆö more than that and the org has decided to keep employing the person; the cap prevents the PIP becoming a perpetual sword. |
| **Close** | Outcome (Succeeded / Demoted / Terminated / Cancelled), required summary (10-5000 chars), 2FA-fresh challenge. Stamps `actual_end_date`, writes the summary. Refuses to close an already-terminal PIP. | `performance.pip_manage` + 2FA | The 2FA gate is the dual-control echo ŌĆö closing a PIP as "terminated" is the most consequential write in the module, and the right point to insist on a fresh challenge. |

> *Notes:* `triggered_by_review_id` is the back-reference from a PIP to the rating that caused it ŌĆö Phase 1 surfaces this as a button on the manager's view of a low-rated review ("Open a PIP for this employee"). Closing with `failed_terminated` is the documented hand-off into Off-boarding (Ch 9); the linkage is by employee_id today, and Phase 1 makes it a direct foreign key so the off-boarding case automatically references the PIP file. The check-ins blob is JSON rather than a child table to keep PIP detail rendering a single query ŌĆö there are typically Ōēż 10 check-ins on a 90-day PIP and the audit trail value comes from the chronological narrative, not from per-check-in querying.

### 9-Box (`/performance/nine-box`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Cycle picker** | Re-fires the request for a different cycle. Defaults to the active cycle. | `performance.manage` | The grid is cycle-scoped ŌĆö a Future Leader last cycle may have slipped to Core Player this cycle, and that's exactly the conversation the chart exists to surface. |
| **3├Ś3 grid** | Rows are potential (high / medium / low), columns are performance (low / medium / high). Each cell carries a canonical label, a one-sentence prescription, a zone tint, and the list of employees who landed in it. Bucketing is fixed: Low Ōēż 2.33, Medium 2.34-3.66, High Ōēź 3.67. | Anyone with view rights on the page | The 9-box is the institute-wide talent map; the prescription text on each cell is deliberately concrete ("Stars: highest potential AND highest performance. Plan succession.") so the conversation in the room has a starting point. |
| **Employee chips** | Each cell renders its employees as small chips with name, position, department, and the two computed averages. | Same | Naming names is the point ŌĆö anonymous bubbles in a 9-box are pretty graphs that do not move careers. |

> *Notes:* The placement is deterministic and computed per render ŌĆö there is no stored `nine_box_cell` column, so a re-rated employee (after a calibration adjustment is applied) moves automatically. Drafts are excluded ŌĆö only submitted and acknowledged reviews contribute. An employee with no rated review for the selected cycle does not appear on the grid at all (rather than landing in Risk by default, which would be misleading).

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores performance data in seven core tables, all keyed off `employee_id` and `cycle_id`:

- **`review_cycles`** ŌĆö the calendar spine. Name (e.g. "H1 2026"), cadence (`annual / half_year / quarterly / probation`), start and end dates, three optional due dates (self / peer / manager), status (`draft / active / closed`), the user who opened it, the timestamp it was closed.
- **`goals`** ŌĆö the working units. Belongs to an Employee and (optionally) a Cycle, supports parent/child cascades (`parent_goal_id` references the same table ŌĆö that's the KPI-cascade column waiting to be wired into the Phase 1 cascade UI), title, description, cadence, target value, current value, unit, weight (0-100), status enum, start and due dates, completed timestamp. Soft deletes.
- **`goal_checkins`** ŌĆö the audit trail. Goal id, user id, progress %, current value, narrative, mood (`green / amber / red`), recorded-at timestamp. No soft deletes ŌĆö check-ins are append-only.
- **`reviews`** ŌĆö the rating records. Cycle id, employee id (subject), reviewer id (the User filling it in), type (`self / manager / peer / skip_level / upward`), three independent 1.00-5.00 ratings (overall, performance, potential), three text blocks (strengths, opportunities, comments), status (`draft / submitted / acknowledged`), submitted-at and acknowledged-at timestamps. Unique on `(cycle_id, employee_id, reviewer_id, type)` so the same reviewer cannot stack two manager reviews on the same person in the same cycle.
- **`performance_contracts`** ŌĆö the PSC contract record. Cycle and employee, optional supervisor, status enum with seven values, a JSON `kpis` blob carrying the KPI list (`{ id, name, weight, target, unit, scorecard, actual, score }`), an optional JSON `balanced_scorecard` blob, a decimal `weighted_achievement`, two timestamp signatures (`employee_signed_at`, `supervisor_signed_at`), the drafter / finaliser users, and mid-year / end-year note text columns. Unique on `(cycle_id, employee_id)`.
- **`calibration_sessions`** ŌĆö one row per session. Cycle, optional department, status enum (`draft / in_progress / locked / applied / cancelled`), facilitator user, three timestamps (opened, locked, applied), applier user, JSON `target_distribution`, free-text notes.
- **`calibration_adjustments`** ŌĆö one row per (session, review) pair. Both ratings as `decimal(3,2)`, optional reason, the adjuster user, the adjusted-at timestamp. Unique on `(session_id, review_id)` so re-adjusting the same review in the same session overwrites the previous nudge rather than producing two adjustment trails.
- **`performance_improvement_plans`** ŌĆö one row per PIP. Employee, optional `triggered_by_review_id`, opener user, optional mentor employee, status enum (Open / In Progress / Succeeded / Extended / Failed Demoted / Failed Terminated / Cancelled), opened/target-end/actual-end dates, extensions used + max, two JSON blobs (target metrics, check-ins), an outcome summary text column. Soft deletes.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **The KPIs JSON blob on a contract is not a child table.** It is intentional: a Performance Contract is a *signed document* at a point in time, and the KPIs ARE the contract. Editing them after signature would be like editing a signed PDF ŌĆö the system refuses. The "kpis" column carries the agreed list; the `actuals` array posted at evaluation is merged in at scoring time but the original weights, targets, and names are never re-keyed.
- **The 1.00-5.00 scale is shared by three different ratings on the Review row.** `overall_rating` is what calibration acts on and what the step-increment job reads; `performance_rating` and `potential_rating` are the two axes of the 9-box. A manager review with only `overall_rating` filled in will appear in calibration but not on the 9-box.
- **Dual control on calibration is enforced in the service, not in the policy.** The `apply` policy method only checks the permission. The "applier must differ from facilitator" check is a hard `DomainException` thrown by `CalibrationService::apply()` ŌĆö defensive depth, because the permission alone is not enough.
- **PIPs are append-only on check-ins.** Once a check-in is added, the only way to remove it is a soft-delete of the entire PIP row. This is deliberate ŌĆö the check-in log is the evidence the employee was given a fair chance, and editing it out after a termination would defeat the purpose.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Step Increment job (Ch 8)** ŌåÆ `App\Services\Establishment\StepIncrementService` runs nightly and reads the most recent closed-cycle manager-type review per employee. If `overall_rating Ōēź 3.0` the step bumps by 1; otherwise the anniversary still rolls forward (so they're re-evaluated next year) and the step stays put. This is the **only** automatic link from Performance to compensation in the MVP ŌĆö promotions, bonuses, merit pay, and grade changes are all still manual decisions outside the system today.
- **`ReviewSubmitted` event** ŌåÆ fired the moment a reviewer presses Submit. No listener is registered today; the wiring exists so that Phase 1 can hang a "new review awaiting your acknowledgement" notification on the subject employee without changing the controller.
- **`GoalCreated` event** ŌåÆ fired the moment a goal is saved. Same shape ŌĆö wired but unlistened. Phase 1 picks this up for the cascade-suggestion engine and for the analytics rollup.
- **Calibration ŌåÆ Reviews** ŌåÆ applying a locked calibration session writes adjusted ratings directly back to the `reviews.overall_rating` column in a single transaction. The 9-box and the step-increment job re-read the underlying review, so a calibration adjustment moves an employee's 9-box placement and step-increment eligibility automatically on the next render / nightly run.
- **PIPs ŌåÆ Off-boarding (Ch 9)** ŌåÆ closing a PIP as `failed_terminated` is the documented hand-off; the off-boarding case today is opened manually with the PIP's outcome summary pasted in, and Phase 1 makes it a direct foreign-key linkage.
- **Performance Contract ŌåÆ Reviews** ŌåÆ today the contract's `weighted_achievement` and the reviews' `overall_rating` are independent. Phase 1 introduces a "auto-populate manager review from contract evaluation" path so that an employee who is Achieved on their contract starts with a 4-star draft manager review.
- **Employees (Ch 3) detail page** ŌåÆ the Overview tab on a person's detail page reads through to their goals, reviews, and (Phase 1) their open contract and any active PIP, so a manager can see the whole picture without bouncing between tabs.

## What's missing in the MVP

A short, honest list ŌĆö every item below is in the Phase-1 backlog (Chapter 46):

- **No 360 / multi-source feedback aggregation.** The `ReviewType` enum has the slots ŌĆö `peer`, `skip_level`, `upward` ŌĆö and the storage works. There is no orchestration that *asks* the system to send a 360 packet to the four peers, two direct reports, and one skip-level a given employee nominated; today every review row is created one-at-a-time by a human pressing "New Review". Phase 1 adds the campaign/invitation layer.
- **No KPI-cascade UI.** `goals.parent_goal_id` is in the schema; the relationships are wired in the model (`parent()` and `children()`); no page surfaces them yet. So today the cascade is a paper exercise.
- **No mid-year contract review form.** The `mid_year_note` column exists; the end-year evaluation form exists; the mid-year form is Phase 1.
- **No compensation / promotion linkage beyond step increments.** The step-increment job is the only automated wire-up. Bonuses, merit pay grids, and promotion eligibility flags driven off the 9-box are all Phase 1.
- **No manager dashboards beyond the analytics page.** The institute-wide Performance Analytics page exists; per-manager span-of-control with rating distribution is Phase 1 work in the Reports & Analytics module (Ch 31).
- **No automatic PIP suggestion.** A review whose rating falls below 2.5 does not automatically prompt the manager to open a PIP. Phase 1 surfaces a button on the manager's view of a low-rated review.

## Standards touchpoints

- **PSC Performance Management Policy Framework (Cabinet, April 2015)** ŌĆö performance contracts with signed KPIs for Public Service Organisation heads are mandatory under this framework. CIHRMS extends the same pattern to every employee. The migration's docblock cites this directly.
- **Balanced Scorecard (Kaplan & Norton, 1992)** ŌĆö KPIs carry an optional `scorecard` quadrant of `financial / customer / process / learning`, and the contract detail page renders the quadrant pill next to the KPI name. The validation rule on `StoreContractRequest` enumerates exactly those four values.
- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.4 (workforce productivity)** ŌĆö every metric on the Performance Analytics page (hires velocity, headcount by department, department efficiency, average resolution hours, top resolvers) is one of the disclosures recommended under ┬¦4.4. The companion analytics chapter (Ch 31) feeds these into the institute-wide ISO 30414 report.
- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.5 (leadership)** ŌĆö the 9-box matrix, span-of-control (Phase 1), and rating distribution per manager are ┬¦4.5 disclosures. The 9-box is the placement chart the standard explicitly suggests for leadership identification.
- **Ghana Labour Act 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦17, ┬¦63** ŌĆö non-disciplinary termination requires documented prior notice and improvement opportunity. The PIP module is the documented audit trail; the migration's docblock cites the section directly. Closing a PIP as `failed_terminated` is what the Labour Act expects to see in court if the termination is challenged.
- **Data Protection Act 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17, ┬¦18, ┬¦40** ŌĆö performance data is personal data. The system relies on the employment-contract basis under ┬¦17, applies data-minimisation under ┬¦18 (calibration adjustments record the *reason* alongside the rating change, so the rating change is justified by data not by hunch), and supports the right-to-erasure under ┬¦40 through the soft-delete on every Performance table ŌĆö true erasure is handled by the separate redaction job (Ch 26).
- Forward to Chapter 44 (Standards Benchmark) for the line-by-line mapping of each Performance Management deliverable against PSC, ISO 30414, and Act 651/843 clauses.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap (8-10 weeks, see Chapter 46) lands six things directly on these screens: (1) a **360 campaign engine** that, given a cycle and an employee, creates the self / peer / manager / skip-level / upward review rows in one shot and tracks completion; (2) a **KPI-cascade UI** that lets a manager nominate a goal as a child of their own and lets HR see the resulting tree; (3) the **mid-year contract review form**, writing to the `mid_year_note` column already in the schema; (4) a **PIP trigger button** on every low-rated review (Ōēż 2.5) that pre-fills the Open-PIP panel with the triggering review id and a sensible default duration; (5) **per-manager dashboards** with span-of-control, rating distribution, and a personal calibration view so a department head can see how their cohort compares before walking into the calibration room; (6) a **compensation linkage** that reads the contract's `weighted_achievement` and the latest review's `overall_rating` into the Phase-1 merit-pay grid (Ch 19), so an Achieved contract with a 4-star manager review becomes a recommended 5 % merit increase rather than a manual spreadsheet exercise.

<!-- ===== 07_learning.md ===== -->

# Chapter 7 ŌĆö Learning & Development

> *In one paragraph.* Learning & Development is the institute's catalogue of internal and external courses, the place where every employee can enrol themselves and log their progress, and the place where HR / L&D can publish new courses, watch skills accumulate across departments through a heat-map, and record the external certifications people walk in with. The gap analysis (Chapter 44) classified an LMS as Phase-3 work, on the grounds that government bodies in Ghana usually pay for a separate platform. In practice the team built a leaner version inside CIHRMS during Wave 13: three pages, two tables, one event-driven skill grant, and one nightly job that reminds people their certifications are about to expire. It is not a content-delivery LMS ŌĆö videos, PDFs, SCORM packages, quizzes, and graded assessments are not stored or played by CIHRMS itself ŌĆö but it is a working register of who has learned what, what they are working on right now, and what credentials they hold, and that turns out to be the part the institute most needs first.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Learning** group (cyan colour code, "school" icon). Expanding the group reveals three entries ŌĆö **Catalogue** (the landing page at `/learning`), **My Learning** (`/learning/my`), and **Skills Matrix** (`/learning/skills-matrix`). The group is gated behind `learning.view`; the Skills Matrix entry carries its own `visible: can('learning.manage')` flag so an ordinary employee never sees a dead link to a page they cannot enter.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every page, every course (including drafts), every enrolment, every certification.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö every page including the Skills Matrix; `learning.manage` lets them publish, edit, unpublish, and delete courses, issue certifications on behalf of any employee, and add new skills to the institute-wide skill catalogue.
    - **dept_head / manager** ŌĆö Catalogue and My Learning plus the Skills Matrix (they carry `learning.manage` in the seeded role grants). They can publish a course, but practically the institute reserves that to HR / L&D.
    - **employee** ŌĆö Catalogue (published courses only) and My Learning. They can enrol themselves, log their own progress, mark themselves complete, and record external certifications against their own employee row. They do not see the Skills Matrix.
    - **finance_officer**, **it_support**, **marketing**, **auditor** ŌĆö `learning.view` only. They can browse the catalogue and use My Learning; they cannot publish, manage, or open the Skills Matrix.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö every enrolment, certification, and granted skill hangs off exactly one `employee_id`, and the same `EmployeeSkill` rows the Skills Matrix reads from are the ones that render on the Employee detail page's Overview tab; Performance (Ch 6) ŌĆö a low rating on a competency-tagged KPI is the trigger the Phase-1 learning recommender will use, and completed courses appear in the calibration-room context for any manager weighing why a previously low rating has improved; Establishment (Ch 8) ŌĆö grade-based competency requirements (a Grade-14 head of internal audit must hold the IA professional cert before stepping in) live in the establishment policy text today and are surfaced through certifications on the employee row; Recruitment (Ch 9) ŌĆö new hires are intended to land on an "Onboarding" category of courses on day one, though the auto-assignment is not yet wired; Reports & Analytics (Ch 31) ŌĆö the workforce-learning metrics on the institute-wide ISO 30414 export read from the same `enrolments` and `certifications` tables this chapter describes; Governance (Ch 27, in part) ŌĆö certification expiry reminders are routed through the same `RecordAnalyticsEvent` listener as policy acknowledgements because both are evidence of statutory compliance.

## The screens

![Course Catalogue ŌĆö hero banner, KPI tiles, filter strip with skill chips, course-card grid](../assets/screenshots/07_learning/catalog.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero banner ŌĆö institutional navy gradient, "Learning library ┬Ę live" eyebrow, big "{n} courses ┬Ę {h}h of content" headline, and three colour-coded KPIs (Published in green, Enrolments in cyan, Hours in gold). Numbers respect the viewer's permissions: an ordinary employee sees only published courses, an HR admin sees drafts too. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Four StatCards under the hero ŌĆö Total courses (cobalt), Published (cyan), My enrolments (magenta), Catalogue hours (gold). ┬Ę ŌØĖ Filter strip ŌĆö debounced free-text search (matches title, description, and provider), category dropdown over the seven `CourseCategory` enum values, format dropdown over the four `CourseFormat` enum values, and a "Clear" link that appears only when at least one filter is set. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Skill-tag chip cloud ŌĆö up to twelve unique tags pulled from the visible cards. Clicking a tag adds an in-browser filter (a card has to carry every selected tag), clicking again removes it. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Course card grid ŌĆö each card has a coloured 28-px hero strip tinted by category, a category badge, a "Draft" amber pill on unpublished cards (visible to managers only), a duration chip, the title and provider, a two-line description, up to four skill-tag pills, a difficulty pill (when set), the format icon and label, the live enrolment count, and three actions in the footer ŌĆö **Enrol / Continue**, **Publish** (managers only, drafts only), **Delete** (managers only, with confirm). ┬Ę ŌØ╗ Clicking anywhere on a card body opens a right-hand "Course Detail" SlidePanel with the same content laid out for reading rather than scanning, plus a prominent "Enrol Now" CTA.*

![My Learning ŌĆö welcome hero with stats, expiry-alert strip, stat cards, active enrolments with progress rings, certifications grid, completed courses list](../assets/screenshots/07_learning/my_learning.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero card ŌĆö institutional navy gradient with a dot-pattern overlay, "Welcome back, {first name}", and three big inline counters (In Progress, Completed, Certs Earned). When at least one certification expires within 60 days an amber alert strip drops below the hero saying "{n} certification(s) expiring within 60 days ŌĆö renew soon." ┬Ę ŌØĘ Four StatCards under the hero ŌĆö In Progress (cobalt), Completed (green), Certifications (purple), Expiring within 60 days (amber). ┬Ę ŌØĖ Active Enrolments panel ŌĆö one card per pending/active enrolment with an SVG progress ring on the left (colour shifts from grey ŌåÆ amber ŌåÆ cobalt ŌåÆ green as the percentage climbs), the course title and provider, three meta chips (duration, "Enrolled {date}", optional "Due {date}"), a thin horizontal progress bar that mirrors the ring, and a full-width **Continue Learning** button that opens the progress slide-over. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Certifications Earned grid ŌĆö each card shows a diploma-style header bar (purple "verified" badge, certificate name, issuer), an expiry pill on the right (red "Expired", amber "{n}d left" at 30 / 60-day buckets, green "Valid"), credential ID, issue date, expiry date, and a **Verify** action that opens the external `verification_url` in a new tab when one was recorded. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Completed Courses list ŌĆö compact rows below the certifications grid showing each finished course with category icon, title, provider, final score (when set), a green "Completed" pill, and a **Certificate** download link when the system stored a generated PDF path on the enrolment row.*

![Update Progress slide-over ŌĆö course summary, draggable slider, four quick-set buttons, optional final-score field at 100%](../assets/screenshots/07_learning/progress.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Course summary block at the top ŌĆö coloured icon tile in the category tint, course title and provider. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Range slider 0-100 with the current percentage shown big on the right; the slider's accent and a thin visual progress bar below it track the live value as the learner drags. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Four "quick-set" buttons (25 / 50 / 75 / 100) that snap the slider to common milestones. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ When the slider hits 100 a Final Score (optional, 0-100) field appears with the line "Setting 100 % will mark this course as completed and add its skills to your profile." This is the trigger that fires the `CourseCompleted` event and the `GrantSkillsOnCourseCompletion` listener.*

![Skills Matrix ŌĆö stat band, filter strip, sticky-column heatmap, proficiency legend sidebar, overall coverage bar](../assets/screenshots/07_learning/skills_matrix.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Four StatCards across the top ŌĆö Skills Tracked (purple), Critical-skill Coverage (green), Employees Tracked (cobalt), Skill Gaps with 0 skills (amber). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Filter strip ŌĆö employee-name / role search, department dropdown sourced from the matrix data, a separate "Filter skillsŌĆ”" search that narrows the columns, a "Top 15 skills" toggle (defaults on, so the page is readable on day one), a "Show gaps only" toggle that tints the rows amber and restricts the list to people with fewer than three skills, and an "Export CSV" button. ┬Ę ŌØĖ The heatmap ŌĆö sticky left column with employee name / position / department, one column per filtered skill with the header rotated 40 ┬░ so a hundred skills are still readable, a sticky right-hand "Total" column with a coloured pill (amber when zero, cobalt when 1-4, green when Ōēź 5). Each cell carries a 6-px rounded square coloured in five intensities of cobalt from L1 Novice through L5 Expert (the matrix accepts both numeric 1-5 levels and the legacy `beginner / intermediate / advanced / expert` strings; an extra fallback panel under the main legend shows the legacy mapping). ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Legend sidebar ŌĆö sticky on scroll, with the five proficiency intensities, the legacy labels, and a duplicate Export CSV button at the foot. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Coverage summary bar at the bottom ŌĆö a single percentage with the underlying ratio ("{n} mapped / {m} with no skills") and a horizontal progress bar painted in the institutional cobalt-to-indigo gradient.*

![Publish Course slide panel ŌĆö title, description, category + format, provider + duration + price, skill-tag chip input, publish-immediately toggle](../assets/screenshots/07_learning/publish.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ "Course Title" (required, max 200) ŌĆö the only required free-text field. The slug is auto-generated by the model's `saving` hook from the title plus a six-character hex suffix, so two courses titled "Procurement Basics" coexist cleanly. ┬Ę ŌØĘ "Description" (optional, 8 000 chars) ŌĆö what learners will gain. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Category and Format selects, side-by-side, both required and validated against the `CourseCategory` and `CourseFormat` enums. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Provider / Duration / Price row ŌĆö provider is free text ("GIFMIS Academy", "Internal", "Coursera"); duration is in minutes and renders as "1h 30m" on the catalogue card via the model's `durationLabel` accessor; price defaults to 0 in GHS and is currently informational only ŌĆö there is no checkout in CIHRMS. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Skill-tag chip input ŌĆö type a tag, press Enter or comma, repeat. Up to 20 tags per course, each up to 60 characters. These tags are the strings that become `EmployeeSkill` rows on every learner who completes the course ŌĆö the system's quiet bridge between Learning and the Skills Matrix. ┬Ę ŌØ╗ "Publish immediately" toggle (on by default) ŌĆö a draft course is invisible to ordinary employees; toggling on at create-time sets `is_published = true` and stamps `published_at = now()` in the same transaction.*

> The five screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.23). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Course Catalogue (`/learning`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Publish Course** (navy CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand "Publish New Course" SlidePanel. | `learning.manage` (typically hr_admin, manager, dept_head, super_admin, ceo) | Catalogue management is HR / L&D's primary workflow on this page; promoting it to the top-right CTA matches every other module in CIHRMS. |
| **My Learning** (outline link, top right) | Navigates to `/learning/my`. | `learning.view` | Shortcut from the public-facing catalogue back to the learner's personal dashboard. |
| **Search box** ("Search courses, providersŌĆ”") | Free-text filter that fires 380 ms after you stop typing. Matches `title`, `description`, and `provider` with `LIKE` scans. | Anyone with `learning.view` | Most catalogue lookups are by title or provider ŌĆö debounced search beats a refresh-on-every-keystroke for feel and database load. |
| **Category dropdown** | Filters by one of the seven `CourseCategory` enum values (Technical, Leadership, Compliance, Wellness, Onboarding, Soft skills, Other). Fires immediately on change. | Same | Compliance and onboarding lookups are the most common reason a learner narrows the list. |
| **Format dropdown** | Filters by one of the four `CourseFormat` enum values (Self-paced, Instructor-led, Blended, External). | Same | Self-paced learners and classroom learners want very different shortlists. |
| **Clear** (appears only when a filter is set) | Resets the three filters and the tag chips, then re-queries. | Same | One-click undo when an experiment didn't pan out. |
| **Skill chip** (in the tag cloud under the filters) | Toggles a client-side filter: a card has to carry every selected tag to remain visible. | Same | Skill-based browsing is the second-most-asked-for view after category; the chips stay on the page rather than in the URL because they re-filter the already-loaded result set without a round trip. |
| **Card body click** (anywhere on a card) | Opens the right-hand "Course Detail" SlidePanel with the full title, description, provider, skill tags, prerequisites (data column is in the resource for Phase 1 ŌĆö UI is wired but the column is not migrated yet), and a prominent "Enrol Now" CTA at the foot. | Same | Whole-card hit target is faster than chasing a small "view" button and matches the directory-row pattern from Chapter 3. |
| **Enrol / Continue** (per-card button) | POSTs to `/learning/courses/{course}/enrol`. If the learner already has an enrolment row the service is `firstOrCreate`, so the click is idempotent and the button label flips to "Continue". For drafts the button is disabled unless the viewer has `learning.manage`. | `learning.view` + an Employee record on their user; `learning.manage` to enrol from a draft | The single highest-frequency action on the page ŌĆö promoting it to a coloured CTA in every card is deliberate. |
| **Publish** (eye-with-tick icon, drafts only) | PATCH to `/learning/courses/{course}/publish`. Sets `is_published = true` and stamps `published_at = now()` if the row didn't already carry one. | `learning.manage` | Two-step authoring ŌĆö save a draft, review it, publish it ŌĆö is the documented workflow. The icon-only affordance keeps the catalogue's card density. |
| **Delete** (trash icon) | Confirms via `window.confirm("Delete \"{title}\"?")`, then DELETEs the course. The Course model carries `SoftDeletes`, so the row stays in the database (recoverable from the console) but disappears from the catalogue, the Skills Matrix, every learner's My Learning, and the enrolment counts. | `learning.manage` | Mistaken publishes happen; soft-delete keeps the audit trail intact while removing the row from every list. |

### Course Detail SlidePanel

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Category badge** | Read-only ŌĆö name and colour from `CourseCategory::color()`. | Anyone viewing | At-a-glance grouping. |
| **Format chip / Difficulty chip / Price chip / "Free" chip** | Read-only meta. "Free" replaces the price chip when `price = 0`. | Anyone viewing | Three answers the learner usually wants before they commit (instructor vs self-paced, hard or easy, will it cost me). |
| **About this course** | Renders the `description` text as a wrapped paragraph. | Anyone viewing | Long-form copy that wouldn't fit on the card. |
| **Provider** | Coloured icon well + provider name (auto-mapped from the column). | Anyone viewing | Some courses live with external partners; the provider name doubles as a click-target in Phase 1 for sso-into-Coursera. |
| **Skills you'll gain** | Renders each skill tag as a pill ŌĆö the same tags that will be added to the learner's profile on completion. | Anyone viewing | Translates the tag list from a developer detail into the learner's headline ("what will I be able to do?"). |
| **Prerequisites** | Bulleted list driven by a `prerequisites` array. The column is not yet migrated, so today the list is always empty ŌĆö Phase 1 adds a `prerequisites` text array to the courses table and a UI for capturing it. | Anyone viewing | Stub for the gap; the UI ships ahead of the data so the column lands without UI churn. |
| **Enrol Now** (footer CTA) | Same call as the card-level Enrol ŌĆö POST to `/learning/courses/{course}/enrol`. Closes the panel on success. | `learning.view` + an Employee record | The detail page is often the last screen before a commitment; the prominent CTA mirrors that intent. |

### Publish Course slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Course Title** | required, max 200 | `learning.manage` | Saved verbatim; the slug is auto-generated by the model's `saving` hook from `Str::slug($title)` plus a six-character hex suffix, so duplicate titles do not collide on the URL. |
| **Description** | optional, max 8 000 | Same | Long-form copy. |
| **Category** | required, one of the `CourseCategory` enum values (`technical`, `leadership`, `compliance`, `wellness`, `onboarding`, `soft_skills`, `other`) | Same | Drives the card-strip colour and the category icon. The Compliance category is the one the Phase-1 mandatory-training engine keys off (see "What's missing"). |
| **Format** | required, one of the `CourseFormat` enum values (`self_paced`, `instructor_led`, `blended`, `external`) | Same | Drives the format icon on the catalogue card; `external` is the label the institute uses for Coursera / edX / LinkedIn-Learning entries with no internal lesson content. |
| **Provider** | optional, max 120 | Same | Free text. |
| **Duration (min)** | optional, integer 0 - 100 000 | Same | Rendered on the card as "1h 30m" via the model's `durationLabel` Eloquent accessor. |
| **Price (GHS)** | optional, decimal Ōēź 0; currency defaults to GHS, 3-letter codes accepted | Same | Informational ŌĆö there is no checkout in CIHRMS. Price > 0 renders the GHS price chip on the detail panel; price = 0 renders the green "Free" chip. |
| **Skill tags** | optional array up to 20, each tag max 60 chars | Same | The strings that become `EmployeeSkill` rows on every learner who completes the course. Entered as a chip-input (Enter or comma to commit a tag, click the ├Ś to remove). |
| **Cover image** *(URL ŌĆö column is in the schema; the UI does not capture it today)* | optional, max 255 | Same | Falls back to a tinted gradient on the card hero when blank. The Phase-1 catalogue editor adds an upload field. |
| **Publish immediately** | boolean, defaults to true | Same | When true, the same transaction sets `is_published = true` and stamps `published_at = now()`; when false, the row is a draft only the manager can see until they click the publish icon on the catalogue card. |

> *Notes:* The whole create is one POST to `/learning/courses`, mediated by `StoreCourseRequest`. The FormRequest's `authorize()` requires `learning.manage`, the rule set is straight Laravel validation, and the service simply spreads the validated payload into `Course::create()` with `created_by` set to the actor's user id. There is no inline edit panel in the MVP ŌĆö the route exists (`PATCH /learning/courses/{course}`) and `UpdateCourseRequest` is wired with `sometimes` rules so partial updates are first-class, but the UI presently only exposes the publish-toggle and delete actions on the catalogue card. A "full edit panel" is the smallest Phase-1 add.

### My Learning (`/learning/my`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Browse Catalogue** (outline link, top right) | Navigates to `/learning`. | Anyone viewing | Round-trip back to the catalogue. |
| **Add Certification** (navy CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand "Add External Certification" SlidePanel. The button is disabled when the viewer has no Employee record on their User ŌĆö the system needs an `employee_id` to attach the cert to. | Anyone viewing | Lets people log credentials they earned outside CIHRMS (ACCA, CPA, professional licences) so the institute has a single picture of who holds what. |
| **Hero card** | Read-only welcome strip with three inline counters (In Progress, Completed, Certs Earned). Computed in the controller as `whereIn('status.value', ['pending', 'active'])->count()`, `where('status.value', 'completed')->count()`, and `certifications->count()`. | Anyone viewing | Identity-and-summary in one band; greeting the learner by first name is a deliberate "this is your space" signal. |
| **Expiry alert strip** | Drops below the hero when `stats.expiring > 0`. Counts certifications with `days_to_expiry > 0 AND Ōēż 60`. | Anyone viewing | Compliance gating belongs above the fold ŌĆö most renewals get missed because nobody saw they were due. |
| **Stat cards** (In Progress / Completed / Certifications / Expiring 60 d) | Read-only KPI tiles, same numbers as the hero. | Same | Same four numbers offered in a higher-density layout for a quick re-read. |
| **Active enrolment card click** | Inert ŌĆö the card itself is not navigable in the MVP. | Anyone | Course-detail navigation lives back on the catalogue today; Phase 1 turns the card into a link to a dedicated "Course Player" page. |
| **Continue Learning** (per card) | Opens the right-hand "Update Progress" SlidePanel pre-filled with the enrolment's current percentage and last-set final score. | The owner of the enrolment, or any user with `learning.manage` | The single most-used action on the page; promoting it to a full-width navy CTA in every card mirrors the catalogue's Enrol button. |
| **Verify** (per certification card) | Opens `verification_url` in a new tab. The link is only rendered when the column is non-null. | Anyone viewing | External issuers (ACCA, ISACA, CIPS) all expose a per-credential verification page; CIHRMS does not duplicate the verification logic, it stores the URL and links out. |
| **Certificate download** (per completed-course row) | Anchors to `/storage/{certificate_path}` in a new tab. The link only renders when the column is non-null. | Anyone viewing | When the course's completion flow produced a generated PDF (uploaded by HR or by the external provider), this is how the learner retrieves it. The CIHRMS MVP does not generate the PDF itself ŌĆö the column carries a path that an HR-attached PDF lives at. |

### Update Progress slide-over

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Progress slider** | required, numeric, 0 Ōēż value Ōēż 100 | The owner of the enrolment, or any user with `learning.manage` | Drives the cobalt ŌåÆ green progress ring and the bar. |
| **Final Score** | optional, numeric, 0 Ōēż value Ōēż 100; appears only when the slider is at 100 | Same | Stamped into `enrolments.final_score`; renders as "Score: 87.5%" on the Completed Courses list. |
| **Save Progress** (footer CTA) | PATCH to `/learning/enrolments/{enrolment}` | Same | The handler is `LearningController::recordProgress`; the controller adds a defence-in-depth ownership check on top of the FormRequest, then delegates to `LearningService::recordProgress($enrolment, $pct)`. The service clamps the input to [0, 100], stamps `last_activity_at = now()`, flips the status from Pending to Active the first time progress crosses zero, and ŌĆö when the value reaches 100 ŌĆö wraps a savepoint around `completeEnrolment()` so the progress bump and the completion are atomic (a failure inside the inner transaction rolls the outer "progress to 100" back too, which keeps the row from drifting to "100 % Active"). On the same call the `CourseCompleted` event fires, the `GrantSkillsOnCourseCompletion` listener queues, and the learner's profile starts gaining the course's skill tags within the next worker tick. |

### Add External Certification slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Certification Name** | required, max 200 | The employee on their own row, or `learning.manage` for anyone | The label that appears on the certification card. |
| **Issuing Organisation** | optional, max 120 | Same | Free text ŌĆö "ACCA", "ISACA", "Coursera". |
| **Credential ID** | optional, max 120 | Same | The cert number. Rendered in mono-font on the card. |
| **Issue Date** | optional, valid date | Same | Used for the "Issued" line on the card. |
| **Expiry Date** | optional, valid date, must be on or after the Issue Date | Same | Drives the expiry pill (red / amber / green) and the 60-day expiry alert strip on the hero. |
| **Verification URL** | optional, valid URL, max 255 | Same | When set, the **Verify** action on the card opens it in a new tab. |
| **Save Certification** (footer CTA) | POST to `/learning/certifications` | Same | The handler is `LearningController::storeCertification`; `StoreCertificationRequest::authorize()` is where the dual-tier check lives ŌĆö the actor either holds `learning.manage` (HR / L&D) or is the employee whose `employee_id` is on the payload (self-service). On save the service inserts the row, fires `CertificationIssued` with the actor User, and returns the fresh model with `employee.user` and `course` eager-loaded for the next paint. |

### Skills Matrix (`/learning/skills-matrix`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Add Skill** (navy CTA, top right) | Opens a right-hand "Add Skill to Catalogue" SlidePanel. | `learning.manage` | The skill catalogue is the master list the matrix's columns come from; HR / L&D add to it as the institute's competencies grow. |
| **Export CSV** (outline link, top right; also at the foot of the table and in the sidebar) | Builds the CSV client-side from the currently-filtered employees ├Ś skills, with a "Total Skills" column on the right, and triggers a download via `URL.createObjectURL`. | `learning.manage` | Offline analysis (a board paper, a printable HR pack) is the single most-asked-for export from this page; the server-side rendering covers the live view, the client-side CSV covers the take-away. |
| **Search box** ("Search employees by name, roleŌĆ”") | Client-side filter against `name`, `position`, and `department`. | `learning.manage` | Fast filtering on a page that may carry hundreds of rows. |
| **Department dropdown** | Client-side filter; the options are computed from the rows the server returned, so a dept_head viewing their team sees only their team's departments (the controller currently does not auto-scope by department ŌĆö see "What's missing"). | Same | Drill-down to one team's skill picture. |
| **Skill search box** ("Filter skillsŌĆ”") | Filters the *columns* of the heatmap; complements the row filter. | Same | A matrix with one hundred skill columns is unreadable; this is how the user narrows it. |
| **Top 15 skills** toggle (on by default) | Restricts the columns to the 15 most-held skills across the visible employees. | Same | The most useful default for the first paint ŌĆö gives a meaningful heatmap rather than a wall of empty cells. |
| **Show gaps only** toggle | Tints rows amber and limits the list to employees with fewer than three skills recorded. | Same | "Who do we need to invest in?" is the question this view answers. |
| **Clear** (appears only when at least one filter is set) | Resets all four filter controls. | Same | One-click undo. |
| **Matrix cell** | Read-only ŌĆö coloured square + numeric (1-5) or legacy-letter (B / I / A / E) label. Hover shows the level name as a tooltip. | Same | Visual coverage at a glance; the colour intensity is the data. |
| **Total column** | Coloured pill ŌĆö amber when 0, cobalt 1-4, green Ōēź 5. | Same | The "is this employee covered at all?" answer in one cell. |
| **Coverage summary bar** | Bottom card ŌĆö one big percentage (`employees with Ōēź 1 skill / total`), the underlying ratio, and a cobalt-to-indigo progress bar. | Same | One-line answer for board reports: "what fraction of the institute has any skill on record?". |

### Add Skill to Catalogue slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Skill Name** | required, max 120, unique on `skill_catalog.name` | `learning.manage` | The string that will appear as a column on the matrix and, via the course's `skill_tags`, on each learner's profile. The unique constraint prevents the "Procurement" vs "procurement" drift. |
| **Category** | optional, one of `technical`, `leadership`, `compliance`, `soft_skills`, `domain` | Same | Used for future filtering of the catalogue itself (not surfaced today). |
| **Description** | optional, max 1 000 | Same | Free text. |

> *Notes:* The catalogue's purpose is not to gate course tags ŌĆö those are free-text in `Course.skill_tags` ŌĆö it is to ensure that even when no employee yet holds a skill, the column for it still appears on the matrix (the service deliberately reads `SkillCatalogItem::pluck('name')` and seeds zero-count columns for any catalogue item not yet held). That's why an L&D admin who adds "Procurement Compliance" to the catalogue sees a new column with count 0 appear in the matrix on the next render ŌĆö the column exists before the first learner does, which lets HR plan the gap rather than discover it after the fact.

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores Learning & Development in five tables, all migrated in Wave 13 (May 2026) with a `reminder_sent_at` column added to certifications in Phase 5 (governance) work three weeks later.

- **`courses`** ŌĆö one row per course. `id`, `title`, `slug` (auto-generated, unique), `description`, `category` enum, `format` enum, `provider` string, `cover_image` URL, `duration_minutes` integer, `price` decimal, `currency` (defaults to `GHS`), `skill_tags` JSON array, `is_published` boolean, `published_at` timestamp, `created_by` foreign key on `users` (`nullOnDelete`), timestamps, soft deletes. Indexed on `(is_published, category)` so the most common filter ŌĆö "published courses in compliance" ŌĆö is a covered scan.
- **`enrolments`** ŌĆö one row per (course, employee) pair, enforced by a unique composite index. `course_id` and `employee_id` both `cascadeOnDelete`, `status` enum (Pending / Active / Completed / Abandoned / Expired), `progress_pct` decimal 0-100, `final_score` decimal 0-100, `certificate_path` string, four timestamps (`enrolled_at` defaulted to `useCurrent()`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, `last_activity_at`), timestamps, soft deletes. Indexed on `(employee_id, status)` so "my active enrolments" is a covered scan.
- **`certifications`** ŌĆö one row per credential held by an employee. `employee_id` (`cascadeOnDelete`), `course_id` (`nullOnDelete` ŌĆö a cert may or may not be linked to a course in CIHRMS, since most are external), `name`, `issuer`, `credential_id`, `issued_at` date, `expires_at` date, `document_path` string, `verification_url` string, `reminder_sent_at` timestamp (added in Phase 5), timestamps, soft deletes. Indexed on `(employee_id, expires_at)` so the per-employee expiry sort is fast, and on `reminder_sent_at` so the nightly job can find unreminded rows cheaply.
- **`skill_catalog`** ŌĆö one row per skill the institute formally tracks. `name` (unique, 120 chars), `category` (40-char string from the same enum as the Add Skill form), `description`, timestamps. No foreign keys ŌĆö the join to `EmployeeSkill` is by *name*, which is deliberate: a skill on a person's profile is allowed to predate its arrival in the catalogue, and the catalogue is allowed to outlive the last person who held a skill. Phase 1 considers tightening this into a real foreign key.
- **`employee_skills`** ŌĆö one row per (employee, skill name) pair. `employee_id`, `name` (the same string the catalogue uses), `level` (`beginner` / `intermediate` / `advanced` / `expert`), `expires_at` date. Created by the listener below; editable by the employee from the Profile portal (Ch 16). Not soft-deleted ŌĆö the row is the live skill, not a record of a past one.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **Enrolment is per-course, not per-lesson.** CIHRMS does not store the lessons inside a course; it does not store videos, PDFs, links, SCORM packages, quizzes, or assessment results. `progress_pct` is a number the learner enters (or that an admin imports). The system trusts the report. Phase 3 ships the content layer; today the catalogue is a register, not a player.
- **The `skill_tags` column on a Course is a free-text JSON array.** It is not a foreign key into `skill_catalog`. Two courses tagged `"Procurement"` and `"procurement"` will create two different `EmployeeSkill` rows. The Phase-1 catalogue editor will tighten the tag input to autocomplete against the catalogue.
- **`final_score` is informational.** There is no pass-mark in the schema. A learner can complete a course at 12 % and the system records it; the question of "did they pass" is a manual one for now.
- **Certificates have expiry, not revocation.** `expires_at` is the only column that tracks the lifecycle of a credential. A revoked-mid-cycle cert has to be soft-deleted (`Certification` carries `SoftDeletes`) ŌĆö the system does not carry a revocation reason column today.
- **Soft delete on every table.** Courses, enrolments, and certifications all carry `SoftDeletes`. A deleted enrolment is recoverable; the same DPA-redaction job (Ch 26) that wipes Employee personal data also wipes the linked Learning rows on a right-to-erasure request.
- **The system does not auto-enrol anybody.** Every enrolment row in the database was created by a person pressing a button ŌĆö either the learner pressing **Enrol** on the catalogue card, or an HR admin issuing an enrolment in their stead. There is no "all employees are mandatorily enrolled in DPA training" pathway. See "What's missing".

## How it talks to other modules

- **`CourseEnrolled` event** ŌåÆ fired the moment an enrolment row is created by the service (with `wasRecentlyCreated` guarding the firstOrCreate against duplicate fires). No listener is registered today; the wiring exists so that Phase 1 can hang a "you've been enrolled" notification on the subject employee without touching the controller.
- **`CourseCompleted` event** ŌåÆ fired the moment an enrolment hits `Completed`. One listener picks it up: **`GrantSkillsOnCourseCompletion`** (queued on the `analytics` queue, auto-discovered by Laravel's listener scanner via the typed `handle()` parameter). It calls `LearningService::grantSkillsFromCourse($enrolment)`, which iterates the course's `skill_tags` and `firstOrCreate`s an `EmployeeSkill` row for each tag at level `intermediate`. The listener logs the number of new skills granted and swallows any throwable into a warning log line so a downstream failure cannot fail the completion call itself.
- **`CertificationIssued` event** ŌåÆ fired the moment a `Certification` row is created. Carries the actor User where one was supplied; today no listener is registered, so the event is a hook for Phase 1's "HR approved the cert" notification.
- **`CertificationExpiring` and `CertificationExpired` events** ŌåÆ dispatched by the **`governance:certification-reminders`** Artisan command, which is scheduled daily at 08:00 in `routes/console.php` and delegates to `GovernanceService::dispatchExpiryReminders($days)`. The service iterates `Certification::needingReminder($days)` in chunks of 100, picks the right event based on whether the expiry has already passed, stamps `reminder_sent_at = now()` so the row is not picked up again, and returns the count to the command. Both events are listened to by **`RecordAnalyticsEvent`** (registered explicitly in `AppServiceProvider::boot()`), which writes an `analytics_events` row so the institute-wide compliance dashboard can chart "how many certs expired this quarter" alongside policy acknowledgement rates.
- **Employees (Ch 3) ŌåÆ Profile Overview tab** reads `EmployeeSkill` rows through the same `skills` relation the Skills Matrix queries, so an employee who just completes a course sees the new skill on their own profile within the same worker tick the listener finishes.
- **Performance (Ch 6) calibration room** ŌĆö managers walking into a calibration session can read each employee's skill list on the employee detail page (Overview tab). Phase 1 surfaces a per-employee "courses completed this cycle" panel on the Reviews page directly.
- **Establishment (Ch 8) grade-based competency** ŌĆö the Phase-1 establishment work picks up `EmployeeSkill` rows against the `position_competency_requirements` table to flag people whose grade requires a skill they do not hold. The schema is in place; the report is not.
- **Reports & Analytics (Ch 31) workforce learning metrics** ŌĆö total enrolments, completions per quarter, distinct learners, average score, and the count of expired certs per department all read directly from `enrolments` and `certifications`. The page is shipped; the export is Phase 1.

## What's missing in the MVP

A short, honest list ŌĆö every item below is in the Phase-1 or Phase-3 backlog (Chapter 46):

- **No in-system content delivery.** No video player, no PDF viewer, no link wrapper, no SCORM player, no xAPI. A "self-paced" course is a row in `courses` with metadata about a thing the learner watches somewhere else. Phase 3's Learning Content Server adds the player; the catalogue and the progress tracker stay as they are.
- **No in-system quizzes or graded assessments.** `final_score` is a single number an admin (or the learner) can write into the row. There is no question bank, no per-attempt history, no anti-cheat, no proctoring. Phase 3.
- **No prerequisites enforcement.** The Course detail panel renders prerequisites if the resource returns any, but the `prerequisites` column is not yet on the `courses` migration and the enrolment service does not gate on it. Phase 1 adds the column; Phase 1 adds the gate.
- **No mandatory-training engine.** A course tagged "Compliance" is not automatically assigned to anybody. There is no rule engine that says "every new hire must complete Anti-Corruption (PSC) within 30 days" or "every staff member with `dept = Internal Audit` must hold the IA cert". Phase 1 wires this with a `MandatoryAssignment` rule table and a daily enforcement job.
- **No compliance dashboards by department / by mandatory course.** The Skills Matrix shows skill coverage; it does not yet show "what fraction of HR-required courses each department has completed". The data is there (`enrolments` joined to `courses` filtered by category); the page is Phase 1.
- **No auto-onboarding curriculum for new hires.** When HR creates an Employee row (Ch 3) the new hire is not auto-enrolled in any course. Phase 1 introduces an `auto_enroll_on_hire` flag on the Course model and an `EmployeeCreated` listener that walks the flag.
- **No recommender driven by Performance gaps.** A low rating on a competency-tagged KPI (Ch 6) does not surface a recommended course. The data shape is in place ŌĆö Performance Review carries `performance_rating` per KPI; Course carries `skill_tags`; Employee carries `skills` ŌĆö but no service reads it. Phase 1.
- **No certificate generation.** When a learner completes a course CIHRMS does not produce a PDF. The `certificate_path` column on `Enrolment` is for an externally-generated file an admin can attach. Phase 1 ships a `dompdf`-backed cert template (it's the same engine the Documents module already uses for letterheads).
- **No certificate revocation tracking.** Lifecycle is expiry-only; if a cert is revoked mid-cycle, soft-deletion is the workaround and the reason lives in HR's notes elsewhere. Phase 1 adds `revoked_at` and `revoked_reason` to the certifications table.
- **No SCORM, xAPI, LTI, or external-LMS sync.** No connectors. Phase 3 picks an open-source SCORM player (likely Articulate's open SCORM Cloud SDK) once the institute's Phase-1 internal-only workflows are stable.
- **No course-edit UI.** The route, the FormRequest, and the controller method exist; the catalogue card does not yet expose an "Edit" affordance. Phase 1's smallest item.
- **No dept_head scoping on the Skills Matrix.** A department head with `learning.manage` sees every employee in the institute, not just their team. The controller passes a `department_id` query parameter through but does not default it from the actor's headed departments. Phase 1.
- **Demo data.** There is no seeder for courses, enrolments, or certifications today. The catalogue is empty on a fresh database ŌĆö the institute's first L&D admin populates it from a CSV import in onboarding, or the Phase-1 demo seeder shipped alongside it.

## Standards touchpoints

- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.6 (training and development)** ŌĆö the disclosures the standard recommends under ┬¦4.6 are: total training hours, training hours per FTE, training cost per FTE, % of employees with development plans, and completion rates for mandatory training. CIHRMS today provides the raw data for the first two (sum `enrolments.completed_at` ├Ś `courses.duration_minutes`, divide by headcount), and the data shape for the last (count `enrolments` whose course has `category = compliance`). The reporting page is shipped via the institute-wide analytics export (Ch 31). Forward to Chapter 44 for the line-by-line mapping.
- **Data Protection Act 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17, ┬¦18, ┬¦40** ŌĆö a learner's enrolment history, progress data, scores, and certifications are personal data. The system relies on the employment-contract basis under ┬¦17; applies data-minimisation under ┬¦18 (no demographic columns on `enrolments`, no IP / device logging on progress updates); and supports right-to-erasure under ┬¦40 through the soft-delete on every Learning table ŌĆö true erasure is handled by the redaction job that walks the linked Learning rows whenever an Employee row is redacted (Ch 26).
- **PSC training policy (Public Services Commission of Ghana, "Training and Development Policy for the Public Services of Ghana", revised 2019)** ŌĆö mandates annual training for every public servant, prioritises induction within the first 12 months, and requires the parent body to maintain a register of all training undertaken. CIHRMS is that register. The mandatory-training engine that turns the policy from "maintain a list" into "enforce a list" is Phase 1 (see "What's missing").
- **WCAG 2.1 AA ŌĆö learning content accessibility** ŌĆö CIHRMS does not deliver content today, so the WCAG obligation in the MVP is restricted to the catalogue and progress pages themselves. The catalogue card uses semantic HTML (`<article>`, `<h3>`, `<button>`), every interactive control has a visible focus ring (the `:focus-visible` styles in the design system), every coloured pill carries its label as plain text alongside the colour cue, and the progress ring's percentage is repeated as a number in the centre of the ring rather than encoded only in the arc length. The bigger WCAG conversation ŌĆö captions on videos, transcripts on audio, alt text on slide imagery ŌĆö is in scope for Phase 3 when CIHRMS itself starts serving content.
- **Ghana Labour Act 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦10** ŌĆö every worker has the right to "be trained and re-trained for the development of his or her skills". CIHRMS does not enforce a training entitlement (e.g. "every employee gets at least 16 hours of training per year"), but the data the analytics export produces lets HR demonstrate the institute is honouring ┬¦10 in aggregate. Forward to Chapter 44.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap (8-10 weeks, see Chapter 46) lands seven things directly on these screens: (1) the **mandatory-training engine** ŌĆö a `MandatoryAssignment` rule table keyed on department, grade, role, or new-hire window, plus a daily enforcement job that creates the missing `Enrolment` rows and a per-department dashboard showing % complete vs target; (2) a **prerequisites column and gate** ŌĆö adding `prerequisites` (JSON array of course IDs) to the `courses` table, surfacing it in the Publish panel, and blocking enrolment when the learner hasn't completed every prerequisite; (3) the **catalogue edit panel** ŌĆö promoting the existing `UpdateCourseRequest` and `PATCH` route into a visible "Edit" affordance on every card, with the same `sometimes`-rule semantics that drove the Employee inline edits in Chapter 3; (4) the **certificate generator** ŌĆö a `dompdf`-driven completion certificate (Course, learner name, final score, issue date, QR-coded verification link) written to `enrolments.certificate_path` automatically on `CourseCompleted`; (5) the **revocation lifecycle** ŌĆö adding `revoked_at` and `revoked_reason` to the certifications table, surfacing a "Revoke" action in the My Learning certifications card for HR, and routing the dispatched `CertificationRevoked` event into the same analytics listener; (6) the **dept_head scoping on Skills Matrix** ŌĆö defaulting `department_id` from the actor's headed-departments list so a department head who lands on `/learning/skills-matrix` sees their team, not the institute; (7) the **Performance ŌåÆ Learning recommender** ŌĆö a per-employee panel on the Reviews page (Ch 6) that reads the lowest-rated `performance_rating` KPI, finds courses whose `skill_tags` overlap the KPI's competency tag, and offers a one-click "Enrol in {course}" button. Phase 3 (12-18 months out) ships the in-system content layer ŌĆö video player, PDF viewer, SCORM 1.2 + xAPI tracking, a quiz engine with pass marks and proctoring, and a WCAG-2.1-AA accessibility pass on every piece of learning content.

<!-- ===== 08_establishment.md ===== -->

# Chapter 8 ŌĆö Establishment (Positions, Grades, Steps, Ceilings)

> *In one paragraph.* Establishment is the approved skeleton of the institute ŌĆö every post the institute is funded to hold, the grade band that post sits on, the salary step inside that band, and the headcount ceiling that says "no more than this many of this grade in this department this fiscal year". The Employees table in Chapter 3 stores **people**; the Establishment tables here store **posts**. A person becomes "established" the moment they are assigned to a post ŌĆö the assignment row is the link, and it carries effective dates so the audit trail can answer "who held this seat on this date" five years later. In Ghana public-sector terms this is the half of the HRMS that exists to satisfy the Public Services Commission, the Controller and Accountant-General's Department (CAGD), and the Auditor-General ŌĆö not the half that pays a salary on the 25th.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** the Positions register is reachable today only by URL (`/positions`) and from the Tier-1 fix test that wires `Establishment/Positions/Show`. The dedicated sidebar group is part of Phase 1's UI follow-up ŌĆö the data layer and the screens ship in this chapter; the nav entry is a separate ticket. Roles that have `positions.view` and land on `/positions` see the full register; roles that don't get a 403 from the route middleware before the Inertia render runs.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every position, every action, including the `establishment.exceed` override that lets them blow through an approved headcount ceiling on purpose (and audit-logged).
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö `positions.view` + `positions.manage` + `grades.manage`. The full CRUD: create posts, assign people, vacate, freeze, edit grade and step tables.
    - **dept_head** ŌĆö `positions.view` only. They can see every approved post in their department, but cannot create, assign, or freeze.
    - **auditor** ŌĆö `positions.view`. Read-only, for the Auditor-General reconciliation pack (Ch 24).
    - **manager / employee / finance_officer / it_support** ŌĆö no establishment permission by default. Finance sees grade-step base salaries indirectly through payroll (Ch 19); everyone else has no business on this page.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö the four dormant columns `current_position_id`, `current_grade_id`, `current_step`, and `step_anniversary_date` are now wired live to the establishment tables; Payroll Engine (Ch 19) ŌĆö pulls base salary directly from the grade ├Ś step lookup via `Grade::baseSalaryFor()`; Performance (Ch 6) ŌĆö the step-increment service refuses to bump a step when the latest closed-cycle review is below 3.0/5.0; Departments (Ch 30) ŌĆö every post rolls up to one department and the establishment ceiling is keyed on (department, grade, fiscal year); Recruitment (Ch 9) ŌĆö job postings reference a position and inherit its grade band; Identity (Ch 25) ŌĆö a Ghana Card-verified employee is a precondition for an Auditor-General-grade assignment record.

## The screens

![Positions register ŌĆö stats band, status filter, table with action buttons](../assets/screenshots/08_establishment/positions_index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Stats band ŌĆö four tiles in a 4-up grid: Total positions, Vacant (warn tone), Filled (success tone), Frozen (neutral). The numbers come from a single page query ŌĆö `Position::count()` plus three filtered counts ŌĆö so the band always reflects the same establishment register the table below shows. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Filter strip ŌĆö one dropdown for status (All / Vacant / Filled / Frozen / Acting). Department and grade filters are accepted by the controller as query string parameters today but the UI exposes only status; the other two are reserved for the Phase 1 UI follow-up. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Position row ŌĆö code (monospace), title, grade code, department name, cost centre, funding source label, and status badge. The action cluster on the right offers Vacate (only when filled or acting), Freeze (anything not already frozen), and Open (always).*

![Position detail ŌĆö attributes header, active assignments table, past assignments history](../assets/screenshots/08_establishment/position_show.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Identity strip ŌĆö back link, "ESTABLISHMENT ┬Ę POSITION" eyebrow, position title, monospace code, department name, and grade name on a single line, with the status badge floated right. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Position attributes card ŌĆö four KPI tiles: Approved headcount (from `headcount_ceiling`), Current fill (count of `position_assignments` rows with `end_date IS NULL`), Reports to (title of the parent position via `reports_to_position_id`), and Grade steps (count of `grade_steps` rows the grade has loaded). The free-text job description renders below the divider if one is set. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Active assignments ŌĆö table of currently-open assignments: employee name, employee number, start date, type (Acting / Substantive), and the assignment reason. A "Position is vacant" empty state replaces the table when no row has a null `end_date`. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Past assignments ŌĆö same table but for closed rows, only visible when at least one closed assignment exists. The whole detail page hydrates from one Inertia render: `PositionController::show` eager-loads `grade.steps`, `department`, `reportsTo`, and `assignments.employee.user`, so there is no N+1 across the two tables.*

> The two screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.21). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Positions register

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Status dropdown** ("All statuses / Vacant / Filled / Frozen / Acting") | Filters the table to one status; fires a new Inertia `router.get` with `preserveState: true` and `replace: true` so the back button still works. | `positions.view` (anyone who can see the page) | Vacant-vs-filled is the question the establishment officer asks first thing every morning; a single dropdown is the fastest way to answer it. |
| **Vacate** (amber inline button, rows where status is `filled` or `acting`) | Opens a `window.prompt` for a reason (required), then POSTs to `/positions/{id}/vacate`.
