The service closes every open `position_assignments` row for that post, clears `employees.current_position_id` for the holders, and flips the position status back to Vacant ŌĆö all inside a single DB transaction. | `positions.manage` | Vacating happens ŌĆö resignations, transfers, terminations. The reason is captured in the same field as the assignment's "reason" so the audit trail says *why* the post is empty, not just *that* it is. |
| **Freeze** (neutral inline button, any row except already-frozen) | Same prompt-then-POST pattern. POSTs to `/positions/{id}/freeze`. The service sets the position status to Frozen and appends "[FROZEN] {reason}" to the job description so the freeze rationale is visible on the detail page without a separate audit join. | `positions.manage` | Frozen posts are how the institute holds an approved headcount line item in reserve ŌĆö usually because the funding has been suspended or the post has been earmarked for the next establishment review. A frozen post cannot be filled (the assign call hard-fails with a `DomainException`) unless the actor holds `establishment.exceed`. |
| **Open** (cobalt link, every row) | Inertia-links to `/positions/{id}` ŌĆö the detail page below. | `positions.view` | Standard affordance. The row is not currently a whole-row click target on this page; the Open button is the only navigation path. |
| **Stats band** (Total / Vacant / Filled / Frozen) | Read-only ŌĆö four scalar counts. | `positions.view` | The five-second answer to "how full is my institute right now?" ŌĆö surfaced above the fold before any scrolling, filtering, or paging. |

> *Notes:* `PositionController::index` paginates 25 per page, eager-loads `grade`, `department`, and `reportsTo`, and orders by `code` (ascending). The `withQueryString()` call on the paginator means status, department, and grade filters survive page navigation. The freeze and vacate POSTs both `preserveScroll`, so the table doesn't jump to the top after an inline action ŌĆö the page-level flash banner is the user's confirmation that something happened.

### Position detail

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back to positions** (top-left link) | Returns to the register. Inertia preserves the table's scroll position and filters. | Anyone viewing | Standard back affordance. |
| **Approved headcount** tile | Read-only ŌĆö `position.headcount_ceiling`. | Anyone viewing | The most-asked question on this page: "how many of these posts are we funded for?" ŌĆö pinned to the top-left of the attributes card. |
| **Current fill** tile | Read-only ŌĆö `assignments.filter(a => !a.end_date).length`. Computed in the Vue template from the eager-loaded assignments array, so the number never lags behind the table below it. | Anyone viewing | The other half of the same question: "and how many of them are sitting in seats?". When Current fill equals Approved headcount the position is materially full; when it exceeds it the `establishment.exceed` permission has been used and an audit row exists explaining why. |
| **Reports to** tile | Read-only ŌĆö title of the parent position via `reports_to_position_id`. | Anyone viewing | Gives the org-chart context without requiring a full chart render. |
| **Grade steps** tile | Read-only ŌĆö count of `grade_steps` rows loaded with the position's grade. | Anyone viewing | Surfaces *how many salary steps the grade carries* so the reader knows the band's depth. The actual step table is consumed by Payroll, not displayed here in the MVP. |
| **Job description** block | Read-only, free-text. Renders below the KPI tiles only when `job_description` is set. Freeze reasons are appended to this field by the service. | Anyone viewing | The job description is also the freeze ledger ŌĆö the latest freeze stamp is always at the bottom of the text, so a reader can see *why* the post is in its current status without leaving the page. |
| **Active assignments** table | Read-only ŌĆö employee name, employee number, start date, type (Acting / Substantive), and reason. Empty state when no rows are open. | Anyone viewing | The substantive vs. acting distinction matters for Auditor-General reporting ŌĆö acting appointees draw acting allowances under Single Spine rules, not the substantive base salary. The badge colour is deliberate (amber for acting, neutral for substantive) so the difference is visible at a glance. |
| **Past assignments** table | Read-only ŌĆö employee, start, end, type. Only renders when at least one closed assignment exists. | Anyone viewing | The five-year-back audit answer is here: who held this seat, between which dates, in what capacity, and (in the active table) for what stated reason. |

> *Notes:* The detail page is read-only in the MVP UI ŌĆö there is no in-page form to assign, vacate, or freeze from the detail view. All three actions are reachable via the inline buttons on the register and via the POST routes for API consumers. The split is deliberate: the detail page is for *reading* the history; the register is for *acting* on the present.

### Create a position (API today, slide-panel in W1.21)

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Code** | required, max 64, unique on `positions.code` | `positions.manage` | The establishment code ŌĆö e.g. `HR-DIR-001`, `FIN-OFF-001`. Public-sector audits trace posts by code, not title, so this is the load-bearing identifier and must be globally unique. |
| **Title** | required, max 255 | Same | The human-readable post title ŌĆö "HR Director", "Senior Accountant III". |
| **Grade ID** | optional, must exist on `grades` | Same | Foreign key to the `grades` table. Optional in the data layer so legacy imports can land before grades are mapped; required by establishment-ceiling enforcement (no grade, no ceiling check). |
| **Department ID** | optional, must exist on `departments` | Same | Same logic ŌĆö optional in the schema, required in practice for ceiling enforcement. |
| **Reports to position ID** | optional, must exist on `positions` (self-reference) | Same | The org-chart parent. Used by the "Reports to" tile and by the supervisor-aware approval routing in Phase 2. |
| **Cost centre** | optional, max 32 | Same | Free-text ŌĆö typically the department code followed by `-ops`, `-cap`, or a project code. Pulled by the payroll engine into the GIFMIS export (Ch 19). |
| **Funding source** | required, one of `gog`, `igf`, `donor`, `idf`, `other` (the `FundingSource` enum) | Same | The Ghana public-sector funding classification: Government of Ghana consolidated fund, Internally Generated Funds, Donor / development partner funded, Internal Development Fund, Other. The Auditor-General pack groups headcount and payroll cost by this attribute. |
| **Status** | required, one of `vacant`, `filled`, `frozen`, `acting` (the `PositionStatus` enum) | Same | A freshly created post is almost always `vacant`; the assign action flips it to `filled` (or `acting` if the assignment is marked acting). Setting `frozen` on create is unusual but legal ŌĆö it reserves a slot without making it fillable. |
| **Headcount ceiling** | required, integer between 1 and 100 | Same | The per-position ceiling ŌĆö for most posts this is 1 (one Director of Finance, one HR Officer #001). A "Senior Driver" post might carry a headcount of 12. Note: this is the *per-position* ceiling, not the per-department-grade ceiling ŌĆö that one lives on `establishment_ceilings` and applies across positions. |
| **Is supervisory** | boolean | Same | Drives org-chart rendering and a future supervisor allowance in payroll. |
| **Job description** | optional, free text | Same | Free-form. Freeze reasons are appended to the same field by the service; HR can edit either at any time. |

> *Notes:* The store endpoint is wired (`POST /positions`) and authorised by `StorePositionRequest::authorize()` against `positions.manage`. The slide-panel UI for creating a position from the register is on the Wave 1 backlog (W1.21); for now positions are seeded via `EstablishmentDemoSeeder`, imported via Tinker, or created by an admin POST through the same route the future panel will use.

### Assign an employee to a position

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Employee ID** | required, must exist on `employees` | `positions.manage` (via `PositionPolicy::assign`) | The employee being assigned. The service closes any open assignment that employee already holds ŌĆö a person is in exactly one substantive post at a time. |
| **Is acting** | boolean (defaults false) | Same | When true, the position's status flips to `acting`, not `filled`. The assignment row has `is_acting = true` so the Single Spine acting allowance can be calculated by payroll (Ch 19). |
| **Reason** | optional string, max 255 | Same | Free-text ŌĆö `assignment`, `transfer`, `promotion`, `acting`, `demotion` are the conventional values. Saved verbatim to the assignment row; surfaces on the detail page's active and past tables. |

> *Notes:* The assign service is the single point of write for *any* establishment change ŌĆö promotion, transfer, demotion, acting appointment, substantive confirmation all hit the same code path with a different `reason` value. The transaction (a) re-fetches the position under a row lock, (b) re-checks the establishment ceiling for `(department, grade, fiscal_year)` under the same lock to close the TOCTOU window two concurrent HR creates would otherwise leak, (c) closes any open assignment for the employee, (d) inserts the new `position_assignments` row with `start_date = today` and the employee's current step copied to `step_at_start`, (e) flips the position status to `filled` or `acting`, and (f) updates the employee's `current_position_id` and `current_grade_id` pointers. If anything in those six steps fails ŌĆö duplicate row, exceeded ceiling, foreign-key violation ŌĆö the whole assignment rolls back. The ceiling check is bypassed only when the actor holds `establishment.exceed`; the bypass itself is audit-logged so the Auditor-General sees both the override and the override-er.

## The data behind it

The establishment register spans five tables, all created in one migration on 2026-05-25:

- **`grades`** ŌĆö the pay-band catalogue. Each row carries a unique `code` (`GS-08`, `GS-20`, etc.), a `name` (`Junior Officer`, `Director`), an integer `level` for ordering org charts, and the `min_step` / `max_step` range that bounds the band. Soft-deletable so a retired grade can be hidden without breaking historical payroll lookups. The demo seeder lays down seven grades ŌĆö GS-08 through GS-20 ŌĆö modelled on the Ghana Single Spine Salary Structure ranks; production deployments are expected to overwrite the seed with the institute's actual approved scale, but the *shape* ŌĆö coded grades with min/max step ranges ŌĆö is fixed by the schema.
- **`grade_steps`** ŌĆö the per-grade salary table. Each row pins a `(grade_id, step, effective_from)` triple to a `base_salary` decimal and a currency (defaults to GHS). The composite unique key includes `effective_from`, which is how the system handles a Single Spine pay revision: the old row keeps its `effective_to` set, a new row carries the new `base_salary` from the revision date forward, and `Grade::baseSalaryFor(step, date)` picks the right one. The demo seeder lays down an 8-step ladder per junior grade (and 4-step or 6-step for senior grades) at a 4.5% increment per step ŌĆö a stand-in for the real Single Spine table that the institute will load on deployment.
- **`positions`** ŌĆö every approved post. The row carries the `code`, the `title`, foreign keys to `grade_id`, `department_id`, and `reports_to_position_id` (a self-reference for the org chart), the `cost_center`, the `funding_source` enum, the `status` enum, the `headcount_ceiling`, the `is_supervisory` flag, and the free-text `job_description`. Soft-deletable so a deprecated post can be archived without losing the assignment history that linked employees to it.
- **`position_assignments`** ŌĆö the history table. One row per assignment, with `position_id`, `employee_id`, `start_date`, `end_date` (null while active), `is_acting`, `step_at_start` (frozen at the moment of assignment), and `reason`. Indexed both ways ŌĆö `(position_id, employee_id)` for "who has held this post" and `(employee_id, start_date)` for "what posts has this person held". This is the table that makes the system audit-ready: every promotion, transfer, acting appointment, and substantive confirmation lands here with effective dates.
- **`establishment_ceilings`** ŌĆö the approved-headcount table, keyed `(department_id, grade_id, fiscal_year)`. Each row carries the `approved_headcount` integer and a free-text `approval_reference` (typically the PSC letter or Cabinet decision number authorising the slot). The unique constraint on the triple means a department can have exactly one approved ceiling per grade per fiscal year ŌĆö the establishment officer cannot accidentally double-set it.

In addition, the migration adds four columns to `employees`:

- **`current_position_id`** ŌĆö foreign key to the position the employee currently holds. Set by the assign service; cleared by the vacate service.
- **`current_grade_id`** ŌĆö foreign key to the grade. Set by the assign service to the position's grade.
- **`current_step`** ŌĆö the step within the grade. Untouched by assignment; advanced by the nightly step-increment job.
- **`step_anniversary_date`** ŌĆö the date the next step bump is due. Advanced one year forward by the increment job whether the step actually moves or not.

The four fields were already in the `employees` migration as "dormant" placeholders (Chapter 3 calls them out by name); the 2026-05-25 establishment migration is what brings them online with real foreign keys and a real increment job.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **A position is not a person.** It is a funded slot. An employee fills it through an assignment row; emptying the slot does not delete the employee, and deleting the employee does not delete the position. This separation is the whole point of establishment control ŌĆö it is what lets the Auditor-General reconcile "approved posts" against "warm bodies" and surface ghost workers.
- **A position can be vacant *and* still cost money.** A frozen post does not draw a salary, but its headcount line item is still on the approved establishment. The Auditor-General reads "approved minus filled minus frozen = funded vacancies" and asks the institute to justify any gap.
- **The ceiling is enforced under a row lock.** Two concurrent HR creates targeting the last slot in a (department, grade) band would, with a naive check, both see "1 slot left" and both create ŌĆö overshooting the approved headcount by one. The service holds a `lockForUpdate()` on the position row, the ceiling row, *and* the count of filled positions in the same band, all in the same transaction, so the second concurrent attempt sees the first one's insert and is rejected with a `DomainException`.
- **The step is frozen at the moment of assignment.** `step_at_start` on `position_assignments` records the employee's step on the day they took the post. The employee's *current* step continues to advance through the increment job; the assignment row remembers where they started so the payroll engine can prorate back-pay calculations against the right starting point.
- **Funding source is not optional.** Every post has to declare whether it is funded by GoG, IGF, donor, IDF, or other. The Auditor-General pack pivots payroll cost on this attribute ŌĆö a donor-funded post that draws salary from the consolidated fund is a finding.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Employees (Chapter 3)** ŌĆö the four dormant fields are now live. `current_position_id` and `current_grade_id` are written by the assign service; `current_step` and `step_anniversary_date` are written by the `StepIncrementService`. The employee detail page reads them via the `currentPosition` and `currentGrade` belongs-to relations.
- **`Grade::baseSalaryFor($step, $date)`** ŌĆö the single function the Payroll Engine (Ch 19) calls to resolve a base salary on a given pay date. The grade's steps are filtered by `effective_from <= date AND (effective_to IS NULL OR effective_to >= date)` and the most recent row wins, so a pay revision dated mid-period is handled correctly without any payroll-side logic.
- **Step increment (the `StepIncrementService::processForDate()` job)** ŌĆö runs nightly. For every employee whose `step_anniversary_date` is today and whose current step is below the grade's `max_step`, the service consults the latest closed-cycle performance review (Ch 6), and: if the rating is Ōēź 3.0/5.0, bumps the step and rolls the anniversary one year forward; if it is below 3.0, rolls the anniversary forward *without* the bump (the employee is re-evaluated next year); if the employee is already at top of grade, rolls the anniversary forward (top-of-grade employees never lose their anniversary, but they also can't progress without a promotion). The four outcomes ŌĆö `processed`, `skipped_top`, `skipped_underperform`, `skipped_no_review` ŌĆö are returned as an array so the nightly job can log them. **Idempotent** ŌĆö running it twice on the same date does *not* double-increment because the anniversary moves forward in the same transaction as the step bump.
- **Performance (Chapter 6)** ŌĆö the step gate above is the closed loop between performance and pay: a manager who awards a rating below 3.0 is, whether they know it or not, freezing that employee's step for a year. The system does not retro-undo the increment if the review is later revised ŌĆö the increment, once granted, is final, but the *next* anniversary is judged on the *next* review.
- **Departments (Chapter 30)** ŌĆö every position rolls up to one department; deleting a department is blocked while any non-soft-deleted position is still assigned to it (the same `DomainException` pattern the Employees module uses). Position counts feed the department dashboard's "approved vs filled" tile.
- **Recruitment (Chapter 9)** ŌĆö a job posting references a position. The grade band on the posting is inherited from the position, and the recruitment-to-onboarding handover writes a `position_assignments` row through the same `PositionService::assign()` call the establishment UI uses, so a hire through recruitment is indistinguishable from a hire through manual HR assignment.
- **Payroll Engine (Chapter 19)** ŌĆö pulls `base_salary` via the grade ├Ś step lookup for every active employee, every pay run. The `step_at_start` field on the assignment row is what the engine uses when it needs to recompute the salary from a back-dated start (most commonly for an arrears payment).
- **Auditor-General pack (Chapter 24)** ŌĆö the headcount-reconciliation page is a direct join of `positions` against `position_assignments` against `employees` against `identity_verifications`: approved posts, current occupants, and whether each occupant has a verified Ghana Card. Discrepancies ŌĆö a filled post with no occupant, an occupant with no Ghana Card, a position outside the ceiling ŌĆö are the ghost-worker findings the pack flags.
- **Identity (Chapter 25)** ŌĆö the Auditor-General-grade assignment record is one where the holder also has a passing `IdentityVerification` row. The establishment register itself does not refuse an assignment when the holder is unverified, but the audit pack does flag it; the policy decision to harden this into a *block* sits with the institute.

## Standards touchpoints

- **Ghana Single Spine Salary Structure (SSSS), 2010** ŌĆö the public-service pay framework. The `grades` and `grade_steps` tables are shaped for Single Spine compliance ŌĆö coded grades, integer step ladders, dated `effective_from` / `effective_to` ranges so a Cabinet-approved pay revision can be loaded as a new step row rather than overwriting the old one. The demo seeder lays down a credible Single Spine *shape* (GS-08 through GS-20, 4-to-8 step ranges, 4.5% step increments) but the actual Single Spine pay table ŌĆö the one the Ministry of Finance gazettes ŌĆö must be loaded by the institute on deployment. The system is Single Spine-*aware*, not Single Spine-*authoritative*; the authoritative source is the Fair Wages and Salaries Commission gazette.
- **Public Services Commission (PSC) establishment control** ŌĆö every public-sector institute is required to operate within an approved establishment, with each post documented and each occupancy traceable. The `positions` table is the approved establishment; the `establishment_ceilings` table is the PSC's approved-headcount sign-off (with `approval_reference` carrying the letter or decision number); the `position_assignments` table is the occupancy history. Together they answer the three questions a PSC audit will ask first: how many posts are approved, how many are filled, and what is the audit trail for every hire, promotion, and transfer.
- **Auditor-General establishment audit** ŌĆö the annual establishment audit's central exercise is reconciling approved posts against warm bodies, with ghost workers being the public-sector readout of an unreconciled count. The audit pack (Chapter 24) reads directly off the tables in this chapter; the row-locked ceiling enforcement closes the most common ghost-worker vector, which is over-establishment by accidental concurrent hire.
- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.7 workforce planning** ŌĆö the standard recommends disclosing approved headcount, filled headcount, and ratio of filled to approved as workforce-planning metrics. The four KPI tiles on the position detail page and the four-up stats band on the register expose the same numbers; the analytics chapter (Ch 13) collects them across the institute for the ISO-aligned export. See Chapter 44.
- **DPA Act 843** ŌĆö the establishment register itself processes minimal personal data (employee_id pointers; no PII on the position rows). The assignment history carries an `employee_id` and dates, which counts as personal data under ┬¦17; the same lawful-basis-of-employment-contract that covers the Employees table covers it here.

## What's planned next

Three pieces of work are in flight directly against this chapter and are scheduled for Phase 1's UI follow-up (W1.21ŌĆōW1.24):

1. **The sidebar entry and the create panel.** The Positions register is reachable today only by URL; the Governance sidebar group will add a "Positions" item for any role with `positions.view`, and the register will gain a cobalt "Add Position" button that opens a right-hand slide panel with the same `StorePositionRequest` fields documented above. The same panel will be reused (with the grade field swapped for a step picker) for a dedicated Promote/Transfer flow that wraps `PositionService::assign()` with a richer reason taxonomy and an effective-date picker for back-dated promotions.

2. **The Single Spine pay table loader.** The demo seeder is a placeholder; the production loader will accept the Fair Wages and Salaries Commission gazette as a CSV upload, validate it against the existing grade codes, and insert one `grade_steps` row per (grade, step) with the gazette's `effective_from` date ŌĆö so a pay revision becomes a one-CSV operation rather than a hand-edited migration. The loader will refuse to import a row that overlaps an existing effective range without an explicit `--supersede` flag, so accidentally double-loading a revision can't happen.

3. **The Auditor-General reconciliation report.** Chapter 24's pack will gain a dedicated "Establishment reconciliation" sheet that joins `positions` ├Ś `position_assignments` ├Ś `employees` ├Ś `identity_verifications` and flags every row where (a) the position is filled but no open assignment exists, (b) an open assignment points at an employee with no `IdentityVerification` row in `verified` status, or (c) the count of filled positions in any (department, grade, fiscal_year) exceeds the corresponding `approved_headcount` ŌĆö i.e. an `establishment.exceed` override has been used and not yet been re-approved. The report runs read-only off the same tables this chapter ships; no schema work is required to produce it.

A fourth piece ŌĆö a tamper-evident audit row for every assign, vacate, freeze, and `establishment.exceed` override, written to the same audit chain the rest of Phase 1 uses ŌĆö is dependency-tracked against the audit chain rollout in Chapter 44 and lands as part of that work rather than this one.

<!-- ===== 09_recruitment.md ===== -->

# Chapter 9 ŌĆö Recruitment + Public Careers

> *In one paragraph.* Recruitment is the two-faced module that takes the institute's hiring story from "we have a vacancy" all the way to "we sent the offer letter for signature" ŌĆö without forcing anyone to leave the system at any point. The HR-facing side, under **Recruitment** in the sidebar, is where postings are drafted, applicants are dragged across a six-column kanban, CVs are downloaded, and offer letters are dispatched to Zoho Sign or DocuSign as a rendered PDF envelope. The public-facing side, served unauthenticated under `/careers/{id}`, is a clean job-detail page with an apply form and a single CV upload ŌĆö no account, no login, no friction. Both sides write to the same `applicants` row, the same `job_postings` row, and the same `applicants.esign_envelope_id` field that the inbound webhook will later update when the candidate signs. The flow stops short of one thing that the architecture is otherwise set up for ŌĆö the actual *hire* (turning a signed offer into an Employee) is still a manual step today.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Workforce** group ŌåÆ **Recruitment** (top-level item, briefcase icon). The page lives at `/jobs` with three Inertia routes underneath: `/jobs/{job}` for the posting + applicant board, `/jobs/{job}/applicants` for the dedicated full-screen pipeline view, and the public twin at `/careers/{job}` (no auth, no sidebar). The "Recruitment" tile is also surfaced in the app switcher (the 3├Ś3 grid in the top bar) so any role with `recruitment.apply` or `recruitment.manage` lands one click away.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö full access through the Gate::before wildcard; every posting, every applicant, every offer.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö holds both `recruitment.manage` and `recruitment.apply`. They are the operational owner of the module: create postings, run the pipeline, send offers.
    - **manager / dept_head / employee / finance_officer / it_support / auditor** ŌĆö `recruitment.apply` only. They can see the job list and the public posting view, but the "Post New Job" CTA, the kanban drag handles, and the "Send offer for e-sign" button are all hidden. The applicant pipeline at `/jobs/{job}/applicants` is gated by `permission:recruitment.manage` middleware so non-HR users get a 403 if they try to reach it directly.
    - **anonymous public** ŌĆö the `/careers/{job}` page and the `POST /careers/{job}/apply` endpoint are explicitly outside the `auth` middleware group. The apply endpoint is `throttle:5,1` (five submissions per minute per IP) to discourage flooding.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö the destination of every hire; today's promotion from Applicant ŌåÆ Employee is still a manual HR step in the Employees module, not an automated event handler. Documents (Ch 13) ŌĆö offer letters are rendered server-side as PDF and dispatched through the `ESignProvider` contract; the contract was built for Documents but is currently wired only to Recruitment's offer flow. Establishment (Ch 8) ŌĆö job postings reference the role textually rather than against the `positions` table in the MVP; Phase 1 promotes the link to a foreign key. Notifications (Ch 16) ŌĆö the candidate receives the eSign provider's email (Zoho Sign / DocuSign), not a CIHRMS email; in-portal stage-change notifications to HR are roadmapped. DPA / Privacy (Ch 26) ŌĆö applicant personal data is processed under the consent + pre-contract basis of Act 843 ┬¦20.

## The screens

![Recruitment library ŌĆö KPI tiles, hiring funnel, posting cards](../assets/screenshots/09_recruitment/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ KPI band ŌĆö four tiles (Total postings, Open positions, Applicants across all roles, Closing within 7 days), all derived client-side from the same `jobs` payload so the page hydrates with one Inertia render. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Hiring funnel ŌĆö five horizontal bars (Applied ┬Ę Shortlisted ┬Ę 1st interview ┬Ę 2nd interview ┬Ę Offer) sized by indicative conversion rates against total applicants; the last stage is gold-accented for the "Sovereign Precision" hierarchy. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Filter strip ŌĆö five status chips (All, Open, Draft, Closed, Filled) plus a search box across title and description; the chips re-fire the client-side filter on click. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Posting card ŌĆö title, status pill, three-line description preview, applicants count, and a tone-coloured closing-window strip (rose if Ōēż 7 days, amber if Ōēż 30, slate after expiry). The left-edge border picks the status pill's dot colour.*

![Posting detail ŌĆö description, kanban board, send-offer modal](../assets/screenshots/09_recruitment/show.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Job description card with a gold hairline on the top edge (the 5% accent in the design system). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Applicant Pipeline kanban ŌĆö six columns (Applied ŌåÆ Shortlisted ŌåÆ Interviewed ŌåÆ Offered ŌåÆ Hired ŌåÆ Rejected). Each card carries an avatar (hashed-name gradient), name, email, days-since-applied, CV download button, and an "Offer sent / viewed / signed / declined / voided" pill that reflects the envelope status pushed back by the webhook. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Send-offer modal ŌĆö annual gross salary in GHS, proposed start date, and "Offer valid for (days)" slider (1ŌĆō30, defaults to 14); the disclaimer explains the candidate will receive an email with the signing link from whichever eSign provider is active.*

![Applicant pipeline ŌĆö full-screen kanban + list view, detail slide panel](../assets/screenshots/09_recruitment/applicants.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ View switcher tab bar ŌĆö Pipeline (kanban) and List (table). ┬Ę ŌØĘ The list view adds a "Filter by stage" dropdown plus per-row inline actions: "Shortlist" (only on `applied` rows) and "Reject" (on anything not yet `hired` or `rejected`). ┬Ę ŌØĖ Applicant detail slide panel ŌĆö hero strip with the same avatar gradient, applied date + relative time, CV download, and a six-button "Move to stage" grid that PATCHes the status directly and closes the panel on success.*

![Public careers page ŌĆö applicant-facing job detail + apply form](../assets/screenshots/09_recruitment/careers.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Public header bar ŌĆö CIHRM Ghana wordmark on the left, "Staff Login" link on the right; no sidebar, no notification bell, no profile menu. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Job hero ŌĆö title, status badge, formatted closing date, location chip ("Accra, Ghana" ŌĆö hard-coded for the MVP), and the cobalt "Apply Now" CTA that reveals the application form. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Apply form sidebar ŌĆö Full Name, Email, CV / Resume (PDF / DOC / DOCX, max 5 MB). On successful submission the form swaps for a green "Application Received" confirmation card.*

> The four screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.27). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Recruitment library (`/jobs`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Post New Job** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for creating a brand-new `JobPosting`. The form has three fields ŌĆö title, description, optional closing date ŌĆö and posts to `jobs.store`. The new posting is created with status `open`, surfaces immediately on the public `/careers/{id}` URL, and the panel closes with a flash toast on success. | `recruitment.manage` (typically hr_admin, super_admin, ceo) | One-step posting ŌĆö HR shouldn't have to draft in Word and then upload. The composer is intentionally minimal in the MVP; salary band, location, position-link, and required-skills fields are all Phase 1 work. |
| **Search box** ("Search title or descriptionŌĆ”") | Free-text filter, client-side, runs as you type. Matches against `job_posting.title` and `job_posting.description` (case-insensitive substring). | Anyone with `recruitment.apply` | The filter set is small (rarely more than 20ŌĆō40 active postings) so client-side filtering is fast and avoids a round-trip. |
| **Status chips** (All / Open / Draft / Closed / Filled) | Restricts the grid to one status. Re-fires the same client-side computed `filteredJobs`. | Same | Most internal queries are "what's still open" ŌĆö single-click filter is the fastest path. |
| **Hiring funnel** (5-bar horizontal chart) | Read-only visualisation. Bars are sized by *indicative* conversion rates (33% Applied ŌåÆ Shortlisted, 42% Shortlisted ŌåÆ 1st interview, 36% 1st ŌåÆ 2nd, 32% 2nd ŌåÆ Offer) applied to the total live applicants count. The last stage carries the gold accent from the design system's 5% rule. | Anyone with view | The single chart most stakeholders want: "where are applicants stuck?" The synthetic conversion is a placeholder ŌĆö Phase 1 swaps it for real per-stage counts from a service method. |
| **Posting card** (whole-card hit target) | Navigates to `/jobs/{id}` ŌĆö the posting detail page. | Anyone with view | Whole-card targets beat dedicated "View" buttons for both pointing speed and tap accuracy on mobile. |
| **Status pill** (per card) | Read-only visual badge; one of Draft, Open, Closed, Filled. The Open chip is cobalt; Filled is the gold success accent; Closed is slate. | Same | Identifies state at a glance without forcing the user into the detail page. |
| **Applicant count** (per card) | Read-only chip with the applicants-count for that posting, sourced from `JobPosting::withCount('applicants')`. | Same | The single most-asked question per posting is "how many applicants?" ŌĆö surfaced on the card so HR can prioritise where to spend the day. |
| **Closing window** (per card) | Computed pill ŌĆö `Open until filled` when `closes_at` is null, `Nd left` otherwise. Tone shifts from neutral ŌåÆ soon (amber, Ōēż 30d) ŌåÆ urgent (rose, Ōēż 7d) ŌåÆ expired (slate). | Same | Time-pressure cue for both HR (need to close the pipeline) and viewers (vacancies that are about to disappear). |

> *Notes:* The library is intentionally not paginated in the MVP ŌĆö the dataset is small enough that the full collection is hydrated into the Inertia page and filtered client-side. Phase 1 swaps `JobPosting::withCount('applicants')->latest()->get()` for a paginator and pushes the filters server-side once the institute's posting volume warrants it. There is no "Edit posting" CTA on the library; the editor is a Phase 1 backlog item ŌĆö for now postings are immutable after creation, which has surfaced as a real-world workflow gap and is one of the honest gaps recorded at the bottom of this chapter.

### Posting detail (`/jobs/{job}`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back** (top right) | Returns to `/jobs`. Inertia keeps the scroll position. | Anyone | Standard back affordance. |
| **Breadcrumbs** (Recruitment ŌåÆ {title}) | Same as Back for the first crumb; the current title is the page indicator. | Anyone | Provides explicit navigational context once you're three clicks deep into a posting. |
| **Job description card** | Read-only display of the saved `description` text (HTML stripped on store; line breaks preserved with `whitespace-pre-line`). The gold hairline on the top edge marks the "headline" hierarchy for the posting. | Anyone | The single source of truth for what the role requires ŌĆö pasted into the offer letter by HR when generating the envelope. |
| **Applicant Pipeline kanban** | Six draggable columns ŌĆö Applied, Shortlisted, Interviewed, Offered, Hired, Rejected ŌĆö backed by `<KanbanBoard>`. Cards are draggable across columns for `recruitment.manage` users; drop fires a PATCH to `/applicants/{id}` with the new `status` and re-fetches the page in place via `preserveScroll`. For non-managers the columns render but drag is disabled and the italic "You can view this pipeline but cannot change applicant status" line appears below. | Drag: `recruitment.manage`. View: anyone with view on the posting (HR, manager, dept_head, hr_admin, super_admin, ceo, auditor) | The kanban is the operational workspace ŌĆö most HR users live on this screen during a hiring round. The six-stage column set is the single source of truth for the `ApplicantStatus` enum. |
| **Applicant card** (per column) | Avatar (initials over a hashed gradient), name, email, "applied N days ago" / "today" line, CV download link (if attached), and ŌĆö when status is `shortlisted / interviewed / offered` and no envelope has been sent yet ŌĆö a "Send offer for e-sign" mini button. | View: anyone with view. The CV link works for anyone with view because the CV is stored on the `public` disk under `applicant-cvs/`. | Cards carry the three pieces of context HR needs to make the next call ŌĆö who, when, and what they sent. The conditional offer button only appears when the applicant has cleared screening but hasn't been sent an envelope yet, so the same applicant doesn't get two letters. |
| **Send offer for e-sign** (per card) | Opens the Send-offer modal. Three fields ŌĆö annual gross salary in GHS (optional), proposed start date (optional), offer-valid-for in days (1ŌĆō30, defaults to 14). On submit, POSTs to `/applicants/{id}/send-offer`. The controller renders the offer letter PDF via DomPDF (the `pdf.offer-letter` Blade view), then fires `OfferEnvelopeRequested` with the base64-encoded PDF in the payload. The listener `SendOfferEnvelopeToApplicant` (queued on the `integrations` connection) calls `$esign->createEnvelope($envelope)`, writes the returned `envelopeId` + provider + sent-at back onto the applicant, and the candidate receives the signing-link email from Zoho Sign or DocuSign directly. | `recruitment.manage` | The single externally-integrated step in the module. Renders ŌåÆ sends ŌåÆ tracks in one click. Note the controller short-circuits with "No e-sign provider is connected ŌĆö open Admin ŌåÆ Integrations to set one up." if `IntegrationManager::isAvailable('esign')` returns false; no PDF is rendered in that case. |
| **CV download** (per card) | Standard `<a target="_blank">` to `/storage/{cv_path}`. The file lives on the `public` disk under `applicant-cvs/`. | Anyone who can see the card | Quick triage ŌĆö the recruiter doesn't have to open a detail panel before reading the CV. |
| **Envelope pill** (per card, after Send) | Five colour-coded states ŌĆö Offer sent (amber), Offer viewed (cobalt), Offer signed (green), Offer declined (rose), Offer voided (slate). The pill is driven by `applicants.esign_status`, which is written by `ESignWebhookController` when the provider POSTs the envelope's status to `/webhooks/esign`. | Anyone who can see the card | Real-time visibility into whether the candidate has opened, signed, or rejected the offer ŌĆö without anyone having to log into the eSign provider's console. |

> *Notes:* The Show page hydrates from one Inertia render ŌĆö `RecruitmentController::show` loads the job with the full applicants relation and the resource pipes through `JobPostingResource` + `ApplicantResource`. There is no N+1 between the kanban columns. The CV URL is constructed as `/storage/{cv_path}` because the underlying disk is `public` with the `public/storage` symlink pointing at `storage/app/public` ŌĆö the storage-layer notes apply identically to the per-employee documents in Ch 3.

### Applicant pipeline page (`/jobs/{job}/applicants`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **View switcher** (Pipeline / List tabs) | Toggles between the kanban and a tabular `<table>` view. The choice is purely client-side ŌĆö there is no server round-trip on switch. | `recruitment.manage` (the whole page is permission-gated) | Two HR workflows: hands-on triage on the kanban, bulk scanning on the list. |
| **Filter by stage** (List view only) | Single-select dropdown over the six `ApplicantStatus` cases. Filters client-side. | Same | The list view is most useful when targeted at one stage at a time. |
| **Pipeline kanban** | Five columns (Applied ┬Ę Shortlisted ┬Ę Interviewed ┬Ę Offered ┬Ę Hired ŌĆö rejected hidden). Drag-and-drop fires the same `applicants.update` PATCH. The card body is click-targeted to open the detail slide panel; CV download is `@click.stop` so it doesn't bubble. | Same | Identical mechanic to the per-posting board on `/jobs/{id}` but full-screen ŌĆö when HR is committed to working a single posting end-to-end, this is the screen. |
| **List view rows** | Avatar, name + email, stage badge, CV download (or em-dash), applied date + relative time, and inline action buttons. | Same | Higher information density than the kanban ŌĆö better for end-of-day "where are we?" scanning. |
| **Shortlist** (inline action, only on `applied` rows) | PATCHes the row's `status` to `shortlisted` and re-fetches. | Same | One-click triage from the most common starting state. |
| **Reject** (inline action, on anything not `hired` or `rejected`) | PATCHes the row's `status` to `rejected`. No confirmation dialog and no required reason in the MVP ŌĆö this is one of the honest gaps documented below. | Same | The single most-clicked terminal action. |
| **Detail slide panel** (opens on row / card click) | Hero strip with the same avatar + email + status badge, two-tile grid (applied date + CV link), and a six-button "Move to stage" grid covering every `ApplicantStatus` case. The current stage's button is disabled with cobalt highlight. | Same | The detail panel is the place where HR can see the full context of one applicant without leaving the pipeline view. |

> *Notes:* The "rejected" column is intentionally hidden from the kanban view on this page (`STAGES.filter(s => s.id !== 'rejected')`) so a busy board doesn't bury "active" candidates under a wall of rejections; rejections are still reachable through the detail panel's "Move to stage" grid and through the list view. There is no bulk-action affordance in the MVP ŌĆö selecting five applicants and moving them en masse is Phase 1 work.

### Posting create form (slide panel)

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Job title** | required, string, max 255 | `recruitment.manage` | Becomes both the page title on `/careers/{id}` and the subject line on the offer letter template. |
| **Description** | required, string, max 10000 | Same | Free text in the MVP ŌĆö the composer does not yet render structured sections (responsibilities, requirements, benefits). Phase 1 introduces a structured form. The description is rendered with `whitespace-pre-line` on both the HR Show page and the public Careers page so line breaks are preserved. |
| **Closing date** | optional, must be `after:today` | Same | Future-only ŌĆö back-dated closings are rejected at the validation layer because they would surface on the public careers page as already-closed and confuse applicants. Null means "open until filled". |
| **Publish posting** (cobalt CTA, panel footer) | n/a ŌĆö button | Same | Posts to `jobs.store`. Service-side, the new row is created with `status = open` so the public page accepts applications immediately. There is no Draft state available from the panel today ŌĆö the `JobPostingStatus::Draft` enum case is reserved for Phase 1's "save draft" workflow. |
| **Cancel** | n/a | Anyone | Closes the panel and resets the form without firing anything. |

> *Notes:* The whole create is a single `JobPosting::create([...])` call wrapped by Laravel's transactional auto-commit ŌĆö there are no downstream listeners on `JobPostingCreated` yet (the event isn't defined; if one is needed for analytics it would be added in Phase 1). On success, the panel closes with a flash toast and the new posting appears at the top of the grid (the index query is `latest()`).

### Public application form (`/careers/{job}` ŌåÆ POST `/careers/{job}/apply`)

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Full Name** | required, string, max 255 | Public (no auth) ŌĆö controller `apply` is in the public route block. `ApplyJobRequest::authorize()` returns `true` for unauthenticated requests (`$this->user() === null || $this->user()->hasPermission('recruitment.apply')`). | Stored verbatim on `applicants.name`. There is no name-formatting normalisation in the MVP. |
| **Email Address** | required, valid email, max 255 | Same | Stored on `applicants.email`. There is no de-duplication ŌĆö the same email can apply to the same posting twice and create two rows. Phase 1 introduces a unique constraint on `(job_posting_id, email)`. |
| **CV / Resume** | optional, file, max 5 MB (5120 KB), MIME-locked to `pdf / doc / docx` | Same | Stored on the `public` disk under `applicant-cvs/{hash}.{ext}`. The service-side pattern is "store the file first, then run the transaction; if the insert fails, delete the orphan file" ŌĆö this is the same orphan-protection pattern used by Documents (Ch 13). The file is **not** routed through the Documents pipeline; it is held in the simpler legacy `applicant-cvs/` directory. |
| **Submit Application** (cobalt CTA) | n/a ŌĆö button | Same | Posts to `careers.apply`. The route is `throttle:5,1`, so an IP can submit at most five applications per minute. On success the controller returns a redirect with `success` flash and the Vue page flips into the green "Application Received" confirmation card. |

> *Notes:* The public application path is intentionally minimal ŌĆö no captcha, no email verification, no phone number, no covering-letter field. This is a deliberate MVP trade-off: friction is the biggest contributor to applicant drop-off, and the institute's recruitment team have stated that they would rather sift more applications than miss qualified candidates because of a captcha. The flip side is the absence of a true anti-bot layer; the throttle is a stop-gap and Phase 1 wires hCaptcha into this exact endpoint.

### Send-offer modal

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Annual gross salary (GHS)** | optional, numeric, Ōēź 0 | `recruitment.manage` | Rendered into the PDF as the headline figure. Left blank for offers that are still being negotiated; the PDF template has a placeholder string in that case. |
| **Proposed start date** | optional, valid date | Same | Rendered into the PDF body. There is no validation that it falls after today ŌĆö back-dated offers are technically possible (rare but real for retroactive contracts). |
| **Offer valid for (days)** | optional, integer, 1ŌĆō30, defaults to 14 | Same | Drives the expiry text in the PDF and is what the eSign provider receives as the envelope's "expire after" parameter when supported (Zoho Sign honours it; DocuSign uses an envelope-level `expirationAfter` setting). |
| **Send for signature** (cobalt CTA) | n/a ŌĆö button | Same | Triggers `applicants.sendOffer`. The controller renders the PDF, dispatches `OfferEnvelopeRequested`, and returns a flash success "Offer letter is being sent for signature." The listener is queued (`queue = integrations`), so the envelope creation happens asynchronously ŌĆö failures are logged but do not surface in the UI. |

> *Notes:* The PDF is rendered with DomPDF (`Pdf::loadView('pdf.offer-letter', [...])`), not with the in-portal TCPDF composer used by Documents (Ch 13). This is the only place in CIHRMS where DomPDF is the renderer; it predates the Documents v2 work. The two-renderer split is a known piece of tech debt ŌĆö the Documents Phase 1 "eSign for the documents pipeline" item is the convergence path.

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores a recruitment process in three logical layers, all anchored on two simple tables:

- **The vacancy** ŌĆö `job_postings` holds title, description (text up to 10 000 chars), optional `closes_at` date, a `status` string backed by the `JobPostingStatus` enum (`draft / open / closed / filled`), standard `timestamps`, and a `SoftDeletes` flag. Soft delete is in place but not exposed in the UI ŌĆö the only operational way to take a posting off the public page today is to flip `status` away from `open`, which is itself not yet wired into the UI either. (See "Honest gaps" below.)
- **The applicant** ŌĆö `applicants` holds the `job_posting_id` foreign key (cascade-on-delete), `name`, `email`, optional `cv_path`, a `status` string backed by the `ApplicantStatus` enum (`applied / shortlisted / interviewed / offered / hired / rejected`), and five eSign columns added in Wave 11: `esign_provider`, `esign_envelope_id`, `esign_status`, `esign_sent_at`, `esign_completed_at`. Same `SoftDeletes` flag. The applicant row is the single record that survives the whole journey from public application to (intended) hire.
- **The envelope** ŌĆö there is no separate `offer_envelopes` table; the five `esign_*` columns on `applicants` *are* the envelope record. The rendered PDF itself is not persisted on the CIHRMS side ŌĆö it lives only at the eSign provider after dispatch. This is a deliberate MVP simplification ŌĆö the institute's compliance position is that the provider's signed-document URL (returned by `ESignProvider::signedDocumentUrl($envelopeId)`) is the authoritative artefact, and the institute downloads it on demand when needed.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **The applicant is not an employee.** There is no foreign key, no automatic promotion, no shared identity. An `applicants.email` of `j.doe@example.com` and a `users.email` of `j.doe@cihrm.gh` are entirely separate rows. Hiring an applicant requires HR to create the Employee record manually in the Employees module (Ch 3) using whichever data they want to carry over from the applicant row. The `ApplicantHired` event class is defined and a listener (`CreateZohoContactOnHire`) is wired, but **no code path dispatches `ApplicantHired` today** ŌĆö moving an applicant to the `hired` column is purely a status update.
- **eSign is the only externally-integrated step.** Everything else ŌĆö posting creation, applicant capture, stage transitions, CV download ŌĆö is entirely internal. The eSign provider is selected via `INT_ESIGN_DRIVER` (`zoho_sign` by default; `docusign` is the alternative). Webhooks land at `/webhooks/esign` and are dispatched against the applicants table by `esign_envelope_id`. There is no provider-side de-duplication ŌĆö re-sending an offer to the same applicant after a previous attempt was voided creates a new envelope and overwrites the columns.
- **The public path is genuinely public.** `/careers/{job}` and `/careers/{job}/apply` are outside the `auth` middleware group entirely. The apply endpoint does not require a CSRF token (it does ŌĆö but the public Inertia page hydrates with one) and the rate limit is per-IP at five-per-minute. Applicant data lands directly in `applicants` with no holding pen, no review step, no email confirmation back to the candidate from CIHRMS (only the eventual eSign provider email).
- **Status moves are not audited.** Unlike the Documents module's `document_events` table, there is no `applicant_events` log. The transition from `applied ŌåÆ shortlisted ŌåÆ interviewed ŌåÆ offered ŌåÆ hired` overwrites the `status` column in place. Phase 1's audit-pack work folds applicant transitions into the tamper-evident audit log that ships across the rest of the system.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö hire flow.** This is the gap. The intent is `ApplicantHired` ŌåÆ listener creates a `User` + `Employee` row from the applicant's `name` + `email` + the offer's salary/start-date ŌåÆ fires `EmployeeCreated` ŌåÆ benefits enrolment + welcome notification + identity-verification queue ŌåÆ done. In code, only step 1's event class and step 2's listener (`CreateZohoContactOnHire`) exist; the actual `Employee` creation is still a manual HR step from the Employees module. This is the single most-impactful Phase 1 deliverable on this module.
- **Documents (Ch 13) ŌĆö offer letter pipeline.** The `ESignProvider` contract was built to be reusable but in the MVP it is wired only to Recruitment's offer letter. The Documents v2 "Phase 1: eSign integration for the documents pipeline" item extends the contract so any routed document can optionally be co-signed via Zoho Sign or DocuSign. The PDF is rendered with DomPDF (not TCPDF, which Documents uses) ŌĆö convergence on TCPDF is in scope for the same Phase 1 work. Note also that CVs are **not** routed through the Documents pipeline; they live in the simpler `storage/app/public/applicant-cvs/` directory, with no annotation, no routing, no version history. Phase 1 promotes CVs to Documents-class objects so that `confidential` classification + per-viewer watermarks apply.
- **Establishment (Ch 8) ŌĆö position references.** Job postings carry a free-text `title` rather than a foreign key to the `positions` table. Phase 1 promotes the field to a `position_id` so postings inherit the grade, step, salary band, and required qualifications from the establishment record. Until then a posting can be created for a role that does not exist in the establishment, which is a small workflow risk.
- **Notifications (Ch 16).** Recruitment does not send any in-portal notifications in the MVP. The candidate receives the eSign provider's own email when the envelope is dispatched (rendered and branded by Zoho Sign or DocuSign, not by CIHRMS). HR receives nothing ŌĆö there is no "new applicant submitted to your posting" mail, no "envelope was signed" mail, no daily digest. This is Phase 1 backlog: a `RecruitmentNotificationMailer` mirroring the Documents pattern.
- **Audit Logs (Ch 24).** Recruitment does not write to the audit log today. Postings created, applicants moved, offers sent, envelopes signed ŌĆö none of these surface in the audit trail. The Phase 1 audit-pack work folds them in via a new `recruitment_events` table mirroring `document_events`.
- **Analytics events.** The base `analytics_events` table exists and is written to from Employees and Leave, but Recruitment does not publish events to it. The `applicants_count` chip on each posting card, the hiring funnel, and the KPI tiles are all derived client-side from the same `jobs` payload ŌĆö there is no aggregate query and no per-stage history.

## Standards touchpoints

- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.2.3 ŌĆö recruitment (turnaround time, quality of hire, time-to-fill).** The data model captures `applicants.created_at` and the timestamp of every status transition (via `updated_at`, which is overwritten on every PATCH) but there is no per-stage history. Turnaround-time can be approximated as `hired_applicant.updated_at ŌłÆ applicant.created_at` on the rare hire that does land, which is a usable proxy but loses fidelity once an applicant cycles back from rejected. Quality-of-hire is unreachable without a post-hire performance link. Phase 1 lands the `applicant_events` log that makes both metrics first-class; the Analytics export (Ch 24) consumes them. See Chapter 44.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦20 ŌĆö basis for processing personal data (consent + pre-contractual).** Applicant data is collected from a public, unauthenticated form ŌĆö the lawful basis is `consent` (the applicant has actively submitted) augmented by `pre-contractual necessity` (processing is necessary to consider entering an employment contract). The public Careers page does **not** display a privacy notice or a consent checkbox in the MVP ŌĆö this is a real DPA gap. The institute's compliance position is that the act of clicking "Submit Application" constitutes informed consent under ┬¦17(1)(b), but a visible notice + tick-box pair is the auditor-defensible pattern and is Phase 1 work. See Chapter 26.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦40 ŌĆö right to erasure.** Soft delete is in place on the `applicants` table but is not surfaced in the UI; an applicant cannot self-request erasure through the public Careers page today. The cross-cutting Data Subject Request flow (Ch 26) accepts public submissions from ex-applicants and routes them to HR, where the deletion has to be done from the database console. Phase 2 wires this up as a real workflow.
- **Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦43 ŌĆö equal opportunity / non-discrimination.** The system itself does not enforce anti-discrimination on shortlist decisions ŌĆö that lives in HR policy, not in the code. What the system *can* show on audit is the full stage-by-stage transition history of every applicant, so an after-the-fact equal-opportunity review can be conducted. Once the `applicant_events` log lands in Phase 1, the institute can report on demographic-stage-transition rates if applicant demographics (optional, with consent) are captured. The MVP does not collect demographic fields.
- **Electronic Transactions Act, 2008 (Act 772) ┬¦10 ŌĆö recognition of electronic signatures.** The offer letter signing path is the highest-stakes electronic-signature workflow in the institute ŌĆö it is the legal acceptance of an employment contract. Wiring it through Zoho Sign or DocuSign (rather than the in-portal `signature_pad` canvas used by Documents) is a deliberate choice: ┬¦10's authentication and integrity requirements are met by the provider's own audit trail, certificate-of-completion PDF, and tamper-evident signature placement. The provider returns a `signedDocumentUrl` that the institute can archive on demand. See Chapter 26.

## Honest gaps

This module ships the public-facing path end-to-end (post ŌåÆ list ŌåÆ apply ŌåÆ kanban ŌåÆ offer envelope) and the eSign integration is real and exercised in tests. What it does not yet do:

- **The offer-letter signed callback does not complete the hire.** When the candidate signs the envelope, `ESignWebhookController::handle` writes `esign_status = 'completed'` and `esign_completed_at = now()` on the applicant row, then dispatches `EnvelopeStatusChanged`. *Nothing listens to `EnvelopeStatusChanged` to advance the applicant's `status` to `hired`, dispatch `ApplicantHired`, or create the Employee + User rows.* The pipeline pill changes colour to green ("Offer signed") and the HR team has to manually move the applicant to the `hired` column and then go to the Employees module and create the record by hand. The pieces are all built ŌĆö the `ApplicantHired` event class, the `CreateZohoContactOnHire` listener that consumes it, the `EmployeeCreated` event that the Employees module fires ŌĆö but the chain is not connected. This is the single most-impactful Phase 1 deliverable on this module.
- **No privacy notice on the public Careers page.** The form collects name, email, and CV with no visible privacy notice, no consent tick-box, and no retention statement. The lawful basis is defensible under DPA ┬¦20, but the UI presentation is not auditor-friendly.
- **No de-duplication on applicant email.** The same email can apply to the same posting twice and create two rows. There is no unique constraint on `(job_posting_id, email)`. Phase 1 backlog.
- **No applicant communication from CIHRMS.** Applicants receive only the eSign provider's own email when an offer is dispatched. There is no acknowledgement email when they apply, no "we have shortlisted you" mail when they move stage, and no rejection email. The public confirmation card is the only feedback they get from CIHRMS itself.
- **Reject is a one-click action with no reason capture.** Rejecting an applicant via the inline "Reject" button or the detail panel's "Move to stage" grid just flips the status ŌĆö there is no required (or even optional) reason field. The Documents module's "Reject route" pattern (mandatory comment) is the target.
- **Postings are immutable after creation.** There is no "Edit posting" UI. Typos in title or description require either an admin to update directly in the database or a soft-delete + recreate. Phase 1 adds an edit slide panel.
- **The "Draft" status on `JobPostingStatus` is unreachable.** The enum case exists; the creation form has no "save as draft" option. The status chip on the library does filter for it, so a posting flipped to draft via the API would render correctly, but the UI cannot produce one.
- **No bulk actions on the applicant pipeline.** Moving five applicants from `applied` to `shortlisted` requires five separate drag operations or five separate clicks in the detail panel.
- **CVs are not in the Documents pipeline.** They live in the simpler `applicant-cvs/` directory with no `confidential` classification, no per-viewer watermarking, and no audit trail on download. Phase 1 promotes them.
- **Closing dates are not enforced server-side.** A posting whose `closes_at` is in the past will display the "Closed" tone on the public page (`v-if="j.status === 'open'"` is the only gate on the apply form), but the apply endpoint itself does not check the date ŌĆö a client that hand-crafts the POST can still submit against an expired posting. The `JobPosting::isExpired()` helper exists but is not called by `RecruitmentController::apply`.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap lands six things on this module: (1) **the hire chain** ŌĆö `EnvelopeStatusChanged` listener that, on `completed`, advances the applicant to `hired`, dispatches `ApplicantHired`, and creates the User + Employee rows from the applicant's name/email and the offer's salary/start-date, firing the existing `EmployeeCreated` cascade; (2) **the audit log** ŌĆö `applicant_events` table mirroring `document_events`, with every stage transition, offer send, envelope status change, and rejection writing an immutable row that the audit pack (Ch 24) consumes; (3) **the consent UI** ŌĆö visible privacy notice + tick-box on the public Careers form, retention statement, and the public Data Subject Request link for applicant erasure; (4) **the establishment link** ŌĆö `position_id` foreign key on `job_postings` so postings inherit grade, step, salary band, and required qualifications from the establishment record (Ch 8), with the free-text `title` field demoted to a display override; (5) **the bulk + edit affordances** ŌĆö multi-select on the pipeline kanban + list view, edit slide panel on the posting library, and a required-reason text field on Reject; (6) **CVs into the Documents pipeline** ŌĆö every uploaded CV becomes a `Document` with confidentiality `confidential`, so the institute's standard share-guard, per-viewer watermark, and download audit trail apply. Phase 2 adds hCaptcha on the public apply endpoint, applicant communication (apply ack, shortlist mail, rejection mail, offer-sent confirmation), and the post-hire performance link that makes ISO 30414 quality-of-hire computable. See the government gap analysis (Chapter 46) for the full sequence.

<!-- ===== 10_offboarding.md ===== -->

# Chapter 10 ŌĆö Off-boarding & Clearance

> *In one paragraph.* Off-boarding is how CIHRMS closes the book on a departing employee in a way that finance, IT, the line manager, the pensions trustee, and the Auditor-General can all reconcile against. Every separation ŌĆö whether it is a clean resignation, a contract running out, a redundancy under Act 651 ┬¦31, a dismissal-for-cause, or a death in service ŌĆö opens a single **off-boarding case**: a tracked ledger of who has signed off what, what the final settlement comes to, who calculated it, who approved it (under dual control), and when the employee's status finally flips to *terminated*. The gap analysis flagged this domain as "missing"; the system in fact ships a full Inertia module ŌĆö `/offboarding` ŌĆö that runs the case from initiation through clearance, settlement, approval, and termination.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Workforce** group ŌåÆ **Off-boarding** (top-level item with the `logout` icon). The link is registered against the `offboarding` module key and uses the sky/cobalt palette in `useIconPalette.js`.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every case, every action.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö sees and initiates all cases; can clear items, calculate settlements, and complete the case (`offboarding.view`, `offboarding.initiate`, `offboarding.clear`, `offboarding.settle`, `offboarding.manage`).
    - **finance_officer** ŌĆö views all cases and is the dual-control approver of the final settlement; can also recalculate before approval (`offboarding.view`, `offboarding.settle`, `offboarding.approve`).
    - **dept_head / manager** ŌĆö no direct grant in the seeder, but `OffboardingCasePolicy::view` lets a department head see the cases for employees in the department(s) they manage (via `User::managesDepartment()`).
    - **employee** ŌĆö sees only their own off-boarding case (the policy returns true when `$case->employee->user_id === $user->id`); the sidebar link is hidden because `can('offboarding.view')` is false.
    - **auditor** ŌĆö read-only across the audit log (no direct module permission in MVP ŌĆö escalates through the `audit.view` lane in Chapter 24).
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö the case writes the employee's status to *terminated* on completion; Leave (Ch 4) ŌĆö unused annual-leave days become leave encashment in the settlement; Payroll (Ch 19) ŌĆö the final settlement carries a `payroll_line_id` so the off-cycle disbursement can be linked; Loans (Ch 21) ŌĆö outstanding loan balances are netted off the gross and the schedule waived on approval; Identity (Ch 25) ŌĆö the `complete` route is gated behind `2fa:fresh` so a fresh second factor is mandatory before the termination is committed; DPA (Ch 26) ŌĆö the cancelled / completed dates and the breakdown JSON are retained for the statutory window.

## The screens

![Off-boarding directory ŌĆö masthead, filter strip, case-card grid](../assets/screenshots/10_offboarding/directory.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Masthead ŌĆö "OFF-BOARDING DOSSIER" eyebrow with the `logout` glyph, headline, and a one-line subtitle that names the legal anchor ("Every separation logged against Act 651 ┬¦17 ┬Ę clearance across IT, Finance, HR, line manager ┬Ę dual-control settlement"). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Filter strip ŌĆö debounced free-text search across case reference and employee name; status dropdown (Draft / In Progress / Awaiting Settlement / Settled / Completed / Cancelled); exit-type dropdown (all eight enum values, see below); one-click "Clear". ┬Ę ŌØĖ Case card ŌĆö gradient avatar with initials, employee name and department, status badge, an exit-type chip with a colour-coded background and a contextual icon (resignation = `exit_to_app`, retirement = `elderly`, dismissal = `gavel`, redundancy = `group_remove`, death = `sentiment_sad`, abscondment = `person_off`, mutual = `handshake`, end-of-contract = `event_busy`), an LWD (Last Working Day) chip that turns amber within 14 days and red within 7, a clearance-progress bar that turns green at 100%, and a Net Settlement row that shows the calculated figure or "Not yet calculated" italicised when no snapshot exists.*

![Off-boarding case detail ŌĆö hero, action bar, clearance accordion](../assets/screenshots/10_offboarding/detail.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero card ŌĆö exit-type glyph in an amber well, employee name, department, employee number (monospace), exit-type chip, LWD countdown chip (urgent ŌåÆ red, past ŌåÆ muted), and three quick stats: clearance signed-off ratio (`6 / 12`), clearance progress percentage, and net settlement. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Four stat-cards ŌĆö Notice Received, Last Working Day, Clearance Progress, Net Settlement ŌĆö each with a 3px left border in a brand colour. ┬Ę ŌØĖ HR Action bar (shown only when the viewer has `complete` or `approve_settle` permission) ŌĆö "Complete Case & Terminate" (green gradient, marked `(2FA)`) appears when the settlement is approved; "Cancel Case" is always shown to a manager, and clicking it reveals an inline reason textbox. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Tabs ŌĆö Clearance ┬Ę Settlement ┬Ę Audit.*

![Clearance tab ŌĆö accordion grouped by area with per-item sign-off](../assets/screenshots/10_offboarding/clearance.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Area header ŌĆö each clearance area (IT & Assets, Finance, HR Records, Library, Stores, Security & Access, Departmental Handover, Pension Discharge, Other) is collapsed into a panel with its own glyph (computer / account_balance_wallet / badge / menu_book / devices / security / manage_accounts / payments / checklist) and a "signed/total" pill. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Item row ŌĆö status icon (filled circle = cleared, dashed circle = pending, half-circle = waived), label, an "optional" pill if `is_required = false`, the department / responsible user if assigned, the notes (italicised), and, when cleared or waived, the actor and timestamp. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Action buttons ŌĆö a pending item shows "Clear" (emerald) and "Waive" (amber) side-by-side; waiving reveals an inline reason input that is `required` (the FormRequest enforces `required_if:action,waive`).*

![Settlement tab ŌĆö parameter overrides, earnings, deductions, workflow buttons](../assets/screenshots/10_offboarding/settlement.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Calculation Parameters panel ŌĆö gratuity months-per-year (default 1.0), severance months-per-year (default 1.5), working days-per-month (default 22), ex-gratia, prorated 13th month, other deductions, and a `pay_paye` checkbox. The panel only renders while the settlement is unapproved or absent; once approved, the figures are locked. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Earnings card ŌĆö Gratuity, Severance (Act 651), Leave encashment (with the day-count in the dt), Pro-rated 13th, Ex-gratia, and a heavy "Gross Settlement" row. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Deductions card ŌĆö Outstanding loans, Garnishments, Other, PAYE on settlement, total, and a highlighted "Net Payable" row in the brand cobalt. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Workflow buttons ŌĆö "Approve Settlement" (emerald, `(2FA)`) is shown to the finance approver while the status is *calculated*; "Complete Case & Terminate Employee" (cobalt, `(2FA)`) takes its place once the settlement is approved.*

> The four screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.10). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Off-boarding directory

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Initiate Case** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for a new case. The same panel auto-opens when the user lands at `/offboarding?new=1` from a Quick Action ŌĆö the flag is then stripped from the URL so a refresh or back-navigation doesn't re-open the panel. | `offboarding.initiate` (hr_admin, super_admin, ceo) | One-step initiation ŌĆö HR shouldn't have to remember a separate URL for "new case". |
| **Search box** ("Search reference or employeeŌĆ”") | Free-text filter that fires 380 ms after typing stops. Matches `offboarding_cases.reference` and the related `users.name` of the employee. | Anyone who can see the directory | Most lookups are by case ref (`OFF-2026-NNNNN`) or by the leaver's name. |
| **All Statuses** dropdown | Filters the case list by `draft`, `in_progress`, `awaiting_settlement`, `settled`, `completed`, or `cancelled`. | Same | "In Progress" and "Awaiting Settlement" together are the daily working queue. |
| **All Exit Types** dropdown | Filters by the eight `ExitType` enum values (Resignation, Retirement, End of Contract, Dismissal, Redundancy, Mutual Separation, Death, Abscondment). | Same | Retirement and end-of-contract have very different payout profiles ŌĆö segregating them in the directory removes confusion. |
| **Clear** (appears only when a filter is set) | Resets all three filters and re-queries. | Same | One-click undo. |
| **Case card row click** | Navigates to `/offboarding/{case}`. | `view` permission on that case (RBAC-checked in `OffboardingCasePolicy::view`) | Whole-card hit target is faster than a small "open" button. |
| **Open Case** (inline link, bottom-right of each card) | Same as card click ŌĆö explicit affordance for screen readers and keyboard users. The `@click.stop` prevents the card-level click from firing twice. | Same | Accessibility ŌĆö a card isn't naturally focusable; this link is. |
| **Pagination** | Standard Inertia pagination ŌĆö 20 per page, range and total displayed when there are enough pages. | Anyone | Keeps the grid responsive when hundreds of historical cases accumulate. |

> *Notes:* The directory query eager-loads `employee.user`, `employee.department`, `settlement`, and `initiator`, so there is no N+1 between the 20 cards. The card avatar uses one of six brand-cobalt gradients indexed off `employee.id` so the same person always renders the same colour. The "Initiate Case" button writes through the `InitiateOffboardingRequest` FormRequest, which validates `last_working_day >= notice_received_on` and that `exit_type` is a valid enum case.

### Off-boarding case detail

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back arrow** (top left) | Returns to the directory; Inertia preserves the scroll position and any filters in place. | Anyone viewing | Standard back affordance. |
| **Hero card** | Read-only display: exit-type glyph in an amber circle, employee name + department + employee number, exit-type chip, LWD countdown chip. | Anyone viewing | Identity-at-a-glance ŌĆö answers "who is leaving, when, and how" in one line. |
| **4 stat cards** | Notice Received, Last Working Day (turns red when past or imminent), Clearance Progress (percentage), Net Settlement (GHS or "ŌĆö" if uncalculated). | Anyone viewing | The four numbers HR rings around on a manila folder. |
| **Complete Case & Terminate** (green-gradient CTA in the HR Action bar, also in the Settlement tab) | POSTs to `/offboarding/{case}/complete`. The service refuses if any required clearance item is still pending OR if the settlement isn't approved. On success it flips the employee row to *terminated*, fires `OffboardingCompleted`, and sets `completed_at` / `completed_by`. Route is gated by `2fa:fresh` ŌĆö the user must have stepped up to 2FA in the current session. | `offboarding.manage` | Termination is the irreversible bit. Forcing a fresh 2FA stops a stolen session from ending someone's employment. |
| **Cancel Case** (red outline button) | Reveals an inline reason textbox; on confirm POSTs to `/offboarding/{case}/cancel` with the reason, which is appended to the case's existing `reason` with a `[CANCELLED]` prefix. The case status becomes *cancelled* and the case is closed (not deleted). | `offboarding.manage` | Withdrawn resignations are a real thing ŌĆö the audit trail wants the reason on the record. |
| **Tabs** | Clearance / Settlement / Audit. Tabs are client-side toggles; the case payload is already in the Inertia render. | Anyone viewing | Cuts the perceived load time ŌĆö there is no second round-trip when changing tab. |

### Clearance tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Clear** (emerald button on a pending item) | POSTs to `/offboarding/{case}/clearance/{item}` with `action=clear`. The service refuses if the item isn't pending, then writes `status=cleared`, `cleared_by`, `cleared_at`, and any notes. If clearing the last required pending item, the case auto-advances from *in_progress* to *awaiting_settlement*. | `offboarding.clear` or `offboarding.manage` | Multi-department sign-off ŌĆö IT marks the laptop returned, Finance marks the loans squared, HR marks the file complete. |
| **Waive** (amber button on a pending item) | Reveals an inline reason input (required), then POSTs `action=waive`. The FormRequest enforces `notes required_if:action,waive`. Waived items count toward "completed" for clearance progress but the reason is captured on the row. | Same | Real life: the laptop was already returned six months ago and was reissued to someone else; HR notes that and moves on without blocking the case. |
| **Item row, when cleared/waived** | Displays the actor and timestamp ("Cleared by Janet on 14 Jun 2026"). | Anyone viewing | Audit-grade attribution at the row level. |

> *Notes:* The default checklist seeded at case creation has 12 items across IT & Assets (3), Finance (2), HR Records (3), Stores (1, optional), Security (1), Departmental Handover (1), and Pension (1). `OffboardingService::addClearanceItem()` lets a case be extended programmatically with department-specific items ŌĆö Phase 1 will surface this on the UI as an "Add clearance item" affordance. The "is_required" flag controls whether a pending item blocks `complete`: optional items can stay open forever without blocking termination.

### Settlement tab

| Field | Validation | Who can use it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Gratuity months/yr** | numeric, 0ŌĆō6 | `offboarding.settle` | Default 1.0 (one month of basic per year of service). For schemes that pay 2├Ś per year, HR enters 2.0 before clicking Calculate. The override is saved into the `breakdown.multipliers` JSON column so the audit trail records what was used. |
| **Severance months/yr** | numeric, 0ŌĆō6 | Same | Default 1.5 ŌĆö applies only to `ExitType::Redundancy` (Act 651 ┬¦31). For every other exit type the calculator forces severance to zero regardless of this input. |
| **Working days / month** | numeric, 15ŌĆō31 | Same | Default 22. Used to derive the daily rate for the leave-encashment formula (`accrued_leave_days ├Ś basic / wd_per_month`). |
| **Ex-gratia (GHS)** | numeric, Ōēź 0 | Same | Discretionary one-off addition ŌĆö useful for long-service or compassionate top-ups. |
| **Prorated 13th month** | numeric, Ōēź 0 | Same | If the org policy pays a 13th cheque, HR types the pro-rated amount here. The calculator does not compute it automatically in the MVP. |
| **Other deductions** | numeric, Ōēź 0 | Same | Anything Finance has flagged outside of loans and garnishments (e.g. an outstanding training-bond balance). |
| **Garnishments** | numeric, Ōēź 0 | Same | Court-ordered garnishees. |
| **Apply PAYE** (checkbox) | boolean | Same | If on, PAYE is computed on the gross settlement via the `PayeCalculator` against the regular monthly bracket table; if off, the gross is paid untaxed. |
| **Calculate Settlement** (cobalt CTA) | ŌĆö | Same | POSTs to `/offboarding/{case}/settlement/calculate`. On success a `FinalSettlement` row is written with the status *calculated*, the case status moves to *awaiting_settlement*, and every input (basic salary, years of service, accrued leave days, multipliers used) is snapshotted into the row. Re-running on an unapproved case soft-deletes the prior snapshot and writes a new one ŌĆö re-calculation is first-class. |
| **Approve Settlement** (green CTA, `(2FA)`) | ŌĆö | `offboarding.approve` (finance_officer, super_admin, ceo) | POSTs to `/offboarding/{case}/settlement/approve`. Refuses if the settlement isn't in status *calculated*. **Hard dual-control gate:** `OffboardingService::approveSettlement()` throws if `calculated_by === approved_by`. On success the settlement row freezes (`approved_by`, `approved_at`), any open loan accounts are netted to zero and their remaining scheduled instalments waived, and the `SettlementApproved` event fires. The route is gated by `2fa:fresh`. |
