A defined retention schedule (typically: resolved ŌåÆ purge after N years; closed ŌåÆ shorter window) is on the Phase 2 roadmap, parallel to the same work on Whistleblower and Employee. See Chapter 26.
- **Public Services Commission (PSC) grievance policy ŌĆö internal-resolution timelines.** The PSC HR Handbook ┬¦5.4 recommends an internal-resolution clock of 14 working days from filing to first response. The MVP does not yet measure, display, or enforce this clock. A SLA-banner on the queue header (red after 14 working days for any case still in `open`, amber within 3 working days of breach) is the parallel of Whistleblower's overdue-triage banner and is on the roadmap. See Chapter 44.
- **ISO 10002 ┬¦7 ŌĆö operation of the complaints-handling process.** The standard prescribes acknowledgement-on-receipt, initial-assessment-and-classification, investigation, response, closure, and closing-the-loop notification. The MVP delivers acknowledgement (the green confirmation card carries the reference), initial assessment (manual ŌĆö by reading the row in the queue), investigation (manual ŌĆö the investigator reads the body and acts off-system), response (manual ŌĆö the investigator calls or messages the submitter outside the system; no in-app message thread exists), closure (the Resolved / Closed status), and notification (only via submitter polling on the track endpoint). The first-class versions of every manual step are on the roadmap. See Chapter 44.

## What's planned next

The MVP shipped the bare-bones grievance channel; the gap analysis (eight days old at the time of writing) marked the module as a Phase 2 priority alongside Whistleblower hardening. Six concrete next steps are on the immediate roadmap:

1. **`ComplaintSubmitted` and `ComplaintStatusChanged` events + notification fan-out.** The single largest functional gap. Today a submitter who files a complaint receives a reference and is then silent until they next poll. Phase 2 fires `ComplaintSubmitted` at intake with a listener that emails the submitter (when a name has been given that resolves to a known user ŌĆö the system would need a soft email-lookup helper for this) and posts an in-app banner to the investigator pool. `ComplaintStatusChanged` fires on every state change and emails the submitter when their case moves to Resolved / Closed. This is the ISO 10002 ┬¦7 closing-the-loop notification, made first-class.
2. **In-app message thread on the detail panel.** Today the detail panel is read-only. Phase 2 adds a `complaint_messages` satellite table (mirror of `whistleblower_messages` but without the encryption cast), a two-way thread on the detail panel, and a submitter-side reply form on the track surface. The submitter re-authenticates by re-supplying their reference, the same pattern Whistleblower uses for the tracking code.
3. **Resolution-summary surface and visible-to-submitter outcome notes.** Today the closure is just a status pill change. Phase 2 adds a `resolution_summary` text column on the row plus a "Closure summary" textarea in the panel; on closure the summary is rendered on the public track card so the submitter sees what was decided. This is the ISO 10002 ┬¦7 response step, made first-class.
4. **SLA timer + overdue banner under Labour Act ┬¦65ŌĆō┬¦66 / PSC 14-working-day clock.** A red banner appears on the queue header when any complaint is still in `open` after 14 working days; an amber banner appears 3 days before breach. Parallel to the existing Whistleblower SLA banner.
5. **Escalation-to-Whistleblower pathway.** When a triager reads a complaint and judges it falls under the ┬¦3 Act 720 protected-disclosure list, today they have to manually close the complaint and re-open the matter on `/whistleblower`. Phase 2 adds an "Escalate to Whistleblower" action on the panel that closes the complaint with `closed_escalated`, opens a new whistleblower case with the body copied across (encrypted-at-rest in the new envelope), and gives the submitter a fresh tracking code. The cross-link between the two cases is recorded for the investigator but not for the submitter.
6. **Throttle + abuse defence on the submission endpoint.** The MVP relies on "you must be logged in" as the abuse defence. Phase 2 adds a per-user `throttle:10,60` (10 per hour) plus an admin-side "block submitter" toggle for the edge case of a single user filing twenty duplicate complaints in an hour. The trade-off ŌĆö making submission less frictionless to defend a tiny edge case ŌĆö is the reason this is item six and not item one.

Each of these is small in code-size but consequential in user-experience surface; collectively they take the module from "grievance form-of-record" to "ISO 10002-compliant complaints management". The work is targeted for the same Phase 2 window as the Whistleblower hardening so the two sibling channels grow up together.

<!-- ===== 13_documents.md ===== -->

# Chapter 13 ŌĆö Documents

> *In one paragraph.* Documents is the institute's electronic registry ŌĆö every letter, memo, scanned form, or signed approval lives here as a single `Document` row that carries a year-numbered reference, a version history, a routing slip, and a layer of moveable annotations. HR composes a letter in the in-portal editor; a department head uploads a scanned invoice; a manager drops a signature on page two of an approval memo and forwards it to Finance. Throughout the journey the original file is never overwritten ŌĆö annotations live in their own table and are only burned onto a flattened PDF at download time. Stamps, letterheads, and watermarks come from per-user, per-department, and organization-wide asset libraries so the same approval mark or letterhead can be reused across thousands of documents without re-uploading. Documents is the connective tissue under Tickets attachments, Recruitment CVs, Complaints evidence, Whistleblower files, and the personnel-file Documents tab.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Workspace** group ŌåÆ **Documents** (top-level item, paper-with-fold icon). The page lives at `/documents` and ships with five tabs across the top of the table: **All**, **Inbox** (badge shows the count of items waiting on your action), **Sent**, **Drafts**, **Archive**. The asset libraries are reached separately under **Settings** ŌåÆ **Stamps / Letterheads / Watermarks**.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **hr_admin** ŌĆö every document, including org-wide override (`documents.manage`) to edit/delete any draft and to grant organization-wide shares. They also hold `document_assets.manage`, which is what unlocks the **organization** scope in every asset library.
    - **ceo** ŌĆö the same view as super_admin via the Gate::before fallthrough.
    - **dept_head / manager / employee / finance_officer / it_support / auditor** ŌĆö `documents.view` and `documents.create` are seeded onto every role by `DocumentPermissionsSeeder`, so any logged-in user can upload, see their own drafts, and respond to a document routed to them. What they *don't* hold by default is `documents.manage` or `documents.share_organization` ŌĆö those are the two privileged extras.
    - **anyone** with a document **routed** to them (`document_routes.to_user_id`) or **shared** with them (individual / department / org-wide) is widened into `DocumentPolicy::view` automatically, even if they don't otherwise have it in scope.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) keeps a per-employee documents drawer that runs on the legacy `employee_documents` table (HR upload + download only ŌĆö no routing); Tickets (Ch 11) accepts attachments stored under the Documents pipeline; Recruitment (Ch 9) parks CVs and offer letters there; Complaints (Ch 12) and Whistleblower (Ch 27) attach evidence; Audit Logs (Ch 24) listens to the `document_events` table for the tamper-evident provenance trail; DPA / Privacy (Ch 26) enforces the confidentiality classifications and burned-PDF watermarking.

## The screens

![Documents library ŌĆö tabs, search, table, upload CTA](../assets/screenshots/13_documents/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Header bar ŌĆö three cobalt CTAs in the top right: **Compose Letter** (in-portal HTML editor with letterhead picker), **Scan** (opens the device-camera Scanner component for paper documents), **Upload Document** (the standard slide-panel file picker). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Tab strip ŌĆö All, Inbox (with rose-tinted badge showing items awaiting your action), Sent, Drafts, Archive. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Table row ŌĆö monospaced `ref_no` (e.g. `CIHRMS/DOC/2026/0042`), title, owner, colour-toned status pill, updated date, and an Open link to the detail page.*

![Document detail ŌĆö viewer, annotation toolbar, routing slip, timeline](../assets/screenshots/13_documents/show.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Header ŌĆö breadcrumb back to the library, confidentiality + status pill (e.g. INTERNAL ┬Ę DRAFT), title, ref, owner, and a row of seven action buttons: Original, Burned PDF, Print, Share, Edit, Delete, and either Route or Withdraw depending on state. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Viewer ŌĆö embedded PDF (or single-page wrap of a scanned image) with two annotation-toolbar buttons above it (Add signature, Add stamp) and an overlay layer where annotations float as draggable, resizable, rotatable boxes. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Right rail ŌĆö when the document is awaiting your action, an amber "Awaiting your action" card with a comment field and two buttons (Sign & forward, Reject); below that the Routing Slip with the ordered list of recipients and their status; below that the Timeline of every event on the document.*

![Compose letter ŌĆö in-portal editor with letterhead picker and live preview](../assets/screenshots/13_documents/compose.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Composer pane (left) ŌĆö title, confidentiality, description, a fifteen-tool formatting toolbar (bold/italic/underline, H1/H2/H3, lists, alignment, blockquote, link, horizontal rule, clear format), and a `contenteditable` body that defaults to a four-line letter template. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Live preview pane (right) ŌĆö an iframe that renders the typed HTML with the selected letterhead exactly as TCPDF will produce the final PDF. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Header ŌĆö letterhead dropdown (every letterhead the user can see from the per/department/org libraries, default pre-selected) and the cobalt "Save as Document" button that pushes the rendered PDF into a fresh Draft document.*

![Stamp library ŌĆö Settings, scope tabs, grid of uploaded stamps](../assets/screenshots/13_documents/stamps.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Upload form ŌĆö name, scope (personal / department / organization), file picker (PNG only, Ōēż 1 MB). The Organization radio is disabled unless the user holds `document_assets.manage`; the Department radio is disabled unless the user is currently assigned to a department. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Scope tabs ŌĆö Personal, Department, Organization. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Stamp tile ŌĆö preview thumbnail, name, Remove link (only on stamps the user uploaded, or on every stamp for `document_assets.manage`).*

![Letterhead library ŌĆö Settings, header-height field, default flag](../assets/screenshots/13_documents/letterheads.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Upload form ŌĆö name, scope, header-height in mm (20ŌĆō80, defaults to 36), file (PNG or JPEG). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Letterhead tile ŌĆö banner preview, name, height label, and a green **default** chip on the seeded organization letterhead. The Remove button is hidden on default rows ŌĆö the policy refuses to delete them so the composer always has a fallback.*

![Watermark library ŌĆö Settings, text vs image, colour & angle](../assets/screenshots/13_documents/watermarks.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Upload form ŌĆö name, scope, type (Text / Image), conditional fields below: text + colour picker for text watermarks, or PNG picker for image watermarks. Opacity defaults to 0.18, angle to ŌłÆ30┬░. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Watermark tile ŌĆö for image watermarks the PNG preview, for text watermarks a rotated text rendering using the saved colour, opacity, and angle so you can see at a glance what the burned PDF will look like.*

![Share modal ŌĆö audience picker, expiry, active shares list](../assets/screenshots/13_documents/share.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Audience selector ŌĆö Individual user / Department / Entire organization. The last two are disabled when the document's confidentiality is `confidential` or `restricted` (an amber banner explains why). The "Entire organization" option is also disabled for users without `documents.share_organization` or `documents.manage`. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Optional `expires_at` datetime ŌĆö null means perpetual. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Active shares list ŌĆö every existing grant with audience type chip, label, optional expiry, and a Revoke link.*

> The seven screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.42). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Documents library

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Compose Letter** (top right) | Navigates to `/documents/compose` ŌĆö the in-portal HTML editor with letterhead picker and live preview. On save, the rendered PDF becomes a normal Document in Draft status. | `documents.create` (every role) | The most common document people create at the institute is a letter or memo. A native composer beats "write it in Word, export to PDF, upload" by three steps and removes the dependency on a desktop Office install. |
| **Scan** (top right) | Opens the Scanner component ŌĆö a camera-driven capture surface that produces a JPEG and hands it to the standard upload form pre-filled with `Scan ┬Ę DD/MM/YYYY` as the title. | `documents.create` | Paper-in, digital-out: clerks can take a photo of a memo or invoice on their phone instead of walking to the office scanner. The captured JPEG is wrapped into a single-page PDF by `DocumentRenderService::imageToPdf` at burn time, so the rest of the pipeline (annotations, routing, restricted watermark) works on it identically. |
| **Upload Document** (cobalt CTA) | Opens the right-hand slide panel. Required fields: Title, File (PDF, DOCX, DOC, PNG, JPG, JPEG; up to 25 MB). Optional: Description (Ōēż 2000 chars), Confidentiality (Internal / Confidential / Restricted ŌĆö defaults to Internal). On save, posts to `POST /documents` and redirects to the new document's Show page. | `documents.create` | Step-zero of every routed approval. The slide panel keeps the user on the library so they can see the row appear without a full reload. |
| **All / Inbox / Sent / Drafts / Archive** tabs | Re-fires the index query with the chosen scope. `Inbox` matches `document_routes.to_user_id = me AND status = in_progress`; `Sent` is the documents the user owns that are in_review or completed; `Drafts` is the user's own drafts; `Archive` is the archive bucket regardless of owner. The Inbox tab shows a rose pill with the count of in-progress routes addressed to the current user. | Anyone with `documents.view` | The five most-asked questions about the registry ŌĆö "what's mine, what needs me, what have I sent, what haven't I sent yet, what's been put to bed?" ŌĆö are surfaced as one click each. |
| **Search box** | Free-text filter ŌĆö case-insensitive `LIKE` across `title` and `ref_no`. Fires on Enter or on clicking the Search button. | Same | Numbered refs (CIHRMS/DOC/2026/0042) are the primary lookup key in paper-trail conversations; matching against the title catches everything else. |
| **Open** (per row) | Navigates to the document detail page. | `view` permission on that document (owner, routed-to/from, shared with, or `documents.manage`) | Whole-row hit target is faster than chasing a small "view" button. |
| **Pagination** (bottom) | Standard Inertia pagination ŌĆö 20 per page, preserves filters and tab through Inertia's `withQueryString()`. | Anyone with view | Keeps the table fast on registries with thousands of documents. |

> *Notes:* The library never trims a result set client-side. `DocumentController::index` reads `tab` and `q` from the request and applies them server-side so a user with no `documents.manage` permission simply never sees a row they shouldn't ŌĆö there is no "ghost row" the frontend has to hide. The Inbox count is one query separate from the table so it stays correct even when the user is on a different tab.

### Document detail

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back / Breadcrumb** | Returns to the documents library, preserving its tab + search + scroll position via Inertia. | Anyone | Standard navigation. |
| **Original** (download) | Streams the original uploaded file as it was uploaded ŌĆö no watermark, no flattened annotations. Backed by a pre-signed URL minted server-side with a 5-minute TTL on `/documents/{document}/download`. The download is logged to `document_events` with a `downloaded` row. | `view` on the document, **unless** confidentiality is `restricted` ŌĆö in that case the controller forces `$burned = true` regardless of the query string and returns a watermarked PDF instead. | Most signed documents are still consumed in their original form by downstream systems (court bundles, archive caches, partner banks). Restricted documents are a deliberate exception. |
| **Burned PDF** (download) | Returns a freshly-rendered PDF that flattens every saved annotation onto the page (signatures, stamps, free text ŌĆö see `DocumentRenderService::burn`) and, when a watermark template is attached or the doc is restricted, draws a diagonal semi-transparent watermark on every page. The renderer caches the result by `sha256(annotation_set)` per version when no per-viewer watermark is involved. | `view` | This is the single deliverable artefact for an external recipient ŌĆö one PDF with every approval mark already on it. |
| **Print** | Opens the burned PDF in a new tab and best-effort triggers the browser's print dialog. Falls back to the browser's built-in PDF toolbar when the popup blocker stops the auto-print (Chrome / Firefox / Safari all handle this differently ŌĆö a single iframe.print() route would behave inconsistently). | `view` | Hard-copy is still the legal form on most government desks; a one-click print path beats Save-then-Open-then-Ctrl-P. |
| **Share** | Opens the Share modal ŌĆö see the dedicated table below. | Owner, or `documents.manage` | A read-only audience separate from the active routing slip. Sharing a memo with the whole Finance department doesn't put it on anyone's action queue. |
| **Edit** | Opens the Edit drawer with title, description, confidentiality, letterhead picker, watermark picker, and watermark-mode dropdown (None / On burn / Always). PATCHes `/documents/{document}`. **Metadata-only** ŌĆö no file replacement (that's done by uploading a new version). Only enabled while the document is still in Draft status. | Owner of a Draft document | Once a document has been routed the metadata is part of the audit trail and cannot be silently rewritten; the Edit affordance disappears the moment the owner clicks Route. |
| **Delete** | Confirmation dialog ŌåÆ soft-delete (`SoftDeletes` trait, the row stays for audit replay and admin can restore from the console). Logs a `deleted` event. | Owner on a Draft document, or `documents.manage` on any document | Mis-uploads happen ŌĆö every test PDF that ever wandered into production needs an undo. Once the document is in review the owner can only Withdraw. |
| **Route** (cobalt CTA, Draft only) | Opens the Route modal with an ordered list of recipients, each with an action_required choice (Sign / Review / Approve / Acknowledge). On submit, `DocumentRoutingService::route` flips the document to `in_review`, creates one `DocumentRoute` row per recipient with sequence numbers, marks the first one `in_progress` and the rest `pending`, fires `DocumentRouted`, sends `DocumentAwaitingAction` to the first recipient. | Owner of a Draft document | This is the moment the document leaves the owner's hands and becomes a live workflow. Sequential by default; parallel routing is on the column but not yet exposed in the UI. |
| **Withdraw** (rose, In Review only) | Confirmation prompt ŌåÆ cancels every pending / in-progress route and flips the status to `withdrawn`. Logs a `withdrawn` event. | Owner of an in_review document, or `documents.manage` | Lets a sender pull back a memo that's gone to the wrong queue without forcing the recipient to manually reject it. |
| **Add signature** (Draft owner, or recipient with an active in_progress route) | Opens the SignaturePad modal ŌĆö a 500├Ś200 HTML canvas backed by the `signature_pad` library. Save emits a PNG data URL into the pending annotation. The user then clicks anywhere on the page to drop it; the click coordinates are converted to `x_pct / y_pct` and POSTed to `/documents/{document}/annotations`. | `DocumentPolicy::annotate` ŌĆö owner-on-draft OR recipient-on-in-progress | Wet signatures, on a touchscreen. The annotation is stored as a separate row, not baked into the file ŌĆö every time you re-open the page the signature is still draggable. |
| **Add stamp** (same gate) | Opens the StampPicker ŌĆö two tabs: **My library** (fetches from `settings.stamps.index` via Inertia-JSON and shows every personal / department / org stamp the user can see) and **Text stamp** (four colour-coded presets ŌĆö APPROVED ┬Ę RECEIVED ┬Ę FOR ACTION ┬Ę CONFIDENTIAL ŌĆö plus a custom-text + colour-picker for ad-hoc text stamps). Library picks load the PNG as a base64 data URL and stamp it on the page; text stamps render as a coloured outlined rectangle with the text inside. | Same | Two flavours: the institutional rubber-stamp PNG that finance keeps re-using, or a one-off coloured text stamp when the user doesn't have an asset for it. |
| **Annotation manipulation** (drag the box, drag a corner, drag the top dot) | Every annotation is wrapped in a `DraggableAnnotation` component: drag the body to move (clamped to the page), drag any of four corner handles to resize (clamped 4ŌĆō80 % per side), drag the top "rotate" dot to rotate. On pointer-up it PATCHes `/documents/{document}/annotations/{annotation}` with the new x/y/w/h/rotation. Each move logs an `annotation_moved` or `annotation_resized` event with the before/after values. | The annotation's creator (until the route it belongs to is `completed`), or the document owner while the document is still Draft | This is the core of the "manipulable, not flattened" claim: positions live in the database, not in a baked PDF. A signer who put their signature too low can drag it up; a Draft-stage owner can move someone else's stamp into the right corner. After sign-off (the route's status is `completed`) the annotation locks ŌĆö `DocumentPolicy::moveAnnotation` returns false ŌĆö so the sign-off can't be silently relocated. |
| **Annotation delete** (rose ├Ś button on hover) | Confirmation ŌåÆ DELETE `/documents/{document}/annotations/{id}`. | The annotation's creator only ŌĆö never the document owner | Same one-way safeguard as document delete. The deletion is logged as a normal `annotated` event with the type metadata so the audit trail records it. |
| **Sign & forward** (amber Awaiting card) | Sets the route's decision to `complete`, advances to the next pending route (or completes the document if none remains), fires the next `DocumentAwaitingAction` notification, logs a `forwarded` or `completed` event. | Recipient of the currently-in-progress route | The one-button "I'm done with this, send it on" path that every signer hits the most often. |
| **Reject** (amber Awaiting card) | Prompts for a reason (mandatory), sets the route's decision to `reject`, cancels every downstream pending route, flips the document to `rejected`, notifies the owner via `DocumentCompletedNotice(rejected: true)`, fires `DocumentRejected`. | Same | The terminal "no" ŌĆö once a recipient rejects, no other recipient gets the document. The reason text is required so the owner has something to act on. |
| **Comment** (textarea above Sign & forward / Reject) | Optional free-text up to whatever the textarea allows; saved to `document_routes.comment` alongside the decision. | Same | The "I'm approving subject to" or "I'm rejecting becauseŌĆ”" note. Visible on the routing slip permanently. |

> *Notes:* The annotation type drives the event type ŌĆö signatures and initials log `signed`, stamps log `stamped`, anything else logs `annotated`. Move and resize log separately so an audit replay can tell "the signature moved by 2 cm" from "the signature was created". The whole event log is rendered by `TimelineRail.vue` in chronological order in the right rail.

### Compose form (in-portal letter editor)

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Title** | required, max 255 | `documents.create` | Becomes both the document title and the suggested PDF filename. |
| **Confidentiality** | one of `internal / confidential / restricted` | Same | Drives the burned-PDF watermark behaviour on download and the share-modal banner. |
| **Description** | optional, max 2000 | Same | Free-text audit-trail note; never appears in the PDF itself. |
| **Letterhead** (dropdown) | optional foreign key on `letterhead_templates` | Same | Defaults to whichever `letterhead_templates.is_default` row the user can see (the seeded `CIHRM-GHANA (default)` letterhead in fresh installs). The picker shows every personal / department / org-scope letterhead the user has access to via `LetterheadTemplateController::index`. Selecting "No letterhead" produces a plain PDF. |
| **Body** (contenteditable) | required (form button stays disabled until both title and body are non-empty) | Same | Backed by the browser's `contenteditable` + `document.execCommand` toolbar ŌĆö bold, italic, underline, H1/H2/H3, bullet and numbered lists, alignment, blockquote, link, horizontal rule, clear formatting. The HTML is sanitised server-side by `DocumentComposerService::sanitizeHtml` to strip `style`, `class`, every `on*` event handler, and `javascript:` URLs before TCPDF renders it. |
| **Live preview** | n/a (read-only iframe) | Same | An iframe that re-renders on every keystroke, watching `body_html` + `letterhead_id`. Uses the same fonts and approximate margins as TCPDF so the user sees what the PDF will look like before pressing Save. |
| **Save as Document** | n/a ŌĆö button | Same | Triggers `documents.compose.store`. The service draws the letterhead PNG as the page header on every page (driven by `letterhead.header_height_mm`), renders the body, saves the PDF as version 1 of a brand-new Document in Draft status, links the letterhead template via `documents.letterhead_id`, fires the `Uploaded` event with `composed: true` in the payload, and redirects to the new Show page. |

> *Notes:* The composer is intentionally toolbar-driven and not based on a heavyweight editor like TinyMCE or Lexical. `execCommand` is technically deprecated by WHATWG but every shipping browser still implements it, and the alternative ŌĆö a full Range/Selection rewrite ŌĆö is a separate project. The HTML allowlist is intentionally narrow (`p`, `br`, headings, lists, link, blockquote, hr) so a user pasting from Word can't smuggle in styles that wreck the PDF.

### Upload form

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Title** | required, max 255 | `documents.create` | Stored on the Document row. |
| **Description** | optional, max 2000 | Same | Free-text audit note. |
| **Confidentiality** | one of `internal / confidential / restricted`; defaults to internal | Same | See the watermark + share-guard sections ŌĆö this is the field everything downstream consults. |
| **File** | required, max 25 MB, MIME-checked: pdf / docx / doc / png / jpg / jpeg | Same | The version is hashed (SHA-256) before move; the hash is stored on `document_versions.sha256` so a future tampering check can re-hash the file on disk and compare. Images get wrapped in a single-page PDF by the burn renderer when they're downloaded; DOCX is held as-is in the MVP because burn-in for Office formats is on the roadmap. |
| **Tags** *(in scope of the FormRequest, not currently in the UI)* | optional array of strings, each Ōēż 40 chars | Same | The column is shipped (`documents.tags JSON`) but the picker is on the Phase 1 backlog. |

> *Notes:* `documents.ref_no` is generated by `DocumentService::nextRefNo`, which calls `SequenceService::next('document_ref:'.year)` ŌĆö that service row-locks the counter, so the historic retry loop on collisions is no longer needed. The format is `CIHRMS/DOC/{YYYY}/{NNNN}`. The Composer route still uses the old count()+1 retry loop because PostgreSQL refuses `FOR UPDATE` on aggregate queries ŌĆö that's a known wart, tracked against the SequenceService convention note.

### Share modal

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Audience type** | required, one of `user / department / organization` | Owner, or `documents.manage` | For documents marked `confidential` or `restricted`, the backend (via `DocumentShareService::assertConfidentialityAllows`) rejects anything other than `user` with a `DomainException` mapped to 422. The frontend mirrors the guard with a disabled radio + amber banner. |
| **Audience id** | required when `audience_type != organization`, integer, must exist on `users` or `departments` respectively | Same | Resolved by `DocumentShareService::assertAudienceExists`. |
| **Expires at** | optional datetime, must be `after:now` | Same | Null means perpetual; `DocumentPolicy::view` ignores expired shares automatically via the `Active` scope. |
| **Share** (cobalt CTA) | n/a ŌĆö button | Same | Idempotent: re-sharing the same (doc, audience_type, audience_id) trio refreshes `expires_at` + `granted_at` instead of inserting a duplicate (the table has a `document_shares_unique` constraint that would refuse a dupe anyway). |
| **Revoke** (per existing share) | n/a ŌĆö link | Same | Deletes the share row and writes an `unshared` event to the audit log with the audience metadata. |

> *Notes:* Org-wide visibility is a privileged action even for owners ŌĆö the controller short-circuits with a 403 unless the user holds `documents.share_organization` (granted with the management bundle) or `documents.manage`. The intent is to keep "broadcast" sharing behind an explicit permission so a regular employee can't share Internal HR memos to every staff inbox with one click.

### Asset libraries (Settings ŌåÆ Stamps / Letterheads / Watermarks)

| Action | Validation | Who can use it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Upload stamp** | name Ōēż 120; owner_scope Ōłł `personal / department / organization`; file PNG Ōēż 1 MB | Personal: anyone. Department: must be a member of that department. Organization: `document_assets.manage`. | Personal stamps are visible only to the uploader; department stamps visible to anyone whose `employee.department_id` matches; organization stamps visible to everyone. Width/height percentages default to 18 % ├Ś 6 %. |
| **Upload letterhead** | name Ōēż 120; scope same; header_height_mm 20ŌĆō80 (default 36); file PNG or JPEG | Same scope gate | Used by both the Compose page (PDF header) and the Edit drawer letterhead picker. The seeded org default (`CIHRM-GHANA (default)`, copied from `public/img/letterhead.png`) is protected ŌĆö `LetterheadTemplatePolicy::delete` returns false on any `is_default` row so the composer always has a fallback. |
| **Upload watermark** | name; scope; type `text / image`; if text: text + colour + opacity (0ŌĆō1, default 0.18) + angle (default ŌłÆ30┬░); if image: PNG file | Same | Text watermarks render the saved text at the saved colour/opacity/angle across the page diagonal. Image watermarks draw the PNG centred at 60 % page width. Either kind can be attached per-document via `documents.watermark_id`. |
| **Remove asset** | n/a | Asset creator, or `document_assets.manage`. Default letterheads can never be removed. | Soft-deletes are not used here ŌĆö the asset row is deleted outright. Documents referencing it via `letterhead_id` / `watermark_id` cascade-null (the foreign keys are `nullOnDelete`). |
| **Preview** | n/a | Anyone with `viewAny` on that asset class (every authenticated user) | Streams the file from `storage/app/assets/...` via a route-bound controller method, so the path never leaks. For text watermarks the preview is rendered in-browser instead. |

> *Notes:* The three asset libraries are perfectly parallel by design ŌĆö same scope enum (`AssetOwnerScope`), same policy shape, same controller methods, same Inertia page layout. This is the canonical pattern for "library of reusable artefacts" features in CIHRMS ŌĆö see the architecture memo on EnumŌåÆFormRequestŌåÆServiceŌåÆResource for the rationale.

## The data behind it

The documents subsystem is built on seven tables that travel together and three new tables for asset libraries.

- **`documents`** ŌĆö the master row. UUID for the route key, year-numbered `ref_no` (unique), title, description, owner, current_version_id, status (`draft / in_review / completed / rejected / withdrawn / archived`), confidentiality (`internal / confidential / restricted`), parallel_routing flag (column exists, UI on the backlog), tags JSON, **letterhead_id** + **watermark_id** + **watermark_mode** (`none / on_burn / always`), soft-delete column.
- **`document_versions`** ŌĆö one row per file revision. version_no auto-increments per document; original_name and MIME preserved for display; SHA-256 stored for integrity check; storage_path is `documents/{uuid}/v{n}/{hash}.{ext}` on the `local` disk.
- **`document_routes`** ŌĆö the sequential routing slip. One row per recipient with sequence number, from_user (the previous signer or the owner for the first stop), to_user, action_required (`sign / review / approve / acknowledge`), status (`pending / in_progress / completed / rejected / cancelled`), optional due_at, acted_at, and comment.
- **`document_annotations`** ŌĆö the manipulable layer. Every signature, stamp, text note, initial, and highlight lives here as its own row with `type`, `page` (1-indexed), `x_pct / y_pct / w_pct / h_pct` (percentages of page dimensions so geometry survives rescaling), `rotation` in degrees, optional `route_id` linking the annotation to the signer's route step, and a JSON `data` blob carrying the PNG base64 / SVG / text / colour.
- **`document_events`** ŌĆö the immutable audit trail. Every meaningful action ŌĆö uploaded, version_added, routed, annotated, signed, stamped, forwarded, rejected, completed, withdrawn, downloaded, archived, updated, deleted, shared, unshared, annotation_moved, annotation_resized ŌĆö writes one row with actor, occurred_at, and a JSON payload of the before/after values. This is the single source of truth for the audit pack.
- **`document_shares`** ŌĆö the read-only widening of `view` permission added by Documents v2 Phase 1. (document_id, audience_type, audience_id) is uniquely constrained so the same audience can't be granted twice; the controller refreshes the existing row on re-grant.
- **`stamp_assets`**, **`letterhead_templates`**, **`watermark_templates`** ŌĆö the three asset libraries. All three share the same shape: scope (`personal / department / organization`), nullable owner_id, name, storage_path + MIME (text watermarks store the text + colour on the row instead), `created_by`, indexed `(owner_scope, owner_id)`.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **Annotations are not flattened.** This is the headline architectural decision. The `document_annotations` table holds the geometry; `DocumentRenderService::burn` only flattens at download time, and the burned PDF is cached per `(version_id, annotation_set_hash)` so re-downloading doesn't re-render. The on-disk original is never modified once stored. This is what makes "drag the signature" possible at all, and it's also what makes the `annotation_moved` audit-log event meaningful ŌĆö there's a real before/after to record.
- **Sign-off lock is real but local to the annotation.** Once an annotation's `route_id` belongs to a `completed` route, `DocumentPolicy::moveAnnotation` refuses every further geometry change. The annotation can no longer be moved, resized, rotated, or deleted by anyone ŌĆö not the creator, not the owner, not `documents.manage`. This is the integrity guarantee the routing slip leans on. (Free-floating annotations placed at Draft stage by the owner are still moveable by the owner.)
- **Restricted ŌćÆ always burned.** Confidentiality is enforced at the download endpoint, not at the storage layer. When `confidentiality = restricted`, `DocumentController::download` flips `$burned = true` regardless of the query string, and the burned PDF carries a per-viewer watermark with the viewer's name + timestamp + classification. A leaked PDF is traceable back to whoever pulled it.
- **Watermark mode is a per-document switch.** `documents.watermark_mode = 'always'` makes the chosen watermark apply to *every* download ŌĆö including the "Original" link. `on_burn` only watermarks the Burned PDF path. `none` disables. The seeded default is `on_burn`, which means most documents look pristine when downloaded as the original and only carry a watermark when the burned-PDF path is taken. This is configurable per document on the Edit drawer.
- **Three-scope ownership.** Personal, department, organization. The same three-radio scope appears on every asset library (stamps, letterheads, watermarks) and on every share audience. The semantics are identical across all six surfaces ŌĆö "owner_id is the user OR the department OR null", and the index query unions the three scopes the user can see. Policies always go: org rows visible to all ŌåÆ department rows visible to that department's members ŌåÆ personal rows visible to the uploader.
- **eSign is plumbed for Recruitment only.** There is a real `ESignWebhookController` and an `ESignProvider` interface with `createEnvelope`, `status`, `signedDocumentUrl`, and `void` methods ŌĆö but in the MVP they're wired to the Recruitment module's offer-letter workflow (matching against `applicants.esign_envelope_id`) and not to the generic Documents pipeline. From Documents, eSign is a roadmap item; signing a routed document inside the portal uses the SignaturePad + annotation pair described above, which is legally valid as an electronic signature under the Electronic Transactions Act (Act 772) ┬¦10 but is not yet anchored to an external eSign provider's audit trail.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö Documents tab.** Uses a separate, legacy `employee_documents` table for the per-employee documents drawer in the personnel file. That drawer is HR-only and has no routing, no annotations, no asset libraries ŌĆö it's the simple "attach a payslip to this employee" path. The full document register is the better home for anything that needs to be signed or routed.
- **Tickets (Ch 11) ŌĆö attachments.** When a ticket has more than a single photo attached, those attachments are pushed into the Documents pipeline (with confidentiality `internal`, owner = the requester) and the ticket row holds the document id. The ticket detail page surfaces them via the share-with relation so a ticket-handler reading the issue gets `view` on the supporting documents automatically.
- **Recruitment (Ch 9) ŌĆö CVs and offer letters.** Every CV uploaded by an applicant is stored as a Document with confidentiality `confidential` (so it can never be org-wide-shared, only person-to-person); every offer letter generated for the applicant is also stored here and is the surface the eSign integration writes back to when the applicant signs.
- **Complaints (Ch 12) and Whistleblower (Ch 27) ŌĆö evidence.** Both modules store the supporting evidence (screenshots, letters, voice memos) as Documents with confidentiality `restricted` so the burned-PDF + viewer-watermark behaviour applies on every download. The whistleblower module additionally writes the original filename out as the document title so the evidence is searchable.
- **Audit Logs (Ch 24).** `document_events` is the single source of truth for the audit replay. Every state transition writes a row; nothing else in the system writes to that table. Audit Logs simply runs `SELECT * FROM document_events WHERE document_id = ? ORDER BY occurred_at` and renders the timeline.
- **DPA / Privacy (Ch 26).** Confidentiality classifications are the policy hook the DPA chapter relies on. The export pack uses `documents.confidentiality` + `document_shares.audience_type` to compute the data-subject access response ŌĆö every document the subject can see is included; every share that names them is listed in the cover note.
- **Notifications (mail, in-app).** Two notifications: `DocumentAwaitingAction` (sent when a route turns `in_progress` ŌĆö to the recipient) and `DocumentCompletedNotice` (sent when the document completes or is rejected ŌĆö to the owner). Both write to the user's notification feed and to email.
- **Events (Laravel listeners).** `DocumentRouted`, `DocumentSigned`, `DocumentCompleted`, `DocumentRejected` ŌĆö these are the four module-level events the Analytics module subscribes to. The Notifications module is the only other current listener.

## Standards touchpoints

- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17 ŌĆö lawful basis for processing personal data.** Every document the system holds is processed under the employment-contract basis when it concerns staff, and under the public-task basis when it concerns external citizens (applicants, complainants). The confidentiality field is the operational hook: documents marked `confidential` or `restricted` cannot be broadcast-shared, which is how ┬¦18 (data minimisation) is enforced in practice. See Chapter 26.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦40 ŌĆö right to erasure.** Soft delete is the default; a tamper-evident audit-log row records every delete. The DPA-grade redaction job that does true erasure (overwriting the file on disk and the row in the database) is roadmapped in Phase 2 of the government-grade plan. The seven-year retention window for HR documents (and the document-type-specific retention table) is also a Phase-2 item; today, the soft-deleted-but-not-purged row stays forever.
- **Electronic Transactions Act, 2008 (Act 772) ┬¦10 ŌĆö recognition of electronic signatures.** The annotation pipeline produces a legally-recognised electronic signature: the signer authenticates with their staff_id + password, the canvas drawing is stored as a PNG owned by their user_id, the route record carries the timestamp and the routing context, and the entire act writes immutable rows to `document_events`. The annotation lock-after-sign-off is the integrity guarantee ┬¦10(d) of the Act calls out. What is **not** yet present is a qualified electronic signature with a third-party trust service ŌĆö that requires an eSign provider integration, currently scoped only to the Recruitment offer-letter workflow.
- **Evidence Act, 1975 (NRCD 323) ┬¦3 ŌĆö admissibility of electronic records.** The combination of (i) immutable per-version SHA-256 hash, (ii) tamper-evident `document_events` log, (iii) signer-locked annotations after completion, and (iv) per-viewer watermarked downloads gives the Documents module a reasonable evidentiary chain. The remaining gap is a court-grade export pack (a single ZIP with the original file, the burned PDF, the events log as CSV, and a manifest signed with an institute key) ŌĆö that's planned in the audit-pack work referenced by Chapter 24.
- **ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A.5.12 / A.5.13 ŌĆö classification and labelling of information.** The three-tier confidentiality enum (`internal / confidential / restricted`) is the classification scheme; the burned-PDF watermark is the visible labelling on every page when classification is non-default; the share-guard is the protective handling control. The classification is intentionally simple (three rungs, not five) to keep operational compliance achievable.
- **ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A.8.10 ŌĆö information deletion.** Soft delete + admin restore + DPA-redaction job (Phase 2) covers the deletion lifecycle. The retention rules per document type are still policy-by-convention; turning them into a per-type retention table is Phase 2 work.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap lands four things on this module: (1) the **bulk routing** workflow ŌĆö pick a stack of documents from the library, attach the same routing slip, fire one batch ŌĆö for the Vice-Chancellor's end-of-year sign-off run; (2) **parallel routing** ŌĆö the `documents.parallel_routing` column has been on the schema since v1, but the controller currently treats every route as sequential; flipping it on requires a small change in `DocumentRoutingService::route` so that every recipient starts in `in_progress` simultaneously and the document only completes when the last one acts; (3) **eSign integration for the documents pipeline** ŌĆö extending the existing `ESignProvider` contract beyond Recruitment so an in-portal signature on a routed document can optionally be co-signed via Zoho Sign or DocuSign for documents that need an external trust anchor; (4) **DOCX/XLSX burn-in** ŌĆö `DocumentRenderService::burn` currently passes Office documents through unmodified because LibreOffice headless conversion is not yet on the deployment image; once it is, annotations can be flattened onto DOCX downloads the same way they are on PDF. Phase 2 adds the per-type retention table (so HR letters auto-archive after seven years, payroll slips after six, ticket attachments after two), the redaction job that satisfies DPA ┬¦40 right-to-erasure, and the court-grade evidence ZIP. See the government gap analysis (Chapter 46) for the full sequence.

<!-- ===== 14_chat.md ===== -->

# Chapter 14 ŌĆö Chat

> *In one paragraph.* Chat is the institute's internal one-to-one messenger ŌĆö the place where two members of staff can have a private, threaded conversation without leaving CIHRMS. It is not the SMS console (that is Messaging in Chapter 15), and it is not the Notifications stream (Chapter 16). It is people-to-people, inside the building, on the same screen as their work. The May 2026 redesign reduced the directory to a single calm column with avatar, name, last-message preview, and a cobalt indicator bar that lights up when there is something unread. Behind the screen are three tables ŌĆö `conversations`, `conversation_user`, and `chat_messages` ŌĆö and a 4-second polling loop that keeps each open thread fresh until the institute moves to real-time websockets in Phase 2.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Organization** group ŌåÆ **Chat** (top-level item, `forum` icon). The same entry-point is wired to the global unread badge in the top bar ŌĆö the number next to the icon is `unreadTotal`, recomputed on every page load and on every poll tick.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **Every authenticated user** ŌĆö Chat is visibility-flat. There is no `chat.view` permission and no `chat.manage` permission; every employee can open the directory, every employee can start a 1:1 with any other employee, every employee can send into a thread they are a participant of.
    - **super_admin, ceo, hr_admin, dept_head, manager, employee, finance_officer, auditor, it_support** ŌĆö identical UI, identical capabilities. The sidebar item is marked `visible: true` unconditionally.
    - **Conversation visibility itself** is gated row-by-row by `ConversationPolicy::view`: you can only open a thread if you are on its participant pivot. A non-participant who guesses an id gets a 403 from the policy before the controller even loads the messages.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö every participant is an Employee + User pair; Messaging admin (Ch 15) ŌĆö the sibling, separate, SMS-and-USSD outbound channel run by HR; Notifications (Ch 16) ŌĆö the in-app notification stream is a *separate* surface, and at MVP there is no listener that converts a new chat message into a notification (see "Honest gaps" below); Audit Logs (Ch 24) ŌĆö message create/delete events are not yet wired into the tamper-evident audit trail (also a gap); DPA & Privacy (Ch 26) ŌĆö message retention and consent are addressed at policy level under DPA Act 843; Standards (Ch 44) ŌĆö ISO 27001 A.13 communications-security mapping lives there; Roadmap (Ch 46) ŌĆö the websocket cut-over (Laravel Reverb), file attachments, group chats, and audit hookup are all on the Phase 2 backlog.

## The screens

There are exactly two screens in this module: the **directory** at `/chat` and the **thread view** at `/chat/{conversation}`. There is no separate "compose" screen ŌĆö composing is the act of clicking a person on the directory, which either opens the existing 1:1 thread with them or creates a brand-new one and drops you straight into it. That is intentional: a private message between two named people does not need a "to" field.

![Chat directory ŌĆö single-column list with recent threads + all people](../assets/screenshots/14_chat/directory.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Page header ŌĆö a small "INTERNAL CHAT" eyebrow, "Messages" as the H1, and a rose-tinted unread pill on the right that only appears when `unreadTotal > 0`. The pill animates a small dot to draw the eye without being shouty. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Search bar ŌĆö single text input with a leading magnifier glyph; submits on Enter or via the explicit "Search" button; a "Clear" affordance shows up next to it once a query is active. The search is server-side, scanning `users.name`, `users.email`, and `users.staff_id` with a `LIKE %q%` against the whole user table. ┬Ę ŌØĖ "Continue chatting" strip ŌĆö your most-recently-touched conversations, up to six, with the freshest at the top. Sorted by `COALESCE(last_message_at, created_at) DESC` so a brand-new "+ New" thread that has no messages yet still appears (it shows the placeholder "Say hello ŌĆö" in place of a preview). ┬Ę ŌØ╣ "All people" list ŌĆö every other user in the institute paginated 18 per page, with the same row shape as Continue chatting but with the position and department shown instead of a last-message preview, and a tiny "Start chat" CTA on the right that turns cobalt on hover. ┬Ę ŌØ║ The cobalt left-edge bar ŌĆö three pixels wide, transparent by default, lights cobalt on hover, persistent-and-glowing on unread.*

![Chat thread ŌĆö left rail of threads, right pane of bubbles + composer](../assets/screenshots/14_chat/thread.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Back arrow ŌåÆ returns to the directory; the rest of the page header is the other party's name, position, and department, with a small "INTERNAL CHAT" eyebrow above. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Threads rail (left) ŌĆö up to 30 of your other conversations, current one highlighted in cobalt tint; each row is avatar, name, last-message preview, time. A "+ New" link at the top right of the rail jumps back to the directory. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Active chat header ŌĆö other party's avatar, name, and a small emerald "Live ┬Ę auto-refreshes" badge that is just a visual cue; the polling cycle is fixed at 4 seconds and is paused while the tab is in the background. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Message stream ŌĆö bubbles aligned right for "your" messages (cobalt gradient, square-corner bottom-right), left for theirs (warm white, square-corner bottom-left); date separators are letter-spaced uppercase JetBrains Mono labels between fading hairlines; each bubble shows the time in monospace at its bottom edge; a "Delete" affordance appears on hover for your own bubbles. ┬Ę ŌØ║ Composer ŌĆö single-row textarea that grows up to 140 px tall, plus an 11├Ś11 cobalt-gradient send button with a paper-plane icon. Enter sends; Shift+Enter inserts a newline; the button is disabled while empty or while a previous send is in flight; the textarea has a `maxlength` of 4000 characters mirroring the server-side cap.*

> The two screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 of the capture pass. Until then the build substitutes a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Chat directory (`/chat`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Search box** ("Search name, staff ID, or emailŌĆ”") | Free-text filter against `users.name`, `users.email`, `users.staff_id` with a `LIKE` scan; submits on Enter (or via the "Search" button). Uses Inertia `router.get(... preserveState, replace)` so it doesn't pile up history entries. | Anyone logged in | Most lookups in an institute of even a few hundred staff are by name; the search is unindexed `LIKE` on purpose because the user table is small and we wanted the staff_id and email matches in the same query. |
| **Search** button | Same as pressing Enter ŌĆö calls `router.get` with the current `q`. | Same | Mouse-and-trackpad users; a visible button doubles as a "did it run?" affordance. |
| **Clear** | Empties the query and re-fires the request without `q`. Only appears when a query is active. | Same | One-click undo. |
| **Continue chatting row** (avatar + name + last-message preview + time) | Clicks through to `/chat/{conversation}` ŌĆö the existing thread. The cobalt left bar lights when `unread_count > 0`; a small red badge on the avatar shows the unread integer (capped to a small badge but never truncated in number). | Anyone logged in (any participant of the thread) | Surfacing your six most recently active threads at the top is how the screen pays for itself ŌĆö it's where 80% of the clicks land. The bar + badge combination gives the eye two signals for the same fact. |
| **All people row** (avatar + name + position + department + "Start chat" CTA) | Clicks through to `/chat/with/{user}` which **opens-or-creates** the 1:1 conversation in a single round-trip and redirects to its show page. Idempotent ŌĆö clicking the same person twice lands on the same conversation id. | Anyone logged in | A directory entry should not require thinking about whether a thread exists yet; the controller's `findOrCreateOneOnOne` (DB-locked) handles both paths transparently. |
| **Pagination** (link strip) | Standard Inertia paginator ŌĆö 18 people per page, current page highlighted in cobalt, disabled chips greyed. | Same | Keeps the page bounded on institutes with hundreds of users. |
| **Unread pill** (page header, only shown when unread > 0) | Read-only ŌĆö shows the institute-wide unread total for *you* across every thread you're a participant of. Computed server-side by `ChatController::unreadTotalFor()` (joins `chat_messages` with the participant pivot and counts messages newer than your `last_read_at`, excluding your own). | Anyone logged in | One number, top-right, that answers "do I have anything waiting?" ŌĆö the same number is the global badge in the sidebar. |

> *Notes:* The directory is intentionally *one* clean list, not a card grid. The pre-redesign version was a 2-column zig-zag of coloured cards with no last-message preview; that gave no information value above what the sidebar already shows. The current layout uses a `rounded-2xl` container with `divide-y` rows, a 56px avatar column on the left, the body in the middle, and the meta on the right. The cobalt accent bar lives at `position: absolute; left: 0` with `transform: scaleY` transitions so the entry and exit are physical, not just colour swaps. On screens narrower than 640 px the time and CTA hide so the row is just avatar + name + preview.

### Chat thread (`/chat/{conversation}`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back arrow** (top left) | Returns to the directory. Inertia preserves the directory's scroll position. | The two thread participants | Standard back affordance. |
| **Threads rail** ŌĆö row click | Navigates to `/chat/{id}` for that other thread; the current thread row is highlighted in `bg-secondary/[0.06]`. | Anyone with a thread (your own threads only) | A live "switchboard" ŌĆö you can hop between active conversations without going back to the directory. The rail is capped at 30, sorted by `COALESCE(last_message_at, created_at) DESC`, so a brand-new conversation that has no messages yet still shows up. |
| **+ New** (top right of threads rail) | Returns to the directory so you can pick someone new. | Same | Discoverable starting point for a fresh thread when you're already inside another. |
| **Chat header** (other party's avatar + name + position/department + "Live ┬Ę auto-refreshes" badge) | Read-only display. The "Live" badge is purely cosmetic ŌĆö there is no websocket; it is the marketing label for the 4-second poll. | Same | Identity at a glance plus a reassuring "this thing updates without me having to F5" signal. |
| **Message bubble** ŌĆö outgoing (cobalt gradient, right-aligned) | Hovering reveals a tiny "Delete" link; clicking it `window.confirm`s and then `DELETE /chat/messages/{id}` soft-deletes the message (sets `deleted_for_everyone_at`). The bubble is removed from your view; the recipient's view replaces the body with `(message deleted)` on the next render or poll. | The original sender only ŌĆö `ConversationPolicy::deleteMessage` returns `true` iff `$message->sender_id === $user->id` | "Retract" semantics ŌĆö a sender's prerogative to take back something they typed, without nuking the audit trail. The row stays in the DB. |
| **Message bubble** ŌĆö incoming (warm white, left-aligned, small teal avatar chip) | Read-only ŌĆö no delete affordance shown to non-senders. | Same | A recipient can't retract someone else's message. |
| **Date separator** (e.g. `2026-05-23`) | Read-only visual divider rendered automatically whenever the date of the next message differs from the date of the previous one. | All readers | Long threads are unreadable without day anchors. The separators are letter-spaced uppercase in JetBrains Mono between fading hairline rules ŌĆö they read as headings without being noisy. |
| **Composer textarea** | Bound to `form.body`. Auto-grows up to 140 px tall. Enter sends. Shift+Enter inserts a newline. `maxlength="4000"` matches the server-side validation. | The two thread participants ŌĆö `ConversationPolicy::send` mirrors `view` | Single most-used input on the page; Enter-sends is the convention everyone expects. |
| **Send button** (paper-plane icon, cobalt gradient) | Pushes an optimistic bubble (with a `tmp-ŌĆ”` id and `_pending: true`) into `liveMessages`, then `form.post('chat.send')`. On 2xx, `form.reset('body')` clears the textarea and the props-watcher replaces `liveMessages` with the fresh server list. On 4xx/5xx, the optimistic bubble is removed and the error is shown under the composer. Disabled while empty or while a previous send is in flight. | Same | Optimistic UI keeps the felt latency at near-zero while the network round-trip happens. The "do not call `pollNow()` from `onSuccess`" rule is in place because doing so used to race the watcher and produce a duplicate bubble ŌĆö see "Honest gaps" notes below. |
| **Body validation error** (under the composer) | Renders `form.errors.body` when the server returns 422 ŌĆö typically "The body field is required." or the 4000-char cap message. | Same | A polite, scoped error rather than a global toast. |

> *Notes:* The thread page hydrates from one Inertia render ŌĆö `ChatController::show` loads the conversation, its participants and their employees + departments, the 30 most recent threads for the sidebar rail, and the latest 200 visible messages. Past that 200 we currently truncate (there is no infinite scroll yet ŌĆö see "Honest gaps"). The "Live" badge is a *poll*, not a websocket: `setInterval(pollNow, 4000)` runs while the tab is visible, calls `GET /chat/{id}/poll?since=<lastSeenId>`, and merges any new messages into `liveMessages` with a `Set` dedupe so a concurrent post-send + poll never adds the same row twice. The `visibilitychange` listener pauses the poll while the tab is in the background and fires one immediate `pollNow()` when the user returns.

### "Compose" ŌĆö the implicit flow

There is no compose dialog. The user journey is:

1. Click a row in **All people** (directory).
2. The browser is sent to `GET /chat/with/{user}`.
3. The controller calls `Conversation::findOrCreateOneOnOne($me, $other)` ŌĆö wrapped in a `DB::transaction` with a `sharedLock` re-check to close the race where two concurrent "open chat with B" calls from A could otherwise create two rows for the same pair.
4. The user is redirected to `/chat/{conversation}` ŌĆö the brand-new thread, ready to receive its first message.

The same path is what gets called when an unrelated module deep-links into chat (for example "Message this employee" buttons in roadmapped surfaces): construct the URL with the *other* user's id, send the browser there, the controller does the rest.

## The data behind it

Three tables sit behind the screens.

- **`conversations`** ŌĆö one row per thread. Columns: `id`, `is_group` (boolean, defaults `false` ŌĆö the schema is group-ready but the MVP creates only 1:1 rows), `title` (varchar, null for 1:1 threads since we render the participants' names client-side), `last_message_at` (timestamp, indexed, null on freshly-created threads), `created_at`, `updated_at`, `deleted_at` (soft-delete column).
- **`conversation_user`** ŌĆö the participant pivot. Columns: `id`, `conversation_id`, `user_id`, `last_read_at`, `archived_at`, `muted_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. A unique compound index on `(conversation_id, user_id)` enforces "each user appears at most once per conversation". A secondary index on `(user_id, last_read_at)` accelerates the unread-counter query. `archived_at` and `muted_at` are present on the schema but **not yet surfaced in the UI** ŌĆö they are the levers for Phase 2's "archive thread" and "mute notifications" affordances.
- **`chat_messages`** ŌĆö the messages themselves. Columns: `id`, `conversation_id`, `sender_id` (FK to `users`), `body` (text, server-validated to Ōēż 4000 chars), `deleted_for_everyone_at` (timestamp, null on live messages), `created_at`, `updated_at`. Two indexes: `(conversation_id, created_at)` for thread-load and `(sender_id, created_at)` for "messages I have sent".

A few invariants the rest of the system depends on:

- **One row per pair.** `findOrCreateOneOnOne` is the only way 1:1 conversations are created, and it does a sharedLock re-check inside a transaction so two parallel "open chat with B" requests from A never produce two `conversations` rows for the same pair. The corresponding test (`opens a 1-on-1 conversation and reuses it on the second open`) asserts this directly.
- **Soft-delete on conversations** (`SoftDeletes` trait) means a thread can be hidden from every list without losing the message history; the column is present but no UI yet exposes the "delete this thread" action ŌĆö see "Honest gaps".
- **Per-message soft-delete via `deleted_for_everyone_at`.** A sender's "Delete" sets the timestamp; `ChatMessage::scopeVisible()` (`whereNull('deleted_for_everyone_at')`) is applied to every read path (`show`, `poll`). The retracted row stays in the DB.
- **Read receipts** are *per-user-per-conversation*, not per-message. The pivot's `last_read_at` is touched in three places: when a participant opens `/chat/{id}` (set to `now()`), when the polling endpoint returns at least one new message (set to `now()` for the active user ŌĆö they're clearly reading), and when a participant sends into the thread (their own `last_read_at` is bumped to the new message's `created_at` so the unread badge doesn't tick up against themselves). The unread count derived from this is "messages in your conversations that came after your `last_read_at`, sent by someone other than you, not retracted".
- **`unreadTotal`** is computed by a single DB-builder query in `ChatController::unreadTotalFor()` ŌĆö it joins `chat_messages` against `conversation_user`, filters on the current user's pivot, excludes the user's own messages and any retracted ones, and counts everything past `last_read_at` (or everything when `last_read_at` is null). It is recomputed on every Inertia render of `/chat` and `/chat/{id}` and on every poll tick, so the global badge stays in step without any client-side accounting.

What every reader needs to keep in mind:

- **Chat is not Notifications.** A new chat message does not, at MVP, create a row in the in-app notifications table, does not send an email, does not send an SMS. The only signal a recipient gets is the unread count on their sidebar ŌĆö and that count is only refreshed when they navigate to a CIHRMS page or already have a chat thread open polling. A user who has Outlook open and no CIHRMS tab will simply not know. This is a real gap, scoped for Phase 2 (see the roadmap).
- **Chat is not Messaging.** Messaging in Chapter 15 is the SMS / USSD outbound channel ŌĆö HR-side, one-to-many, gated behind `messaging.view` and `messaging.send`. It writes to `sms_messages` and `inbound_sms`, runs through a `SmsDispatcher` provider, and has nothing to do with this module. The two modules share zero database tables and zero controllers; the only thing they have in common is the noun "message".
- **Polling, not websockets.** The badge "Live ┬Ę auto-refreshes" is a 4-second `setInterval` against `GET /chat/{id}/poll`, not a real-time channel. Two users in the same thread will see each other's messages with up to 4 seconds of latency (less if either of them happens to send something, because the responding side's optimistic UI + next poll closes the gap). Phase 2 swaps this for Laravel Reverb broadcasting with the same DB schema underneath.
- **No group chats yet.** The schema supports them (`conversations.is_group` + arbitrary `conversation_user` membership), but every code path that creates a conversation in the MVP creates a 1:1 with `is_group = false`. There is no UI for naming a thread, adding people, or removing them. `Conversation::otherParticipant($me)` returns `null` for groups by design, so any group-rendering surface needs to special-case the title ŌĆö that work is on the Phase 2 list.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Employees (Ch 3)** ŌĆö every participant on a conversation is a User, and almost every User in CIHRMS has exactly one Employee row attached to it. The directory query (`User::query()->with(['employee:id,user_id,position,department_id,avatar_path', 'employee.department:id,name'])`) eager-loads the Employee + department on every directory page so each row can render a position + department subtitle without an N+1 fan-out. The thread view does the same for the two participants. A user without an Employee row (rare ŌĆö typically the seeded `super_admin` initial account) shows as just a name with the default "Employee" placeholder subtitle.
- **Messaging admin (Ch 15)** ŌĆö *no relationship at runtime*. The two modules are deliberately separate; Chat is internal employee-to-employee, Messaging is org-wide outbound SMS. They share the `User` table only insofar as both end up addressing users by their phone-or-name. The class name `ChatMessage` (rather than just `Message`) was chosen explicitly to avoid colliding with the existing `MessagingDispatcher` / `MessagingController` / `SmsMessage` layer.
- **Notifications (Ch 16)** ŌĆö **gap.** There is no `NewChatMessage` notification class, no listener that fires on `ChatMessage::created`, no entry in the institute's in-app notification stream when a message arrives. The unread badge is the only signal. Phase 2 wires this: a database-channel notification per recipient, with the same per-message debouncing the SMS layer uses, plus an opt-out toggle stored on `conversation_user.muted_at` (the column is already on the schema).
- **Audit Logs (Ch 24)** ŌĆö **gap.** Message create/update/delete events do not write to the tamper-evident audit log at MVP. Soft-deletes on messages (`deleted_for_everyone_at`) leave a DB-level breadcrumb but not a hashed audit row. Phase 2 emits `ChatMessageCreated`, `ChatMessageRetracted`, and `ConversationCreated` events and routes them through the same audit listener used by Employees and Finance.
- **Top-bar unread badge** ŌĆö the layout reads `unreadTotal` from every Inertia render in the auth zone (the shared prop) and renders the badge against the sidebar's `forum` icon. That number is recomputed by `unreadTotalFor()` on every page load and every chat poll, so the sidebar badge is at most one navigation or four seconds stale.

## Standards touchpoints

- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17 ŌĆö lawful basis for processing personal data.** Chat messages are personal data the moment they identify the sender. The institute relies on the *employment-contract* basis for processing ŌĆö staff are notified at onboarding (via the acceptable-use policy) that internal communications carried over CIHRMS are subject to retention and lawful disclosure. The notification is the consent gate; absent it, this module cannot lawfully store conversation history. The full text and signing flow live in Chapter 26.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦40 ŌĆö right to erasure / ┬¦41 right to rectification.** Per-message retraction (`deleted_for_everyone_at`) is the operational answer to ┬¦41 ŌĆö a participant can have a message corrected by retracting it and re-sending. True erasure of historical messages under ┬¦40 is handled by a dedicated redaction job (Phase 2) that nulls out `body` while keeping the audit metadata, not by the per-message Delete affordance.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦27 ŌĆö security of personal data.** Chat data lives behind the same Laravel session-auth + CSRF + HTTPS-in-production stack as the rest of the application; the database column for `body` is TEXT (not encrypted at rest at MVP ŌĆö encryption at rest is a Phase 2 deliverable for sensitive columns across the application, see Chapter 26). The conversation policy enforces zero-trust access at the row level: even a privileged admin cannot read into a conversation they are not a participant of without first joining its participant pivot.
- **ISO 27001 Annex A.13 ŌĆö communications security.** The 1:1 conversation policy (`participants()->where('users.id', $user->id)->exists()`) is the practical implementation of A.13.2.1 (information transfer policies and procedures) ŌĆö message access is strictly bound to participant-pivot membership. A.13.2.3 (electronic messaging) maps to the 4-second poll plus the optimistic-UI feedback loop; the websocket cut-over in Phase 2 brings the same control under a real-time channel without changing the policy boundary.
- **ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A.8 ŌĆö asset management.** Chat messages are an information asset in the institute's information-asset register; their classification is "Internal" by default with the right to be re-classified to "Confidential" by HR on a per-thread basis (the UI to do that is on the Phase 2 list ŌĆö schema-side `conversations.title` and a new `sensitivity` column will carry the label).

## What's planned next

Phase 2 of the roadmap (see Chapter 46) lands six things on this screen, in the following order:

1. **In-app notifications + email digest** on new chat messages ŌĆö fires `NewChatMessage` notification per recipient, gated by `conversation_user.muted_at`. This is the single highest-impact gap on the module today.
2. **Realtime via Laravel Reverb** ŌĆö the same controller plus a `MessageSent` broadcast event on the `private-conversation.{id}` channel; the Vue page swaps the `setInterval` for `Echo.private(...).listen('MessageSent', ...)`. The polling endpoint stays as a fallback for connectivity-degraded modes.
3. **Audit log wiring** ŌĆö `ChatMessageCreated` / `ChatMessageRetracted` / `ConversationCreated` events into the tamper-evident audit table (Chapter 24), with full hash-chained provenance for compliance read-outs.
4. **File attachments + image previews** ŌĆö `chat_message_attachments` table, S3-or-local storage, MIME-typed inline previews for common image formats and PDFs (Ōēż 10 MB per file, matching the Documents module).
5. **Group chats** ŌĆö UI to name a thread, invite participants, remove them, and rename; the schema already supports it. Includes a "leave thread" affordance on the participant pivot.
6. **Encryption at rest for `chat_messages.body`** ŌĆö application-level envelope encryption with per-tenant KEK, addressing the ┬¦27 DPA gap noted above. Decryption gated by the same policy as read access today.

Beyond Phase 2: pinned messages, message search across all your threads (Meilisearch-backed), typing indicators, presence (online/last-seen), read receipts at message granularity (not just last_read_at), and emoji reactions. None of these block the MVP shipping; all of them are on the post-Phase-2 backlog.

<!-- ===== 15_messaging_admin.md ===== -->

# Chapter 15 ŌĆö Messaging Administration

> *In one paragraph.* Messaging Administration is CIHRMS's out-of-app reach into staff phones ŌĆö SMS for everyone and USSD for the ones whose phones can't run a browser. It is a deliberately narrow module: a one-off SMS sender for HR, a 4-digit-PIN issuer that gates the `*920*HR#` self-service dial code, an outbound log of every message the system has ever sent, and an inbound stream of every short-code reply. Chat (Chapter 14) is staff talking *inside* CIHRMS; Messaging is CIHRMS talking *outside* it ŌĆö to a handset on the MTN, Vodafone, or AirtelTigo network. There is no campaign builder, no audience picker, no scheduler, and no template designer in the MVP. The provider stack is pluggable (Hubtel by default in Ghana, Twilio as the international fallback, a no-op log driver for staging), the consent model is opt-in for WhatsApp and assumed-implied for SMS to your own employees, and every outbound message is persisted with provider, segment count, cost, and a delivery receipt for the auditor.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Administration** group ŌåÆ **Messaging** (top-level item, `sms` icon). The module is visible only to roles holding `messaging.view`; the link uses the `sms` Material Symbol so it sits visually next to "Chat" in the sidebar even though it is a different sibling concept. There is no entry in the app-switcher grid in the MVP ŌĆö Messaging is staff-comms plumbing, not a daily-use module for line staff.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every screen, every action. Both pass the `messaging.view`, `messaging.send`, and `messaging.manage` checks via the wildcard / mirrored-permission paths in `User::hasPermission`.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö the primary operator. Holds all three Messaging permissions in the seed; runs the outbound log, sends one-off SMS, issues USSD PINs.
    - **manager**, **dept_head**, **employee**, **finance_officer**, **it_support**, **auditor** ŌĆö *not* seeded with any `messaging.*` permission in `RolePermissionSeeder`. They never see the sidebar item and a direct URL hit on `/admin/messaging` returns 403 from the `permission:messaging.view` middleware.
- **Related modules:** Chat (Ch 14) ŌĆö the in-app sibling; the two share neither table nor model so an HR broadcast is not a chat thread; Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö the audience: every outbound SMS resolves a phone via the `employees.phone` or `users.whatsapp_phone` column; Notifications (Ch 16) ŌĆö the *channel router* that decides email vs WhatsApp vs Slack/Teams for system events, layered on top of the SMS dispatcher described here; Bank Changes (Ch 22, Disbursements) ŌĆö the busiest non-PIN caller of the SMS dispatcher, sending the 6-digit confirmation code that the subject types back into USSD option 5; Whistleblower (Ch 27) ŌĆö the public anonymous channel also accepts a USSD path through the same shortcode; DPA (Ch 26) ŌĆö consent storage and erasure for the messaging audit trail; Standards (Ch 44) ŌĆö DPA Act 843 ┬¦20 direct-marketing language, NCA SMS-sender registration, and ISO 27001 A.13 communications-security coverage.

## The screens

![Messaging ŌĆö Hero band with live KPIs, log filters, outbound table, inbound stream, send-SMS and PIN slide-overs](../assets/screenshots/15_messaging_admin/dashboard.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Editorial hero band ŌĆö indigo gradient with a cyan glow, "X messages dispatched today ┬Ę Y% delivered" as the headline, a live emerald dot signalling the gateway is connected, and four inline KPIs (Sent ┬Ę Delivered ┬Ę Inbound ┬Ę PIN-enrolled, with PIN-enrolled rendered in the institutional gold as the flagship metric). The animated cyan-and-gold ribbon along the top edge is the visual confirmation that data is currently flowing in from the gateway. ┬Ę ŌØĘ KPI tile grid ŌĆö five tiles repeating the same numbers in a denser layout (Sent today, Delivered, Failed, Inbound, PIN-enrolled). Each tile has its own colour personality from the design system: cyan for delivery, magenta for inbound, gold for the flagship enrolment count. The flagship card also gets a 1px gold hairline along the top edge so it reads as the headline number even at small size. ┬Ę ŌØĖ "Outbound log" filter strip ŌĆö a phone-number search box, five status pills (All ┬Ę Queued ┬Ę Sent ┬Ę Delivered ┬Ę Failed) with their cyan/emerald/rose colour signals, and a one-click "Clear" that returns when any filter is active. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Outbound table ŌĆö When ┬Ę To ┬Ę Provider ┬Ę Body ┬Ę Seg ┬Ę Cost ┬Ę Status. The body column truncates at one line; the cost column shows GHS to four decimal places (Hubtel and Twilio both bill in fractions of a cedi). ┬Ę ŌØ║ "Inbound stream" ŌĆö a vertical list of the last 30 messages that arrived at the short code, each tagged with the parsed intent pill (PAYSLIP ┬Ę LEAVE ┬Ę CLOCK_IN ┬Ę CLOCK_OUT ┬Ę TRACK ┬Ę OPTOUT) when the keyword parser matched. ┬Ę ŌØ╗ Two flagship CTAs in the header ŌĆö "Issue USSD PIN" with a subtle gold border (this is the only place gold appears as a primary action surface outside the hero/flagship KPI) and "Send SMS" in the standard indigo gradient.*

![Messaging ŌĆö Send SMS slide-over with phone, body, and live segment counter](../assets/screenshots/15_messaging_admin/send.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ "Single SMS segments hold 160 GSM characters" cyan info banner ŌĆö sets expectations before the user types into the body. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Recipient phone field ŌĆö free-text, accepts either local `0200000099` or international `233200000099` form; the Hubtel provider normalises both to `233XXXXXXXXX` and the Twilio provider normalises both to `+233XXXXXXXXX`. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Message body textarea (max 1600 chars = 10 segments) with a live character counter underneath that turns amber once the message has crossed the 160-character single-segment boundary and shows "Will split" once it has. The segment math is `ceil(length/160)` ŌĆö exactly what both carriers bill. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ "Send SMS" cobalt CTA with a built-in spinner that swaps in while the Inertia post is in flight; the panel closes and a green toast appears on success.*

![Messaging ŌĆö Issue / rotate USSD PIN slide-over with employee, phone, validity, and the 2FA-required notice](../assets/screenshots/15_messaging_admin/pin.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Gold-bordered info banner ŌĆö "A 4-digit PIN is generated server-side and SMS'd to the employee's registered phone. The plaintext PIN is never stored ŌĆö only the bcrypt hash. **2FA required to issue.**" The gold tinting is intentional: this is the only flagship-tier institutional action on the page and matches the gold flagship KPI in the hero band. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Employee ID (numeric, validated against `employees.id`). ┬Ę ŌØĖ Phone field ŌĆö accepts either E.164 (`+233200000099`) or local (`0200000099`) form. The PIN SMS goes to this number, not whatever is on the employee record, so HR can re-target if an employee changed handsets. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Validity in days ŌĆö defaults to 365, clamped 1ŌĆō730 server-side.
