After this many days `pin_expires_at` triggers a forced re-issue. ┬Ę ŌØ║ "Issue PIN (2FA)" CTA ŌĆö same indigo base as Send SMS but with a thin gold rim and gold glow on hover to flag it as the 2FA-gated flagship action. The HTTP route enforces `2fa:fresh` middleware so the operator must have completed TOTP within the last few minutes.*

> The three screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.36). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Messaging dashboard (outbound log + inbound stream)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Live ribbon** (top right, `*920*HR#`) | Read-only badge showing the institute's registered USSD shortcode in a monospace font with a pulsing cyan dot. Sourced from `config('messaging.ussd.shortcode')`. | Anyone with `messaging.view` | Operators forget the dial code constantly ŌĆö printing it on the page they use every day is the simplest possible cure. |
| **Issue USSD PIN** (gold-bordered CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for issuing or rotating an employee's 4-digit PIN. | `messaging.manage` **plus** a fresh TOTP confirmation (`2fa:fresh` middleware) | Issuing a PIN is the gateway to letting an employee read their payslip and clock in over USSD ŌĆö it is a 2FA-grade action. Gold border is the institutional cue that this is the flagship verb on the page. |
| **Send SMS** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for sending an ad-hoc SMS to any phone. | `messaging.send` | Day-to-day HR comms ŌĆö "ping a recruit", "remind a clinician about a shift", "tell a vendor the cheque is ready". |
| **Hero band ŌĆö "X messages dispatched today ┬Ę Y% delivered"** | Read-only headline. Numbers come from the `stats` payload built by `MessagingController::index`: sent_today + delivered_today + failed_today; delivered / (delivered + failed). | Anyone with `messaging.view` | One-glance answer to "is the gateway working today?" ŌĆö the auditor's first question on every visit. |
| **Hero band ŌĆö Inline KPIs** (Sent ┬Ę Delivered ┬Ę Inbound ┬Ę Enrolled) | Four large numbers, each in a different colour. "Enrolled" is the flagship gold metric ŌĆö total rows in `staff_phone_pins`. | Same | The three operational counts plus the institutional health metric (how many staff can actually use USSD). |
| **KPI tile grid** (5 cards) | Sent ┬Ę Delivered ┬Ę Failed ┬Ę Inbound ┬Ę PIN-enrolled. Identical numbers as the hero, denser layout, hover lift. | Same | The hero is editorial, the tile grid is operational ŌĆö same data, two reading modes. |
| **Phone-number filter** (outbound log) | Free-text input; submits on Enter; sends `to_phone` as a query string. The controller applies it as `WHERE to_phone LIKE %v%` with no debounce ŌĆö the user controls when to fire. | Anyone with `messaging.view` | "Did we ever text this number" is the second most-asked-for view. |
| **Status pills** (All ┬Ę Queued ┬Ę Sent ┬Ę Delivered ┬Ę Failed) | One click filters the table to that status. The selected pill flips to the cobalt-filled "secondary" style. | Same | Inverted-pyramid filtering for the support agent who is debugging a non-delivery. |
| **Clear** (appears when any filter is set) | Resets both filters and re-fires the query. | Same | One-click undo. |
| **Outbound row** ŌĆö When | The wall-clock timestamp of `sent_at` if set, else `created_at`. Always rendered in the `en-GH` short date + 24h time. | Same | Provider receipts are seconds behind reality; `sent_at` is when the provider accepted, which is the auditable point. |
| **Outbound row** ŌĆö To | E.164-style phone number in monospace. | Same | Phone numbers want a fixed-width font so digits line up vertically. |
| **Outbound row** ŌĆö Provider | `hubtel`, `twilio`, or `log` ŌĆö the driver name that handled the message. Capitalised in CSS only. | Same | If Hubtel is silently rejecting and the system falls back to log, this column is how the operator notices. |
| **Outbound row** ŌĆö Body | The text of the message, truncated to one line. Hovering does not expand ŌĆö this is intentional; reading full bodies happens on a dedicated detail screen in Phase 1. | Same | One line keeps the log scannable. |
| **Outbound row** ŌĆö Seg | Segment count (`ceil(length/160)`). Hubtel sometimes returns its own `rate` field which we trust over our local count if present. | Same | Carriers bill by segment, so the operator wants this column to spot the inadvertent 4-segment SMS. |
| **Outbound row** ŌĆö Cost | GHS to four decimal places, or "ŌĆö" when the provider returned 0 (the `log` driver always returns 0). | Same | Phase 1 totals these into the monthly comms cost line ŌĆö the column exists today so the data is there when the reports want it. |
| **Outbound row** ŌĆö Status pill | Coloured chip: amber `Queued`, cyan `Sent`, emerald `Delivered`, rose `Failed`, slate `Expired`. | Same | The whole point of a delivery log. |
| **Inbound stream** ŌĆö row | Sender phone (monospace, primary colour), arrow, the short code it came in on (monospace, muted), the body (full text, no truncation), and a small `Intent ┬Ę ŌĆ”` pill if the keyword parser matched. | Same | Inbound is a much lower volume than outbound ŌĆö full bodies are affordable and useful. |
| **Empty states** | "No SMS in this view" and "No inbound messages yet" ŌĆö different copy, both via the shared `EmptyState` component with the `sms` and `inbox` Material Symbols. | Same | Different empty cases need different prompts; "send a one-off message" vs "wait for a trigger". |

> *Notes:* The dashboard is one Inertia render ŌĆö `MessagingController::index` returns the paginated outbound, the last 30 inbound, and the five stats counts in a single trip. The `stats` payload is unfiltered (org-wide); the outbound and inbound queries do respect the phone-number and status filters. Pagination is 40 per page on the outbound log and there is no infinite scroll ŌĆö operators searching for a specific number use the filter rather than paging deep. The "Live ribbon" emerald dot is purely cosmetic ŌĆö it does not poll the gateway. A failed-gateway test is the operator clicking Send SMS with a known number and watching the row go Sent ŌåÆ Delivered.

### Send SMS slide-over

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Recipient phone** | required, string, max 32 chars | `messaging.send` | No format check on the controller ŌĆö the provider normaliser handles `0XX`, `233XX`, `+233XX`, or any other variant. The MVP trusts the operator on this field; bad numbers come back as `failed` rows with the carrier's reason in `failure_reason`. |
| **Message body** | required, string, max 1600 chars (10 GSM-7 segments) | Same | Long messages are accepted; Hubtel and Twilio both split server-side and bill per segment. The UI live-counts the segments so the operator can see what they are about to pay for. |
| **Send SMS button** (cobalt CTA with shimmer) | POST `/admin/messaging/send`; spinner during processing | Same | One-shot ŌĆö the panel closes and a success toast appears as soon as the dispatcher returns. The dispatcher persists a `queued` row first, then updates to `sent`/`failed` based on the provider response ŌĆö so a "fire and forget" send always leaves an audit row. |
| **Cancel** | Closes the panel without firing | Anyone | Standard escape. |

> *Notes:* The send path is synchronous ŌĆö `SmsDispatcher::send()` POSTs to Hubtel (or Twilio) right inside the controller before redirecting. A slow gateway will block the operator's request; a dead gateway times out at 10 seconds and the row lands as `failed` with the timeout reason captured. Phase 1's roadmap moves dispatch into a queue worker so the UI returns instantly and the gateway is allowed to be slow. The "Reply STOP to opt out" string is **not** appended automatically by the dispatcher ŌĆö it is the operator's responsibility to include the opt-out phrasing for any message that could be classed as marketing under DPA Act 843 ┬¦20. The dedicated USSD PIN sender (next section) prints it explicitly; the operator-driven Send SMS does not. This is a documented gap that needs to be closed in Phase 1 (see "What's planned next").

### Issue / rotate USSD PIN slide-over

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Employee ID** | required, integer, must exist on `employees.id` | `messaging.manage` + `2fa:fresh` | Numeric id, not staff number ŌĆö Phase 1 swaps in an autocomplete that resolves by name or staff number. |
| **Phone** | required, string, max 32 chars | Same | This is the phone the SMS goes to *and* the phone that will be on `staff_phone_pins.phone` ŌĆö so subsequent USSD logins are tied to it. Updating an employee's personal phone on the Profile portal does **not** rotate the USSD PIN; HR has to come here. |
| **Validity (days)** | optional, integer, 1ŌĆō730, defaults to 365 | Same | Server-side the dispatcher writes `pin_expires_at = now() + N days`. Setting validity to "0 or null" produces an expiry-less PIN ŌĆö accepted for the initial pilot, deprecated in Phase 1. |
| **Issue PIN (2FA)** button (gold-rimmed indigo CTA) | POST `/admin/messaging/pins`; spinner during processing | Same | The plaintext PIN is generated server-side via `random_int(1000, 9999)`. The hash is written to `staff_phone_pins.pin_hash` in a transaction; the SMS is sent **after** the transaction commits ŌĆö if the gateway then fails, the PIN is still rotated and the employee can be re-sent the same fresh PIN by HR. The previous order (SMS-then-write) risked locking the employee out of USSD if the SMS provider returned a 500 between persist and the operator noticing. |
| **Cancel** | Closes the panel without firing | Anyone | Standard escape. |

> *Notes:* The PIN-issue flow is the only place in the MVP that uses the `2fa:fresh` middleware outside of disbursements and DPA fulfilment ŌĆö issuing a PIN is materially equivalent to issuing a credential, and it gets the same treatment. The bcrypt hash means that even a database leak does not expose the four digits. Failed USSD attempts increment `failed_attempts`; five misses (configurable via `messaging.ussd.pin_max_attempts`) lock the account for 15 minutes (`messaging.ussd.pin_lock_minutes`) and a sixth miss rotates the lock ŌĆö there is no permanent lockout. The PIN SMS includes `"Do not share. Reply STOP to opt out."` ŌĆö the only outbound point in the system that prints the DPA opt-out language by default.

## The data behind it

Messaging stores four tables, all created by the WS18 migration:

- **`sms_messages`** ŌĆö every outbound message, ever. Columns include the `to_phone`, the optional `from_sender` (an alphanumeric sender ID like `CIHRMS`, registered with the NCA), the message `body`, the `provider` slug (`hubtel` / `twilio` / `log`), the `status` (`queued` ŌåÆ `sent` ŌåÆ `delivered`, or `failed`, or `expired`), the `provider_message_id` returned by the carrier (used to match delivery receipts back), the `segments` count, the `cost` to four decimal places, an optional `failure_reason`, a polymorphic `context_type` + `context_id` pair (so a bank-change SMS knows it belongs to a `PendingBankChange`, a payroll notification knows it belongs to a `PayrollRun`), the `triggered_by` user id, and `sent_at` + `delivered_at` timestamps. Indexes on `(to_phone, status)` and `(context_type, context_id)` keep the log fast.
- **`inbound_sms`** ŌĆö every message that arrived at the short code from a staff handset. Columns include the `from_phone`, the `to_shortcode` it came in on, the `body`, the carrier `provider`, the carrier's `provider_message_id`, a `parsed_intent` slug (`PAYSLIP` ┬Ę `LEAVE` ┬Ę `CLOCK_IN` ┬Ę `CLOCK_OUT` ┬Ę `TRACK` ┬Ę `OPTOUT`, or null if the parser didn't match), an optional `parsed_args` JSON blob for future structured replies, a `received_at` timestamp, an optional `processed_at` (when a future worker has acted on it), and a `reply_sent` body field. The intent parser is deliberately simple ŌĆö first-token, case-insensitive keyword match ŌĆö and it does *not* dispatch downstream actions in the MVP. It tags the row and waits for an HR operator (or, in Phase 1, an automated worker) to act on it.
- **`ussd_sessions`** ŌĆö the running state of every active dial of `*920*HR#`. One row per provider `sessionId`, with `phone`, `shortcode`, `state` (a 13-value enum from `welcome` to `whistleblower_status`), an optional `employee_id` once the caller has authenticated, a free-form `context` JSON for breadcrumbs, the `last_input` and `last_response` for support diagnostics, and an `expires_at` set 180 seconds in the future on every step (USSD sessions are short-lived by definition). The state machine is provider-agnostic; the controller normalises Hubtel, mNotify, and AfricasTalking payloads into the same `{sessionId, msisdn, shortcode, text}` tuple before handing them off.
- **`staff_phone_pins`** ŌĆö one row per employee that has been onboarded to USSD. Columns: `employee_id` (unique ŌĆö only one PIN per employee), `phone`, `pin_hash` (bcrypt), optional `pin_expires_at`, `last_used_at` for "when did this employee last self-serve over USSD", a `failed_attempts` counter, and a `locked_until` timestamp. The hash is `$hidden` from JSON serialisation so it never leaks into an Inertia payload.

In addition, two columns added to **`users`** by the Wave 12 migration carry the per-user messaging preferences:

- **`notification_channels`** (JSON) ŌĆö a map of channel slug (`email` ┬Ę `in_app` ┬Ę `whatsapp` ┬Ę `slack` ┬Ę `teams`) to a boolean. Email and in-app default to true; the three out-of-app channels default to false (opt-in only). Read by `User::notificationPreferences()`.
- **`whatsapp_phone`** (string, indexed) and **`whatsapp_consent_at`** (timestamp) ŌĆö the WhatsApp business-channel address and the consent capture timestamp. `User::hasWhatsappConsent()` is the canonical gate used by `MessagingDispatcher::sendWhatsApp` ŌĆö a user with consent revoked never has a template message dispatched to them.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **SMS, not in-app chat.** A message in the outbound log is bytes on a cellular network, not a row in the chat thread table. The two systems do not share a primary key, a service, or a Vue page. A reply to an SMS lands in `inbound_sms` and is invisible to Chat; a message in Chat never produces a row in `sms_messages`.
- **`log` is not "fake-send".** The `log` driver writes the message to the configured log channel and returns a synthesised message id ŌĆö a row still lands in `sms_messages` with `provider=log`, status `sent`, cost 0. This is the staging-environment default; switching to Hubtel in production is a single `SMS_DRIVER` env var change. The log driver is also what keeps the test suite from hitting a real gateway.
- **WhatsApp is not on this page.** The WhatsApp Cloud driver exists (see `MessagingDispatcher` and `WhatsAppWebhookController`) but it is wired into the *notifications* layer ŌĆö system events fan out via the user's preference map ŌĆö not into the operator-facing "Send SMS" flow. There is no UI on the Messaging dashboard that sends a WhatsApp template from a slide-over. That is on the Phase 1 list.
- **There are no templates and no campaigns in the MVP.** Every outbound is either a one-off typed body or a system-generated string (`"CIHRMS PIN for {$employee->employee_no}: {$pinPlain}ŌĆ”"`, `"Your bank-change code is {$code}ŌĆ”"`). There is no `sms_templates` table, no audience picker, no scheduled send, no A/B variant, no segmentation. This is a documented gap, not an oversight ŌĆö the MVP scope was deliberately narrow.
- **The "STOP" opt-out is parsed but not enforced.** The webhook controller recognises `STOP` and `UNSUB` as the `OPTOUT` intent and writes them to `inbound_sms.parsed_intent`. Nothing in the outbound path then checks that intent before sending the next message. An operator with `messaging.send` can send an SMS to any number, opt-out or not. This is a real DPA gap ŌĆö see "Honest gaps" and Phase 1.

## How it talks to other modules

- **System triggers that send SMS via `SmsDispatcher`** in the MVP: (1) the one-off "Send SMS" panel on this page; (2) the "Issue USSD PIN" panel on this page; (3) `BankChangeRequestService::initiate()` ŌĆö sends the 6-digit code to the subject's registered employee phone when HR (or the subject themselves) initiates a bank-account change. That is the complete trigger surface. There are no payroll-payslip SMS, no leave-approved SMS, no shift-reminder SMS in the MVP ŌĆö those are notifications routed through email and in-app, not the SMS gateway.
- **USSD self-service paths** that the issued PIN unlocks: latest payslip readout, current-year leave balance, clock-in / clock-out, and bank-change confirmation. The whistleblower case-tracking path is reachable from the welcome screen without a PIN (option 0). See the `UssdSessionHandler` state machine in `app/Services/Messaging/Ussd/`.
- **Inbound delivery receipts** flow through `webhooks/sms` (signature-verified with `webhook.signature:hubtel_sms`). The provider POSTs `{type: "delivery_receipt", messageId}` and the `SmsDispatcher::markDelivered()` method flips the matching `sms_messages` row from Sent to Delivered with a `delivered_at` timestamp.
- **Inbound short-code messages** flow through the same `webhooks/sms` endpoint with `type: "inbound_message"`. The body is persisted, the first token is keyword-parsed into an intent, and the row sits in `inbound_sms` waiting to be acted on. Phase 1 wires a worker that auto-handles `PAYSLIP`, `LEAVE`, and `OPTOUT` without HR intervention.
- **USSD callbacks** flow through `webhooks/ussd` (signature-verified with `webhook.signature:hubtel_ussd`). Every key press is one round trip ŌĆö the controller reads the cumulative `text`, slices off the last segment, dispatches it to the state machine, and returns either `CON <body>` (keep the session open) or `END <body>` (terminate). USSD sessions live in `ussd_sessions` for 180 seconds.
- **WhatsApp Cloud webhooks** at `webhooks/whatsapp` (GET for Meta's `hub.challenge` handshake, POST for messages). Inbound WhatsApp keywords (`LEAVE 3 SICK`, `URGENT printer broken`) are parsed into draft `LeaveRequest` and `Ticket` rows directly ŌĆö this is the live cross-channel ingestion path, and it is documented here for symmetry with the SMS short code even though the operator never sees it on the Messaging dashboard.
- **Notification channel preferences** (Chapter 16) live on the User row (`notification_channels` JSON, `whatsapp_phone`, `whatsapp_consent_at`, `slack_user_id`). When a domain event needs to reach an employee, the `MessagingDispatcher` (note: a *different* class to the `SmsDispatcher` documented above; they share a name family but not a contract) fans the body out to the channels the user has opted into, respecting the WhatsApp consent gate and the Slack/Teams driver availability.
- **Permission seeding** is in `RolePermissionSeeder`. Only super_admin, ceo, and hr_admin hold `messaging.view`, `messaging.send`, and `messaging.manage` in the MVP. Any other role hitting the URL gets a 403 from the middleware before the controller runs.

## Standards touchpoints

- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦20 ŌĆö direct marketing.** Section 20 requires a clear opt-out mechanism on any direct-marketing communication. The PIN-issue SMS does it correctly (`"Reply STOP to opt out."`); the operator-driven Send SMS does not, today, append the phrase automatically. The keyword `STOP` is parsed into the `OPTOUT` intent but not enforced on subsequent sends. See Chapter 26.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦17 ŌĆö lawful basis.** Employment-contract is the lawful basis for SMS / USSD reach to staff; consent (┬¦17(1)(a)) is the lawful basis for WhatsApp template messages (Meta's commercial policy is stricter than the DPA's floor). The `whatsapp_consent_at` timestamp is the audit-grade evidence of that consent.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦40 ŌĆö right to erasure.** When a data subject requests erasure, the existing redaction job covers user PII; the `sms_messages` and `inbound_sms` audit rows are retained for the regulatory window and then redacted in bulk. Detail in Chapter 26.
- **National Communications Authority (NCA) ŌĆö Alphanumeric sender ID registration.** Ghana's NCA requires the alphanumeric sender ID (`CIHRMS`) to be pre-registered with the carrier. The system carries no validation that the ID is registered ŌĆö it is an operational checklist item before going live with Hubtel. See Chapter 44.
- **NCA ŌĆö Inbound short-code provisioning.** The `*920*HR#` USSD code is shared / leased per institute and is provisioned out-of-band by the carrier. The `messaging.ussd.shortcode` env var simply mirrors whatever the institute has registered.
- **WhatsApp Business Policy ŌĆö 24-hour customer-care window.** The `MessagingDispatcher::sendWhatsApp` path enforces Meta's rule that free-form text is allowed only inside the 24-hour reply window; outside it the caller **must** pass a pre-approved template name (the HSM template id). The interface signature on `MessagingProvider::sendTemplate` makes this explicit. See Chapter 44.
- **ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A.13 ŌĆö Communications security.** Webhook signature verification (`webhook.signature:hubtel_sms`, `webhook.signature:hubtel_ussd`, `webhook.signature:whatsapp`) gates every inbound; bcrypt-hashed PINs satisfy A.9 credential storage; the per-channel kill-switch on each driver's `isConfigured()` returns false if env vars are missing, which means a half-provisioned environment cannot send mistaken messages. See Chapter 44.
- **PCI / payment-channel adjacency.** The bank-change confirmation flow described above means the SMS gateway sits in the trust path between HR and an employee's bank account. The system mitigates this by sending only a one-time code (no account numbers), masking the new account to its last four digits on the USSD confirmation screen, and requiring the subject to explicitly approve via option 5. The code itself is single-use and short-lived. See Chapter 22 and Chapter 44.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap (8ŌĆō10 weeks, see the gap analysis) lands six things directly on this screen:

1. **Templates.** A first-class `sms_templates` table with named templates (`PAYSLIP_READY`, `LEAVE_APPROVED`, `SHIFT_REMINDER`, `BANK_CHANGE_CODE`), Mustache-style variable substitution (`{{employee_name}}`, `{{period_label}}`, `{{net_amount}}`), and a template-picker on the Send SMS panel. WhatsApp templates piggyback on this ŌĆö same row, additional `whatsapp_template_name` and `whatsapp_language` columns.
2. **Campaigns and audience selection.** A "New campaign" flow lets HR pick a template, an audience (all employees ┬Ę by department ┬Ę by role ┬Ę by an arbitrary employee-list CSV upload ┬Ę by a leave-status filter ┬Ę by a payroll-payment-status filter), and a send-time (now / scheduled). A `messaging_campaigns` table tracks the run; per-recipient rows in `sms_messages` link back via `context_type = 'campaign'`.
3. **Queue-backed dispatch.** `SmsDispatcher::send()` moves behind a `DispatchSms` job so the UI returns instantly and the gateway is allowed to be slow. Failed sends retry with exponential backoff up to five times; the row stays `queued` between attempts and only flips to `failed` when retries are exhausted.
4. **DPA-grade opt-out enforcement.** A new `sms_optouts` table records every `OPTOUT` intent observed on inbound. Before any outbound the dispatcher checks the recipient against this table; an opt-out blocks the send (with `failure_reason = 'opted_out'`) unless the message is a transactional class (PIN-issue, bank-change confirmation) that survives opt-out per DPA Act 843 ┬¦20(2). The operator-driven Send SMS panel surfaces an inline warning when the typed number is opted out and refuses to submit unless the operator ticks an explicit "transactional override" box.
5. **WhatsApp send from the dashboard.** The MVP's WhatsApp wiring is inbound-and-system-notifications only. Phase 1 surfaces a "Send WhatsApp" panel that picks a pre-approved template + parameters, audiences against `users.whatsapp_phone WHERE whatsapp_consent_at IS NOT NULL`, and logs each send into `sms_messages` alongside SMS for a single unified outbound log.
6. **Delivery analytics.** A dedicated Reports & Analytics tile (Chapter 31) chart-builds delivery rate by hour-of-day, by carrier (MTN / Vodafone / AirtelTigo derived from the MSISDN prefix), and by template ŌĆö answering "which template is the most read", "which carrier is silently dropping us", and "when in the day is the gateway most reliable". The data already exists on `sms_messages`; only the chart wiring is outstanding.

Phase 2 adds bulk-import audience uploads (CSV/XLSX), inbound auto-handling (a worker that responds to `PAYSLIP` with a real payslip summary instead of waiting for HR), and a per-message bilingual rendering (English + Twi) for any template that has a translation row in the new `sms_templates` table. The MVP's narrow scope was deliberate; the foundations described in this chapter ŌĆö provider abstraction, `sms_messages` as the canonical log, consent columns on the user ŌĆö are the pieces every Phase 1 / Phase 2 item builds on top of.

<!-- ===== 16_notifications.md ===== -->

# Chapter 16 ŌĆö Notifications

> *In one paragraph.* Notifications is the in-app nervous system of CIHRMS ŌĆö every other module fires events here when something happens that a person needs to know about. A leave request approved (Ch 4), a ticket assigned (Ch 11), a complaint closed (Ch 12), a document awaiting your signature (Ch 13), a payroll run marked paid (Ch 19), a Ghana Card expiring (Ch 25), a tamper-evident audit chain broken (Ch 26) ŌĆö they all funnel through the same `notifications` table, surface through the same bell in the top bar, and write through the same channel-preference layer so each person decides where they want to be reached. The chapter that follows covers the bell dropdown, the full notifications page, the per-user channel preferences screen, the three-pack sound subsystem that gives the bell its voice, and every event source that feeds the queue.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** Notifications is **not** a sidebar item. It lives in the top bar of the authenticated layout, between the **Sound effects toggle** and the **theme toggle** ŌĆö a single bell icon with a red counter badge. Clicking the bell opens a 360-pixel dropdown of the ten most recent unread notifications; the dropdown footer links to the full page at `/notifications`. The channel-preferences screen is reached from the user-account dropdown (top-right avatar) under **Notification Channels**, and from the **Channels** button on the full page header.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **Every authenticated user**, regardless of role, sees their own bell, dropdown, and full-page index. The notifications shown are scoped through Laravel's `Notifiable` trait to the signed-in `User` row ŌĆö you only ever see notifications addressed to you.
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** see no special view of other people's notifications; the audit log (Ch 26) is the surface for cross-user activity, not this one.
    - **dept_head / manager / hr_admin** receive role-specific notifications (a leave-approval reminder, an incident assigned, a document awaiting their action), but the screens themselves are the same as every other user's.
- **Related modules:** every other workflow chapter is a *producer* of notifications. The top five sources by volume in the MVP are **Leave (Ch 4)** ŌĆö request submitted, approved, rejected, withdrawn, plus the 3-day approval reminder; **Tickets (Ch 11)** ŌĆö assigned, resolved, closed; **Documents (Ch 13)** ŌĆö awaiting your action, completed, rejected; **Employees (Ch 3)** ŌĆö new-hire welcome mail; **Identity (Ch 25)** ŌĆö Ghana Card re-verification reminders. **Incidents (Ch 27)**, **Audit (Ch 26)**, and **Public DPA (Ch 26)** also write here, with more producers landing in later phases (Payroll, Complaints, Performance, Loans). The full inventory is in *How it talks to other modules* below.

## The screens

![Notification bell ŌĆö top-bar trigger with red badge, ringing animation, click-out dropdown](../assets/screenshots/16_notifications/bell.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ The bell icon itself ŌĆö outlined `notifications` glyph from Material Symbols, 20 pixels, sitting inside a 36-pixel rounded hit target. When at least one unread notification exists the icon switches to its filled variant and tints red (`#dc2626`) to read as "alive" from across the room. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Red counter badge ŌĆö pinned to the top-right of the bell, minimum 16-pixel circle, displays the count or `99+` when over. The badge intentionally keeps cross-platform red (iOS, Slack, Gmail, GitHub all use red for unread); the Sovereign Precision palette would lose scan-time recognition here. The override is documented in `docs/QA_REPORT.md` ┬¦M-3. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Animation states ŌĆö three of them: a one-shot 1.6-second **swing** (`nb-ring`, ten-step decreasing wobble) when a new notification arrives, a persistent gentle 3-second **pulse** (`nb-pulse`, three-step wobble) while the count is greater than zero and the bell isn't actively ringing, and a 2.2-second **badge heartbeat** with a 6-pixel expanding red glow. All three are disabled under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (WCAG 2.3.3 compliant).*

![Notification bell dropdown ŌĆö 360-pixel popover with last 10 unread, Mark all read, footer link](../assets/screenshots/16_notifications/bell_dropdown.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Header band ŌĆö left side reads "Notifications" in 13-pixel bold; right side shows a "Mark all read" link in 11-pixel cobalt that only renders when `count > 0`. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Body ŌĆö at most ten items, vertically stacked, divided by 1-pixel hairline rules. Each item is a single message line in 12-pixel medium plus a relative timestamp in 10-pixel muted ("just now", "5 m ago", "2 h ago", "3 d ago", or `DD MMM` for anything older). The list takes the next-ten-unread from the API and is **not** the full archive ŌĆö for that you click through. ┬Ę ŌØĖ "All caught up" empty state ŌĆö outlined `notifications_none` glyph at 32 pixels above a single muted sentence, shown when the user has no unread notifications. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Footer ŌĆö single Inertia `<Link>` to `/notifications` ("View all notifications") that closes the popover and navigates with a partial reload.*

![Notifications index ŌĆö full-page archive with executive header, filter pill bar, paginated list](../assets/screenshots/16_notifications/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Executive header ŌĆö small "NOTICES" eyebrow in 10-pixel uppercase secondary tint, then the page title "Notifications" in 1.6-rem extra-bold, then a one-line description. On the right are two CTAs ŌĆö a **Channels** button (outline pill, `tune` icon) that links to the preferences screen and a **Mark All Read** button (cobalt gradient, `done_all` icon) that only renders when unread > 0. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Filter pill bar ŌĆö three pills inside a rounded outline container: **All**, **Unread**, **Read**. The active pill takes a 10% secondary tint and a glow; the Unread pill shows a small numeric counter chip when there is unread. Filter is client-side over the already-loaded page (server-side pagination is on `latest()` order). ┬Ę ŌØĖ List ŌĆö each row carries an unread dot on the left (only when `read_at` is null), a coloured 40-pixel type icon (per-type palette table below), the message text, a "type tag" pill in the upper left (Leave / Service / Employee / Payroll / System), an optional one-line key/value preview pulled from the JSON `data` payload, and a relative time on the upper right. Hover surfaces a `chevron_right` indicator on the trailing edge. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Pagination ŌĆö standard Inertia paginator across the bottom of the card; 20 per page.*

![Notification channels ŌĆö toggle stack, WhatsApp consent block, Slack workspace identity](../assets/screenshots/16_notifications/channels.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Channel toggle stack ŌĆö five rows, each a 40-pixel coloured icon well plus the channel name plus a 12-pixel descriptor plus an iOS-style toggle on the far right: **Email** (cobalt `mail`), **In-app** (cobalt `campaign`), **WhatsApp** (green `sms`), **Slack** (aubergine `chat`), **Microsoft Teams** (cobalt `forum`). Channels whose backing driver is not configured show a "Driver not connected" amber pill and the toggle is disabled. ┬Ę ŌØĘ WhatsApp consent block ŌĆö only rendered when the WhatsApp Cloud driver is configured. Carries an explanation, the phone-number input (validated `+?\d{8,15}`), a consent checkbox, and an amber warning banner when WhatsApp is toggled on but consent or phone is missing. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Slack mapping block ŌĆö single text field for the Slack member id (`U01ABCŌĆ”`). Without it, Slack notifications fall back to the configured HR channel. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Save preferences ŌĆö cobalt gradient CTA at the bottom-right; PATCHes the form back and returns with a "Notification preferences updated." flash.*

> The four screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.19). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Notification bell (top bar)

| Button / element | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Bell button** | Toggles the 360-pixel dropdown open/closed. A document-level click handler closes the dropdown when the user clicks anywhere outside it; the handler is removed on `onBeforeUnmount` so navigation never leaks. | Every authenticated user | The fastest path to "what just happened" ŌĆö visible from any page in the app. |
| **Red badge** | Read-only counter, pinned top-right of the bell. Shows the live unread total; clamps at `99+`. | Same | Universal cross-platform convention for unread ŌĆö keeps scan-time recognition. The intentional red override of the brand palette is documented in `docs/QA_REPORT.md` ┬¦M-3. |
| **Ringing animation** | Fires once when a new notification arrives (detected by id-set diff between the previous and the next `notifications` page prop). The bell swings for 1.6 s with `nb-ring`, then yields to the persistent pulse. | Triggered automatically | Tells the user something is genuinely new ŌĆö Inertia partial polls don't chime unless a new id appeared. |
| **Persistent pulse** | A 3-second three-step wobble that loops while `count > 0` and the bell is not actively ringing. | Triggered automatically | Sustained, polite "you have something here" without being loud. |
| **Sound effect on arrival** | Calls `useSound().play(<key>)` with a key chosen by ŌĆö in order ŌĆö the notification's explicit `kind` field (`ticket.completed`, `ticket.assigned`, `event.created`), then a keyword scan of the message (`resolved` / `completed` / `closed` ŌåÆ `task.completed`, `assign` ŌåÆ `assigned.you`, `event` ŌåÆ `event.created`), then a fallback to the generic `notification` key. | Every user, respects the sound-effects toggle and pack (see below) | Audible feedback closes the loop on arrival ŌĆö useful when the user has the tab in the background. |
| **"Mark all read" link** (dropdown header) | POSTs `notifications.readAll`; the server runs `unreadNotifications()->update(['read_at' => now()])` on the signed-in user. On success the dropdown closes and the bell silences. Only rendered when `count > 0`. | Every user (only their own rows) | One-click clean-out for a noisy day. |
| **Row in dropdown** | Static read-only list item ŌĆö message + relative time. There is **no per-row click handler** in the MVP; the dropdown is a preview surface, not the action surface. The full page is where rows are read and dismissed. | Every user | A deliberate constraint ŌĆö the dropdown stays small and predictable; deeper actions live on the full page. |
| **Empty state** | Renders when `notifications.length === 0` ŌĆö outlined `notifications_none` glyph, "You're all caught up." | Every user | A pleasant zero state replaces the embarrassing blank space. |
| **"View all notifications" footer link** | Closes the dropdown and Inertia-navigates to `/notifications`. | Every user | The escape hatch when ten rows isn't enough. |

> *Notes:* The bell's data is pumped in by `HandleInertiaRequests` as two **deferred** Inertia props ŌĆö `notifications` (the last ten unread, mapped to `{ id, type, message, kind, time }`) and `notificationCount` (an integer). Deferred means the layout paints first; the bell hydrates a moment later with a follow-up Inertia request, so a slow notifications query never blocks navigation on any other page. The cost is one extra round-trip per page load ŌĆö a fair trade for sub-second TTI everywhere else.

### Notifications index (`/notifications`)

| Button / element | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Channels** (outline pill, top-right) | Navigates to `/notifications/channels`. | Every user (managing only their own preferences) | One click from the archive to "where do I want these to land?". |
| **Mark All Read** (cobalt CTA, top-right) | POSTs `notifications.readAll`; updates every unread row for the signed-in user. Only rendered when `unreadCount > 0`. | Every user | Bulk clean-out ŌĆö the most-asked-for action when scrolling a stale inbox. |
| **Filter pill ŌĆö All** | Switches the client-side filter to show every row on the current page. | Every user | Default view. |
| **Filter pill ŌĆö Unread** | Filters the current page down to `read_at === null`. Carries a numeric counter chip when at least one row matches. | Every user | The most-asked-for view when you land here. |
| **Filter pill ŌĆö Read** | Inverse of Unread. | Every user | Useful when looking back for something you already saw. |
| **Row body** | Static ŌĆö type icon (coloured per the `TYPE_META` map), type-tag pill (Leave / Service / Employee / Payroll / System), message, optional key/value preview, relative time. There is **no per-row "Open linked entity" link in the MVP** ŌĆö the JSON `data` payload carries entity ids (`leave_id`, `ticket_id`, `document_uuid`, `incident_report_id`, `route`) but the row does not navigate. Acting on a notification means going to the source module via the sidebar. | Every user | The list is currently a *record*, not a router. Roadmap item ŌĆö see *What's planned next*. |
| **Unread dot** (left of row) | Pulsing 8-pixel cobalt dot rendered only when `read_at === null`. | Every user | At-a-glance scan of which rows are new since last visit. |
| **Pagination** | Standard Inertia paginator at the foot of the card; 20 per page; controlled server-side via `paginate(20)` over `latest()`. | Every user | Keeps the page responsive on accounts with thousands of historical rows. |
| **Empty state** | Renders when no rows match the current filter ŌĆö outlined `notifications_off` glyph with one of two captions depending on whether the filter is Unread ("You're all caught up.") or All/Read ("Nothing here yet ŌĆö you'll see new system activity as it happens."). | Every user | Self-explanatory; matches the bell empty state. |

> *Notes:* There is currently **no per-row Mark as read, no per-row Delete, no Filter-by-type dropdown, and no Open linked entity link** in the MVP. The only state transition available from the page is the bulk "Mark all read". The roadmap section below names this gap and the proposed shape ŌĆö `PATCH /notifications/{id}/read` plus `DELETE /notifications/{id}` plus type-grouped tabs. The `NotificationResource` already exposes the data payload so a row-click handler will be a UI-only patch when it lands.

### Notification channels (`/notifications/channels`)

| Field / control | What it does | Who can change it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Email** toggle | Sets `notification_channels.email` on the user. Defaults to `true` for every new user. | The signed-in user (own row only) | The lowest-common-denominator channel ŌĆö universally available, archived in the user's mailbox, the only one always-on by default. |
| **In-app** toggle | Sets `notification_channels.in_app`. Defaults to `true`. | Same | Controls whether the database channel is rendered through the bell/index ŌĆö turning this off doesn't stop the row from being written, only from being shown. (Roadmap: hard-mute support in Phase 1.) |
| **WhatsApp** toggle | Sets `notification_channels.whatsapp`. Defaults to `false` (opt-in only). The toggle is force-disabled if the WhatsApp Cloud driver isn't configured. | Same | Meta requires explicit opt-in before we can send template messages ŌĆö the toggle alone is necessary but not sufficient. See WhatsApp consent below. |
| **Slack** toggle | Sets `notification_channels.slack`. Defaults to `false`. Force-disabled when the Slack driver isn't configured. | Same | Useful for institutes that already run on Slack ŌĆö the dispatcher DMs the user if a `slack_user_id` is mapped, falls back to the configured HR channel otherwise. |
| **Microsoft Teams** toggle | Sets `notification_channels.teams`. Defaults to `false`. Force-disabled when the Teams driver isn't configured. | Same | Adaptive cards posted into the configured HR channel ŌĆö institute-wide, not per-user, until per-user Teams routing lands. |
| **WhatsApp phone** | Text field, validated client-side against `/^\+?\d{8,15}$/`. Server-side `nullable | string | max:20`. | Same | International format keeps the dispatcher driver simple ŌĆö country code is required, leading `+` optional. |
| **WhatsApp consent** checkbox | Saving with the box ticked **and** a non-empty phone stamps `whatsapp_consent_at = now()` (only if not already set). Unticking the box, clearing the phone, or saving with either missing clears `whatsapp_consent_at` to null and force-flips the WhatsApp channel toggle to false. | Same | DPA Act 843 ┬¦18 ŌĆö explicit consent to messaging on a personal phone is a separately-recorded fact, not a side effect of a toggle. The controller stamps it only when both halves of the consent pair are present. |
| **Slack member id** | Text field, validated server-side `nullable | string | max:30`. Format hint shown in the help text (`U01ABC234`). | Same | Without a mapping the Slack driver falls back to a shared HR channel ŌĆö fine for broadcasts, wrong for personal DMs. |
| **Save preferences** (cobalt CTA, bottom-right) | PATCH to `/notifications/channels`. Returns to the same page with a "Notification preferences updated." flash. | Same | Standard PATCH-with-flash pattern ŌĆö no redirect because the user's mental model is "stay here, confirm the save". |
| **Driver-not-connected pill** | Read-only amber pill rendered next to any channel whose driver returns `false` from `isConfigured()`. The toggle is disabled but the row stays visible. | Every user (informational only) | Tells the user "this is a system-level missing dependency, not a permission gap". |

> *Notes:* The full list of channels available to the *controller* is `{email, in_app, whatsapp, slack, teams}`. The User model adds `slack` and `teams` defaults of `false` while `email` and `in_app` default to `true`. There is **no `sms` channel** ŌĆö the dispatcher has a WhatsApp driver but no direct SMS provider in the MVP. The amber warning banner under the WhatsApp block carries the contractual language: "WhatsApp delivery will stay disabled until both consent and a valid phone are provided. Saving now will keep WhatsApp off." ŌĆö that's the rule the controller enforces; the banner just makes the rule visible.

### Sound subsystem (top-bar toggle + popover)

| Control | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Volume icon / mute button** | Single-click toggles a per-browser mute flag (`localStorage:sfx.muted`). The icon swaps between `volume_up` (live, cobalt) and `volume_off` (muted, grey). | Every user (per-device) | Fastest path to "turn this off in the meeting". |
| **Settings gear** | Opens the 280-pixel sound-preferences popover. | Every user | Houses everything else ŌĆö volume, pack picker, preview. |
| **Master Sound effects** toggle (in popover) | Same as the mute button ŌĆö switches the persistent mute flag. | Every user | A second affordance for the same control because the popover is the natural home for it once open. |
| **Volume range** (in popover) | Sets the per-browser master gain (`localStorage:sfx.volume`, 0ŌĆō1, default 0.45). Disabled while muted. | Every user | The mute toggle is binary, but presentations and quiet offices want a softer setting, not silence. |
| **Sound pack ŌĆö Musical** | Switches `localStorage:sfx.pack` to `musical`. Synthesised abstract tones ŌĆö the original CIHRMS palette. | Every user | The minimal, unobtrusive default for users who find the cinematic pack too literal. |
| **Sound pack ŌĆö Cinematic** (default) | Switches to `cinematic`. Real-world sound approximations ŌĆö doorbell, train horn, cash register, bell ŌĆö synthesised in `utils/cinematic-synth.js`. | Every user | The institutional feel ŌĆö what the team wanted for a government deployment. |
| **Sound pack ŌĆö Gamified** (added 2026-05-23) | Switches to `gamified`. Arcade chiptune ŌĆö square/sawtooth oscillators on the hits, sine on held tails, `slideTo` glissandi on celebratory finals. Punchy "achievement unlocked" feel for welcome, success, `task.completed`, and approval events. | Every user | The cinematic bell is dignified but not rewarding; the musical pack is calm but not assertive ŌĆö gamified fills the gap. |
| **Preset preview** (`play_arrow` chip per preset key) | One-shot plays the named event in the active pack (so the user can audition without provoking a real event). | Every user | "Show me what `payment.released` sounds like" ŌĆö the only honest way to pick a pack. |
| **File overrides** | All three packs read from `public/sounds/<pack>/<key>.{mp3,ogg,wav}` first. If the file exists, the decoded `AudioBuffer` is played and cached in memory. Synth fallback only kicks in for keys with no file. | Operator (drops files into the volume) | Per-event-key override ŌĆö drop one MP3 in, the other 14 events keep their synth voices until each is replaced. No big-bang migration. Recommended source for gamified is Kenney's CC0 audio bundles (UI Audio + Sci-Fi Sounds + Casino Audio); see `docs/sound_pack_sources.md`. |
| **Throttle** | Identical-key events within 600 ms are silently dropped (`stats.lastKey` + `stats.lastPlayedAt` check in `play()`). | Automatic | Stops toast bursts from overlapping sustained tails into a cacophony. |
| **Reduced-motion respect** | All three bell animations (`nb-ring`, `nb-pulse`, `nb-badge-heartbeat`) are disabled under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. The sound subsystem is independent ŌĆö silence is governed by the mute toggle, not by motion preference. | Automatic | WCAG 2.3.3 ŌĆö Animation from Interactions. |

> *Notes:* Persistence keys are local to the browser: `sfx.muted`, `sfx.volume`, `sfx.pack`. There is no per-user persisted-on-server sound preference in the MVP ŌĆö the choice is per-device. The Web Audio context is created lazily on first user gesture (a single one-shot click/keydown/touchend listener) to satisfy autoplay policy; subsequent plays reuse the single `AudioContext`. Decoded buffers are cached in a `Map` keyed by `${pack}/${eventKey}`; files we've tried and failed to load go into a `failedFiles` `Set` so we never retry. The volume button is wrapped in a `.sfx-wrap` so the document-click listener can tell "click inside the popover" from "click anywhere else" by walking the DOM ancestry.

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores notifications in a single canonical Laravel table:

- **`notifications`** ŌĆö a UUID primary key; `type` (the fully-qualified notification class name, e.g. `App\Notifications\LeaveStatusChanged`); a polymorphic owner pair (`notifiable_type`, `notifiable_id`) that points back to the `User` who should see this row; `data` (a JSON column ŌĆö `text` in the migration, cast to array on read); a nullable `read_at` timestamp; and the standard `created_at` / `updated_at` pair. The schema is the stock Laravel `notifications` migration (`2026_05_14_082201_create_notifications_table.php`) ŌĆö no per-row delivery channel column, no priority, no expiry, no soft delete. A row exists if and only if Laravel's `database` channel was in the notification's `via()` array.

Per-user channel preferences and consent are stored on the `users` table itself, added in `2026_05_18_000001_add_messaging_consent_columns_to_users.php`:

- **`notification_channels`** ŌĆö a nullable JSON column whose key set is `{email, in_app, whatsapp, slack, teams}` mapped to booleans. When the column is null the resolver falls back to the per-channel defaults (`email: true, in_app: true, whatsapp: false, slack: false, teams: false`).
- **`whatsapp_phone`** ŌĆö a `string(20)` indexed for lookup. Nullable. Stored in international format with optional leading `+`.
- **`whatsapp_consent_at`** ŌĆö a nullable timestamp stamped only when the user explicitly ticks the consent box *and* a phone is present. Cleared when either condition fails. This is the legal record of opt-in; the toggle alone is not.
- **`slack_user_id`** ŌĆö a nullable indexed `string(30)`. Without it, Slack notifications fall back to the configured HR channel.

There is no separate **`notification_channel_preferences`** table, no per-event opt-out (every event uses the channel matrix as a whole), and no per-channel TTL/quiet hours setting. Those are all roadmap items.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **In-app delivery is the `database` channel.** Laravel writes a `notifications` row whenever the notification's `via()` array contains `'database'`. The `in_app` toggle on the channels screen is **not yet** consulted by every producer ŌĆö the dispatcher honours it for the WhatsApp/Slack/Teams paths today, but the database write happens unconditionally for every notification that lists `database` in `via()`. Phase 1 closes this gap by routing every producer through a single preference-aware dispatcher.
- **Mail delivery is the `mail` channel** and uses the SMTP/SES config in `mail.php`. Notifications with `mail` in `via()` build a `MailMessage` from the notification's `toMail()` method.
- **Broadcast / realtime push is not wired.** No notification declares the `broadcast` channel in `via()`, no Laravel Echo client is registered in `resources/js/app.js`, and no Pusher/Reverb dependency is in `composer.json` or `package.json`. The bell is **polled** through the deferred Inertia prop ŌĆö every page navigation re-fetches the last ten unread plus the count. Phase 1 will add Reverb/Echo for sub-second delivery.
- **Per-row state is two-valued.** A row is unread (`read_at` is null) or read (`read_at` is a timestamp). There is no "archived" or "dismissed" state and the only mutation in the controller is `unreadNotifications()->update(['read_at' => now()])` ŌĆö *all at once*. Per-row mark-read and per-row delete will land with the row-action UI in Phase 1.
- **Data payloads are conventions, not contracts.** Each notification's `toArray()` returns a free-shape associative array. A few keys are honoured by the UI (`message` is the primary line shown in the bell and on the index; `kind` is read by the bell to pick a sound; `route` is read in some places ŌĆö `incident.assigned` sets `route: 'incidents.show'` ŌĆö but the index page does not currently navigate on row click). Every producer should include at least `message` and a `*_id` pointer back to the source entity; most include both.
- **Soft delete?** Not on this table. A notification row sticks around forever (subject to the operator's pruning policy in Phase 1). Right-to-erasure (Ch 26) handles deletion as part of a DPA workflow, not as a user-facing action.

## How it talks to other modules

Every other CIHRMS module is a *producer* of notifications ŌĆö the table below is the live inventory in the MVP plus the integration channel each producer uses today.

- **Leave (Ch 4)** ŌĆö `LeaveStatusChanged` fires from the `SendNotifications` listener on every `LeaveStatusUpdated` event (approved, rejected, withdrawn). `via()` is `['database', 'mail']`. The companion `LeaveApprovalReminder` is fired by the 3-day-stale cron over `['mail', 'database']`. The separate **`NotifyManagerOfLeaveRequest`** listener runs against `LeaveRequested` and DMs the manager through whatever Slack/Teams/WhatsApp channels they've configured via the dispatcher ŌĆö that path does *not* write a `notifications` row (it's a one-shot external message).
- **Employees (Ch 3)** ŌĆö `NewEmployeeWelcome` fires on `EmployeeCreated`. `via()` is `['mail']` only ŌĆö the welcome email goes out but no in-app row is written, because the new hire's bell will already be empty on first sign-in.
- **Tickets (Ch 11)** ŌĆö `TicketAssigned` (`via: ['database']`) on `TicketService::assign`, and `TicketResolved` (`via: ['database']`, with `kind: 'ticket.completed'`) when the service moves a ticket to Resolved or Closed. The `kind` field is what the bell reads to pick the celebratory `task.completed` sound.
- **Documents (Ch 13)** ŌĆö `DocumentAwaitingAction` (`via: ['database', 'mail']`) fires from `DocumentRoutingService::route` when a route step is created and again when a step advances; `DocumentCompletedNotice` (`via: ['database', 'mail']`) fires to the document owner on rejection or completion.
- **Identity / Ghana Card (Ch 25)** ŌĆö `IdentityExpiringReminder` (`via: ['mail', 'database']`) fires from the `identity:expiring` artisan command ŌĆö Ghana Card re-verification 30/15/7 days before the 12-month window closes.
- **Audit (Ch 26)** ŌĆö `AuditChainBroken` (`via: ['mail', 'database']`) fires from the `audit:verify-chain` cron when the SHA-256 hash chain over `audit_logs` fails end-to-end verification. Routed to super_admins; Slack and SMS are deliberately *not* used (the audit log itself is the primary record of who saw the alert).
- **DPA / Public Data Subject (Ch 26)** ŌĆö `DpaVerificationLink` (`via: ['mail']`) fires from `PublicDpaController::store` to the public submitter's email via `Notification::route('mail', $email)` ŌĆö an on-demand notifiable because public subjects don't have a User row.
- **Incidents (Ch 27)** ŌĆö five listeners in `app/Listeners/Incident/` write directly into the `notifications` table with `kind: 'incident.assigned'`, etc. They construct `Notification::create(...)` directly rather than going through `notify()` ŌĆö a shortcut that bypasses the `via()` array entirely.
- **Payroll (Ch 19)** *(roadmap)* ŌĆö `PaymentMarkedPaid` exists in the UI type palette but the producer hasn't shipped; the bell already knows how to render it.
- **Complaints (Ch 12), Performance (Ch 6), Learning (Ch 7), Loans (Ch 21), Disbursements (Ch 22)** *(roadmap)* ŌĆö none of these write notifications today; Phase 1 ports their event hooks onto the same `SendNotifications` listener pattern.

The fan-out today is mostly direct from each service: a service constructs the notification, calls `$user->notify(...)` (or `Notification::send(...)` for fan-out to multiple recipients), and Laravel handles the rest. The `SendNotifications` listener registered in `AppServiceProvider::boot()` is the canonical event-driven path ŌĆö `EmployeeCreated` and `LeaveStatusUpdated` route through it; the others call `notify()` from inside the originating service. Phase 1 unifies this by moving every producer onto the listener pattern with a single `NotifyService` that consults the user's channel preferences before each `via()` is constructed.

## Standards touchpoints

- **WCAG 2.1 AA ┬¦1.4.3 (Contrast)** ŌĆö every notification colour pair (red badge on cobalt bell, type-tag pill on tinted background, unread dot on the row stripe) is tested against the 4.5:1 ratio for normal text and 3:1 for the icon target. The amber driver-not-connected pill is paired against a near-white surface for 4.5:1 contrast.
- **WCAG 2.1 AA ┬¦2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide)** ŌĆö the persistent bell pulse loops indefinitely while unread > 0; users can stop it by clearing notifications. The badge heartbeat is a 2.2 s loop. Reduced-motion preference disables both animations and the ring entirely (`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` in `NotificationBell.vue`'s `<style>` block).
- **WCAG 2.1 AA ┬¦2.3.1 (Three Flashes or Below Threshold)** ŌĆö no part of the bell or list flashes at or above the seizure-inducing threshold; the brightest pulse step is a single colour ramp at sub-second intervals (3 s for pulse, 2.2 s for heartbeat).
- **WCAG 2.1 AA ┬¦4.1.3 (Status Messages)** ŌĆö the bell's red counter badge is a status message (it changes without focus). It is paired with the `notifications` glyph's filled state so the cue is not colour-only.
- **WCAG 2.1 AA ┬¦1.4.13 (Content on Hover or Focus)** ŌĆö the bell dropdown is opened by click (not hover) and stays open until the user clicks outside or selects "Mark all read"; no transient hover panels.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17 (lawful basis)** ŌĆö in-app and email notifications about the user's own employment data run on the employment-contract basis. WhatsApp, Slack, and Teams are *separate* processing operations under ┬¦18 (consent) ŌĆö each has its own toggle, and WhatsApp additionally records an explicit consent timestamp (`whatsapp_consent_at`) because Meta requires it. Revoking consent clears the timestamp and force-disables the channel.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦40 (right to erasure)** ŌĆö notification rows are pruned by the DPA redaction job (Ch 26) when the underlying subject is erased; the rows are not user-deletable from the UI yet.
- **Mental Health Act, 2012 (Act 846) / minor-PII flag** ŌĆö none of the notification payloads in the MVP carry health information; the type palette deliberately has no "medical" or "clinical" category, so a future leave-with-medical-cert notification will need a sensitive-payload channel rule before it ships (tracked in Phase 1).

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap lands four things directly on this screen: (1) **Realtime push** ŌĆö Laravel Reverb is wired as the WebSocket broker, every notification class adds `broadcast` to its `via()` array, and `NotificationBell.vue` subscribes to a private `App.Models.User.{id}` channel via Echo so the bell rings *sub-second* instead of polling on every navigation; (2) **Row-level actions** ŌĆö `PATCH /notifications/{id}/read`, `DELETE /notifications/{id}`, and a per-row click handler that uses the `route` and entity-id fields in the JSON payload to navigate to the source module (the data is already there ŌĆö only the UI is missing); (3) **Preference-aware dispatcher** ŌĆö a `NotifyService` that consults `notification_channels` *before* building the `via()` array, so the `in_app` toggle finally hard-mutes the database write; per-event-type opt-outs land in the same release; (4) **Sound preferences persisted on the server** ŌĆö the per-device localStorage choice is mirrored on the `users` row so a user who picks "gamified" on their laptop hears the same pack on their phone. The roadmap also adds quiet hours, a digest-mode option ("daily 5pm summary instead of per-event"), and SMS as a first-class channel through MTN/Vodafone Ghana for sites that don't run WhatsApp.

<!-- ===== 17_announcements.md ===== -->

# Chapter 17 ŌĆö Announcements

> *In one paragraph.* Announcements is the institute's loudspeaker ŌĆö one short table, one admin page, and one piece of UI that everybody sees. HR and the executive write notices ("office closes 1pm Friday for the retreat", "all-hands at 09:00 in the Atrium", "new tax form in payroll"); the system then streams those notices across the top of every authenticated page as a live ticker, layered on top of three streams it generates itself (upcoming birthdays, approved-leave events, freshly assigned tasks). The Notifications module in Chapter 16 is for *you-shaped* messages ŌĆö this is for *everyone-shaped* ones. The Messaging admin in Chapter 15 is for outbound email and SMS to the public ŌĆö this one stays inside the building.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **System** group ŌåÆ **Notice Board** (top of the System section, megaphone icon `campaign`). It lives in the System group rather than Workforce because composing notices is an administrative act, not a workforce one ŌĆö the same group that contains Integrations, Audit Logs, DPA Requests, Whistleblower, and User Management. The same `announcements.manage` permission gates both the sidebar item and the route.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin**, **ceo** ŌĆö full administrative access (composing, editing, removing notices) and the sidebar item is always visible.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö the canonical author. `announcements.manage` ships in the seeded role permission set.
    - Every other authenticated role ŌĆö `employee`, `manager`, `dept_head`, `finance_officer`, `it_support`, `auditor`, `marketing` ŌĆö does **not** see the admin page or the sidebar item, but does see the ticker at the top of every page. Read-only consumers, never authors.
    - Guests (unauthenticated) ŌĆö the ticker does not load on the login screen at all; the deferred prop is gated on `$request->user()` in `HandleInertiaRequests`.
- **Related modules:** Notifications (Ch 16) ŌĆö same screen real estate's spiritual sibling, but per-user and click-to-read instead of broadcast; Messaging admin (Ch 15) ŌĆö external broadcast (email/SMS to contacts and the public) vs. this chapter's internal broadcast (in-app ticker to staff); Audit Logs (Ch 24) ŌĆö every create/update/destroy is sealed into the tamper-evident log; Leave (Ch 4) ŌĆö the source of the auto-generated "event" entries on the ticker; Performance (Ch 6) ŌĆö the source of the "task" entries (new goal assignments); Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö the source of the birthday entries.

## The screens

There are only two surfaces in this module ŌĆö the admin **Notice Board** at `/announcements` (one page, no detail view), and the **ticker** rendered at the top of every authenticated page by `AnnouncementTicker.vue`. We document both here because they are two halves of the same module: one is where you write, the other is where the institute reads.

![Notice Board admin ŌĆö hero banner, KPI tiles, register, compose drawer](../assets/screenshots/17_announcements/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero banner ŌĆö a single navy gradient block declaring how many notices are live on the ticker right now, with a cobalt accent count, the pinned count in gold, and "queued for future" / "expired" counters in indigo. The "Communications ┬Ę ticker" eyebrow tells the reader which module they are in without consulting the breadcrumb. ┬Ę ŌØĘ KPI strip ŌĆö four tiles (Live now, Pinned, Scheduled, Expired) that mirror the hero numbers in tile form for screen-reader users, each with a coloured `icon-tile` matching the donut palette below. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Composition band ŌĆö a 180px donut of notices by type (Notice ┬Ę Event ┬Ę Birthday ┬Ę Task ┬Ę System) on the left, a horizontal-bar severity mix on the right, plus three quick-filter pills (Pinned / Scheduled / Expired) under the bars. Clicking any legend item or quick-filter pill drives the table below. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Notice register ŌĆö filter bar (search, type, severity, status, clear) on top of a divider-separated list. Each row carries a coloured left rail keyed to type, the type tile, title, pills (Pinned / Inactive / Scheduled / Expired / severity), a two-line body, and a meta row (type ┬Ę audience ┬Ę start ┬Ę end ┬Ę author ┬Ę external link).*

![Compose drawer ŌĆö title, body, type pills, severity, audience, window, preview](../assets/screenshots/17_announcements/compose.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Title (required, 180 chars max) and Details (optional, 2000 chars max). The body is intentionally lightweight ŌĆö long-form announcements belong on the intranet behind a `link_url`. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Type pill row ŌĆö five pills with brand-keyed accents (Notice cobalt, Event cyan, Birthday magenta, Task gold, System sky-indigo) that snap to a high-contrast selected state when clicked. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Severity / Audience / Link ŌĆö three side-by-side fields. Severity has three values (Info, Important, Urgent), Audience defaults to "Everyone" and offers each system role as a scoping target, Link is an optional `https://ŌĆ”` URL that turns the ticker item into an anchor. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Start / End / Pin / Active ŌĆö the publish window plus two toggles. Empty start = "publish immediately"; empty end = "never expires". ┬Ę ŌØ║ Live preview band ŌĆö a dark gradient block at the bottom showing exactly how the notice will look on the ticker, including the pin icon, the severity pill, and the type accent. The preview re-renders on every keystroke so the author never has to publish-and-check.*

![Ticker ŌĆö leading label, scrolling rail, controls bar](../assets/screenshots/17_announcements/ticker.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Leading label ŌĆö a gold-tinted "NOTICE BOARD" chip pinned to the left edge with a pulsing "live" dot. The dot turns cyan and spins while a refresh is in flight, so users get unambiguous feedback that the ticker is talking to the server. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Scrolling viewport ŌĆö two cloned tracks scrolling left at a speed proportional to the item count (32 s minimum; longer rails run slower). Hovering the rail pauses both tracks; clicking the pause button does the same. Items with a `link_url` render as `<a>` tags; the rest are inert spans. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Controls ŌĆö synced-ago timestamp ("synced 42s ago"), pause, refresh-now, dismiss. Dismiss is local to the tab only; refreshing the page restores the ticker.*

> The three screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.32). Until then the build substitutes a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Notice Board admin

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **New notice** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the inline compose drawer. The drawer toggles in-place above the donut band; there is no slide-over panel. Clicking the same button (now labelled "Cancel") closes the drawer without firing anything. | `announcements.manage` | One-button publish ŌĆö composing a notice is the only action on this page, so it gets headline placement. The label flips between "New notice" and "Cancel" so the user always knows what the button will do next. |
| **Live on ticker chip** (top right, next to "New notice") | Read-only pill showing the live count with a pulsing cyan dot. | Anyone viewing the admin page | Mirrors the chip the rest of the institute sees on the ticker ŌĆö confirms the admin's view matches what staff are reading. |
| **Hero banner** | Read-only block: live count (cyan), pinned count (gold), scheduled, expired, plus the explanatory phrase "scrolling on every authenticated page top". | Same | "How loud is the loudspeaker right now?" answered in one glance. |
| **KPI tiles** (Live now, Pinned, Scheduled, Expired) | Read-only tiles repeating the hero numbers, each with its own icon and a one-line subtitle. | Same | Hero is decorative; KPI tiles are the screen-reader path. |
| **Type donut** (left of the composition band) | SVG donut keyed to the five types with the brand accent colours. The centre repeats the live count. Each legend row is clickable ŌĆö clicking a legend filters the register below by that type, clicking again clears the filter. | Same | "What kind of notices do we run?" ŌĆö at-a-glance composition that doubles as a one-click filter. |
| **Severity mix bars** (right of the composition band) | Three horizontal bars (Info cyan, Important gold, Urgent magenta) with the per-severity counts and a percentage on the right. Clicking a bar toggles the severity filter on the register below. | Same | The guardrail prompt: "Urgent should be rare". When the urgent bar starts dominating, the chart calls it out before HR has to. |
| **Pinned / Scheduled / Expired quick-filter pills** (under the severity bars) | Three pills wired straight to the status filter. Toggling sets/clears the filter. | Same | The three states most worth isolating get a one-tap path. |
| **Search box** ("Search title or bodyŌĆ”") | Client-side substring filter across title and body. No debounce ŌĆö the list is in memory because the controller already returned the page. | Same | The Notice Board never paginates past 20 in practice, so a client-side filter is faster than a round-trip. |
| **Type dropdown** (`All types` Ō¢Š) | Filters by `notice`, `event`, `birthday`, `task`, or `system`. | Same | The five values map 1:1 to the `AnnouncementType` enum. |
| **Severity dropdown** (`All severities` Ō¢Š) | Filters by `info`, `important`, or `urgent`. | Same | Same idea, `AnnouncementSeverity` enum. |
| **Status dropdown** (`All statuses` Ō¢Š) | Filters by computed lifecycle state ŌĆö `active`, `pinned`, `scheduled`, or `expired`. Status is computed in the page from `is_active`, `starts_at`, and `ends_at` against the current clock, not stored on the row. | Same | The user thinks "show me the queued ones"; the database thinks "rows where `starts_at > now`". This dropdown bridges the two vocabularies. |
| **Clear** (appears only when at least one filter is set) | Resets all four filters at once. | Same | One-click escape hatch after an exploratory filter run. |
| **Row (whole row)** | Read-only display ŌĆö there is no detail page. To act on a notice, use the Edit or Remove icons on the right. | Same | Notices are short ŌĆö title + body + window is everything the row already shows. A detail page would be an empty repeat. |
| **Edit** (pencil icon, per row) | Opens the compose drawer pre-filled with that notice. The form below the drawer becomes a `PATCH`. | Same | Same drawer, same fields, same validation ŌĆö only the verb differs. |
| **Remove** (trash icon, per row) | Confirms ("Remove this notice?") and then `DELETE`s. There is no soft delete on this table ŌĆö the row is gone. The audit log keeps a record. | Same | A notice is ephemeral by design ŌĆö once it's off the ticker, it's off. The audit log is the long-term memory. |
| **Footer count** (bottom of the register) | "Showing N of M notices" + a "Filters active" indicator when relevant. | Same | Standard table-foot ŌĆö confirms that "no rows" means "no matches", not "no data". |

> *Notes:* The page hydrates from a single Inertia render ŌĆö `AnnouncementController::index` returns `announcements` (paginated 20 per page, pinned first, then most-recent first), plus three small aggregate payloads (`stats`, `typeBreakdown`, `severityBreakdown`). The aggregate payloads are computed with `selectRaw('ŌĆ” COUNT(*)') ->groupBy(ŌĆ”) ->pluck()` against the full table ŌĆö not the current page ŌĆö so the donut and KPIs always reflect the whole register, not just what's on screen. There is no N+1: the row's `author` relation is eager-loaded with `with('author:id,name')`.

### Compose drawer

| Field | Validation | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Title** | required, string, max 180 chars | Saved verbatim to `announcements.title`. This is the line that scrolls on the ticker ŌĆö keep it short and concrete. |
| **Body** | optional, string, max 2000 chars | Not shown on the ticker ŌĆö only visible on the admin register and in the audit trail. Use it for context that the author wants future readers (or auditors) to see, not for content the staff need to read in real-time. |
| **Type** (pill row) | required; must be one of `notice`, `event`, `birthday`, `task`, `system` (the `AnnouncementType` enum) | Defaults to `notice`. `birthday` and `task` are kept on the pill row even though the system also generates them automatically ŌĆö an HR officer can manually add a one-off birthday card or a system-level "everyone has a new mandatory task" notice. |
| **Severity** | required; must be one of `info`, `important`, `urgent` (the `AnnouncementSeverity` enum) | Defaults to `info`. `urgent` styles the ticker item magenta ŌĆö the compose drawer warns the author with "use sparingly" copy. |
| **Audience** | optional; free-string up to 40 chars on the server, but the UI offers the seven seeded role slugs (`hr_admin`, `manager`, `employee`, `finance_officer`, `it_support`, `marketing`, plus the empty value for "Everyone") | Null = visible to everybody. Set to a role = visible only to users whose `users.role` enum matches. Audience scoping happens server-side in `AnnouncementService::manual()` via the `Announcement::scopeForRole` scope, so a hidden notice is never sent down the wire. |
| **Link** (`link_url`) | optional, valid URL, max 500 chars | When set, the ticker item renders as `<a target="_blank" rel="noopener">`; when empty, the item is an inert span. Use it to link to the intranet post that has the full story. |
| **Starts at** | optional, valid date(time); must be Ōēż `ends_at` (validator runs `after_or_equal:starts_at` on the end side) | Empty start = "publish immediately". The Vue form binds the `<input type="datetime-local">` to the local clock; the controller stores it through Eloquent's `datetime` cast which normalises to UTC. |
| **Ends at** | optional, valid date(time), must be `after_or_equal:starts_at` | Empty end = "never expires" (the notice stays on the ticker until an admin removes it or sets `is_active = false`). |
| **Pin to front** | boolean checkbox styled as a chunky toggle pill | Pinned notices float to the top of the ticker rail regardless of severity ŌĆö the sort is *pinned first, then severity weight, then chronological*. Use sparingly for the same reason as Urgent. |
| **Active** | boolean checkbox, same styling as Pin | When `false`, the notice is "saved as draft" ŌĆö visible on the admin register with an "Inactive" pill, but absent from the ticker. The state is independent of the start/end window ŌĆö a draft scheduled for next Tuesday is still a draft. |
| **Live preview band** | read-only | Rerenders on every input event so the author sees the final ticker styling ŌĆö title, pin, type icon, severity pill ŌĆö before publishing. |
| **Publish notice** (cobalt CTA) | submits | `POST /announcements` on create, `PATCH /announcements/{id}` on edit. Both use the same `StoreAnnouncementRequest`. On success the drawer closes, a flash toast fires ("Notice published." / "Notice updated."), and the page partial-reloads. |
| **Cancel** | closes drawer | No fields are reset on close ŌĆö re-opening the drawer in the same session preserves whatever you typed (Vue's `useForm` state). |

> *Notes:* The whole compose flow is gated at three layers ŌĆö the sidebar item is gated on `can('announcements.manage')`; the route is gated by the `permission:announcements.manage` middleware; the `StoreAnnouncementRequest::authorize()` re-checks the permission server-side before validation even runs. A user without the permission gets a 403 in three places before the row touches the database. The `created_by` field is stamped from `$request->user()->id` in the controller, never accepted from the request body ŌĆö so an attacker who somehow reached the endpoint cannot impersonate another author.

### The ticker (`AnnouncementTicker.vue`)

| Element | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Leading label** ("NOTICE BOARD" + live dot + count badge) | Read-only chip pinned to the left of the rail. The live dot pulses gold; while a refresh is in flight, the dot turns cyan and the chip's ring animates. The count badge shows the number of items currently in the rail. | Every authenticated user | Establishes "this is the institute's loudspeaker", not "this is a banner ad" ŌĆö the live dot is the trust signal. |
| **Item** (one per notice / birthday / event / task) | Anchor or span containing an icon, the title, and an optional pin marker. Items with a `link_url` are anchors that open in a new tab with `rel="noopener"`; the rest are inert spans. The left-rail accent colour is keyed to the type; the title colour is keyed to the severity. | Same | The single highest-traffic UI in CIHRMS ŌĆö it appears on every page after login. The styling difference between Info / Important / Urgent is the only thing distinguishing routine notices from things that need attention. |
| **Pause** (pause icon) | Pauses the marquee scroll. Hovering the rail also pauses it via CSS ŌĆö the button is the explicit affordance for keyboard / screen-reader users. | Same | Vestibular-safe ŌĆö anyone reading a long title needs to be able to stop the rail to finish. |
| **Refresh** (refresh icon) | Triggers an Inertia partial-reload of the `announcementTicker`, `notifications`, and `notificationCount` props. The icon spins while the request is in flight. | Same | Sometimes the 60-second poll isn't fast enough ŌĆö for instance, the user just acknowledged a goal and wants to confirm the task item left the ticker. |
| **Dismiss** (close icon) | Hides the rail for the current tab session only. Reloading the page restores it. | Same | A non-destructive escape ŌĆö staff who need a few minutes of distraction-free screen can hide the rail without filing a ticket. |
| **Synced X ago** (monospace timestamp) | Shows how long since the last successful refresh; updates every 10 seconds. | Same | Diagnoses "is the ticker actually live?" ŌĆö if the count says "synced 6h ago" the user knows the tab was backgrounded and the data is stale. |
| **Auto-poll** (background, no UI) | Every 60 seconds the ticker partial-reloads its three deferred props. The poll pauses when the tab is hidden (`document.visibilitychange`) and resumes when it returns. | Same | Reasonable freshness without hammering the server ŌĆö the average reader doesn't need sub-minute resolution on a notice that's been on the rail for an hour. |
| **Chime on new item** (background, no UI) | When the poll returns an item that wasn't in the previous rail, the matching sound plays from the sound-pack composable ŌĆö `announcement` for notices and birthdays, `assigned.you` for tasks, `event.created` for events, `warning` for urgent severity. Cold-load is silent (the first poll seeds the known-id set without firing). | Same | Audible cue for the cases where the visual scroll might miss the user ŌĆö a notice arrives while they're reading email in another column. |
| **Reduced-motion fallback** | The marquee, the pulse ring, and the spinner all suspend animation when `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is set. | Same | WCAG 2.1 ┬¦2.3.3 ŌĆö motion can be a barrier; this respects the user's OS-level preference automatically. |
| **Mobile collapse** | Below 640px the leading-label text and the synced-ago timestamp drop out; only the icon, count badge, and item rail remain. | Same | Phone users still need the loudspeaker; the chrome doesn't fit. |

> *Notes:* The ticker is mounted once in `AuthenticatedLayout.vue` at the very top of the page, above every Inertia page. It receives its data through the Inertia `announcementTicker` shared prop, populated by `HandleInertiaRequests::share()` via `Inertia::defer(...)`. The defer is important ŌĆö the initial Inertia render does not block on the three database queries the ticker needs (aggregate, scope, sort); the page paints first, then the ticker fills in on the follow-up request. The same defer carries the bell-badge notifications, so one round-trip refreshes both UIs.

## The data behind it

A notice is one row in `announcements` and nothing else. The table has 14 columns plus the standard `timestamps` pair:
