- **Content** ŌĆö `title` (Ōēż 180 chars, required), `body` (Ōēż 2000 chars, optional), `link_url` (Ōēż 500 chars, optional), `icon` (Ōēż 40 chars, optional ŌĆö falls back to the type's default Material Symbols glyph).
- **Categorisation** ŌĆö `type` (enum: `notice` / `event` / `birthday` / `task` / `system`), `severity` (enum: `info` / `important` / `urgent`).
- **Targeting** ŌĆö `audience_role` (free-string Ōēż 40, null = everyone). Indexed.
- **Lifecycle** ŌĆö `is_active` (boolean, default true), `pinned` (boolean, default false), `starts_at` (nullable timestamp), `ends_at` (nullable timestamp). The composite index `(is_active, starts_at, ends_at)` is the one that makes `Announcement::activeNow()` a single index scan even on tables of thousands of rows.
- **Provenance** ŌĆö `created_by` (foreign key to `users`, `nullOnDelete` so that removing an author user leaves the historical notice intact), plus the Eloquent timestamps.

There are no associated tables ŌĆö no attachments, no acknowledgement receipts, no per-user view counters, no comments. Announcements are deliberately a *flat* concept in the MVP. Anything richer (acknowledgement workflows, mandatory read-receipts, document attachments) is roadmapped behind specific compliance triggers and described in *What's planned next* below.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **Lifecycle is computed, not stored.** A notice's effective status ŌĆö Active / Scheduled / Expired / Inactive ŌĆö is derived in real time from `is_active`, `starts_at`, and `ends_at` against `now()`. There is no `status` column. Scheduling a notice for next Tuesday does not flip a "scheduled" flag; it sets a `starts_at` in the future. The ticker's `activeNow()` scope (and the admin index's status filter) re-evaluates this on every read.
- **Audience scoping is enforced server-side.** The Vue ticker never receives notices outside its audience ŌĆö `AnnouncementService::manual()` filters with `Announcement::scopeForRole` before mapping into the response payload. Browser tools cannot reveal scoped notices the user isn't entitled to see.
- **The ticker is composite, not a one-to-one mirror.** Only the *Notice / Event / System* rows the admin sees on this page come from the `announcements` table. The Birthday and Task entries on the ticker are computed at request time from `employees.date_of_birth` (next 7 days) and `goals.created_at` (last 7 days, the viewer's own goals). Removing a manual "birthday" notice from this page does not stop the system from auto-broadcasting actual upcoming birthdays.
- **Hard delete by design.** Unlike Employees (Ch 3) or Tickets (Ch 11), there is no `SoftDeletes` trait on `Announcement`. Removing a notice is irreversible ŌĆö the row leaves the database. The audit log (Ch 24) records the create, update, and destroy actions including the full payload of the deleted row, so the institutional memory of *what was announced* survives even after the row is gone.
- **No "draft" status ŌĆö use `is_active = false`.** A notice with `is_active = false` is the canonical draft state. It appears on the admin register with an "Inactive" pill but does not stream on the ticker. The Vue compose drawer surfaces this as the `Active` toggle.

## How it talks to other modules

- **`HandleInertiaRequests::share()`** ŌĆö the middleware that injects shared props into every Inertia response wires `announcementTicker` to `AnnouncementService::ticker($request->user())`. This is the only path by which a notice reaches a user's screen; there is no public route, no broadcast channel, no email fan-out. The shared prop is wrapped in `Inertia::defer(...)`, so the ticker hydrates after the page paints rather than blocking the first byte.
- **Notifications (Ch 16)** ŌĆö sibling, not parent. The two modules sit side-by-side under the same defer block in the Inertia share, refresh on the same 60-second poll, and share a UX language (icon-tile, severity, pinned). They are functionally distinct: a Notification is *for one user, has a read state, and demands action*; an Announcement is *for everyone (or everyone-in-a-role), has no read state, and is informational*. Nothing currently fans an announcement out into per-user notifications, but the doors are wired for it (see roadmap below).
- **Messaging admin (Ch 15)** ŌĆö the *external* twin. Messaging admin pushes to email, SMS, and the public website; Announcements pushes to the in-app ticker. They share no tables; their only relationship is that the same author often uses both ŌĆö a single "office closes 1pm Friday" message typically goes out through Announcements (in-app) and Messaging admin (SMS) at the same time.
- **Employees (Ch 3)** ŌĆö read-only consumer. `AnnouncementService::birthdays()` queries `employees.date_of_birth` for the next seven days to layer auto-generated birthday items onto the ticker. Birthdays are computed in PHP rather than SQL so the same code runs identically across SQLite, MySQL, and PostgreSQL (MONTH/DAY math differs between engines).
- **Leave (Ch 4)** ŌĆö read-only consumer. `AnnouncementService::events()` queries `leave_requests` for approved leave starting in the next fourteen days; each one becomes an `event`-type ticker item. The link is purely visual ŌĆö the ticker links nowhere; staff who want detail open the Leave module separately.
- **Performance (Ch 6)** ŌĆö read-only consumer. `AnnouncementService::tasks()` queries the viewer's own `goals` created in the last seven days (excluding completed and cancelled) and surfaces them as `task`-type items. The ticker item links to `route('performance.goals.index')` so a click jumps straight to the goals page.
- **Sound packs (Ch 23, see project memory)** ŌĆö the ticker calls into `useSound` from `@/composables/useSound` when a new item arrives, mapped per-type. A user who has chosen "musical" / "cinematic" / "gamified" hears the right sound for that pack; a user who muted the app hears nothing. All packs ship a `announcement`, `assigned.you`, `event.created`, and `warning` sound, so the mapping never falls off an edge.
- **Audit Logs (Ch 24)** ŌĆö write-only consumer. Every successful create, update, and destroy is captured into the tamper-evident audit log by the global audit observer; the deleted-row payload is preserved verbatim so a removed notice is fully reconstructible from the audit trail.

## Standards touchpoints

- **WCAG 2.1 AA (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines)** ŌĆö the ticker is constructed to honour four specific success criteria. **┬¦1.4.3 (Contrast)** ŌĆö the navy-gradient shell, the gold leading-chip text, and the per-severity item colours all clear the 4.5:1 ratio against the cobalt-to-indigo background. **┬¦2.2.2 (Pause, stop, hide)** ŌĆö the marquee can be paused by hover, by the explicit pause button, and by dismissing the rail; the dismiss is keyboard-reachable and announced via `aria-label`. **┬¦2.3.3 (Animation from interactions)** ŌĆö the marquee animation, the pulse ring, and the refresh spinner all suspend under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. **┬¦4.1.2 (Name, role, value)** ŌĆö the rail carries `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"`, every control has an `aria-label`, and the pause/resume button's label flips with state so assistive tech narrates "Pause scrolling" / "Resume scrolling" correctly. See Chapter 44 for the institute-wide accessibility audit.
- **WCAG 2.1 ┬¦3.1.5 (Reading level)** ŌĆö the 180-character title cap is deliberate. It forces authors to lead with the action ("Office closes 1pm Friday for the retreat") rather than burying the news under bureaucratic preamble. Longer prose belongs in the optional body field or behind a `link_url`.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17 ŌĆö lawful basis** ŌĆö the institute relies on legitimate-interest grounds for processing employee personal data in the auto-generated birthday and task layers. The system minimises this exposure by (a) showing only the employee's name and the relative day of their birthday (not the year of birth), (b) excluding birthdays beyond a seven-day horizon, and (c) running the entire layer behind the `prefers-reduced-motion` and dismiss controls so any individual user can opt out for the session. An institute-level opt-out (per-employee preference) is roadmapped (see below).
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦18 (data minimisation)** ŌĆö birthday items expose name plus relative day only. Date of birth, age, and year are never sent to the browser.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦40 (right to erasure)** ŌĆö when an employee row is redacted (the institute's erasure procedure), the redaction job also nullifies `date_of_birth`, which removes that employee from the auto-generated birthday layer on the next ticker poll. Manual notices stamped with `created_by = <that user>` keep the title and body (the institutional record); the foreign key nulls out under the `nullOnDelete` constraint so the row stands without an author rather than dangling.
- **National Communications Authority ŌĆö accessibility advisory for state institutions** ŌĆö the live-region pattern (`aria-live="polite"`), the pause control, and the reduced-motion fallback together satisfy the NCA's 2024 accessibility advisory for institute-wide announcement channels.

## Audience scoping in detail

There are exactly three audience targets the admin can compose against:

- **Everyone** (the empty `audience_role`). The notice streams on every authenticated user's ticker regardless of role. This is the default and the most common choice ŌĆö "office closes Friday" is for everyone.
- **A single system role** ŌĆö one of `hr_admin`, `manager`, `employee`, `finance_officer`, `it_support`, or `marketing`. The notice streams only on the ticker of users whose `users.role` enum matches. A notice scoped to `manager` reaches department heads and people-managers; a notice scoped to `finance_officer` reaches finance staff alone.
- **A single department** (roadmapped, Phase 1). The MVP does not scope to departments ŌĆö the seven role values are the entire vocabulary today. The schema is ready for a future `audience_department_id` column; the UI will gain a second selector at that point.

The scoping is enforced in the `Announcement::scopeForRole` query scope on the model ŌĆö `whereNull('audience_role')->orWhere('audience_role', $role)`. The scope runs inside `AnnouncementService::manual()` before the rows are mapped into the ticker payload, so a notice scoped to a different role *is never sent to the browser at all* ŌĆö there is no client-side filter that could be defeated by DevTools.

There is no "audience excludes" scoping (no way to write a notice for "everyone except marketing"), and no multi-role scoping (no way to write a notice for "HR + finance only"). The product position is that if a notice cuts across roles, it usually applies to everyone; if it doesn't, it should be split into two notices.

## Publish workflow

Announcements have no formal review or approval workflow ŌĆö they go live the moment the author clicks "Publish notice". The implicit workflow is encoded in the lifecycle states the user sees on the admin register:

- **Draft** ŌĆö `is_active = false`. The author saved the notice without making it public. Appears on the admin register with the grey "Inactive" pill; absent from the ticker. There is no second-author approval step in the MVP ŌĆö drafts are a tool for the author, not a control over the author.
- **Scheduled** ŌĆö `is_active = true` and `starts_at > now()`. The notice will appear on the ticker automatically when `starts_at` falls behind the clock. There is no scheduler job; the `Announcement::activeNow()` scope re-evaluates the windows on every read. Appears with the cyan "Scheduled" pill.
- **Active** ŌĆö `is_active = true`, `starts_at IS NULL OR starts_at <= now()`, `ends_at IS NULL OR ends_at >= now()`. On the ticker right now. The default visible state.
- **Expired** ŌĆö `is_active = true` and `ends_at < now()`. The notice has passed its end window. Appears on the admin register with a grey "Expired" pill; absent from the ticker. Expired rows are retained in the table until an admin explicitly removes them ŌĆö the institute keeps them so authors can clone "we did this last year" notices into next year's equivalents.

The four states are computed on the fly from three columns (`is_active`, `starts_at`, `ends_at`) ŌĆö the admin UI re-derives them client-side in the `noticeStatus()` helper to populate the per-row pill; the controller re-derives them server-side to populate the KPI counts. There is no synchronised "status" column to drift out of step.

Two control surfaces let the author transition between states without re-opening the drawer:

- **The `Active` toggle in the compose drawer** ŌĆö flips `is_active`. Use it to take a notice off the ticker without deleting it.
- **The `Remove` (trash) row action** ŌĆö hard-deletes the row. Use it when the notice is genuinely no longer relevant.

There is no separate "archive" verb ŌĆö the convention is that an expired notice can be left in place (becomes a future template) or removed (genuinely irrelevant). Archive-on-write is Phase 1 work, alongside the acknowledgement-receipt feature.

## Engagement metrics

The MVP does **not** track per-user view counts, click-throughs, or acknowledgements on announcements. This is a deliberate scope decision: the ticker is a broadcast medium, not an engagement medium. The metrics the admin sees on the index page are aggregates of the register itself, not of the audience:

- **Live now** ŌĆö count of rows currently satisfying `is_active = true AND starts_at IS NULL OR <= now AND ends_at IS NULL OR >= now`.
- **Pinned** ŌĆö count of `pinned = true AND is_active = true` rows. Excludes inactive pins on the assumption that the author meant to publish.
- **Scheduled** ŌĆö count of `is_active = true AND starts_at > now` rows.
- **Expired** ŌĆö count of `ends_at < now` rows (regardless of `is_active`).
- **Type breakdown** ŌĆö `GROUP BY type` over the full table; feeds the donut and the legend.
- **Severity breakdown** ŌĆö `GROUP BY severity` over the full table; feeds the horizontal bars and the percentage labels.

The aggregates are recomputed on every page load ŌĆö there is no cache. With a table that typically sits in the tens-to-low-hundreds of rows even after a year in production, the `COUNT(*) GROUP BY` queries return in single-digit milliseconds; there is no need for materialised summaries.

Real engagement metrics (per-user view counts, click-through rates, acknowledgement workflows for mandatory-read notices, audit-grade evidence that "every member of staff has acknowledged the new code of conduct") are deferred to Phase 1 as part of the *Compliance Announcements* feature described below.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap lands four enhancements on this module, in priority order:

1. **Department-level audience scoping** ŌĆö a second selector on the compose drawer ("scoped to which departments?") backed by a new `announcement_departments` pivot table. A notice can target any combination of {everyone, role, department}; the query scope expands to handle the union. This unblocks the "for the Finance department only" use case that today has to fall back to the role scope, which is too broad.
2. **Acknowledgement receipts (Compliance Announcements)** ŌĆö an optional flag on a notice ("requires acknowledgement"), backed by a new `announcement_acknowledgements` table with one row per (user, announcement). Required notices sit on the ticker until the user clicks an explicit "I acknowledge" button, after which the row leaves their ticker but stays on everyone else's. The admin register gains an "Acks: 142 of 198" column that drives the auditor's evidence pack. This is the prerequisite for the Auditor-General compliance requirement that policy updates be demonstrably read by every member of staff.
3. **Attachments and richer body** ŌĆö a notice can attach a PDF (the policy itself, the timetable, the seating plan) through the same documents pipeline used by Employees (Ch 3) and Tickets (Ch 11). The body field grows a Markdown-lite renderer so authors can format short policy text without falling back to the link-to-intranet pattern.
4. **Cross-channel fan-out** ŌĆö a "broadcast to email + SMS as well" checkbox on the compose drawer that, when set, queues the notice through the Messaging admin (Ch 15) pipeline at the same time it publishes to the ticker. This collapses the current two-step "announce in-app, then announce externally" choreography into a single act, with one audit-log row covering both channels.

See Chapter 46 (Roadmap) for the full schedule; the four items above are sized at roughly two engineering weeks combined and gated behind the Auditor-General compliance trigger.

<!-- ===== 18_payments.md ===== -->

# Chapter 18 ŌĆö Payments

> *In one paragraph.* Payments is the **one-off payment ledger** ŌĆö every cedi that leaves CIHRMS *outside* the monthly Payroll Engine (Chapter 19) gets written here. A reimbursement for a HR officer who paid for the team's lunch out of pocket; a single ex-gratia bonus for a security guard who stopped a break-in; a Christmas hamper top-up; a one-time advance against an unbanked driver's salary; a one-shot consultancy fee for an external trainer who is not on payroll ŌĆö all of these are Payment rows. The screen is deliberately humble: pick an employee, type a description, type an amount, save. It lives next to the per-employee Ghana payslip generator that turns a single basic-salary number into a fully-statutory payslip with PAYE and SSNIT bands shown. What the Payments module is **not** is the monthly statutory salary machine ŌĆö Payroll Engine (Chapter 19) owns dual-control approval, batch PayrollLine generation, GRA/SSNIT/NHIA/Tier-2 schedule exports, the IPPD2/IPPD3 file, and the GIFMIS journal. This chapter is the chequebook; Chapter 19 is the salary register.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Workforce & Finance** group ŌåÆ **Payroll** (top-level item, `payments` icon). The label still reads "Payroll" in the MVP UI for historical reasons ŌĆö this is the screen long-tenured users will type-ahead to when they look for "make a payment". Routes are mounted under `/payments`, the controller is `PaymentController`, the model is `Payment`, the URL slug stays as `payments` so the API and saved bookmarks survive. A separate **Payroll Runs** entry on the same menu group leads to the batch engine in Chapter 19.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every payment, every field. `PaymentPolicy::before()` grants `super_admin` a blanket pass.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö creates, marks paid, generates per-payment payslips. Holds `payroll.manage`.
    - **finance_officer** ŌĆö same operational permissions in the MVP (`payroll.manage` is the single gate on the route group); the splitting between "HR creates" and "Finance disburses" that exists for AP Payments (Chapter 20) is *not* enforced here yet.
    - **employee** ŌĆö does not reach `/payments` directly; their own payments surface as the Payroll tab on their employee detail page (Chapter 3) and via the Profile portal (Chapter 32). They never see another employee's payments.
    - **auditor, dept_head, manager, it_support** ŌĆö no access in the MVP. The whole route group sits behind `middleware('permission:payroll.manage')`, so anyone without that gate is bounced before the controller is even constructed.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö every payment points at one `Employee` (`employee_id`); Departments (Ch 15) ŌĆö analytics groups top earners by department via the joined Employee row; Payroll Engine (Ch 19) ŌĆö the monthly statutory salary machine that this module *is not* (see "Distinction from Payroll Engine" below); Loans (Ch 21) ŌĆö loan disbursements are issued through this module today, pending the dedicated loan-disbursement flow noted in Chapter 22's roadmap; Disbursements (Ch 22) ŌĆö off-cycle one-offs run through `PaymentService` rather than `BatchDisbursementService`, with no GhIPSS batch file; Audit Logs (Ch 24) ŌĆö every create and every mark-paid would land here once the dispatch is wired (see "Honest gaps" below); Notifications (Ch 16) ŌĆö `PaymentMarkedPaid` is in the bell's icon palette today, ready for the producer to fire; Profile Portal (Ch 32) ŌĆö the employee-facing read view; Reports (Ch 33) ŌĆö `PayrollExport` reads from this table for the month-scoped Excel pull.

## The screens

![Payments index ŌĆö masthead, compliance strip, analytics, table](../assets/screenshots/18_payments/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Editorial-Sovereign masthead ŌĆö "PAYMENT RECORD" eyebrow, "Payroll" headline, subhead naming the statutory anchor of the per-payment payslip path ("PAYE ┬Ę SSNIT Tier-1/Tier-2 ┬Ę GRA remittance ŌĆö Ghana-compliant payslip processing"), and two CTAs on the right: "Generate Payslip" (opens the Ghana payslip generator panel) and the cobalt "Quick Payment" (opens the simple create panel). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Compliance strip ŌĆö two tone-coded cards (green / amber / orange / red) showing the next SSNIT-14th and PAYE-15th remittance deadlines, the obligation month they cover, and the days-left counter. The tone is computed client-side from today's date ŌĆö no backend round-trip. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Hero analytics row ŌĆö four cards: This month paid (cobalt gradient with delta-vs-last-month pill), Pending payout (amber, with awaiting-approval count), Lifetime paid (green, with completed-payouts count), Earnings vs Deductions split (with a Net cumulative figure). All four read from `PaymentService::analytics()`, which is `Cache::remember('payroll_analytics', 60, ...)` so the row hits the database at most once a minute. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Table row ŌĆö employee name + employee_no, free-text description, amount in formatted currency (`en-GH` locale, two decimals, monospace), `StatusBadge`, paid-on date, and inline actions (view, mark paid).*

![Payment detail ŌĆö payslip body, statutory split, employer cost, remittance schedule](../assets/screenshots/18_payments/show.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Header card with a cobalt-and-gold hairline accent on the top edge, employee identity (name, employee_no), and a status badge. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Line items panel ŌĆö Earnings (green), Statutory Deductions (red, badged "GRA / SSNIT"), Voluntary Deductions (amber, includes Tier-3 and any custom voluntary lines), and a cobalt "Net Pay" card at the bottom that does the arithmetic in the browser from the `items` JSON. The split between statutory and voluntary is purely a UI affordance: deductions whose label matches `/^(SSNIT Tier 1|PAYE)/i` go to the statutory bucket, everything else falls to voluntary. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Sidebar ŌĆö Status, Amount, Created, Paid On, Processed By, plus a computed "Employer Cost" panel that calculates Tier-1 13% and Tier-2 5% from the Basic earning line (only shown when a "Basic" earning exists on the payslip), and a "Remittance Schedule" reminder card naming SSNIT-by-the-14th and PAYE-by-the-15th. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ "Mark as Paid" CTA in the masthead ŌĆö only rendered when status is `pending`; the same `PATCH /payments/{id}/paid` endpoint is also reachable from the index row.*

![Quick Payment slide panel ŌĆö simple four-field form](../assets/screenshots/18_payments/create.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Four fields only ŌĆö Employee (required select), Description (required free text up to 255 chars), Amount (required, two-decimal numeric, between 0.01 and 99,999,999.99), Currency (defaults to GHS, also offers USD/EUR/GBP). ┬Ę ŌØĘ "Create Payment" submits to `POST /payments` ŌĆö the resulting row is always created in the `pending` state with `processed_by` stamped from `Auth::id()`. No statutory deductions are computed; this panel is for one-off payments where the gross is already the agreed gross. ┬Ę ŌØĖ The same panel auto-opens when arriving from a dashboard Quick Action with `?new=1` in the URL ŌĆö the flag is stripped on mount so the panel doesn't re-trigger on refresh.*

![Generate Payslip slide panel ŌĆö Ghana statutory calculator + live preview](../assets/screenshots/18_payments/payslip.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Left pane (input form) ŌĆö Employee + Pay Period (YYYY-MM regex enforced), Basic Monthly Salary, an add/remove allowances list (default seeded with Transport + Rent/Housing), an SSNIT Tier-3 voluntary deduction field with its tax-deductible cap labelled live ("Deductible up to 16.5% of basic salary"), and an add/remove voluntary-deductions list. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Right pane (live preview) ŌĆö PAYE band-by-band breakdown, SSNIT employer 13% / employee 5.5% / Tier-2 employer 5%, the cap-aware Tier-3 figure, chargeable income, net pay, and employer total cost. The same arithmetic runs server-side in `PayrollCalculator::calculate()` so the preview never lies. ┬Ę ŌØĖ A "Mark paid" checkbox at the bottom decides whether the generated Payment row is created `paid` (with `paid_at = now()`) or `pending`. On submit the form POSTs to `/payments/payslip/generate`, the service writes the Payment + every PayrollItem inside a single DB transaction, and the user lands on the new payslip's detail page.*

> The four screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.18). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Payments index

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Quick Payment** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for creating a brand-new one-off Payment in the `pending` state. Four fields, single POST to `/payments`. | `payroll.manage` (typically hr_admin, finance_officer, super_admin) | One-off payments ŌĆö a reimbursement, an ex-gratia, a top-up ŌĆö are the most common reason anyone visits this page. The two-click path (open panel, submit) deserves the most prominent affordance. |
| **Generate Payslip** (secondary CTA) | Opens the Ghana payslip generator ŌĆö a two-pane slide panel with the statutory calculator on the left and a live preview on the right. POSTs to `/payments/payslip/generate` and lands the user on the new payment's detail page. | Same | One-off payments where HR wants to *show* the employee a statutory breakdown ŌĆö typically the first month's salary after a mid-month hire, a back-pay catch-up, or a contractor who insists on seeing the workings. The monthly batch path lives in Chapter 19. |
| **All Payments / Pending / Paid tab strip** | Three buttons that swap `localFilters.status` and re-fire the query. Filtering happens server-side in `PaymentService::list()`. | Anyone who can see the index | "What's still owed?" is the question almost every visitor asks first. A tab strip beats a dropdown for that question. |
| **Month picker** (top right of the tab strip) | A `<input type="month">` that posts the YYYY-MM string back to the controller; the service then `whereYear` + `whereMonth` on `created_at`. | Same | Bookkeeping is monthly; a quick "show me only May" is the second most-asked filter after status. |
| **Row click** (anywhere on the body) | Navigates to `/payments/{id}`. | `view` permission on that payment (the policy returns true for anyone with `payroll.view` or `payroll.manage`, and also for the employee whose Payment it is) | Whole-row hit target is faster than chasing a small "view" button. |
| **View** (eye icon) | Same as row click ŌĆö explicit affordance for screen readers and keyboard users. | Same | Accessibility ŌĆö a `<tr>` isn't natively focusable; the inline button is. |
| **Mark paid** (green check, only on `pending` rows) | PATCHes `/payments/{id}/paid`. Server-side, `PaymentService::markPaid()` flips status to `paid`, stamps `paid_at = now()`, and overwrites `processed_by` with the current user id. The page refreshes in place via `preserveScroll`. | `payroll.manage` | The most-clicked action on this page ŌĆö promotes it to a one-click row affordance rather than forcing a trip into the detail page first. |
| **Pagination** (bottom-right) | Standard Inertia pagination ŌĆö 20 per page, range and total shown at the bottom-left. Pagination is `withQueryString()` so filters survive paging. | Anyone who can see the index | Keeps the table responsive on institutes that issue hundreds of one-offs per month. |
| **Hero analytics row** | Four read-only cards. "This month paid" sums `Payment::where(status, paid)->where(paid_at, >=, startOfMonth)->sum('amount')`. Delta-vs-last-month is computed against the prior calendar month. "Pending payout" sums `status = pending`. "Lifetime paid" is the all-time `paid` sum. "Earnings vs Deductions" reads from `PayrollItem` (the per-payment payslip child rows). | Same | The four numbers every approver looks at first; surfacing them above the table is non-negotiable. |
| **Charts row** (Monthly Payroll Volume area chart + Status Breakdown donut) | Read-only ŌĆö twelve-month area chart of `paid` amounts grouped by `YearMonth(paid_at)`, plus a donut of all-status counts with colour-coded legend (pending amber, paid green, failed red, cancelled grey). | Same | The same "shape of the work" disclosure as the workforce stats band on Employees (Ch 3). Year-over-year comparisons live here. |
| **Top Earners insight row** | Read-only ŌĆö six highest-paid employees in the last six months by `SUM(amount)`, ranked 1ŌĆō6 with gold / silver / bronze badges on the first three. | Same | A one-glance answer to "who is most frequently paid through one-offs?" ŌĆö the same question the audit team asks at every quarter-end. |
| **Currency Mix card** | Read-only ŌĆö paid volume per currency. Most rows are GHS in practice, but USD / EUR / GBP are accepted on the create form and rendered here when used. | Same | Multi-currency payments are rare but real ŌĆö visiting consultants, conference reimbursements. The card makes them visible rather than buried in the table. |
| **Compliance strip** (SSNIT-14th + PAYE-15th deadlines) | Two read-only cards that compute the next SSNIT and PAYE remittance deadlines from today's date. Tone shifts from `ok` ŌåÆ `warning` (Ōēż 7 days) ŌåÆ `urgent` (Ōēż 3 days) ŌåÆ `overdue` (negative). | Same | The deadlines are statutory under Section 64 of the SSNIT Act and Section 116 of the Income Tax Act (Act 896). Surfacing them on the same page where payments live keeps the operator from forgetting them. The actual statutory schedules and the filing engine sit in Chapter 19; these cards are reminders, not workflow. |

> *Notes:* Every analytics number on this page comes from a single `PaymentService::analytics()` call, which is wrapped in `Cache::remember('payroll_analytics', 60, ...)`. A 60-second TTL means the screen can be reloaded freely without hammering the database; the trade-off is that a freshly-created or freshly-paid payment is reflected in the table immediately (the table is a separate query) but in the analytics row up to 60 seconds later. The cache is invalidated implicitly on the next minute boundary ŌĆö there is no manual bust today.

### Payment detail

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back** (top right) | Returns to the index. | Anyone viewing | Standard back affordance ŌĆö Inertia keeps the index's scroll position and any filters that were set. |
| **Mark as Paid** (green CTA, only on `pending` rows) | Same PATCH endpoint as the inline row button ŌĆö `/payments/{id}/paid`. Stamps `paid_at = now()`, flips status, overwrites `processed_by` with the current user id. | `payroll.manage` | Headline action on the detail page. Once paid, the button disappears. |
| **Header card** | Read-only ŌĆö employee identity (name, employee_no), payment description (the masthead `<h2>`), and a `StatusBadge`. The thin cobalt-and-gold hairline on the top edge is the only decorative element. | Anyone viewing | Identity at a glance; status above the fold; no scroll required. |
| **Line items panel ŌĆö Earnings** (only if `items` has earning rows) | Read-only ŌĆö every `PayrollItem` of `type = earning` linked to this payment, rendered in green with a per-line amount and a subtotal. Earnings include "Basic Salary" and every cash allowance line written by `generatePayslip()`. | Anyone viewing | The payslip workings, not just the net. The same numbers will sit on the printable PDF when the bulk-payslip-PDF emission lands (see "What's planned next" in Chapter 19). |
| **Line items panel ŌĆö Statutory Deductions** (only if `items` has rows matching the regex `/^(SSNIT Tier 1|PAYE)/i`) | Read-only ŌĆö SSNIT Tier-1 employee 5.5% and PAYE income tax bands, rendered in red with a "GRA / SSNIT" badge and a subtotal. | Anyone viewing | Visually separates the two non-negotiable deductions from the voluntary ones, so the employee sees what was taken at source vs what they consented to. |
| **Line items panel ŌĆö Voluntary Deductions** (only if `items` has deduction rows that are not statutory) | Read-only ŌĆö SSNIT Tier-3 voluntary, loan repayments, salary advances, SACCO, union, welfare, and any custom line written by `generatePayslip()`. Rendered in amber. | Anyone viewing | Same separation principle. The amber tone matches the "Pending payout" hero card ŌĆö every voluntary line is, by convention, an opt-in. |
| **Net Pay card** (cobalt gradient at the bottom of the line items panel) | Read-only ŌĆö `totalEarnings ŌłÆ totalDeductions`, computed in the browser from the `items` JSON. The card has the 5% gold-hairline accent above it and a radial glow in the top-right corner. | Anyone viewing | The one number every employee opens this page to see. The arithmetic is duplicated client-side so the page doesn't depend on a server-side calculation having been cached anywhere. |
| **No line items fallback** | When a Payment was created through the **Quick Payment** panel (which writes no PayrollItem children), the line items panel collapses to a single "No detailed line items recorded for this payment." card and the gross amount is shown in monospace below it. | Anyone viewing | Quick Payments are explicitly *not* payslips ŌĆö they're a single agreed amount. The UI says so out loud rather than rendering an empty payslip table. |
| **Sidebar ŌĆö Status / Amount / Created / Paid On / Processed By** | Read-only fact strip. `Processed By` only renders if the relation is loaded and non-null. | Anyone viewing | The five facts an auditor needs to see at a glance. |
| **Sidebar ŌĆö Employer Cost** (only renders when a "Basic" earning line is present) | Read-only ŌĆö computes Gross paid + SSNIT Tier-1 13% + SSNIT Tier-2 5% from the Basic amount, capped at GHS 61,000 (Maximum Insurable Earnings). | Anyone viewing | The same number Finance asks for every time HR books a Ghana payslip ŌĆö what does this *cost the employer* once SSNIT is added on top? Surfacing it next to the employee net pay closes the loop. |
| **Sidebar ŌĆö Remittance Schedule** (only renders when statutory deductions exist on this payment) | Read-only reminder card naming the SSNIT-by-the-14th and the PAYE-by-the-15th deadlines and what each one covers. | Anyone viewing | The same statutory reminder as the index compliance strip, scoped to this specific payslip. Belt-and-braces ŌĆö auditors approve. |

> *Notes:* The Show page loads the payment with `['employee.user', 'processedBy', 'items']` in one query (`PaymentController::show`); there is no N+1 between the line-items panel, the sidebar, and the employer-cost calculator. Salary visibility is gated at the `PaymentPolicy::view` layer ŌĆö anyone who is neither the employee on the row nor a holder of `payroll.view` / `payroll.manage` is bounced before the resource is rendered. There is no field-level salary mask here as on the Employees module (Ch 3) because the page is itself a salary disclosure ŌĆö the whole row either renders or doesn't.

### Quick Payment slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Employee** | required, must exist on `employees.id` | `payroll.manage` | Populated from `Employee::active()` ŌĆö terminated and on-leave staff are filtered out client-side. The dropdown shows "Name ┬Ę CIHRM-NNNN" so HR can disambiguate by employee number. |
| **Description** | required, string, max 255 chars | Same | Free text ŌĆö typically "May 2026 reimbursement", "Ex-gratia bonus", "Salary advance ŌĆö June period", or the conference / trip / receipt this is reimbursing. This is the only narrative attached to the row ŌĆö there is no separate `category` or `type` enum in the MVP. |
| **Amount** | required, numeric, between 0.01 and 99,999,999.99 | Same | The agreed gross. **No statutory deductions are computed.** If the operator wants statutory deductions, they pick "Generate Payslip" instead. |
| **Currency** | optional, 3-letter ISO code, defaults to GHS | Same | The form offers GHS / USD / EUR / GBP in the MVP. There is no FX conversion ŌĆö the analytics row shows the multi-currency split as separate bars rather than translating to a base currency. |

> *Notes:* `PaymentService::create()` is a one-line wrapper around `Payment::create()` with `status = pending` and `processed_by` stamped from the request. There is no transaction, no event, no audit dispatch in the MVP ŌĆö the absence of an event-driven audit hook is the single biggest gap on this module (see "Honest gaps" below). The panel closes on success and a flash toast confirms; the table refreshes via the back redirect.

### Generate Payslip slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Employee** | required, must exist on `employees.id` | `payroll.manage` | Same dropdown as Quick Payment. |
| **Pay Period** | required, string matching `/^\d{4}-\d{2}$/` (YYYY-MM) | Same | Drives the auto-generated description (`Payroll ŌĆō 2026-05`). There is no link to the `payroll_runs` row for the same period ŌĆö the per-payment payslip is independent of the batch run. |
| **Basic Monthly Salary** | required, numeric, between 0.01 and 99,999,999.99 | Same | The single input the calculator reads. SSNIT base = `min(basic, 61_000)`; the cap is applied before any of the three Tier rates are multiplied. |
| **Cash Allowances** | array; each `label` and `amount` are required together (`required_with`); label max 120 chars, amount numeric Ōēź 0 | Same | All cash allowances are treated as fully taxable income under the Income Tax Act (Act 896). The form pre-seeds two rows (Transport, Rent/Housing) because they're the two most-frequently-added; both can be deleted. |
| **SSNIT Tier-3 (voluntary, tax-deductible)** | optional, numeric Ōēź 0 | Same | The deductible cap is computed live as `basic ├Ś 16.5%` and shown under the field. Amounts above the cap are paid through but only the cap portion is included in the PAYE-chargeable reduction; the rest still comes out of net (matching the legal treatment). |
| **Voluntary Deductions** | array; each `label` and `amount` are required together; same validation as Cash Allowances | Same | Free-form deductions ŌĆö loan installments, SACCO, union dues, anything not in the four statutory buckets. They reduce net but not chargeable income. |
| **Mark paid** (checkbox at the bottom) | boolean, optional | Same | If ticked, the resulting Payment is created with `status = paid` and `paid_at = now()`. If unticked, it is created `pending` and the operator must come back and click "Mark as Paid" later. |

> *Notes:* `PaymentService::generatePayslip()` runs the calculator first (read-only, pure), then writes the Payment + every PayrollItem inside a single `DB::transaction`. The Payment description is auto-generated as `Payroll ŌĆō YYYY-MM`. PayrollItem rows are written in this order: Basic Salary (earning), one row per allowance (earning), SSNIT Tier 1 5.5% employee (deduction, negative), PAYE Income Tax (deduction, negative), Tier-3 if > 0 (deduction, negative), one row per voluntary deduction (deduction, negative). The Show page reconstructs the statutory-vs-voluntary split from the labels at render time ŌĆö there is no `category` column on `payroll_items`.

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores Payments in a single table with one child table for line items:

- **`payments`** ŌĆö one row per off-cycle payment. Carries `employee_id` (nullable, with `nullOnDelete` ŌĆö a terminated employee's payments survive their deletion), `processed_by` (the User id that last touched the row), `description` (free text, max 255), `amount` (decimal(12,2)), `currency` (string, default `GHS`), `status` (string, defaults to `pending`, cast to `PaymentStatus` enum on read), `paid_at` (nullable timestamp), and the standard Eloquent timestamps plus `SoftDeletes`. There is **no `reference` column, no `period` column, no `category` column**, and no `payroll_run_id` foreign key in the MVP ŌĆö the description carries the period as English ("Monthly Salary - May 2026"), and a one-off Payment is structurally independent of any Payroll Run.
- **`payroll_items`** ŌĆö one row per line item on a generated payslip. Carries `payment_id`, `label`, `type` (`earning` or `deduction`), and `amount` (signed ŌĆö deductions are stored as negative numbers so the sum-over-amount of every line item equals the payment's net pay).

The `Payment` model casts `status` to the `PaymentStatus` enum, which has four cases:

| Case | Value | Label | When it appears |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Pending` | `pending` | Pending | Default on create. Awaiting "Mark as Paid". |
| `Paid` | `paid` | Paid | Set by `markPaid()` or by `generatePayslip(mark_paid: true)`. Stamps `paid_at`. |
| `Failed` | `failed` | Failed | Reserved ŌĆö no UI path writes this in the MVP. The status exists on the enum so a future payment-gateway integration can flip the row when a Paystack/MoMo callback returns a hard failure. |
| `Cancelled` | `cancelled` | Cancelled | Reserved ŌĆö no UI path writes this either. The intent is "soft-delete with a reason"; today the only way out is `SoftDeletes` (via the model trait), and the slide-panel doesn't expose a Cancel button. See "Honest gaps" below. |

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **Currency is recorded but not converted.** A USD payment for an external trainer sits in the same table as a GHS payment for a salary advance. Analytics splits them per currency (the "Currency Mix" card) rather than translating to a single base. Reporting that needs a base-currency view today reads each currency separately.
- **`processed_by` is overwritten on every touch.** When a row is created it captures the creator; when it is marked paid it captures the *marker*. The two are often the same person but not always ŌĆö there is no separate `created_by` column. An audit team that wants to know "who created this and who marked it paid" must reach for the audit log (Chapter 24) once the dispatch is wired.
- **No `payroll_run_id` foreign key.** Payments and Payroll Runs (Chapter 19) are two completely separate tables. A per-payment payslip generated from this module does *not* appear on any Payroll Run, does *not* contribute to the cached totals on `payroll_runs`, and does *not* contribute to the GIFMIS journal voucher. It is a peer of the batch system, not a child of it.
- **Statutory deductions on a generated payslip are stored as PayrollItem rows, not as separate `paye_amount` / `ssnit_amount` columns.** The split between "what is statutory" and "what is voluntary" is purely a render-time decision on the Show page ŌĆö it regexes the PayrollItem labels. Any change to the label format will require a coordinated change to the render-time matcher.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Employees (Ch 3)** ŌĆö every Payment row points at one `Employee`, and `Employee::payments()` is the `hasMany` relation the Employee detail page (Ch 3, Payroll tab) reads to show the most recent eight payments. The Employee row's `user.name`, `employee_no`, and `department.name` are joined into the analytics row's "Top Earners" card.
- **Departments (Ch 15)** ŌĆö the analytics "Top Earners" card joins through the employee row to surface the department name. There is no per-department aggregation today ŌĆö the analytics are global.
- **Payroll Engine (Ch 19)** ŌĆö see "Distinction from Payroll Engine" below. The two modules share the `PayrollCalculator` (statutory bracket logic), the per-employee payslip lineage, and the legacy `Payment` model name, but they are structurally independent: a one-off Payment has no `payroll_run_id`, does not appear on the Payroll Run detail page, and does not flow through the dual-control approval workflow.
- **Loans (Ch 21)** ŌĆö loan disbursements are issued through this module today (per Ch 22's note: "Payments (Ch 18) ŌĆö off-cycle one-offs ŌĆ” run through `PaymentService` and reuse the same provider clients indirectly. They are not part of a batch and do not appear on the ledger; they get their own Payment record."). The dedicated loan-disbursement journal voucher and the loan-side ledger entries are out of scope until the loan-disbursement workflow lands in Phase 2.
- **Disbursements (Ch 22)** ŌĆö Payments are **off-cycle** by definition. They do not enter `BatchDisbursementService`, do not get a `disbursements` row, and do not contribute to the GhIPSS bank-credit file. Ch 22 explicitly carves them out: "they are not part of a batch and do not appear on the ledger".
- **Audit Logs (Ch 24)** ŌĆö the audit log is the same `audit_logs` infrastructure as every other module. Today, no event fires on Payment create or markPaid (see "Honest gaps"); the audit footprint of a one-off payment is the row itself plus the `processed_by` stamp.
- **Notifications (Ch 16)** ŌĆö `PaymentMarkedPaid` is in the bell's icon palette already (Ch 16 confirms: "PaymentMarkedPaid exists in the UI type palette but the producer hasn't shipped; the bell already knows how to render it"). When the producer is wired, the notification template, icon, and colour tag are ready.
- **Profile Portal (Ch 32)** ŌĆö employees see their own one-off Payments on the Payroll tab of their profile. The portal honours the same `PaymentPolicy::view` gate as the HR-facing detail page.
- **Reports (Ch 33)** ŌĆö `App\Exports\PayrollExport` reads from the `payments` table filtered by `YearMonth(created_at) = ?`, with headings `[Employee, Description, Amount, Currency, Status, Paid At]`. The export is reached from the Reports module's monthly-payroll card, not from the Payments index ŌĆö the Payments index has no explicit Export button in the MVP UI (see "Honest gaps").
- **Per-payment payslip mirror to OneDrive/Drive** ŌĆö `App\Events\PayslipGenerated` exists with the `UploadPayslipToCloud` listener (gated by the `mirror_documents_to_cloud` feature flag). The listener uploads to `CIHRMS/Payslips/{year}/{employee_no}/{filename}` when an integration is connected. **The event is never dispatched anywhere in the codebase today** ŌĆö Ch 19 explicitly calls this out: "the per-payment payslip lineage is the only path that has the cloud-mirror listener wired, but the producer is missing." See "Honest gaps" below.

## Distinction from Payroll Engine (Chapter 19)

This is the single most important section of the chapter. The two modules sit next to each other in the sidebar; they share the `PayrollCalculator`; they share the legacy `Payment` model name on certain join paths. They are not the same thing.

| Aspect | Payments (Ch 18) | Payroll Engine (Ch 19) |
|---|---|---|
| **Cadence** | One-off, on-demand. A row is created when an operator clicks "Quick Payment" or "Generate Payslip". | Monthly, batch. A `PayrollRun` is created for a (year, month, scope) and runs over every active employee at once. |
| **Trigger** | Manual ŌĆö operator opens the slide panel, fills the form, clicks Save. | Manual create ŌåÆ calculate ŌåÆ approve ŌåÆ paid lifecycle, with dual-control on approve (the creator can't approve their own run) and fresh-2FA on approve and reverse. |
| **Statutory deductions** | **Optional**. Quick Payment writes zero deductions; Generate Payslip writes the full Ghana statutory set (PAYE + SSNIT Tier-1 5.5% + Tier-3 voluntary if entered + every cash allowance as fully taxable income). | **Mandatory**. Every PayrollLine has the four statutory deductions (PAYE, SSNIT Tier-1 employee 5.5%, SSNIT Tier-1 employer 13%, NHIA 2.5% split, Tier-2 employer 5%) plus voluntary deductions with the 1/3 take-home floor. |
| **Approval workflow** | None. The single `markPaid` action flips status ŌĆö the same user who created it can mark it paid. | Dual control. `PayrollRunPolicy::approve` refuses if `created_by === user_id`; the route sits behind `2fa:fresh` middleware. |
| **Output files** | None today. The per-payment payslip can in principle be mirrored to OneDrive / Drive via `PayslipGenerated` + `UploadPayslipToCloud`, but the producer hasn't shipped. | Full statutory export ŌĆö GRA PAYE return CSV, SSNIT Tier-1 schedule, NHIA allocation, one Tier-2 schedule per active NPRA trustee, GhIPSS bank-credit file, plus the IPPD2/IPPD3 CAGD file and the GIFMIS sub-ledger journal voucher. |
| **Ghost-worker gates** | None. The operator selects an employee from the dropdown ŌĆö no identity-verification check, no zero-attendance gate. | Two hard gates before producing a line: `Employee::hasUsableIdentity()` (Ghana Card verified against NIA) and `AttendanceService::aggregatePeriod()` (days_worked + days_on_leave > 0). |
| **GIFMIS journal voucher** | Never generated. A one-off Payment does not contribute to any sub-ledger JV. | Minted on the Paid transition (when `payroll.gifmis.auto_mint_on_paid` is on) with the balanced double-entry that the GIFMIS bulk loader accepts. |
| **Disbursement file** | Never generated. The Finance team pays the employee through whatever rail they already use; the Payment row records the fact. | `MaterialiseDisbursements` listener creates one Disbursement row per PayrollLine on approval, ready for GhIPSS / MoMo push. |
| **Reversal** | None. The only way out is `SoftDeletes` from the console. | Full `reverse` lifecycle with a required reason Ōēź 10 chars, fresh-2FA, audit-log signature, and `PayrollRunReversed` event that fans out to the loan-repayment ledger. |
| **Use case** | Reimbursements, ex-gratia bonuses, salary advances, ad-hoc consultancy fees, one-time top-ups, hire-month proration, mid-period back-pay, loan disbursements (today). | The monthly salary register ŌĆö payroll for the institute, end to end, every month. |

The simplest rule for an operator: **if it happens every month for every employee, it's Payroll Engine. If it happens once, for one employee, with no statutory schedule waiting at the GRA portal, it's Payments.**

## Standards touchpoints

- **Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896), ┬¦116 ŌĆö Withholding tax on employment income (PAYE)** ŌĆö the per-payment payslip path runs the same `PayrollCalculator` as the batch engine, which transcribes the seven-band monthly PAYE schedule from Act 896 Sixth Schedule as amended for FY 2026 (0% to GHS 490, 5% to 600, 10% to 730, 17.5% to 3,896.67, 25% to 19,896.67, 30% to 50,416.67, 35% above). The compliance strip on the index page reminds the operator of the 15th-of-following-month deadline for filing and remittance. The actual filing happens through Chapter 19's GRA PAYE return generator ŌĆö this module does not file. See Chapter 44.
- **National Pensions Act, 2008 (Act 766), ┬¦3 ŌĆö Mandatory three-tier pension scheme** ŌĆö Tier-1 (5.5% employee + 13% employer to SSNIT), Tier-2 (5% employer to NPRA-licensed trustee), and Tier-3 (voluntary, up to 16.5% combined, tax-relieved) are all computed by the calculator on Generate Payslip. The SSNIT Maximum Insurable Earnings cap (GHS 61,000/month effective 1 Jan 2025) is honoured. The Tier-3 cap (16.5% of basic) is displayed live under the field. See Chapter 44.
- **Social Security and National Insurance Trust Act, 1991 (PNDCL 247), ┬¦64 ŌĆö Contribution remittance deadlines** ŌĆö the index compliance strip and the per-payment Remittance Schedule card both name the 14th-of-following-month SSNIT deadline. The actual SSNIT Tier-1 schedule generator that the contribution remittance is filed against sits in Chapter 19. See Chapter 44.
- **National Health Insurance Act, 2012 (Act 852), ┬¦29 ŌĆö NHIA contribution** ŌĆö the 2.5% portion of SSNIT Tier-1 employer that routes to NHIA is in the `PayrollCalculator` and is part of the Generate Payslip workings, but a one-off Payment does not by itself produce a separate NHIA allocation file ŌĆö that's the Chapter 19 statutory return generator's job. See Chapter 44.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦┬¦17, 18 ŌĆö Lawful processing of personal financial data** ŌĆö salary and bank-account information surfaced on the per-payment payslip is gated at `PaymentPolicy::view`. An employee can view their own Payments; another employee cannot. Bulk export through the Reports module (Ch 33) is behind a separate permission. See Chapter 26 and Chapter 44.
- **IFRS ŌĆö Expense recognition (matching principle)** ŌĆö every Payment row is an expense at the point it is created, not at the point cash leaves the bank account. The `status = pending` state is the accruals view; `status = paid` is the cash view. Until the GIFMIS journal hookup lands (Phase 2), the bookkeeping is done by the Finance team off-system. The Lifetime paid analytics card and the monthly volume area chart are the two operational views that approximate the accrual schedule for the period. See Chapter 44.
- **Electronic Transfer Levy Act, 2022 (Act 1075) ŌĆö E-Levy** ŌĆö out of scope for this module. E-Levy is applied at the disbursement layer (`BatchDisbursementService::E_LEVY_FALLBACK_RATE = 0.015`), and one-off Payments do not enter the batch disbursement service. If a one-off Payment is settled by mobile-money out-of-system, the levy is borne by the rail, not by CIHRMS. See Chapter 22 and Chapter 44.

## On the SequenceService reference convention

Every reference in the Finance build-out (F2 onwards) is minted by `App\Services\SequenceService::next($prefix, $padding, $year)`, which `lockForUpdate`s a counter row in the `sequences` table and atomically increments it. This was put in place after PR #21 closed the C3 race-condition gap that came from generating references with `Model::count() + 1` (per the `project_finance_sequences.md` memory entry). The AP Payment module (Chapter 20) mints `APP-2026-NNNN`, the AR Receipt module `ARR-2026-NNNN`, the Payment Intent module `PI-NNNNNN`, the Journal Engine `JE-NNNNNN` ŌĆö all through the same service.

**The Payments module does not use SequenceService today.** The `payments` table has no `reference` column at all. The user-facing identifier on the Show page is the integer primary key (`Payment #123`); the masthead breadcrumb on Show reads `Payroll / Payslip #{id}`; the row in the table has no reference column. This is fine for the MVP because the row's natural key is the (employee, description, amount, created_at) tuple ŌĆö the description carries the period as English text. But adding a `PAY-YYYY-NNNN` reference is the correct path forward, and when it lands it must go through `SequenceService::next('PAY', 4, now()->year)` rather than `Payment::count() + 1`. The convention is enforced by the audit pass that landed with PR #44 ("V2 audit complete" in the memory entries).

## Honest gaps

The Payments module is one of the oldest in the codebase and shows it. The gaps are:

- **No domain events fired on create or markPaid.** `PaymentService::create()` and `PaymentService::markPaid()` neither dispatch nor `event(...)`. The `PaymentMarkedPaid` icon and colour token already sit in the Notifications palette (Ch 16) waiting for a producer; the `audit_logs` table never sees a Payments-module row today. Wiring `event(new PaymentCreated(...))` and `event(new PaymentPaid(...))` is a one-day change.
- **`PayslipGenerated` event is defined but never dispatched.** The `App\Events\PayslipGenerated` class with the `UploadPayslipToCloud` listener exists, the integration manager is plumbed, and the `mirror_documents_to_cloud` feature flag is in `config/integrations.php` ŌĆö but no line of `app/` code calls `PayslipGenerated::dispatch(...)`. Until the dispatch is added at the end of `PaymentService::generatePayslip()`, no payslip PDF ever reaches OneDrive / Drive ŌĆö and there isn't a PDF emitter wired either. Ch 19 lists "bulk payslip-PDF emission" as Phase 1 work; the per-payment producer is part of the same arc.
- **No "Cancel" or "Mark failed" action in the UI.** The `PaymentStatus::Cancelled` and `PaymentStatus::Failed` cases exist on the enum but no route, controller method, or button writes them. The only way to retire a mistaken Payment today is `SoftDeletes` from a console command. A "Cancel with reason" affordance with a required Ōēź 10-char justification, modelled on Chapter 19's reverse flow, is the natural next step.
- **No `reference` column.** Auditors who ask "show me Payment PAY-2026-0042" are answered with "show me Payment #42" instead. See the SequenceService section above.
- **No per-employee filter in the index UI.** `PaymentService::list()` honours an `employee_id` filter (the controller passes it through), but the index page does not render an employee dropdown ŌĆö only status tabs and the month picker. The route accepts it via query string, so deep-linking from the Employee detail page would already work; the UI control just hasn't been added.
- **No Export button on the Payments index.** `PayrollExport` exists and is reachable from Reports (Ch 33), but the Payments page itself has no inline "Export this month" CSV button. The data is there; the affordance is missing.
- **Single permission gate.** Every action on the module ŌĆö viewing, creating, marking paid, generating payslips ŌĆö is behind the single `payroll.manage` permission. There is no separate `payroll.view` for read-only access in the UI today (the policy supports it, the route doesn't). HR and Finance both fall under the same gate; the duties-separation that Chapter 19 enforces between hr_admin (creates) and finance_officer (approves) is not enforced here.
- **No reversal trail.** If a Paid Payment turns out to be wrong, the operator soft-deletes it. There is no Reversed status, no required reason, and no reversal event ŌĆö the audit trail for a corrected payment is just two rows (the original, soft-deleted, and the new one). This is the single largest gap relative to Chapter 19's approach.
- **`processed_by` is overwritten, not appended.** The same column captures both the creator and the marker, so the row only ever remembers the last person to touch it. A separate `created_by` column (and an immutable one at that) is the right fix.
- **No link back to a Payroll Run.** If a per-payment payslip was generated for the same employee in the same period that the batch ran, nothing in the data model knows. A nullable `payroll_run_id` would let the Profile portal show "this is in addition to your May payroll" rather than rendering two unrelated rows.

These are all known and all small. The honest framing is: the module works for what HR uses it for today (reimbursements, ex-gratia bonuses, salary advances, the occasional ad-hoc consultancy fee), but the audit posture, reversal posture, and event-driven cross-module hookups are weaker than every other Wave-1 module.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap (see Chapter 46) lands four things directly on this module: **(1)** Wire `PaymentCreated` and `PaymentPaid` events (and `PaymentCancelled` when the Cancel action ships) so the audit log captures every state change; the listeners follow the same shape as `PayrollRunApproved`. **(2)** Wire `PayslipGenerated::dispatch(...)` at the tail of `PaymentService::generatePayslip()` so the existing `UploadPayslipToCloud` listener can mirror per-payment PDFs to OneDrive / Drive; the PDF renderer reuses the same composer that Chapter 19's bulk payslip work needs. **(3)** Add a `PAY-YYYY-NNNN` reference column on `payments` minted through `SequenceService::next('PAY', 4, now()->year)`, and surface it on the index table, the show masthead, and the Reports export. **(4)** Add a "Cancel with reason" action that flips status to `Cancelled`, records the reason on a new `cancelled_reason` column, stamps `cancelled_by` / `cancelled_at`, and refuses to fire on a `Paid` row (reversal of a Paid one-off goes through the eventual unified reversal workflow). Phase 2 adds the duty-separation between `payroll.create` and `payroll.approve` permissions so HR and Finance can be split, brings a nullable `payroll_run_id` foreign key onto the table so a per-payment payslip can be associated with the matching batch run, and adds the link in the Profile portal that says "this top-up is in addition to your May 2026 payroll line". Phase 3 promotes one-off Payments into the GIFMIS journal as a separate sub-ledger feed alongside the batch-run feed, so the Consolidated Fund picture stays balanced across both flows.

<!-- ===== 19_payroll_engine.md ===== -->

# Chapter 19 ŌĆö Payroll Engine

> *In one paragraph.* Payroll Engine is the period-locked, dual-approval machine that turns the workforce master (Chapter 3) into a balanced set of monthly statutory schedules and a single net-pay disbursement file. Each payroll run reads salaries, allowances, deductions, attendance, and loan repayments for one calendar month; computes PAYE under the Ghana Revenue Authority's 2026 monthly bracket, SSNIT Tier-1 at 13/5.5%, NHIA at the 2.5% routed split, Tier-2 at 5% per NPRA-licensed trustee, and any court-ordered or voluntary deductions with a 1/3 take-home floor; locks the result on a tamper-evident PayrollLine; and on approval (by someone other than the creator) regenerates the GRA PAYE return, SSNIT Tier-1 schedule, NHIA allocation, Tier-2 trustee schedules and a GhIPSS bank-credit file as side-effects. The same run can mint the Oracle IPPD2/IPPD3 upload file and the GIFMIS journal voucher for state accountants, with the JV refusing to export unless debits equal credits. This is the chapter that closes the gap analysis line "statutory payroll engine MISSING."

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Workforce & Finance** group ŌåÆ **Payroll Runs** (top-level item, badge icon). The legacy "Payroll" tile that previously pointed at one-off payments has been re-aimed at this index for any user with `payroll.view_all`; users without that permission still see the per-employee payslip view on their profile (Chapter 32).
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every run, every line, including the salary numbers.
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö creates runs, calculates and recalculates, downloads statutory returns; cannot approve their own runs (dual-control).
    - **finance_officer** ŌĆö approves and reverses; cannot create. Holds `payroll.approve`, `payroll.reverse`, `statutory.export`, `payroll.disburse`. Approval requires a fresh 2FA challenge.
    - **dept_head** ŌĆö sees only runs scoped to their department (`run.department_id` matched against `User::managesDepartment()`), read-only.
    - **employee** ŌĆö does not reach `/payroll-runs` directly; their payslip line surfaces on the Profile portal (Chapter 32) and through the mobile/API channel (Chapter 28).
    - **auditor** ŌĆö read-only across every run, every line, every statutory return ŌĆö same as the wider audit posture in Chapter 24.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö every line resolves salary, allowances, deductions, bank, SSNIT, Tier-2 trustee, Ghana Card identity from the employee row; Leave (Ch 4) ŌĆö paid leave days roll into attendance so the calculator never silently drops a day of pay; Attendance (Ch 5) ŌĆö the zero-attendance gate is the first line of defence against ghost workers; Loans (Ch 21) ŌĆö scheduled installments are picked up at calculate time and posted on approve; Benefits (Ch 23) ŌĆö voluntary deductions can carry benefit premiums; Disbursements (Ch 22) ŌĆö every calculated line materialises into a Disbursement row at approve time, ready to be pushed to GhIPSS / MoMo; Finance Hub (Ch 20) ŌĆö the GIFMIS exporter mints the balanced sub-ledger journal voucher; Audit Logs (Ch 24) ŌĆö every state transition (Draft ŌåÆ Calculated ŌåÆ Approved ŌåÆ Paid ŌåÆ Reversed) is event-sourced; Profile portal (Ch 32) ŌĆö employees see their own slice; Standards benchmark (Ch 44) ŌĆö every section of this chapter has a touchpoint there.

## The screens

![Payroll Runs index ŌĆö masthead, filter strip, table of runs](../assets/screenshots/19_payroll_engine/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Editorial-Sovereign masthead ŌĆö "STATUTORY PAYROLL" eyebrow, page title "Payroll Runs", a one-line subhead that names the legal anchor of every column ("Act 896 PAYE ┬Ę SSNIT ┬Ę Tier-2 ┬Ę NHIA ŌĆö dual-control approved, auditor-ready Ghana Audit Service packs"), and the cobalt "Create Run" CTA on the right. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Filter strip ŌĆö status dropdown (Draft / Calculated / Approved / Paid / Reversed) and a year input; both fire an Inertia partial reload on change with `preserveState`. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Table row ŌĆö monospace `PR-2026-05-ORG` reference, period label, scope (department name or "Whole organization"), status badge, line count with "+N skipped" if any were rejected, gross and net totals in GHS-formatted cedi, and an "Open" link.*

![Payroll Run detail ŌĆö stat-card band, action row, lines tab, returns tab](../assets/screenshots/19_payroll_engine/show.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Eight stat cards: lines processed (with "skipped" sublabel), gross, net pay, PAYE, SSNIT 5.5% (employee), SSNIT 13% (employer), NHIA (2.5% split), Tier-2 (5% employer). All denominated in GHS with two-decimal precision. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Action row ŌĆö "Calculate / Recalculate" (visible while Draft or Calculated), "Approve (2FA required)" (visible to a non-creator with `payroll.approve` once Calculated), "Mark as paid" (visible after Approved), "Reverse" (visible to anyone with `payroll.reverse` once Approved or Paid). ┬Ę ŌØĖ Tab bar ŌĆö Lines (paginated 50 at a time, with skipped rows highlighted in amber and their `skip_reason` shown inline) and Statutory returns (one row per generated file, with record count, total, and a download link). ┬Ę ŌØ╣ The reversal-reason textarea expands inline when the reverser is permitted; the reason is required, must be at least 10 characters, and ends up on the audit log and the StatutoryReturn re-generation provenance.*

![Create Payroll Run slide panel ŌĆö year, month, optional reason](../assets/screenshots/19_payroll_engine/create.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Three fields only ŌĆö Year (defaults to current), Month (defaults to current, 1ŌĆō12 validation), and an optional Reason textarea (1,000 chars max). ┬Ę ŌØĘ "Create draft" submits to `POST /payroll-runs`. The server enforces a unique constraint on `(period_year, period_month, department_id)` so the same scope cannot mint two open drafts. ┬Ę ŌØĖ A successful create redirects to the new run's show page in the Draft state, ready for the calculate action ŌĆö no period numbers are computed in the panel itself.*

> The three screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.19). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Payroll Runs index

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Create Run** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for creating a new draft run (year, month, optional reason). | `payroll.run` (hr_admin, finance_officer, super_admin) | Creating a run is the single most-used action ŌĆö it earns the headline position rather than hiding under a menu. |
| **Status dropdown** | Filters the table by `draft`, `calculated`, `approved`, `paid`, `reversed`. Refires the query immediately on change. | Anyone who can see the index | "What's still open?" is the question stakeholders ask before they ask anything else. |
| **Year input** | Restricts the table to one calendar year. | Same | Year-over-year comparisons and audit pulls both work cleanly when this filter is the first thing to set. |
| **Row Open link** | Navigates to the run's detail page. | `view` permission on that run (RBAC-checked in `PayrollRunPolicy::view`) | A row link beats hiding the action behind an icon ŌĆö the whole row is part of an audit trail. |
| **Pagination** (bottom-right) | 15 per page, `withQueryString()` so filters survive paging. | Anyone who can see the index | Keeps the table responsive even with five years of monthly runs accumulated. |

### Payroll Run detail

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back arrow** | Returns to the index. | Anyone viewing | Inertia preserves the scroll position and any filters that were set. |
| **Stat-card band** | Read-only ŌĆö eight totals pulled from the cached aggregates on the `payroll_runs` row (`gross_total`, `net_total`, `paye_total`, `ssnit_tier1_employee_total`, `ssnit_tier1_employer_total`, `nhia_total`, `tier2_employer_total`, plus a derived "voluntary" tile in some contexts). | Anyone viewing | These nine numbers are what every approver, auditor and finance officer looks at first; surfacing them above any drill-down is non-negotiable. |
| **Calculate / Recalculate** | POSTs to `/payroll-runs/{run}/calculate`. Server-side, `PayrollService::calculate()` deletes any existing lines, re-reads every active employee, runs the full statutory calculator, and persists fresh PayrollLine rows plus updated totals on the run. | `payroll.run` while the run is Draft or Calculated | A calculated run is recomputable until it is approved ŌĆö so a late allowance correction does not require throwing the run away. The transition is atomic (wrapped in `DB::transaction`) and refuses to run on Approved/Paid (`DomainException`). |
| **Approve (2FA required)** | POSTs to `/payroll-runs/{run}/approve` behind the `2fa:fresh` middleware. The service refuses if the approver is the same user as the creator (`DomainException: Dual-control violation`). On success: flips status to Approved, stamps `approved_by`/`approved_at`, posts each line's loan repayments through `LoanService::postRepayment`, and fires `PayrollRunApproved` ŌĆö which queues `GenerateStatutoryReturns` and `MaterialiseDisbursements`. | Non-creator with `payroll.approve`, while the run is Calculated | This is the legal sign-off: the statutory schedules and disbursement file are *not* generated until this happens. Forcing it through fresh 2FA means a stolen session cannot quietly approve a payment run. |
| **Mark as paid** | POSTs to `/payroll-runs/{run}/mark-paid`. Stamps `paid_at`, flips status to Paid, and fires `PayrollRunPaid` ŌĆö which (when `payroll.gifmis.auto_mint_on_paid` is on) triggers `MintGifmisJournal` to build the balanced GIFMIS JV CSV. | `payroll.approve`, while the run is Approved | Approval and payment are deliberately separate states. The money may move hours or days after the run is approved; the GIFMIS journal should post only when the cash has actually left the org bank account. |
| **Reverse** | Opens an inline textarea (reason, Ōēź 10 chars). POSTs to `/payroll-runs/{run}/reverse` behind `2fa:fresh`. Service flips status to Reversed, stamps `reversed_by`/`reversed_at`/`reason`, marks every PayrollLine as `reversed`, and fires `PayrollRunReversed`. | `payroll.reverse`, while the run is Approved or Paid | Mistakes happen ŌĆö a typo on a grade step, a late-arrived court order, a fraudulent enrolment caught after the fact. Reversal stops the bleed without rewriting history. The reason is required so the audit log has a real explanation, not "fixing it." |
| **Lines tab** | Paginated table (50 per page) of every PayrollLine in the run. Each row shows employee name + employee_no, grade/step, basic, allowance total, SSNIT 5.5%, PAYE, net, and status badge. Skipped lines highlight in amber and show their `skip_reason` ("Identity unverified ŌĆö Ghana Card validation required." or "No attendance recorded in N working days ŌĆö potential ghost worker."). | Anyone viewing the run | The lines tab is the audit pane. Every employee who was processed (or refused) is here, with the same numbers that flow into the GRA / SSNIT / NHIA / Tier-2 / GhIPSS files. |
| **Statutory returns tab** | One row per generated `StatutoryReturn` file ŌĆö PAYE return, SSNIT Tier-1 schedule, NHIA allocation, one Tier-2 schedule per assigned trustee, and the GhIPSS bank file. Each row exposes the record count, the total, and a "Download" link. | `statutory.export` for the download | Returns are generated as a queued side-effect of approval. Until they exist, the tab shows "Approve the run to generate statutory return files." |
| **Download IPPD export** | GET `/payroll-runs/{run}/ippd-export` ŌĆö regenerates the Oracle IPPD2/IPPD3 CAGD pipe-delimited file from current PayrollLine rows on every request. Header line "H" + one "D" line per employee + trailer "T" with totals; amounts in pesewas (integer ├Ś 100), names ASCII-transliterated, fixed-width columns padded to spec. | `statutory.export` | Public-service MDAs pushing monthly payroll into the Integrated Personnel & Payroll Database receive a file that exactly matches the CAGD spec ŌĆö same column count, same widths, same pesewa convention. Regenerating on every request means a stale download is impossible. |
| **Download GIFMIS export** | GET `/payroll-runs/{run}/gifmis-export` ŌĆö builds the balanced sub-ledger journal voucher (debits: salary expense, employer SSNIT, employer Tier-2; credits: net-pay payable, PAYE, SSNIT employee and employer split, NHIA, Tier-2, Tier-3, voluntary). Throws `RuntimeException` (422 with the residual) if total debits ŌēĀ total credits to two decimals ŌĆö that's a calculator bug, not a user error, and the state accountant should see it before it reaches GIFMIS. | `statutory.export` | The Ghana Integrated Financial Management Information System accepts bulk JV uploads from MDAs. The CIHRMS exporter emits the exact column set GIFMIS expects (`journal_id|line_no|gl_code|cost_centre|dr_amount|cr_amount|narration|period|source_doc|reference`) plus a trailer with the totals so the reconciler can verify balance before import. |

> *Notes:* The "Approve" button is gated by both `PayrollRunPolicy::approve` (which requires the user not to be the creator and the run to be Calculated) and the `2fa:fresh` middleware. The same gate covers reversal ŌĆö combined with the audit-log handler in Chapter 24, every approval and reversal is signed, dated, and reversible only by a non-self party with a fresh second-factor challenge. Calculate, recalculate, mark-paid and the export endpoints do not require fresh 2FA: they either don't move money or they don't change the run's legal posture.

### Create Payroll Run slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Year** | required, integer, 2000ŌĆō2100 | `payroll.run` | Defaults to the current calendar year via `new Date().getFullYear()` in the Vue form. |
| **Month** | required, integer, 1ŌĆō12 | Same | Defaults to the current month. There is no calendar widget ŌĆö payroll periods are explicit, not picked. |
| **Department (server-side only in the MVP)** | optional, must exist on `departments`; the unique constraint `payroll_runs_period_unique` over `(period_year, period_month, department_id)` blocks duplicate scopes | Same | The UI today creates whole-org runs (`department_id = null`); the unique index already permits one per-department run alongside the whole-org one, which is what Phase 2 will surface in the panel. |
| **Reason** | optional, string, max 1,000 chars | Same | Free-text justification ŌĆö useful for special / back-dated / supplementary runs. Flows into the `reason` column on the row and into the audit log. |

> *Notes:* `PayrollService::createDraft()` runs the insert inside a transaction, sets the human-readable reference (`PR-YYYY-MM-ORG` or `PR-YYYY-MM-Dn` for a department-scoped run), stamps `created_by`, and fires `PayrollRunStarted`. On success the panel closes and the user lands on the new run's detail page in the Draft state.

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores the payroll computation across **four** tightly-coupled tables, plus three reference tables that exist purely to keep historical runs reproducible:

- **`payroll_runs`** ŌĆö one row per (year, month, scope) period. Carries the lifecycle status, the dual-approval audit columns (`created_by`, `approved_by`, `reversed_by`, plus the matching `*_at` timestamps), nine cached totals (`gross_total`, `net_total`, `paye_total`, `ssnit_tier1_employee_total`, `ssnit_tier1_employer_total`, `nhia_total`, `tier2_employer_total`, `tier3_total`, `voluntary_deductions_total`), and counters for lines processed and skipped. The cached totals are rewritten on every `calculate()`; the source of truth remains the line rows.
- **`payroll_lines`** ŌĆö one row per (run, employee). Carries the resolved salary basics (`basic`, `allowance_total`, `gross`, `overtime_hours`, `overtime_pay`), the statutory deduction outputs (`ssnit_base`, `ssnit_tier1_employee`, `ssnit_tier1_employer`, `nhia_split`, `tier2_employer`, `tier3_employee`, `paye`), the voluntary-deduction total, the net, the full calculator snapshot as JSON in `breakdown`, the line status (`calculated` / `skipped` / `reversed` / `paid`), and a `skip_reason` when the gate rejected the employee. The `breakdown` JSON is what makes a closed run reproducible ŌĆö re-running tomorrow would read the same band rates from the historical effective intervals.
- **`statutory_returns`** ŌĆö one row per generated file (PAYE, SSNIT Tier-1, NHIA Split, one per Tier-2 trustee, and the GhIPSS bank file). Carries the kind, optional `trustee_id`, the on-disk `file_path` under `storage/app/returns/YYYY/MM/`, the total amount and record count, the generation timestamp, and submission tracking (`submitted_at`, `submitted_by`, `submission_reference`) so the auditor can see which schedules were filed and when.
- **`disbursements`** ŌĆö one row per (run, line). Materialised at approve time by `MaterialiseDisbursements` (Chapter 22), with the channel resolved from the employee's preference (`bank_transfer` / `mobile_money` / `cash` / `ghipss`), the E-Levy applied on MoMo channels per the Electronic Transfer Levy Act 2022 (Act 1075, 1.5%), and the net-to-recipient amount after the levy.

The three reference tables are effective-dated by design ŌĆö every calculator queries them with the run's period-end date, never "now":

- **`tax_brackets`** ŌĆö versioned PAYE bracket table per jurisdiction + cadence. The 2026 monthly seed has seven rows: 0% on the first GHS 490, then 5% / 10% / 17.5% / 25% / 30% / 35% bands ending at the open-ended top.
- **`statutory_rates`** ŌĆö versioned scalar rates and caps: `SSNIT_EMPLOYER` 13%, `SSNIT_EMPLOYEE` 5.5%, `NHIA_SPLIT` 2.5%, `TIER2_EMPLOYER` 5%, `TIER3_MAX_COMBINED` 16.5%, plus `MAX_INSURABLE_EARNINGS` of GHS 61,000/month (effective 1 Jan 2025, carried forward).
- **`pension_trustees`** ŌĆö NPRA-licensed corporate trustees for Tier-2. The seeder ships five active rows (Enterprise Trustees Ltd, Petra Trust, Glico Pensions, Standard Pensions Trust, Old Mutual Pensions Trust) with their NPRA licence numbers and the schedule format the trustee accepts.

### What the gross-pay calculation reads

For each active employee in scope:

| Input | Source |
|---|---|
| **Basic salary** | `Grade::baseSalaryFor($step, $periodDate)` if the employee has a current grade and step; falls back to the legacy `employees.salary` column. Resolved by `PayrollService::resolveBasicSalary()`. |
