Carries the human-readable `reference` (`JE-YYYY-NNNNNN` minted by `SequenceService::next('journal:<year>')`; reversal references use the `JR-` prefix and `journal_reversal:<year>` key), `entry_date`, narration, status (`JournalEntryStatus`: Draft / Posted / Reversed), `source_type` (`JournalSourceType`: Manual / VendorInvoice / ApPayment / ArInvoice / ArReceipt / BankAdjustment), polymorphic `source_id` pointing at the originating row, `reversal_of_id` when this entry is a reversal, audit columns (`created_by`, `posted_at`, `posted_by`, `reversed_at`, `reversed_by`).
- **`journal_lines`** ŌĆö one row per debit or credit. Carries `journal_entry_id`, `line_no` (1..n in display order), `gl_account_id`, `debit_amount`, `credit_amount` (exactly one of debit/credit is non-zero per line for a non-zero amount), per-line narration. The balance invariant is enforced at posting time by `JournalEntry::isBalanced()` ŌĆö `sum(debit_amount) == sum(credit_amount)` to two decimals.

### Accounts Payable

- **`vendors`** ŌĆö code (unique), name, tax id, status (`VendorStatus`: Active / Inactive / Suspended), contact details, three default-GL columns (`default_expense_gl_account_id`, `default_ap_gl_account_id`, `default_bank_account_id`), notes, soft-delete.
- **`vendor_invoices`** ŌĆö reference (`API-YYYY-NNNNNN`, unique), `vendor_id`, optional `vendor_invoice_no` (per-vendor unique when present), invoice date, due date, status (`VendorInvoiceStatus`: Draft / PendingApproval / Approved / PartiallyPaid / Paid / Cancelled), subtotal, tax_amount, total, `amount_paid`, currency, notes, `accrual_journal_entry_id` (FK to the JE that posted the accrual on approval), `cancellation_journal_entry_id`, audit columns. Soft-delete.
- **`vendor_invoice_lines`** ŌĆö line_no, description, quantity, unit price, tax rate, line total, tax amount, `gl_account_id` (expense GL).
- **`ap_payments`** ŌĆö reference (`APP-YYYY-NNNNNN`, unique), `vendor_id`, payment date, amount, currency, `org_bank_account_id`, narration, status (`ApPaymentStatus`: Pending / Processed / Voided), `external_ref` (nullable, indexed ŌĆö F5 Tier-1), `payment_journal_entry_id`, `void_journal_entry_id`, audit columns.
- **`ap_payment_invoice_allocations`** ŌĆö `ap_payment_id`, `vendor_invoice_id`, `allocated_amount` ŌĆö the many-to-many between payments and invoices.

### Accounts Receivable

- **`customers`** ŌĆö code (unique), name, tax id, status (`CustomerStatus`: Active / Inactive / Suspended), contact, three default-GL columns (`default_income_gl_account_id`, `default_ar_gl_account_id`, `default_bank_account_id`), notes, soft-delete.
- **`ar_invoices`** ŌĆö reference (`ARI-YYYY-NNNNNN`, unique), `customer_id`, optional `customer_invoice_no` (per-customer unique when present), invoice date, due date, status (`ArInvoiceStatus`: Draft / PendingApproval / Approved / PartiallyPaid / Paid / Cancelled / WrittenOff), subtotal, tax_amount, total, `amount_received`, currency, notes, `accrual_journal_entry_id`, `write_off_journal_entry_id`, audit columns. Soft-delete.
- **`ar_invoice_lines`** ŌĆö same shape as `vendor_invoice_lines` but the GL is an income account.
- **`ar_receipts`** ŌĆö reference (`ARC-YYYY-NNNNNN`, unique), `customer_id`, receipt date, amount, currency, `org_bank_account_id`, narration, status (`ArReceiptStatus`: Pending / Processed / Voided), **`external_ref`** (nullable, indexed ŌĆö F4 webhook + F5 Tier-1), `receipt_journal_entry_id`, `void_journal_entry_id`, audit columns.
- **`ar_receipt_invoice_allocations`** ŌĆö `ar_receipt_id`, `ar_invoice_id`, `allocated_amount`.

### Paystack gateway

- **`payment_intents`** ŌĆö `reference` (`PI-YYYY-NNNNNN`, unique), `customer_id`, `ar_invoice_id`, amount, currency, status (`PaymentIntentStatus`: Created / Pending / Success / Failed / Abandoned / Expired / Refunded), `paystack_reference` (unique ŌĆö initialized on first gateway call), `paystack_access_code`, `authorization_url`, `callback_url`, `last_paystack_response` (json), `expires_at` (+24h from initialization), `paid_at`, `ar_receipt_id`, `refund_paystack_ref`, `refund_settled_at`, soft-delete.
- **`paystack_webhook_events`** ŌĆö append-only audit log of every webhook delivery; `public $timestamps = false`. `paystack_event_id` UNIQUE (the idempotency guard), `event_type`, `paystack_reference`, raw `payload` (json), `received_at`, `processed_at`, `processing_error`, polymorphic links to the resulting `payment_intent_id` and `ar_receipt_id` once known.

### Bank reconciliation

- **`bank_statements`** ŌĆö `org_bank_account_id`, `statement_date`, `opening_balance`, `closing_balance`, `total_lines`, `reconciled_lines`, `format` (csv/ofx/mt940), `file_hash` (SHA-256, UNIQUE ŌĆö idempotency guard), `file_path`, `imported_at`, `imported_by`. Soft-delete.
- **`bank_statement_lines`** ŌĆö `bank_statement_id`, `line_no`, `transaction_date`, **`amount`** (signed ŌĆö positive = credit, negative = debit), `description`, optional `reference`, polymorphic `matched_type` (string class name: `App\Models\ApPayment` / `App\Models\ArReceipt` / `App\Models\JournalEntry`) + `matched_id`, `confidence` (high/medium/low/manual), `reconciled_at`, `reconciled_by`.
- **`bank_transaction_matches`** ŌĆö append-only audit log. `bank_statement_line_id`, `matched_type`, `matched_id`, `confidence`, `matched_at`, `matched_by`, `unmatched_at`, `unmatched_by`, `unmatched_reason`. Unlink stamps the unmatched columns rather than deleting the row.

### Reference generation

All Finance human-readable references (`APP-`, `API-`, `ARI-`, `ARC-`, `PI-`, `JE-`, `JR-`) are minted by `App\Services\Finance\SequenceService::next(string $key): int` against a `finance_sequences` table with `DB::transaction` + `lockForUpdate()`. The PR #21 fix replaced the legacy `count() + 1` pattern that could race under concurrent transactions. Existing keys: `app_payment`, `ap_invoice`, `ar_invoice`, `ar_receipt`, `payment_intent`, `journal`, `journal_reversal`. New finance services must inject `SequenceService` and use the same scheme; the migration `2026_06_11_000001_create_finance_sequences.php` seeds `current_value` from the historical MAX so the first call after deploy can never collide.

## The double-entry invariants

Three invariants make the rest of the system trustable. Every Finance audit and every dossier reader's confidence rests on these.

1. **Every journal entry is balanced at posting.** `JournalEntry::isBalanced()` returns true if `sum(debit_amount) == sum(credit_amount)` across its `journal_lines` to two decimals. `JournalPostingService::post()` refuses any entry where this is false, with a `DomainException` that names the residual. No business event in CIHRMS ŌĆö vendor invoice approval, AP payment record, AR invoice approval, AR receipt record, AR write-off, bank adjustment, manual JE ŌĆö can post without satisfying this check at the line level. Reversals are constructed by swapping debits and credits on the original, so they are balanced by construction.

2. **Every posted line moves `gl_account_balances` through `JournalPostingService` and nothing else.** The natural-delta formula is `Asset / Expense ŌåÆ debit ŌłÆ credit`; `Liability / Equity / Income ŌåÆ credit ŌłÆ debit`. The balance row is locked with `lockForUpdate()` inside the posting transaction; concurrent postings serialise on the lock. A missing `gl_account_balances` row is a domain error, not a silent zero-balance create ŌĆö the seeder is expected to populate one row per `gl_accounts` row at provisioning time. Reversal mints a new JE, posts it through the same `post()` (so it really mutates balances), then marks the **original** as Reversed; the reversal itself stays Posted because it is a real business transaction that contributed to the balance. The balance-invariant query is `whereIn('status', [Posted, Reversed])` for this reason.

3. **Every business event's source is reconstructable from its JE.** `journal_entries.source_type` (the `JournalSourceType` enum) plus `source_id` point back at the originating row. Going the other way, every AP/AR/payment-intent row carries a JE-id column for each event it triggered (`accrual_journal_entry_id`, `payment_journal_entry_id`, `void_journal_entry_id`, `write_off_journal_entry_id`, `cancellation_journal_entry_id`). The auditor can follow either direction ŌĆö JE to source, or source to JE ŌĆö without crossing into application-level inference.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Payroll Engine (Ch 19)** ŌĆö the payroll engine's GIFMIS journal voucher (one balanced JV per approved run; debits salary expense + employer SSNIT + employer Tier-2, credits net-pay-payable + PAYE + SSNIT employee/employer split + NHIA + Tier-2 + Tier-3 + voluntary) lands as a downloadable CSV today and as an auto-minted `journal_entries` row in Phase 2's `payroll.gifmis.auto_mint_on_paid = true` path. The exporter refuses to write a file whose debits don't equal credits to two decimals ŌĆö same invariant the Finance posting engine enforces. The Finance Hub's Statutory Compliance band surfaces the per-kind latest return so the close-of-period dashboard knows whether PAYE / SSNIT / NHIA / Tier-2 schedules have been filed.
- **Payments (Ch 18)** ŌĆö one-off payslip-style payments not tied to a payroll run land as an `ap_payment` with the `vendor_id` pointing at a synthetic "Internal ŌĆö Ad-hoc Payee" vendor; the journal is the same Dr AP-liability / Cr cash-at-bank shape.
- **Disbursements (Ch 22)** ŌĆö payroll disbursements (the `disbursements` table ŌĆö Chapter 22) materialise from approved payroll runs and carry the E-Levy split (1.5% on MoMo channels per the Electronic Transfer Levy Act 2022 (Act 1075)); when each batch clears, an `ap_payment` is recorded against the payroll-net-pay-payable GL and the corresponding org bank GL. Paystack also appears in Ch 22's outbound rail catalogue alongside Zoho for partner payouts.
- **Loans (Ch 21)** ŌĆö loan disbursement debits the loan-receivable GL (asset) and credits the funding-bank GL via a manual JE through `journal.post_manual`. Repayments collected by the payroll engine (`LoanService::postRepayment()` called during run approval) flip a portion of the receivable into cash through the same path.
- **Audit Logs (Ch 24)** ŌĆö `JournalEntryPosted`, `PaymentIntentCreated`, `PaymentIntentSucceeded`, `PaystackWebhookReceived`, `BankStatementImported`, `BankStatementLineLinked`, `BankStatementLineUnlinked`, `BankAdjustmentPosted` all dispatch with the actor identity, the affected reference, the run id where applicable, and the 2FA challenge id when behind `2fa:fresh`. They land in the immutable audit log.
- **Webhooks (Ch 27)** ŌĆö outbound webhooks for `vendor_invoice.approved`, `ap_payment.recorded`, `ar_invoice.approved`, `ar_receipt.recorded`, `payment_intent.succeeded`, and `bank_statement.imported` are dispatched through the `FanOutWebhooks` listener so a partner MDA's downstream ledger or a Big-Four auditor's continuous-audit pipeline can react in real time.
- **Profile portal (Ch 16)** ŌĆö customers who are individuals (members, certificate holders, training enrolees) see their own AR side from the Profile portal ŌĆö open invoices, paid receipts, payment links sent to them, statements they can self-generate.
- **Public API v1 (Ch 28)** ŌĆö `GET /api/v1/finance/journal/entries`, `/journal/entries/{id}`, `/ap-invoices`, `/ar-invoices`, `/payment-intents`, and `/reconciliation/statements` expose the ledger and the operational tables behind the `api.scope:finance:read` middleware; write endpoints are deliberately not in the MVP API surface ŌĆö every finance write must go through the Inertia UI or the internal service layer for now.

## Standards touchpoints

Forward references all go to **Chapter 44 ŌĆö Standards & Statute Index**.

- **IFRS ŌĆö general principles, IAS 1 (Presentation of Financial Statements), IAS 8 (Accounting Policies, Changes and Errors)** ŌĆö the journal posting engine implements the double-entry invariant at posting time; the `gl_account_balances` row is the natural-sign sum of every posted line and the balance-invariant query that reconciles the ledger reads `whereIn('status', [Posted, Reversed])` so reversal accounting matches IAS 8's expectation that errors are corrected by retrospective restatement rather than silent edit. The chart of accounts permanently retires codes on archive (the unique index includes soft-deleted rows) to preserve historical comparability. See Chapter 44.
- **IFRS 9 ŌĆö Financial Instruments / Expected credit loss** ŌĆö the AR write-off path posts a separate bad-debt-expense JE (Dr 5600 Bad Debt Expense, Cr AR) for the **outstanding residual** rather than reversing the original accrual, so revenue stays recognised and the impairment is recognised in the period the recoverability changed. The aging buckets (current / 1ŌĆō30 / 31ŌĆō60 / 61+ overdue against `due_date`) are the inputs an IFRS 9 ECL provisioning model would use; the explicit provisioning model is roadmapped in Phase 2. See Chapter 44.
- **GIFMIS ŌĆö Ghana Integrated Financial Management Information System sub-ledger JV format** ŌĆö the payroll engine's `GifmisJournalExporter` emits the pipe-delimited CSV that the GIFMIS bulk-loader accepts (`journal_id|line_no|gl_code|cost_centre|dr_amount|cr_amount|narration|period|source_doc|reference`) and refuses to write a file whose debits don't equal credits to two decimals; the GL code map is per-MDA via env vars under `config/payroll.php` ŌåÆ `gifmis.gl_codes`, so the same exporter serves every Controller & Accountant-General-mapped CAGD chart. Phase 3 promotes this from "download and upload" to a live REST push under `payroll.gifmis.auto_mint_on_paid = true`. See Chapter 19, Chapter 44.
- **CAGD ŌĆö Controller and Accountant-General's Department chart of accounts** ŌĆö public-sector MDAs adopting CIHRMS map their existing 5-segment CAGD chart (Fund / Function / Programme / Sub-programme / Source-of-funds + Object Code) onto the system's `gl_accounts.code` field; the env-driven GL code map for GIFMIS export is what carries the cross-reference. The seeded chart ships with the universal CAGD-aligned families (1xxx assets, 2xxx liabilities, 3xxx equity, 4xxx income, 5xxx expense) so a fresh install is operable on day one and gradually replaced by the MDA's authoritative chart through Edit + Archive. See Chapter 44.
- **Bank of Ghana ŌĆö Foreign Exchange Act 2006 (Act 723) and transaction reporting circulars** ŌĆö multi-currency posting is explicitly out of scope for the MVP (GHS-only); when Phase 3 introduces FX revaluation, the `currency` column on every Finance table is the hook, and the FX-gain/loss GL pair (4900 / 5900) is already in the seeded chart for the day the FX module turns on.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843), ┬¦┬¦17ŌĆō18 ŌĆö Lawful basis and data minimisation for vendor / customer personal data** ŌĆö when a vendor or customer is an individual (a sole-trader supplier, a member, a trainee), their `name`, `email`, `phone`, `address`, and `tax_id` are personal data under the Act. The Finance Resources do not expose them to viewers without the matching permission; the Profile portal slice for individuals is the same row, gated by `whenLoaded` on the relationships; bulk export (the customer statement print) is behind `statements.view` rather than `customers.view` alone. See Chapter 44.
- **Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921), ┬¦11 ŌĆö Internal controls and separation of duties** ŌĆö dual control on every approval is enforced at the service layer (`VendorInvoiceService::approve`, `ArInvoiceService::approve` both throw `DomainException: creator cannot approve own record`) and at the policy layer where applicable. Fresh 2FA is required on every cash-moving endpoint ŌĆö AP payment store and void, AR receipt store and void, AR write-off, manual JE store, payment intent creation, refund (single and bulk), and bank-adjustment post. The combination satisfies ┬¦11's separation-of-duties expectation for any disbursement against the Consolidated Fund. See Chapter 19, Chapter 44.
- **Electronic Transfer Levy Act, 2022 (Act 1075) ŌĆö E-Levy on MoMo disbursements** ŌĆö applied at 1.5% in `BatchDisbursementService` (sourced from `statutory_rates.E_LEVY_RATE`, fallback 0.015) on every MoMo-channel disbursement; bank-channel and GhIPSS-channel disbursements are not levied. The levy is computed and recorded against the Disbursement row separately from the net-to-recipient amount so the auditor can see who received less than their gross net because of the levy. See Chapter 22, Chapter 44.
- **Cheque Act, 1961 (Act 70) and Bills of Exchange Act, 1961 (Act 55)** ŌĆö bank reconciliation's three-tier matcher accepts cheque-line descriptions ("CHQ 100234" / "CHEQUE PAID 100234") via the Tier-2 substring search on `description ŌŖć reference`, so a cheque-only operating environment reconciles without operator intervention provided the AP payment's reference matches the issued cheque number.
- **ISO 27001 ŌĆö webhook signature verification** ŌĆö `VerifyPaystackSignature` middleware applies `hash_hmac('sha512', $rawBody, $secret)` with constant-time `hash_equals()` comparison; the raw body is read via `getContent()` BEFORE JSON parsing so a JSON-canonicalisation attack cannot fool the verifier. See Chapter 44.
- **ISO 30414 ┬¦┬¦4.8 / 4.9 (workforce cost, productivity)** ŌĆö the AP-outstanding tile, the AR-outstanding aging, and the Cash Position number all feed the institute-wide finance disclosure pack in Chapter 13 (Analytics). The line-level breakdown is what makes the disclosure defensible.

## Honest gaps

- **No operator UI for Paystack refunds outside super_admin.** `gateway.refund` is reserved for the super_admin wildcard in the RBAC seeder; the per-row and bulk refund modals are wired and tested, but the seeder grants the permission only to super_admin in the MVP. Phase 1 adds `gateway.refund` to `finance_officer` behind an explicit grant in the seeder.
- **No automatic intent-expiry cron.** `PaymentIntentService::expireStale()` exists and works (flips Pending intents past `expires_at + grace` to Expired), but is not yet scheduled. The operator filter on the Payment Links page surfaces the same set; Phase 1 wires a nightly `php artisan schedule:command` to call it.
- **No printable bank-rec report binding.** The Reconciliation detail page exposes "View / Print" and "Download PDF" buttons, the routes (`finance.reconciliation.print`) are wired, but the PDF template itself is a placeholder shell ŌĆö the full audit-ready layout (header, opening balance, line-by-line match table, adjustments summary, closing balance, signature block) is on the F5-era backlog.
- **No bulk operator actions on reconciliation.** "Accept all Tier-3 suggestions" and "Unlink all low-confidence matches" are deliberately out for F5 ŌĆö auto-linking at low confidence is already the default, so the bulk action is mostly a convenience for unlinking after a corrective import.
- **Manual JE reversal lacks a route.** `JournalPostingService::reverse()` is callable from service code (used by every cancel and void path in AP / AR), but there is no public route to reverse a manually-posted JE without going through the originating subsystem. The Phase 1 ledger-admin pack adds `POST /finance/journal/{entry}/reverse` behind `journal.post_manual` + `2fa:fresh`.
- **AP `external_ref` not auto-populated from GhIPSS.** F5's Tier-1 matcher reads `ap_payments.external_ref` but the column is operator-populated for AP today (manually keyed in, or back-populated when an operator links a bank statement line to an AP payment). The GhIPSS settlement-message bridge that populates it automatically lives in Disbursements' roadmap; until it lands, AP reconciliation is one or two manual links per batch rather than full auto-match.
- **MT940 parser is permissive.** Tested with a synthetic fixture in `tests/Fixtures/Finance/Statements/sample.mt940`; real-world bank exports vary widely in the `:61:` subfield ordering. The first production failure on a real MT940 will sharpen the parser; until then the import-time `StatementParseException` is the only safety net.
- **`ApPaymentService::void()` lacks `lockForUpdate` on the allocation rows.** `record()` correctly locks the target invoices; `void()` does not. The race is between a concurrent record-and-void on the same payment, which is a narrow window; the fix is on the next Finance hardening pass.
- **Multi-currency posting is GHS-only.** Every Finance table carries a `currency` column for the day FX revaluation comes online (Phase 3); for now the FormRequests refuse anything other than GHS at validation, and the cash-position query does not multiply by an exchange rate because there is no FX rate table yet.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the Finance roadmap (Chapter 46) closes the seven explicit gaps above in roughly the order they hurt the most: (1) **Operator refund grant** ŌĆö add `gateway.refund` to `finance_officer` behind a deliberate seeder change so the existing modal is reachable without super_admin escalation; (2) **Stale-intent cron** ŌĆö schedule `PaymentIntentService::expireStale()` nightly; (3) **Bank-rec PDF template** ŌĆö replace the placeholder with the audit-ready layout the auditor expects; (4) **Manual JE reversal route** ŌĆö the `POST /finance/journal/{entry}/reverse` endpoint behind `journal.post_manual` + `2fa:fresh`; (5) **AP external_ref auto-population** ŌĆö the GhIPSS settlement-message bridge writes back when a batch clears; (6) **Bank-fee adjustment chart pre-seeding** ŌĆö ship GL 5500 Bank Charges and GL 4500 Interest Income in the universal seeder so the adjustment modal works out of the box on a fresh install; (7) **Concurrency-safe `ApPaymentService::void`** ŌĆö add the missing `lockForUpdate` on the allocation rows. Phase 2 introduces IFRS-grade trial-balance and income-statement report packs (PDF + Excel) on top of the live `gl_account_balances`, an IFRS 9 ECL provisioning model that reads the aging buckets and writes a separate `bad_debt_provision` JE per period-end, and the GIFMIS auto-mint promotion that flips `payroll.gifmis.auto_mint_on_paid` to true for pilot MDAs. Phase 3 unlocks multi-currency posting with effective-dated FX rate tables, an FX-gain/loss recogniser per period close, and a Stripe + Flutterwave second-gateway pair that follows F4's exact pattern (store-layer idempotency UNIQUE, signature middleware, queued processor, delegation to `ArReceiptService::record()`) so the F4 architecture pays its dividend.

<!-- ===== 21_loans.md ===== -->

# Chapter 21 ŌĆö Loans & Advances

> *In one paragraph.* Loans & Advances is CIHRMS's staff lending desk. Employees apply against a curated product catalogue (salary advance, emergency, personal, education, vehicle, housing); HR or Finance approves under dual control; Finance disburses, and from that moment the loan rides the monthly payroll run ŌĆö one scheduled installment claimed per period, balance ticking down to zero, repayment paid off automatically. Termination doesn't leave a loose end either: any outstanding balance is netted against the off-boarding final settlement in the same database transaction that closes the case.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **Loans** (top-level item, `request_quote` icon). Visible only to roles that hold `loans.view` or `loans.apply` ŌĆö that excludes line managers and most operational staff by default.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö everything, every loan, every product. (The policy's `before` hook short-circuits on super-admin; CEO holds `loans.view` + `loans.manage` + `loans.product_manage`.)
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö full directory + product catalogue management. Can apply on behalf of any employee, but cannot approve their own initiations (`approve` is not in the hr_admin grant in the seeder).
    - **finance_officer** ŌĆö full directory + apply + **approve** + **disburse**. Finance is the dual-control countersignature on every loan and the only role that can release funds.
    - **employee** ŌĆö `loans.apply` only. They see their own loan list (via `LoanAccountPolicy::view` falling back to `employee.user_id === user.id`) and can submit a new application. They never see other employees' loans.
    - **manager / dept_head** ŌĆö *no* default access. The chain of command does not approve loans in this design; HR and Finance do.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Ch 3) ŌĆö every loan hangs off `employee_id`; Payroll Engine (Ch 19) ŌĆö the monthly run claims and posts scheduled repayments; Off-boarding (Ch 10) ŌĆö outstanding balance is settled out of the final settlement; Audit Logs (Ch 24) ŌĆö *not yet wired in* (see honest gaps below); Finance F1ŌĆōF5 (Ch 20) ŌĆö journal entries for disbursement and repayment are *not yet automatic* (see honest gaps).

## The screens

![Loans index ŌĆö masthead, KPI tiles, 12-month disbursement bars, portfolio donut, filter strip, loan cards](../assets/screenshots/21_loans/index.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ KPI tiles ŌĆö Active loans (count of `disbursed` + `repaying`), Pending approval, Disbursed (YTD) in GHS, and Total outstanding. The dollar amounts are short-formatted ("GHS 1.2M") so the tile stays readable. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Visual band ŌĆö a 12-month disbursement-trend `<LiveBars>` chart (cobalt with a gold peak highlight) plus an all-time portfolio composition donut (pending in gold, disbursed in cobalt, repaying in cyan, paid off in green, rejected in rose). The donut's centre shows the % of the portfolio that's actively repaying. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Filter strip ŌĆö debounced text search across `reference` and employee name (380 ms, like the rest of the app), status dropdown, product dropdown, one-click "Clear". ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Loan cards ŌĆö one per loan, grid layout, two columns on large screens. Each card shows initials, employee name, employee number, the product chip with annual rate badge, principal / monthly / term tiles, a progress bar for disbursed/repaying loans, and the applied date. Whole card is clickable; the "Open ŌåÆ" link inside is for explicit screen-reader / keyboard affordance. The card's left border is colour-coded by status from the shared `STATUS_PILL_REGISTRY`.*

![Loan detail ŌĆö hero card, four stat cards, action bar, schedule tab](../assets/screenshots/21_loans/detail.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero card ŌĆö large reference number (`LOAN-YYYY-NNNNN`), product name with annual-rate chip, employee chip with avatar, and three "quick amount" tiles (Principal ┬Ę Monthly EMI ┬Ę Outstanding) separated by vertical dividers. Below them, a slim repayment-progress bar that only renders when the loan is `disbursed` or `repaying`. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Four stat cards mirror the headline numbers (Principal, Monthly EMI, Outstanding, Months Remaining) with palette-aligned left borders. "Months Remaining" counts unpaid installments in the schedule, not calendar months ŌĆö so it stays accurate after a deferral. ┬Ę ŌØĖ HR/Finance action bar ŌĆö only renders if the current viewer's `can.approve` or `can.disburse` is true. Approve / Reject buttons show when status is `pending_approval`; Disburse shows when status is `approved`, with a `first_repayment_period` month picker beside it (defaults to next month if left blank). Both Approve and Disburse buttons carry a small "(2FA required)" subtitle ŌĆö the route is guarded by the `2fa:fresh` middleware. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Tab bar ŌĆö Schedule ┬Ę Repayments ┬Ę Audit. The Schedule tab is loaded by default.*

![Loan detail ŌĆö Schedule tab with current-period highlight and paid rows](../assets/screenshots/21_loans/schedule.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Schedule table ŌĆö one row per installment (1..term_months), with columns for the month label, EMI, principal portion, interest portion, balance after, and status. Sticky header so scrolling a 60-month mortgage schedule doesn't lose context. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Current-period row gets a blue tint and a "Current" pill ŌĆö that's the installment payroll is about to claim. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Paid rows get an emerald tint; missed / waived / deferred carry their own status pill via the shared `<StatusBadge>` component. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ The Repayments tab is the same table filtered to rows with `paid_amount > 0` or `status = paid` ŌĆö useful for the "what's actually been collected" view that auditors ask for. The Audit tab pairs Application metadata (applied by, applied at, purpose) with Approval/Disbursement metadata (approver, approved at, disbursed at) and a four-tile parameter strip (interest rate, term, total interest, total repayable). If the loan was rejected, the rejection reason renders in a red callout box.*

![Apply for Loan ŌĆö slide panel with product picker, principal/term, live quote](../assets/screenshots/21_loans/apply.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Information banner (cyan tint) ŌĆö "Applications are reviewed by HR and approved by Finance. Disbursed loans deduct automatically from monthly payroll under Labour Act ┬¦70 limits." This is the contract with the employee at submit time. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Product picker ŌĆö only shows products with `is_active = true`. Each option's label includes the annual rate ("Personal Loan ŌĆö 18.00% p.a.") so the employee compares before clicking. Selecting a product reveals a small info card with the product's min/max amount, min/max term, and amortisation method. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Live quote ŌĆö as soon as `product_id`, `principal`, and `term_months` are all set (on field blur), the panel POSTs to `loans.preview` and renders four tiles: Monthly installment, Total interest, Total repayable, Installments. The preview is purely server-side from `AmortizationCalculator` ŌĆö same code path the disbursement will use, so the numbers the employee saw on Tuesday are the numbers Finance will see on Friday. ┬Ę ŌØ╣ Submit posts to `loans.store`. If `principal` or `term_months` falls outside the product's window, the service throws a `DomainException` which the controller surfaces as a flash error ("Principal 80000 outside product limits [2000, 50000].") without losing the entered values.*

![Manage Loan Products ŌĆö slide panel (catalogue admin)](../assets/screenshots/21_loans/products.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Existing products table ŌĆö code, name, annual rate, amount range, status. Edit and delete buttons per row; delete is blocked server-side if any loan references the product (Finance must deactivate, not delete, in that case). ┬Ę ŌØĘ Create / edit form ŌĆö code, name, type, amortization method, min/max amount, min/max term, annual rate (decimal 0ŌĆō1 ŌĆö 0.18 = 18%), max DTI ratio, requires-guarantor / requires-collateral checkboxes, approvals-required count (defaults to 2 = dual control), effective-from / effective-to dates. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Only roles with `loans.product_manage` see the "Manage Products" button on the index page header.*

> The five screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.21). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Loans directory

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Apply for Loan** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel for a new application. Pre-populates `employee_id` to the actor's own employee record; HR can override it via the API to apply on behalf of someone else (the field is `nullable` in the FormRequest). | `loans.apply` | One-step submission. The form lives in the same page so the employee sees their existing loans next to the new application ŌĆö the friction of "go to a separate apply screen" is removed. |
| **Manage Products** (outlined button, top right) | Opens a separate slide panel listing all loan products and a create/edit form. | `loans.product_manage` (typically finance_officer, hr_admin, ceo, super_admin) | Pricing is a Finance decision; surfacing the catalogue inside the same page means a rate change does not require a separate admin tool. |
| **KPI tiles** | Active loans, Pending approval, Disbursed YTD, Total outstanding. Read-only. | Same RBAC scope as the table | The four numbers Finance asks at every monthly close, surfaced before scroll. |
| **Monthly disbursements** (`<LiveBars>` chart) | Cumulative principal disbursed in each of the last 12 calendar months. Peak month highlighted in gold. Hover reveals exact GHS. | Same | Spotting seasonality (end-of-year school-fees spikes, etc.) at a glance. |
| **Portfolio composition donut** | All-time distribution across pending / disbursed / repaying / paid off / rejected. Centre tile shows the % of all loans that are actively repaying. | Same | The single most-asked question at audit time ŌĆö "how much of the portfolio is live right now?" ŌĆö turned into one ring. |
| **Search box** ("Search reference or employeeŌĆ”") | Debounced free-text filter (380 ms). Matches `loan_accounts.reference` and `users.name` via the employee join. | Same | Most lookups are by `LOAN-2026-00042` or "find me Akua's loan". |
| **Status dropdown** | Filters to Pending approval / Approved / Disbursed / Repaying / Paid off / Rejected. | Same | The "show me what needs my attention right now" view. |
| **Products dropdown** | Filters the list to a single product. | Same | Useful for catalogue analysis ŌĆö "how many vehicle loans are live?". |
| **Clear** (appears only when filters are set) | Resets the three filters. | Same | One-click undo. |
| **Loan card** (whole card clickable) | Navigates to the loan detail page. Border colour reflects status. | View permission on that loan (RBAC-checked in `LoanAccountPolicy::view`) | Whole-card hit target plus an explicit "Open ŌåÆ" link for screen readers. |
| **Pagination** | Standard Inertia 20-per-page, query-string preserved. | Same | Keeps the page snappy on portfolios of hundreds of loans. |

> *Notes:* The donut, KPI tiles, and disbursement chart are all derived in `LoanAccountController::index` from the same queries; there is no separate analytics endpoint. The 12-month disbursement aggregate uses `DbExpr::yearMonth('disbursed_at')` so the query runs natively on both SQLite (tests) and PostgreSQL (production). The card border, the status pill, and the donut segment colour all read from the shared `STATUS_PILL_REGISTRY` so a future status palette change happens in one file.

### Loan detail ŌĆö header and hero

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Back arrow** | Returns to the directory; Inertia preserves the scroll and filter state. | Anyone viewing | Standard back affordance. |
| **Status badge** (top right) | Renders the loan's current status via the shared `<StatusBadge>`. | Same | One-glance state. |
| **Quick amount tiles** (Principal, Monthly EMI, Outstanding) | Read-only summary in monospace tabular figures. | Same | The three numbers everyone wants first. |
| **Repayment progress bar** | Renders only when status is `disbursed` or `repaying`. Width = (total_repayable ŌłÆ outstanding_balance) ├Ę total_repayable. Beneath it, "X installments paid ┬Ę Y remaining". | Same | Live progress against the schedule without scrolling to the table. |
| **Four stat cards** | Mirror the hero amounts plus a "Months Remaining" tile that counts unpaid installments (not calendar months ŌĆö so a deferral doesn't break it). | Same | Dashboard-style read at the top of the page; secondary palette accents pick out the human-side time accounting. |

### Loan detail ŌĆö HR / Finance action bar

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Approve Loan** (emerald CTA) | POSTs to `loans.decide` with `decision: approve`. The service transitions `pending_approval ŌåÆ approved`, stamps `approved_by` + `approved_at`, fires `LoanApproved`. **Refuses if the approver is also the applicant** ŌĆö dual control is enforced in the service, not just the UI. | `loans.approve` AND not the applicant (`LoanAccountPolicy::approve`) AND a fresh 2FA challenge has been completed within the configured window | The single most consequential click in the module. The 2FA gate matches `payroll.reverse` and `payroll.disburse` ŌĆö anything that touches money under dual control passes the same challenge. |
| **Reject** (red outlined button) | Reveals an inline reason textarea, then POSTs `decision: reject` with the typed reason. The service transitions to `rejected`, stamps `approved_by` + `approved_at` (yes, the rejector is recorded as the "approver" for audit symmetry), stores the `rejection_reason`. | Same RBAC as Approve ŌĆö the same `loans.approve` permission covers both outcomes. | Rejection is part of the same decision; forcing a separate "rejection" permission would just multiply seat-cost in the role matrix. |
| **Disburse Loan** (cobalt CTA) | POSTs `loans.disburse`. The service generates the full amortisation schedule (one `LoanRepayment` row per month), sets `disbursed_at`, `disbursed_amount = principal`, `outstanding_balance = total_repayable`, `first_repayment_period`, `expected_end_period`, transitions the loan to `disbursed`, and fires `LoanDisbursed`. Wrapped in a single DB transaction ŌĆö schedule and status flip together or not at all. | `loans.disburse` AND a fresh 2FA challenge | Disbursement is the moment money leaves the institute; same 2FA shield. The 60-row schedule for a 5-year loan is inserted in one transaction so a half-built schedule is impossible. |
| **First repayment period** (month picker) | Optional. Defaults server-side to the first day of the *next* calendar month. | `loans.disburse` | Allows Finance to time-shift the first repayment to the next pay run when a loan is disbursed mid-period. |

> *Notes:* Both action buttons live behind `L.can.approve` and `L.can.disburse` ŌĆö the `LoanAccountResource` injects these booleans by calling the policy server-side, so a user without permission *never sees the button*, not just gets a 403 on click. Dual control is enforced twice ŌĆö once in the policy (`approve` returns false if `applied_by === user.id`) and once in the service (`approve` throws `DomainException('Dual-control violation: approver must differ from applicant.')`). Either layer alone is enough; both is by design.

### Loan detail ŌĆö Schedule, Repayments, Audit tabs

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Schedule tab** (default) | Full amortisation table ŌĆö installment number, period (Mmm YYYY), EMI, principal, interest, balance after, status. Sticky header. Current-period row tinted blue with a "Current" pill; paid rows tinted emerald. | Anyone viewing | The single source of truth for what the loan owes and when. Auditors print this. |
| **Repayments tab** | Same row set filtered to `paid_amount > 0` or `status = paid`. Adds a "Posted at" column. | Anyone viewing | The "what actually got collected" view, distinct from "what's scheduled". |
| **Audit tab** | Two columns ŌĆö Application (applied by, applied at, purpose) and Approval/Disbursement (approver, approved at, disbursed at). Below, a four-tile parameter strip (interest rate, term, total interest, total repayable). If the loan was rejected, a red callout box renders the rejection reason. | Anyone viewing | The "paper trail" view auditors look at after the schedule. Note: this is the **booked** rate and method ŌĆö see "What the booking snapshot does" below. |

### Apply for Loan slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Loan product** | `required`, `integer`, `exists:loan_products,id` | `loans.apply` | Only active products appear in the dropdown. The product determines amortisation method, interest rate, and min/max windows. |
| **Principal (GHS)** | `required`, `numeric`, `min:1` | Same | Server-side then checked against the product's `[min_amount, max_amount]` window ŌĆö out-of-window throws a `DomainException` from `LoanService::validateApplication`. |
| **Term (months)** | `required`, `integer`, `min:1`, `max:360` | Same | Then checked against the product's `[min_term_months, max_term_months]`. 360 is the absolute outer cap (30-year mortgage) regardless of what the product permits. |
| **Purpose** | `nullable`, `string`, `max:1000` | Same | Free-text ŌĆö drives nothing automatic; visible to approvers as context. |
| **`employee_id`** (API-only) | `nullable`, `integer`, `exists:employees,id` | `loans.apply` AND has permission to apply on behalf (HR) | Not exposed in the UI dropdown; HR uses it to submit on behalf of an employee who's offline, illiterate, or in a satellite office. The applicant of record on the row is still the HR user (`applied_by`), so dual control still works ŌĆö Finance can countersign because Finance is not HR. |
| **Submit** | POSTs `loans.store` | Same | On success, redirects to the new loan's detail page with a flash success including the auto-generated reference. |

> *Notes:* The whole apply flow does **no eligibility checks beyond product window and active status**. There is no max-loan-as-multiple-of-salary rule, no no-outstanding-loans rule, no minimum-tenure check, no debt-to-income computation ŌĆö even though the product carries a `max_dti_ratio` column. The catalogue column is captured; the enforcement is not built. (See "Honest gaps" below.)

### Manage Loan Products slide panel

| Field | Validation | Who can set it | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Code** | `required`, `string`, `max:30`, unique | `loans.product_manage` | Short stable identifier (SA-001, EM-001, etc.) ŌĆö used in references and exports. |
| **Name** | `required`, `string`, `max:120` | Same | What employees see in the apply dropdown. |
| **Type** | `required`, one of the `LoanProductType` enum (`salary_advance`, `personal`, `emergency`, `education`, `housing`, `vehicle`, `other`) | Same | Drives the icon on the loan card and a couple of small UI hints. |
| **Min / max amount** | `min_amount` Ōēź 0; `max_amount` > 0 | Same | The window enforced by the apply flow. |
| **Min / max term (months)** | both `integer`, 1ŌĆō360 | Same | The term window. |
| **Annual interest rate** | `numeric`, 0ŌĆō1 (decimal ŌĆö 0.18 = 18%) | Same | Snapshotted to `booked_interest_rate` on the loan at application time, so a later catalogue rate change does not re-price the running loan. |
| **Amortization method** | `required`; `straight_line`, `reducing_balance`, or `flat_rate` | Same | Drives `AmortizationCalculator`. Straight-line = principal/n (interest-free). Reducing balance = PMT formula. Flat rate = total interest = P ├Ś r ├Ś years split evenly. The product UI today exposes only "Reducing balance" and "Flat" as options; "Straight-line" is reachable from the seeder / API. |
| **Max DTI ratio** | `nullable`, 0ŌĆō1 | Same | Captured; **not currently enforced** at application time. |
| **Requires guarantor / collateral** | booleans | Same | Captured; **not currently enforced** by the apply flow (no guarantor-consent UI, no collateral upload). |
| **Approvals required** | `integer`, 1ŌĆō5; default 2 | Same | Captured; the codebase currently does single-step approval regardless of this value. Dual control is implemented as "applicant ŌēĀ approver", not as a multi-step chain. |
| **Effective from / to** | `effective_from` `required`, `effective_to` `after:effective_from` | Same | Captured; the apply flow does not yet check effective window ŌĆö it only checks `is_active`. |
| **Active** | boolean | Same | When false, the product disappears from the apply dropdown but existing loans referencing it continue normally. |
| **Description** | `nullable`, `string`, `max:1000` | Same | Free-text shown nowhere in the apply UI today; intended for an info-popover in a future iteration. |
| **Delete** (trash) | Soft-deletes the product. **Blocked** if any loan references it ŌĆö Finance must deactivate instead. | Same | One-way safeguard. |

## The data behind it

CIHRMS stores three tables for loans, plus a guarantor-pivot stub:

- **`loan_products`** ŌĆö the catalogue. Code, name, type, amount window, term window, annual rate (decimal 4 dp), amortisation method, optional max DTI ratio, guarantor / collateral flags, approvals-required count, effective-from / effective-to dates, active flag, soft-delete. Six demo products ship in `DemoLoanProductSeeder`: Salary Advance (0% interest, 1 month, straight-line), Emergency (8% reducing, 3ŌĆō12 months), Personal (18% reducing, 6ŌĆō48 months, guarantor required), Education (12% reducing, 12ŌĆō60 months, guarantor required), Vehicle (16% reducing, 24ŌĆō60 months, guarantor + collateral), Housing (14% reducing, 60ŌĆō240 months, guarantor + collateral).
- **`loan_accounts`** ŌĆö one row per loan agreement against an employee. Reference (`LOAN-YYYY-NNNNN`, year-scoped sequential generated by `SequenceService::next("loan:{year}")` so concurrent applications cannot collide), employee FK, product FK (nullable ŌĆö survives product deletion), status (enum), principal, term in months, `booked_interest_rate` and `booked_amortization_method` (snapshotted from the product at booking ŌĆö a later product edit never re-prices a running loan), the calculated `monthly_installment`, `total_interest`, `total_repayable`, the live `outstanding_balance` and `installments_paid`, the workflow timestamps (`applied_by/at`, `approved_by/at`, `disbursed_by/at`), the dates (`first_repayment_period`, `expected_end_period`, `actual_end_date`), purpose, rejection reason, notes. Soft-deletes.
- **`loan_repayments`** ŌĆö one row per scheduled installment. Loan FK, installment number (1..term), `due_period` (always YYYY-MM-01), `scheduled_amount`, `principal_portion`, `interest_portion`, `balance_after`, `paid_amount`, status (`scheduled` / `paid` / `missed` / `deferred` / `waived`), `payroll_run_id` and `payroll_line_id` (filled in when payroll claims and posts the row), `posted_at`, notes. Unique constraint on `(loan_account_id, installment_no)`. Inserted in bulk at disbursement; mutated only through `LoanService::postRepayment` (called from payroll's run approval) or through off-boarding's settlement netting (which waives all remaining scheduled rows in one update).
- **`loan_guarantors`** ŌĆö pivot of `(loan_account_id, employee_id)` with `has_consented` and `consented_at`. The `LoanAccount::guarantors()` relation exists; no UI surfaces it yet (no guarantor-add panel, no consent-collection flow).

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **The booking snapshot is sacred.** When an application is filed, the calculator runs with the product's *then-current* rate and method, and those values are written to `booked_interest_rate` and `booked_amortization_method` on the loan row. If Finance edits the product the next day, every running loan keeps its original terms; only new applications see the new rate. The Audit tab's "Interest Rate" tile shows the booked rate, not the catalogue's current rate. This is the right behaviour for fair lending under Borrowers and Lenders Act ┬¦11 ŌĆö the rate disclosed at application is the rate the employee owes.
- **Disbursement is the irreversible step.** Up to "Approved", a loan can be rejected with no schedule, no outstanding balance, no payroll claim. The moment Finance clicks Disburse, the full amortisation schedule lands in `loan_repayments`, the loan flips to `disbursed`, and the next payroll run for the relevant period will pull the first installment into a payslip line. There is no "un-disburse" button ŌĆö a mistaken disbursement is corrected through Off-boarding-style settlement or a Finance journal entry, not a status flip.
- **Repayments are payroll-only.** There is no "Pay extra" button, no "Manual repayment" form, no MoMo-driven additional payments. Every repayment is `LoanService::postRepayment(repayment, payrollRunId, payrollLineId)`, called from `PayrollService::approve()` for every scheduled installment whose `due_period` matches the run's period. Early repayment, top-ups, and lump-sum payoffs are not built (see "Honest gaps").
- **Status flips are guarded by service-layer invariants.** `approve()` throws if the loan isn't `pending_approval`; `reject()` throws if the loan isn't `pending_approval`; `disburse()` throws if the loan isn't `approved`; `postRepayment()` is idempotent on already-paid rows (it returns the loan as-is, doesn't double-credit). The status enum is the contract; the service enforces it.
- **Dual control means applicant ŌēĀ approver, not 4-eyes-by-role.** The policy and the service both refuse the case where the same user applies and approves. They do not ŌĆö yet ŌĆö require approval by two distinct users (e.g. HR review then Finance countersign). The current implementation is "any Finance officer with `loans.approve` who is not the applicant". When HR applies on behalf, the *HR user* is `applied_by`, so a Finance officer (who is naturally not HR) can approve cleanly.

## How it talks to other modules

- **`LoanApproved` event** ŌåÆ fired at the end of every successful approve. Picked up by `FanOutWebhooks` and broadcast as `loan.approved` to webhook subscribers (reference, employee_id, principal, term_months, monthly_installment). No other listener; no internal side-effect.
- **`LoanDisbursed` event** ŌåÆ fired at the end of every successful disburse. Currently has no listeners ŌĆö no automatic finance journal posting, no notification, no analytics breadcrumb. The event is declared and dispatched for future use.
- **`LoanFullyRepaid` event** ŌåÆ fired by `postRepayment()` when the final installment is posted (balance hits zero or paid count hits term). Currently has no listeners ŌĆö same story.
- **Employees (Ch 3)** ŌĆö every loan FK's `employee_id` on Employee. The employee directory's profile detail page does not yet show a "Loans" tab; that's a planned addition. The `Employee` model has no convenience `outstandingLoans()` accessor today.
- **Payroll Engine (Ch 19)** ŌĆö the two-phase integration. At **calculate time**, `PayrollService::collectLoanRepayments($employee, $run)` finds every `LoanRepayment` whose `status = scheduled`, whose `due_period` equals the run's period (first of the month), and whose loan is `activeForRepayment` (i.e. `disbursed` or `repaying`). The rows are *not* mutated ŌĆö they're pulled into the line's `breakdown.loans.lines[]` as `{loan_reference, installment_no, amount, repayment_id}`. At **approve time**, the run iterates each calculated line's `breakdown.loans.lines`, looks up the `LoanRepayment`, and calls `LoanService::postRepayment($repayment, $runId, $lineId)`. That method flips the row to `paid`, sets `paid_amount = scheduled_amount`, `payroll_run_id`, `payroll_line_id`, `posted_at`, then row-locks the parent loan and decrements `outstanding_balance` by the scheduled amount, increments `installments_paid`, transitions to `repaying` (from `disbursed`) or `paid_off` (when balance Ōēż 0 or paid count Ōēź term). If the run is rolled back, the postRepayment is rolled back with it ŌĆö same DB transaction. This means the payroll module also enforces Labour Act ┬¦70 limits on net pay via its deduction floor (gross ├Ś 1/3 take-home minimum); loan repayments respect that floor and can be partially applied with the remainder deferred. See Chapter 19 for the deduction-priority chain and the floor mechanics.
- **Off-boarding (Ch 10)** ŌĆö when a final settlement is approved, `OffboardingService::closeOutstandingLoans()` row-locks every loan with status `disbursed` or `repaying` for that employee, marks every remaining `scheduled` `LoanRepayment` as `waived` with a note pointing back to the settlement, and updates the loan to `paid_off` with `outstanding_balance = 0` and `actual_end_date = today`. The "Outstanding Loan Balance" line on the settlement card is computed by `OffboardingService::outstandingLoanBalance($employee)` summing the `outstanding_balance` of those same active loans. The netting is automatic; there is no separate "settle loan" button ŌĆö finalising the off-boarding settlement *is* the loan settlement.
- **Finance F1ŌĆōF5 (Ch 20)** ŌĆö **no automatic journal entries**. Disbursement does not post a journal (Debit Loans Receivable / Credit Cash). Repayment posting from payroll does not post a journal (Debit Cash / Credit Loans Receivable, plus interest income). Loan settlement during off-boarding does not post a journal. The hooks exist (`LoanDisbursed`, `LoanFullyRepaid` events) but no listener is wired. This is a real gap and a Phase 2 priority ŌĆö see "Honest gaps" below.
- **Audit Logs (Ch 24)** ŌĆö the loan service currently writes nothing to the audit-log table. The status transitions are recorded on the row itself (timestamps + actor FKs), but there is no append-only, tamper-evident log entry per action. Webhook fan-out for `loan.approved` is the only durable external breadcrumb.
- **Webhooks** ŌĆö `loan.approved` is the only loan event broadcast today. `loan.disbursed`, `loan.repayment.posted`, and `loan.paid_off` are roadmapped (the events exist on the PHP side; the dispatcher mapping is the missing piece).

## Standards touchpoints

- **Borrowers and Lenders Act, 2008 (Act 773) ŌĆö interest-rate disclosure, fair lending.** The product catalogue is the institutional rate schedule, the apply flow shows the annual rate before submission ("Personal Loan ŌĆö 18.00% p.a."), and the booking snapshot guarantees that the rate disclosed at application is the rate the employee owes for the life of the loan. The full repayment schedule (principal, interest, balance after) is visible to the borrower on the loan's detail page from disbursement onwards ŌĆö Act 773 ┬¦11 requires this disclosure and the system makes it permanent. The act's *cooling-off* and *advertising disclosure* requirements are not implemented in the apply flow (no statutory wording on the panel, no cancellation window). See Chapter 44.
- **Bank of Ghana Lending Guidelines ŌĆö affordability and DTI.** The product schema captures `max_dti_ratio`, but the apply flow does **not enforce it**. There is no salary-to-installment ratio check at application time, no "you already have N outstanding loans" rule, no minimum tenure rule (e.g. must have completed probation). The Payroll module's deduction floor (net Ōēź gross ├Ś 1/3) provides a runtime safeguard against over-collection from a payslip, but it cannot prevent the loan being approved in the first place. This is the single largest gap in the module ŌĆö see "Honest gaps" and Chapter 44.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ŌĆö credit data as personal data.** Loan amounts, balances, and repayment history are personal data and are scoped accordingly: the directory only returns loans the viewer is entitled to (`LoanAccountPolicy::view` falls through to "the row is yours") and the loan detail page does not expose any data outside the employee + the actor's RBAC scope. The Act's ┬¦40 right-to-erasure provisions are not loan-specific ŌĆö soft delete on `loan_accounts` is the operational default; true erasure of historical lending data conflicts with audit-retention requirements and would be handled through the redaction job in Chapter 26.
- **IFRS 9 ŌĆö financial-instrument classification.** Loans are originated assets (held-to-collect), classified at amortised cost. The `loan_accounts.outstanding_balance` and the per-installment `principal_portion` / `interest_portion` split are the data foundation for an effective-interest revenue recognition schedule. The schedule is generated and stored; the **revenue-recognition journal entries are not posted** (see Finance integration above). The data is there for the auditor; the GL movements are not.
- **Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651) ┬¦70 ŌĆö limits on deductions from wages.** Payroll's deduction floor of gross ├Ś 1/3 (default, configurable on `PayrollService` construction) protects the employee's take-home from over-collection in any one month. Loan repayments respect the floor and can be partially applied with the remainder deferred (see Chapter 19's deduction-priority chain).

Forward to Chapter 44 for the full standards mapping.

## Honest gaps

This is what the team should know before claiming "Phase 2 loans is done":

1. **No affordability / DTI enforcement.** `max_dti_ratio` is captured per product but never checked. An employee earning GHS 2,000/month can apply for and have approved a Personal Loan with a GHS 1,800 monthly installment ŌĆö and the *only* thing that will protect them at payroll time is the Labour Act ┬¦70 floor. Bank of Ghana lending guidelines require pre-approval affordability checks; this is a Phase 2 must-do.
2. **No "no outstanding loans" check.** The same employee can hold an active Personal Loan, an Education Loan, and a Vehicle Loan simultaneously. The catalogue's guarantor / collateral flags are also captured but never enforced ŌĆö there is no guarantor-consent UI and no collateral-upload flow.
3. **No minimum-tenure check.** A brand-new hire on day one can apply for and receive a 60-month housing loan. Public-sector lending typically requires 6ŌĆō12 months of confirmed service before any non-emergency loan is eligible.
4. **Single-step approval, not configurable.** `approvals_required` is in the schema (defaults to 2 = dual control) but the apply flow is "applicant ŌēĀ approver" ŌĆö *one* countersigner, full stop. A future multi-step chain (HR review ŌåÆ Finance approval ŌåÆ Director sign-off for amounts over a threshold) is not built. The 2FA challenge is the second safety net, not a second approver.
5. **No automatic finance journal entries.** Disbursement, repayment posting, settlement netting, and write-offs all happen without touching the General Ledger. The `LoanDisbursed` and `LoanFullyRepaid` events exist but have no listeners. The auditor's question ŌĆö "show me how the loans-receivable balance on the trial balance ties to the sum of outstanding balances on the loan-accounts table" ŌĆö currently has no automated answer. This is the most operationally critical gap.
6. **No audit-log entries.** Status transitions (apply, approve, reject, disburse, post repayment, settle) write nothing to the tamper-evident audit log. The row-level timestamps and actor FKs survive on `loan_accounts` itself, but a deletion or hand-edit would erase them. The Auditor-General pack in Chapter 24 will need a hook.
7. **No early repayment, top-up, or restructure.** Once disbursed, the schedule is fixed. An employee who wants to pay off early, make an extra MoMo payment, or refinance from a 36-month at 18% into a 60-month at 14% has no in-product path ŌĆö Finance has to write a manual journal and then hand-update the loan row.
8. **No default handling.** The `LoanStatus::Defaulted` and `LoanStatus::WrittenOff` cases exist in the enum but no service method transitions a loan into them. There is no "30 days past due ŌåÆ Missed ŌåÆ 90 days past due ŌåÆ Defaulted" job, no `loan_repayments.status = missed` recovery flow.
9. **No guarantor workflow.** The `loan_guarantors` pivot exists, the relation is wired on the model, but there's no UI to nominate guarantors, no consent-collection flow, no notification to the guarantor, no "your guarantee is being called" path when a loan defaults.
10. **No employee-facing reports.** There's no "my outstanding balance" tile on the employee dashboard, no PDF statement of account, no schedule export. The detail page covers it, but the cross-module visibility is missing.
11. **No deduction-limit safety at apply time.** Payroll's gross ├Ś 1/3 floor saves the employee *per pay period*; it does not save the institute from approving a loan whose monthly installment, combined with the employee's other active loans and statutory deductions, will cause every single subsequent payroll run to defer.
12. **`loans.disbursed` webhook event is missing.** Only `loan.approved` is broadcast; the disbursement (the moment money moves) is silent to integrations.

The module is genuinely useful for the simple case ŌĆö Salary Advance, Emergency Loan, Personal Loan to a stable senior employee ŌĆö and the data model is comprehensive enough that fixing the above is mostly listener-wiring and FormRequest hardening, not a re-architecture. But until items 1, 5, and 6 land, the chapter should not claim "Bank of GhanaŌĆōcompliant lending".

## What's planned next

Phase 2 of the government-grade roadmap (8ŌĆō10 weeks, see the gap analysis) takes this module from "structured loan-tracking" to "compliant staff lending":

1. **Affordability gate** ŌĆö a `LoanEligibilityService` invoked from `ApplyLoanRequest::after()` (Laravel 10+ `after` validation closure) computes the employee's net basic salary minus statutory deductions minus existing loan installments and refuses the application if `(this_installment + existing_installments) / disposable_income > product.max_dti_ratio`. Also enforces no-outstanding-loans rule when set on the product, and a minimum confirmed-service-months rule pulled from the employee's `hire_date`.
2. **Finance journal hooks** ŌĆö three new listeners on `LoanDisbursed`, the existing `postRepayment` path (via a new `LoanRepaymentPosted` event), and the off-boarding `closeOutstandingLoans` path. Each posts a balanced journal via `JournalService` to the Loans Receivable, Cash, and Interest Income accounts mapped on the loan product.
3. **Audit-log hook** ŌĆö every status transition (apply, approve, reject, disburse, post, default, write-off, settle) writes an `audit_logs` row with actor, action, before/after JSON, and a hash-chained signature so a Phase 3 Merkle-tree extension can prove non-repudiation.
4. **Manipulable schedule** ŌĆö early repayment, top-up, and restructure paths. Early repayment computes the next-installment-only credit (no interest-pre-payment penalty in the MVP), top-up regenerates a new amortisation tail from today's principal and rate, restructure cancels the running schedule and creates a successor `LoanAccount` linked by `parent_loan_id`.
5. **Default detection job** ŌĆö a daily scheduled job marks `LoanRepayment` rows whose `due_period` is past and status is still `scheduled` as `missed`, escalates the loan to `defaulted` after a configurable threshold (default 90 days past due), and fires a `LoanDefaulted` event that notifies the employee, the guarantors, and Finance.
6. **Guarantor workflow** ŌĆö a panel on the application for nominating up to two guarantors from the employee directory, a notification + acknowledgement flow with click-through consent ("I, [name], understand I am being asked to guarantee a GHS X loan to [employee]ŌĆ”"), and a "call the guarantee" path that creates a deduction line on the guarantor's payroll when the loan defaults.
7. **Employee self-service surface** ŌĆö a "Loans" tab on the Profile portal (Ch 16) showing outstanding balances, next repayment date, full schedule, and a "download statement" PDF button. The dashboard tile follows.

The data model is largely ready for all seven; the bulk of the work is service-layer, FormRequest, and Vue UI.

<!-- ===== 22_disbursements.md ===== -->

# Chapter 22 ŌĆö Disbursements

> *In one paragraph.* Disbursements is the rail that moves real money out of the institute and into the hands of an employee, a vendor, or a settled loan account. The Payroll engine (Chapter 19) computes a net-pay figure; Disbursements is what turns that figure into a credit on a bank statement, a notification on a MoMo phone, or a row on a paper cheque register. Every disbursement is a per-attempt record ŌĆö channel, gross amount, the 1.5% E-Levy where the rail attracts it, a provider reference, a status, and a final settlement timestamp ŌĆö so the audit trail is one row per push, not one row per employee. The screen in this chapter is the **Disbursement Ledger**: a read-only queue where Finance watches MoMo and GhIPSS settlements happen, sees what failed and why, and reconciles against provider responses.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** Reached from the **Finance** dashboard (Ch 20) ŌĆö the Disbursement-channel card and the "Disbursement" quick-action tile both deep-link to `/disbursements`. There is no top-level sidebar entry in the MVP; the screen is a Finance utility, not a daily-driver dashboard. The Payroll module (Ch 19) also links to it from a per-run "Dispatch & Reconcile" footer (see "How it talks to other modules" below).
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö full view, plus the Dispatch and Reconcile actions.
    - **finance_officer** and **hr_admin** ŌĆö anyone with `payroll.view_all` can read the ledger; only those with `payroll.disburse` can press Dispatch or Reconcile.
    - **dept_head / manager / employee** ŌĆö no access. Disbursement is org-wide treasury; it is not scoped by department.
- **Related modules:** Payroll (Ch 19) ŌĆö every batch starts as an approved payroll run; Payments (Ch 18) ŌĆö one-off off-cycle payments share the same provider clients but go through `PaymentService` rather than `BatchDisbursementService`; Loans (Ch 21) ŌĆö loan-issuance disbursements use the same rails when they ship; Finance F1ŌĆōF5 (Ch 20) ŌĆö the journal voucher that mirrors a settled batch posts via `MintGifmisJournal` on `PayrollRunPaid`; Identity (Ch 25) ŌĆö high-value money movement is gated behind a fresh 2FA challenge; Audit Logs (Ch 24) ŌĆö every Dispatch and Reconcile click is logged.

## The screens

![Disbursement ledger ŌĆö stat band, channel filter, per-row provider reference](../assets/screenshots/22_disbursements/ledger.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Stat band ŌĆö four counters (Pending, Sent ŌĆö awaiting settlement, Settled, Failed) plus two cumulative tiles (MoMo settled YTD, E-Levy paid YTD). All numbers are scoped to the rows the signed-in Finance user can see ŌĆö which, today, means the whole institute. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Filter strip ŌĆö numeric "Run ID" input, channel dropdown (GhIPSS Bank ┬Ę MTN MoMo ┬Ę Vodafone Cash ┬Ę AirtelTigo Money ┬Ę Cash ┬Ę Cheque), and status dropdown (Pending ┬Ę Sent ┬Ę Settled ┬Ę Failed ┬Ę Reversed). Filters apply immediately on change; the URL updates so a link can be shared. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Table row ŌĆö payroll run reference (monospace), employee name + masked beneficiary account, channel chip (colour-coded), gross amount, E-Levy column (dash on non-MoMo rails), net to recipient, status badge, and the provider reference returned by MTN/Vodafone/AirtelTigo (also monospace). Failed rows tint pink and show the failure reason in red beneath the status badge.*

> The screenshot file referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.22). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Disbursement ledger (`/disbursements`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Run ID** (number input) | Filters the table to a single payroll run. Press Enter to apply. | `payroll.view_all` | The most common drill-down ŌĆö "show me the disbursements for the run we just approved". A numeric input rather than a search-by-name is intentional: the run reference (PR-YYYY-MM-NN) is what every other screen quotes, and pasting the ID is the fastest path. |
| **All channels** dropdown | Filters to one of the six channel values: `ghipss_ach`, `mtn_momo`, `vodafone_cash`, `airtel_tigo`, `cash`, `cheque`. | Same | Finance watches MoMo channels closely on month-end (because of E-Levy exposure and the spike in failure rates around the second-to-last business day) ŌĆö a one-click filter makes that easy. |
| **All statuses** dropdown | Filters to `pending`, `sent`, `settled`, `failed`, or `reversed`. | Same | "Failed" is the most-asked-for view; this is how. |
| **Row click / hover highlight** | The row tints on hover. Failed rows are already tinted pink. No click action ŌĆö the ledger is read-only. | Anyone with view | Disbursements aren't edited by hand; they're modified by provider webhooks and by the Dispatch/Reconcile commands. A non-actionable row is the correct affordance. |
| **Status badge** | Reads `pending` (slate), `sent` (amber), `settled` (emerald), `failed` (rose), or `reversed` (slate). | Same | Visual scan of a 50-row page should let an operator answer "is this run done?" in under two seconds. |
| **Provider reference column** | The opaque identifier returned by the rail: `MOMO-{uuid}` (MTN), Vodafone `transactionId`, AirtelTigo `transactionId`, or the deterministic `GHIPSS-{run_id}-{disbursement_id}` for ACH. Empty (`ŌĆö`) until the row leaves Pending. | Same | When a payee disputes a credit, this is the string Finance quotes to the provider's support desk. |
| **Pagination** (bottom) | 50 rows per page. Standard Inertia links. | Same | A monthly payroll run with 1,200 employees produces 1,200 rows; pagination keeps the page snappy. |

> *Notes:* The ledger does **not** expose `Dispatch` or `Reconcile` buttons in the MVP UI. Both actions are wired as named routes (`disbursements.dispatch` and `disbursements.reconcile`) and are reachable from the Payroll-run detail page (Chapter 19), where it makes sense to dispatch *this run's* disbursements rather than picking a run from the ledger. The named routes both require `payroll.disburse` and a fresh-within-15-minutes 2FA challenge (`2fa:fresh`).

### Dispatch run (`POST /disbursements/runs/{run}/dispatch`)

| Field / behaviour | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Trigger** | A button on the Payroll-run detail page (Ch 19) ŌĆö typically labelled "Dispatch disbursements". POSTs to this route with the `PayrollRun` model in the URL. | `payroll.disburse` + `2fa:fresh` (a 2FA challenge within the last 15 minutes) | Money movement is a high-value gate; the 2FA refresh stops a stolen session from emptying the treasury. |
| **Effect** | `BatchDisbursementService::dispatch($run)` iterates every Disbursement row in status `pending` for that run, looks up the provider for the row's channel, calls `provider->send($d)`, and updates the row with the returned status, provider reference, and raw response. Each row's update is its own transaction ŌĆö one row's transport error does not back out the others. | Same | Idempotency at the row level: re-running Dispatch after a partial failure picks up only the still-pending rows. |
| **Response** | A flash message: "Dispatched: N sent, M failed, K skipped (manual channels)". K is the count of Cash + Cheque rows ŌĆö they have no automated provider and are intentionally skipped. | Same | A single sentence is enough ŌĆö Finance opens the ledger immediately afterwards to see the breakdown row by row. |
| **Failure modes** | Provider HTTP non-2xx, connection timeout (15 s default), token-acquisition failure (MTN OAuth + AirtelTigo OAuth + Vodafone HMAC), or a missing beneficiary account / MSISDN. Each is captured into `failure_reason` on the row and the row goes to `failed`. | Same | Failure is a row-level fact, not a batch-level fact. A failed MoMo number does not stop the next employee's GhIPSS transfer from being staged. |

### Reconcile run (`POST /disbursements/runs/{run}/reconcile`)

| Field / behaviour | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Trigger** | A button on the Payroll-run detail page (Ch 19) ŌĆö typically labelled "Reconcile with providers". POSTs to this route with the `PayrollRun` model in the URL. | `payroll.disburse` | Reconciliation is read-mostly (it polls providers) so it does not require a fresh 2FA challenge, only the disburse permission. |
| **Effect** | `BatchDisbursementService::reconcile($run)` finds every Disbursement row in this run that has been `sent` for at least 5 minutes (i.e., long enough for the provider to have moved it) and calls `provider->refreshStatus($d)` for each. If the polled status differs from the persisted status the row is updated to `settled` or `failed`; the provider's raw response is saved into `provider_response`. | Same | The webhook path (see below) is the primary signal; this poll is the fallback for when a webhook is lost, throttled, or arrives at a server that's mid-deploy. |
| **Response** | A flash message: "Reconciliation: N disbursement(s) updated.". | Same | Same one-sentence pattern as Dispatch ŌĆö Finance opens the ledger to see what moved. |

## The data behind it

Two things are stored. The first is per-employee preference; the second is per-attempt history.

**Employee preference** lives on three columns added to the `employees` table by the disbursement migration:

- `disbursement_channel` (string, default `ghipss_ach`) ŌĆö which of the six rails the employee is paid through.
- `mobile_money_number` (string, nullable) ŌĆö the MSISDN; required if the channel is one of the three MoMo values; ignored otherwise.
- `mobile_money_network` (string, nullable) ŌĆö kept for cross-checking when a number is portable across networks; not currently used by the providers (they each only accept numbers from their own network).

**Per-attempt history** is the `disbursements` table itself. One row is created for every (`payroll_run_id`, `payroll_line_id`) pair ŌĆö a retry produces a new row, not an update to the prior one. The row carries:

- **Routing** ŌĆö `payroll_run_id`, `payroll_line_id`, `employee_id`, `channel`, `status`.
- **Money** ŌĆö `gross_amount` (the net pay being sent out), `e_levy` (the 1.5% on MoMo channels, zero otherwise), `provider_fee` (currently always zero, reserved for the day a rail charges us), and `net_to_recipient` = `gross_amount ŌłÆ e_levy ŌłÆ provider_fee`.
- **Beneficiary** ŌĆö `beneficiary_account` (a bank account number for GhIPSS, an MSISDN for MoMo channels) and `beneficiary_name`.
- **Provider tracking** ŌĆö `provider_reference` (the rail's opaque ID for the transfer) and `provider_response` (the last full JSON the rail returned, stored verbatim for forensics).
- **Lifecycle** ŌĆö `sent_at`, `settled_at`, `failed_at`, `failure_reason`, and `retry_count`.
- **Soft delete** so a Disbursement record never vanishes from the audit trail.

The five statuses are exhaustive:

- **`pending`** ŌĆö the row exists (it was materialised when the payroll run was approved) but the rail hasn't been called yet.
- **`sent`** ŌĆö the rail accepted the instruction. The money has not necessarily landed yet ŌĆö MoMo settlements are asynchronous and can take seconds to minutes; GhIPSS settles overnight.
- **`settled`** ŌĆö confirmed by the rail. For MoMo, this means the provider reported `SUCCESSFUL`/`SUCCESS`/`COMPLETED` on the webhook or the polled status. For GhIPSS, it means the bank-statement reconciliation step (out of scope for v1) flipped the row.
- **`failed`** ŌĆö the rail rejected the instruction or reported a settlement failure. `failure_reason` is always populated.
- **`reversed`** ŌĆö a settled disbursement that was later clawed back. Currently set only by the operator console; no public surface in v1.

Three things every reader of this screen needs to keep in mind:

- **Disbursements are not edited by hand.** The ledger is read-only. State transitions are driven by `BatchDisbursementService` (dispatch, reconcile) and by the configured webhook handlers (`PaystackWebhookController` for finance-gateway events, and the per-MoMo-provider webhooks that flip rows to `settled` or `failed` via the same provider clients).
- **One row per attempt.** If a MoMo push fails because the recipient's wallet is closed, a Finance operator who fixes the MSISDN and re-triggers will get a **new** Disbursement row, not a mutation of the failed one. The old row stays as evidence; the new one carries the retry.
- **The E-Levy is real money.** It comes off the gross before the net is sent, and it is paid to the Ghana Revenue Authority through the MoMo provider's own settlement file ŌĆö CIHRMS does not separately remit it. The Disbursement row records exactly what we deducted, so when GRA's quarterly E-Levy reconciliation arrives, the institute can prove the per-employee math.

## The six channels

`DisbursementChannel` is the enum at `app/Enums/DisbursementChannel.php`. Six values, three behaviours:

| Channel | Value | Automated? | E-Levy? | Provider client |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **GhIPSS Bank Transfer** | `ghipss_ach` | Bulk file (not REST) | No | `GhIpssAchProvider` ŌĆö generates a per-row token, then `GhIpssBatchFileBuilder` produces the CSV the sponsor bank uploads. |
| **MTN MoMo** | `mtn_momo` | Yes (OAuth2 + REST) | Yes (1.5%) | `MtnMomoProvider` ŌĆö MTN MoMo Open API, sandbox/production switched by config. |
| **Vodafone Cash** | `vodafone_cash` | Yes (API key + HMAC) | Yes (1.5%) | `VodafoneCashProvider` ŌĆö B2C `/b2c/send`; signs every body with HMAC-SHA256. |
| **AirtelTigo Money** | `airtel_tigo` | Yes (OAuth2 + REST) | Yes (1.5%) | `AirtelTigoProvider` ŌĆö `/disbursement/v1/transfer`. |
| **Cash** | `cash` | No | No | None ŌĆö Dispatch skips these and Finance pays the employee in person from petty cash. |
| **Cheque** | `cheque` | No | No | None ŌĆö same as Cash; the cheque is written off-system. |

`DisbursementChannel::isAutomated()` returns true for the first four; `BatchDisbursementService::dispatch()` skips any row whose channel has no registered provider (which is how Cash and Cheque pass through quietly).

### What "wired" means today

The four automated providers are all **coded** ŌĆö they each implement `DisbursementProvider` (the contract at `app/Services/Disbursement/Contracts/DisbursementProvider.php`), and the `DisbursementServiceProvider` binds the `BatchDisbursementService` singleton with the providers that have `enabled => true` in `config/disbursement.php`. The default config state:

- **`ghipss_ach`** ŌĆö `enabled` defaults to **true**. Bulk-file generation works against any disk; the sponsor's sort code and originator name are read from env (`GHIPSS_SPONSOR_SORT_CODE`, `GHIPSS_ORIGINATOR_NAME`). The artisan command `php artisan disbursement:ghipss-export {run}` writes the CSV; `--print` streams it to stdout for smoke-testing.
- **`mtn_momo`** ŌĆö `enabled` defaults to **false** (`MOMO_MTN_ENABLED=false`). Setting the four env vars (`MOMO_MTN_BASE_URL`, `MOMO_MTN_SUBSCRIPTION_KEY`, `MOMO_MTN_API_USER`, `MOMO_MTN_API_KEY`) and flipping the flag to true is all that's needed to engage the live API.
- **`vodafone_cash`** ŌĆö `enabled` defaults to **false**. Three env vars (`MOMO_VF_BASE_URL`, `MOMO_VF_API_KEY`, `MOMO_VF_SIGNING_SECRET`).
- **`airtel_tigo`** ŌĆö `enabled` defaults to **false**. Three env vars (`MOMO_AT_BASE_URL`, `MOMO_AT_CLIENT_ID`, `MOMO_AT_CLIENT_SECRET`).

The Paystack stack (`PaystackGatewayService`, `PaystackWebhookProcessor`, `PaystackWebhookController`) is also present in the codebase, but it is the **inbound** rail for the Accounts Receivable hosted checkout (Finance F4 ŌĆö Ch 20) ŌĆö customers paying invoices, not employees being paid. It does not push money out and is not bound into `BatchDisbursementService`. The Zoho webhook (`ZohoWebhookController`) is a CRM contact-sync receiver, not a disbursement channel. The mention of "Paystack/Zoho disbursement" in the gap-analysis preamble is therefore best read as "infrastructure for webhooks is in place" rather than "Paystack and Zoho are payout rails" ŌĆö the actual payout rails are the four channels above.

## E-Levy ŌĆö how it is computed and disclosed

The **Electronic Transfer Levy Act, 2022 (Act 1075)** levies 1.5% on electronic transfers, including B2C mobile-money payments. CIHRMS applies it at materialisation time, not at dispatch time.

The mechanism is in `BatchDisbursementService::materialise()`:

1. The service asks `StatutoryRate::lookup('E_LEVY_RATE', $run->period_end)` for the effective rate on the run's last day. Statutory rates are effective-dated rows on the `statutory_rates` table (the same machinery that drives SSNIT, NHIA, and Tier-2 percentages ŌĆö Ch 19).
