| **Report card** (Headcount / Leave Summary / Payroll Export / Ticket SLA / Staff Turnover) | Selects the report ŌĆö the card gains a cobalt ring + check, the detail panel below repaints with the matching breakdown, and the export panel switches its filter set (year for Leave; month for Payroll; none for the other three). | `reports.view` | A click-to-select grid is faster than a dropdown when there are only five options, and the sparkline + metric on each card answers "is this report worth opening?" before you commit. |
| **Card sparkline** | 200├Ś50 inline SVG, gradient-filled area under a stroked line. For Headcount it's the top-6 departments by active count; for Leave it's the year-to-date breakdown by leave type; for Payroll it's the last 5 months of paid totals; for Tickets it's resolved-count by priority; for Turnover it's the last 11 months of terminations. The series is computed by `ReportsController::previews()` and cached server-side for 60 seconds under the key `reports_previews`. | Same | Survey-the-shape before opening the export. The cache key isn't user-scoped so a heavily-loaded org still only computes the previews once a minute, period. |
| **Detail panel ŌĆö horizontal bars** | Same `previews` payload, re-skinned as labelled bars (12-column grid: 3 label / 7 bar / 2 value). The bar gradient matches the card accent so the visual rhyme is preserved. | Same | The card's tiny sparkline tells you the shape; the detail panel tells you the names ŌĆö which department has the most staff, which leave type dominates the year, etc. ŌĆö without leaving the Reports page. |
| **Year dropdown** (only for Leave Summary) | Four-option dropdown ŌĆö current year and the three before it. Persists in component state; doesn't fire a request until Download is clicked. | Same | Audit periods are annual; backfilling a past year is a one-click trip back. |
| **Month picker** (only for Payroll Export) | Standard `<input type="month">`. Defaults to the current YYYY-MM. | Same | Payroll's natural unit is a month ŌĆö anything finer (a pay period) is reachable from the Payroll module itself (Ch 19). |
| **Download {Report}** (gradient CTA) | Anchor tag (not a form post) to `GET /reports/export?type={key}&year=...&month=...`. The endpoint instantiates the matching `App\Exports\*` class and returns an XLSX via `Maatwebsite\Excel\Facades\Excel::download()`. Filename is `{type}-report-YYYYMMDD.xlsx`. | Same | Anchor + query string means the browser handles the download natively ŌĆö no JS download blob, no progress bar to maintain. |
| **(All other reports)** placeholder | When the selected report has no additional filters, the export panel shows the italic "This report has no additional filters. Export captures the full current dataset." | Same | Removes the doubt that there's something missing to configure when the filter slots are empty. |

> *Notes:* The five report cards are all that ship in the MVP. There is no "build your own report" canvas yet ŌĆö that's a Phase 1 ask (saved report definitions, parameterised query builder, scheduled email delivery). The `previews` cache is busted only by TTL ŌĆö there is no explicit invalidation on create/update of the underlying tables, which is fine for a 60-second window but does mean a brand-new hire won't show up in the Headcount sparkline for up to a minute. The Headcount card carries the gold accent because of the masthead policy ŌĆö only the institute's two flagship surfaces (Dashboard and Reports) are allowed the 5% gold tint, and Headcount is the headline of Reports.

### Auditor-General Report Pack (`/reports/auditor-general`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Fiscal Year** input (number, default = current year) | Sets the `year` field on the form. Validated server-side as integer between 2000 and 2100. | `reports.view` to see the page; `statutory.export` to actually submit | Year-end is when the AG comes ŌĆö the field accepts a past year so an auditor can re-pull last year's pack any time. |
| **Jurisdiction** dropdown | One option in the MVP ŌĆö Ghana. The form submits a `jurisdiction=GH` field that the pack writes into MANIFEST.md. | Same | Future-proofs the pack for a possible second jurisdiction (UK Charity Commission, for example) without changing the API. |
| **Generate pack (2FA required)** (primary CTA) | POSTs the form to `/reports/auditor-general`. The route stack is `permission:statutory.export` + `2fa:fresh` ŌĆö the second middleware rejects the request unless a TOTP code has been confirmed in the last 5 minutes. On success, `AuditorGeneralReportPack::generate()` builds the ZIP at `storage/app/ag-reports/AG-{year}-{Ymd-His}.zip` and the page re-renders with the new pack on top of the list. | `statutory.export` + fresh 2FA | This is the single most consequential export the system produces ŌĆö it goes to a constitutional officer. Both gates are intentional and non-removable. |
| **Existing packs table** | One row per ZIP file in `storage/app/ag-reports/`. Columns: file name (monospace), size (KB/MB), generated-at timestamp, Download link. Sorted newest-first. The "No packs generated yet" empty state is the only thing that renders when the directory is empty. | `reports.view` to see; `statutory.export` to download | Auditors re-request the same pack weeks later. Re-downloading shouldn't require re-running a 30-120-second generation. |
| **Download** (per-row link) | Anchor to `/reports/auditor-general/download/{filename}`. The controller defensively validates the basename against a `/^AG-\d{4}-\d{8}-\d{6}\.zip$/` pattern before touching the disk ŌĆö no path traversal, no escape from the `ag-reports/` directory. | `statutory.export` | Downloads from anywhere on the Existing Packs list. The filename pattern is also the pack's natural key ŌĆö sortable, parseable, no collisions. |
| **What's bundled** card (gold hairline + 8 green checks + 1 amber info) | Read-only ŌĆö exact list of what the pack contains. The amber "info" row makes the Whistleblower Act 720 segregation explicit on the screen, not buried in documentation. | Anyone with the permission to see the page | Auditors should never have to guess what's in the box. The card mirrors the actual file layout (`payroll/`, `statutory/`, `identity/`, `loans/`, `offboarding/`, `whistleblower/`, `audit/`). |

> *Notes:* The pack itself is built by `App\Services\Reports\AuditorGeneralReportPack` and can also be generated from the command line via `php artisan reports:auditor-general --year=2026 --jurisdiction=GH` ŌĆö the controller and the command share the same service. Every file inside the ZIP is independently SHA-256 hashed and the hash list lives in `MANIFEST.md` at the root of the ZIP. The `audit/chain_verification.txt` file is the raw output of `php artisan audit:verify-chain` ŌĆö if the audit chain has been tampered with the auditor sees the failure directly rather than a sanitised "all good" message. The pack ships at version 1.0; the version string lives at `AuditorGeneralReportPack::VERSION` and the manifest carries it for forward-compatibility.

### Dashboard (`/dashboard`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Module switcher** (Overview / Employees / Tickets / dept portals) | URL-driven (`?module=...`); each click does a `router.get` with `preserveState: true` so the dashboard shell stays mounted and only the inner slot re-fetches. Department portals are guarded server-side by per-portal permissions (`portal.it`, `portal.hr`, `portal.marketing`, `portal.finance`) so direct-URL guessing is harmless. | `dashboard.view` plus the relevant portal permission | One Vue component, four lazy-loaded panes ŌĆö keeps the initial bundle 600 lines lighter than rendering everything up-front. |
| **Executive donut rings** (Workforce / Service load / Leave backlog) | Three SVG donuts driven by `stats.employees`, `stats.openTickets`, `stats.pendingLeave`. Each maps to a 0-100 scale against a fixed cap (1,500 / 50 / 25). Colour shifts cool ŌåÆ amber ŌåÆ red as the percent crosses 50 / 80. Workforce stays cyan because it's a capacity gauge, not a stress gauge. | `dashboard.view` | One-glance answer to "is anything on fire?" ŌĆö colour does the talking before the numbers do. |
| **KPI sparkline cards** (Active Staff / Open Tickets / Pending Leave / Pending Payroll) | Each card carries a deep-link `href` (Employees / Tickets / Leave with `?status=pending` / Payments), a current count from `stats`, and a 30-day sparkline from `sparkSeries`. The sparkline data is `AnalyticsEvent` rows grouped by `DATE(created_at)` (works on SQLite, MySQL, Postgres ŌĆö no driver split). | Same | Click-through to the source module is the whole point ŌĆö the dashboard is a launchpad, not a destination. |
| **Live activity feed** | A 5-row rotating window over the latest 12 analytics events. Each row pulls icon + colour from a static `EVENT_PRESENTATION` table on the service. New events appear within 60 seconds of being fired (the listener is queued on the `analytics` queue and the page polls in via Inertia partial reload). | Same | Real-time pulse, kept on-page so the dashboard doesn't feel like a static snapshot. |
| **Finance snapshot** (only for `finance_officer`, `super_admin`, `ceo`, `hr_admin`) | A `RoleFinance.vue` block with a treasury-bulletin header, payroll-runs-by-status totals, paid-YTD net, disbursement queue health (pending / sent / settled / failed), pending payments amount, and a "Statutory due posture" mini-block (generated / submitted / overdue, where "overdue" is >7 days since `generated_at` with no `submitted_at`). Cached for 60 seconds under the key `dashboard.finance`. | Roles listed | A finance officer hits refresh constantly ŌĆö caching these SUM() queries for a minute keeps the dashboard responsive without losing meaningful freshness. |
| **Manager snapshot** (only for `manager`, `dept_head`) | `RoleManager.vue` ŌĆö team size, active count, pending-leave inbox (8 latest, sortable on the page), open tickets for direct reports (8 latest). Scoped by `Employee.manager_id = currentUser.employee.id`. Cached for 60 seconds under `dashboard.manager.{userId}`. | Roles listed | "What does my team need me to do?" ŌĆö surfaced before they have to click anywhere. |
| **Dept-Head snapshot** (only for `dept_head`) | `RoleDeptHead.vue` ŌĆö department headcount, on-leave-today count, open-tickets count, open-complaints count (org-wide, since complaints have no employee FK by design), and a 6-row recent-leave feed. Cached for 60 seconds under `dashboard.deptHead.{userId}`. | `dept_head` | A department head's scope is wider than a line manager's ŌĆö the whole department, not just direct reports. Two snapshots, two cache keys, no double counting. |
| **Live sync pill** ("Live ┬Ę 12s") | A small chip in the masthead that ticks every second to show time since the last successful Inertia reload. Resets when a partial reload completes. | Anyone | Confidence cue ŌĆö the dashboard isn't dead, it's just waiting. |

> *Notes:* Every snapshot block is its own cache key ŌĆö `dashboard_stats_{userId}_{role}`, `dashboard.finance`, `dashboard.manager.{userId}`, `dashboard.deptHead.{userId}`, `dashboard.timeseries.{metric}.{days}`. The 60-second TTL is uniform (`DashboardService::STATS_TTL`) so a single cache flush across the dashboard refreshes everything in step. The role-targeted snapshots are `null` for any role that doesn't qualify, so the Vue layer renders them with `v-if` and no role-introspection runs in the template. The dashboard's flagship gold accent is the same `#ffd700` the Reports group uses ŌĆö the only two surfaces in the institute permitted the 5% tint.

## The data behind it

CIHRMS treats the report layer as a *read* surface ŌĆö almost nothing originates here. Three stores feed everything you see on these screens:

- **The live operational tables** (`employees`, `leave_requests`, `payments`, `tickets`, `departments`, `payroll_runs`, `disbursements`, `statutory_returns`, `loan_accounts`, `offboarding_cases`, `identity_verifications`, `whistleblower_reports`, `audit_logs`) ŌĆö the source of truth for every count, sum and breakdown. Reports query these directly with the visibility scopes already enforced on each model (a department head sees their slice; the AG pack reads the whole table because the pack runs under a permission that already implies institute-wide visibility).
- **`analytics_events`** ŌĆö an append-only, queued event log. Schema is intentionally minimal: `id`, nullable `user_id` (so system-fired events are honestly attributed), `event` (a dotted-string name like `leave.requested`), `meta` (JSON cast, free-form payload ŌĆö usually two or three keys naming the resource id and a state value), `created_at`, `updated_at`. There is no migration to evolve, no schema-per-event-type ŌĆö Wave 1 chose flexibility over rigidity here on purpose. The `RecordAnalyticsEvent` listener (`ShouldQueue`, queue `analytics`) is the single insert path; it pattern-matches 24+ event classes and writes one row per event.
- **`storage/app/ag-reports/`** ŌĆö the on-disk archive of every Auditor-General pack ever generated, named `AG-{year}-{Ymd-His}.zip`. The listing on the Auditor-General page is a `Storage::disk('local')->files('ag-reports')` scan filtered to `.zip`. Each pack carries its own MANIFEST.md with per-file SHA-256 hashes; the pack is the audit artefact, the directory is the storage location.

What every reader of these screens needs to keep in mind:

- **The Reports landing is a *re-query* surface, not a *materialised* one.** Hitting Download regenerates the XLSX from the live database every time. There is no nightly snapshot, no warehouse, no "report run history". The 60-second preview cache is the only cache between you and the data ŌĆö and it's busted by TTL alone.
- **The Dashboard is a *cached* surface.** Six different cache keys, all on the 60-second `STATS_TTL`. A change made now will appear on the dashboard within one minute, not instantly. This is a deliberate trade-off for query cost; an explicit cache flush isn't exposed in the UI.
- **`analytics_events` rows are immutable.** The listener inserts; nothing updates. Wave 1 left the meta column as schemaless JSON and the event names as free strings, which means a typo (`leave.requuested`) lives forever ŌĆö the safeguard is the `match` block in the listener that only writes well-known names. Anything coming through the public `POST /analytics` endpoint (the `AnalyticsController::store` action used by client-side JS for things like "modal opened") is validated for shape but not for vocabulary.
- **The Auditor-General pack is a *sealed* surface.** Every file in the ZIP is independently SHA-256 hashed and the hash list is committed to MANIFEST.md inside the same ZIP. The pack is the audit artefact; once generated it is not edited. Re-downloading the same pack returns byte-for-byte the same bytes.
- **Whistleblower data is segregated by statute.** The pack contains *counts only* (category ├Ś severity ├Ś status) from `whistleblower_reports`. Case content is NOT in the pack ŌĆö Act 720 (Whistleblower Act, 2006) puts case content under the designated investigator's separate cover, and the manifest restates that on every pack so the auditor can't accidentally request it via this channel.

## How it talks to other modules

Reports & Analytics is the *sink* for the rest of the system. The data flow is one-way: modules fire events, the analytics listener queues them, the Dashboard and Reports layer read them back. The ten biggest event producers in the codebase today, in volume order:

1. **Employees (Ch 3)** ŌĆö `employee.created`. Hires per day are the spine of the Active Staff sparkline.
2. **Leave (Ch 4)** ŌĆö `leave.requested`, `leave.status_updated`. Drives Pending Leave KPI + Leave Summary export sparkline + the dept-head's "on leave today" count.
3. **Tickets (Ch 11)** ŌĆö `ticket.created`. Drives Open Tickets KPI + Ticket SLA Report preview + Service-load donut.
4. **Payroll (Ch 19)** ŌĆö `payslip.generated`, `payment.created`, `payment.paid`. Drives the Pending Payroll KPI, the Payroll Export preview, and the Finance snapshot's paid-YTD figure.
5. **Recruitment (Ch 9)** ŌĆö `recruitment.applicant.created`. Drives the catch-all `RoleOverview.vue` quick actions for Marketing.
6. **Attendance (Ch 5)** ŌĆö `attendance.correction.requested`, `attendance.correction.decided`. Not surfaced on the main dashboard yet ŌĆö the events are recorded so Phase 1 can pivot the dashboard's "attendance posture" tile.
7. **Assets** ŌĆö six event types (`asset.assigned`, `asset.returned`, `asset.maintenance.logged`, `asset.maintenance.completed`, `asset.retired`, `asset.lost`). Feeds the Live activity stream; per-asset analytics are a Phase 1 ask.
8. **Benefits (Ch 23)** ŌĆö five event types (`benefit.plan.created`, `benefit.enroled`, `benefit.dependant.added`, `benefit.claim.submitted`, `benefit.claim.decided`). Same role on the dashboard as assets.
9. **Governance (Ch 28)** ŌĆö six event types (`policy.drafted`, `policy.version.added`, `policy.published`, `policy.acknowledged`, `certification.expiring`, `certification.expired`). Will drive the compliance posture sparkline once it switches off the literal-array fallback.
10. **(Anything new)** ŌĆö any event class not pattern-matched in the listener falls through to the default branch and is recorded with `event = class_basename($event)` and empty `meta`. The system fails *open* on new events ŌĆö it's strictly easier to add a richer mapping later than to lose the record.

Downstream:

- **Audit Logs (Ch 24)** ŌĆö Reports does not write audit logs itself, but the Auditor-General pack *consumes* the audit log: `audit:verify-chain` runs against `audit_logs` and the output goes into `audit/chain_verification.txt`. Tampering anywhere on the chain shows up as a non-zero exit code in that file.
- **Payroll (Ch 19)** ŌĆö the Auditor-General pack pulls `PayrollRun::whereBetween('period_start', [$start, $end])` and concatenates per-run statutory return files into yearly PAYE / SSNIT / Tier-2 / NHIA CSVs. Bank disbursement files (the GhIPSS-compatible ones) are copied verbatim into `statutory/bank_files/` so the auditor can match what was sent to the bank against what the payroll engine recorded.
- **Identity verification (Ch 25)** ŌĆö the pack writes `identity/verifications.csv` with the Ghana Card number *masked* to its last digit ("GHA-ŌĆóŌĆóŌĆóŌĆóŌĆóŌĆóŌĆóŌĆóŌĆó-N") so the auditor sees who is verified, not the raw PII. The full PIN stays in the encrypted database column.
- **Loans (Ch 21)** ŌĆö the pack writes both `loans/accounts.csv` (every loan booked in the fiscal year, full lifecycle dates) and `loans/outstanding.csv` (open balances at year-end, sorted by `outstanding_balance DESC`).
- **Off-boarding (Ch 10)** ŌĆö the pack writes `offboarding/cases.csv` with one row per case whose `last_working_day` falls in the fiscal year, joined to the settlement row for gross/deductions/net.
- **Whistleblower (Ch 27)** ŌĆö counts only, by category ├Ś severity ├Ś status. The pack restates the Act 720 segregation note in `MANIFEST.md` and on the on-screen "What's bundled" card.

## Standards touchpoints

- **ISO 30414:2018 ŌĆö Human capital reporting** ŌĆö the international standard for what an institutional people-report should contain. The Reports landing covers a meaningful subset of its 23 disclosures *today*: workforce composition (┬¦4.1) from the Headcount export and the Workforce donut; leadership ratios (┬¦4.5) from the Employee directory's manager relationship surfaced by the analytics layer; recruitment metrics (┬¦4.7) from the `recruitment.applicant.created` event stream that feeds the catch-all overview; succession planning surfaceable from the `EmployeeService::stats()` query; productivity and revenue-per-employee (┬¦4.6) computable from Payroll + Recruitment but not yet shipped as a named report; turnover (┬¦4.9) from the Staff Turnover export with tenure-in-days; learning & development (┬¦4.10) from Governance/Learning chapters once the LMS lands. See Chapter 44 for the full crosswalk.
- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.1 (workforce composition)** ŌĆö directly served by the Headcount export columns (Name, Staff ID, Department, Position, Hire Date, Status) and the Dashboard's Workforce donut + department breakdown.
- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.9 (turnover)** ŌĆö directly served by the Staff Turnover export, which includes hire date, termination date, and tenure-in-days computed at row emission time.
- **Audit Service Act, 2000 (Act 584) and the Audit Service constitutional mandate (Article 187)** ŌĆö the Auditor-General Report Pack is the operational answer to "what does the AG need from CIHRMS on a routine visit?" The pack version (1.0) is committed to MANIFEST.md so the AG's office can adapt their ingestion if the schema evolves.
- **Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921)** ŌĆö the eyebrow above the AG page reads "AUDITOR-GENERAL PACK ┬Ę ACT 921" precisely because Act 921 is the modern statutory frame for public-sector financial management; the pack's payroll + statutory + bank-file bundle is the dataset Act 921 audits hinge on.
- **Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896) ŌĆö PAYE returns**, **Social Security and National Insurance Trust Act, 2008 (Act 766) ŌĆö Tier-1 returns**, **National Pensions Act, 2008 (Act 766) ŌĆö Tier-2 trustee schedules**, **National Health Insurance Act, 2012 (Act 852) ŌĆö NHIA share** ŌĆö all four statutory return CSVs ship inside the pack under `statutory/`, concatenated across periods, headers preserved.
- **Whistleblower Act, 2006 (Act 720)** ŌĆö Section 11 of Act 720 places case content under the designated investigator. The pack respects the segregation in code (`writeWhistleblowerSummary()` selects only `category, severity, status, count(*)`) and in the on-screen card (the only amber "info" line on "What's bundled"). The manifest restates the rule for the auditor.
- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦17 (lawful basis) and ┬¦18 (data minimisation)** ŌĆö every personally identifying number that leaves the system through a report is minimised by default. Ghana Card numbers in the AG identity register are masked to the last digit; salary is gated by `employees.view_salary` on the live exports too. The Reports layer does not introduce a new lawful-basis category ŌĆö it inherits the employment-contract basis the underlying record already relies on.

## What's planned next

Phase 1 of the government-grade roadmap lands four things directly on these screens. (1) **Scheduled report delivery** ŌĆö saved report definitions with parameter binding, run on cron, emailed to a list. The plumbing is already in place: the five `App\Exports\*` classes are ready to be invoked from a queued job, only the schedule + recipients UI is missing. (2) **Real-time activity stream** ŌĆö Inertia partial reloads on a tighter interval, optionally with Laravel Echo, so the dashboard's "Live ┬Ę 12s" pill stops being an approximation. (3) **Compliance posture sparkline** ŌĆö the dashboard's fourth KPI sparkline currently uses a literal array because there's no `compliance.*` event family yet; Phase 1 adds policy-acknowledgement + certification-expiry rollups so the sparkline shows real numbers. (4) **AG pack v1.1** ŌĆö add a per-month payroll variance report (vs the previous month and vs the same month last year), include an HR establishment summary (positions ├Ś grades ├Ś actual counts) for the AG's establishment-control check, and write the verification hash of the previous pack into the current pack to chain the packs themselves into the audit trail.

<!-- ===== 32_profile_portal.md ===== -->

# Chapter 32 ŌĆö Profile / Employee Self-Service Portal

> *In one paragraph.* The Profile Portal is the employee-facing window onto the very same `employees` row that HR edits in Chapter 3. Every signed-in user lands on `/profile` and sees a tabbed self-service hub ŌĆö Profile, My Leave, My Pay, Benefits, Documents, Tickets, Security ŌĆö that lets them keep their personal record current, see how much annual leave they have left, check their last six payslips, confirm which benefit plans they are enrolled in, pull HR-uploaded documents, glance at their open service tickets, and change their password. It is also where the password-must-change wall lands new hires on their very first login, where the data subject reaches the Act 843 self-service queue from Chapter 26, and where notification-channel preferences from Chapter 16 are surfaced. The portal writes to the same `PATCH /employees/{id}` endpoints behind the scenes only via dedicated `/profile/*` routes that always operate on `$request->user()->employee`, so an employee can never escape their own row ŌĆö and it 404s cleanly when an admin account without an attached Employee record opens it.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** The portal is not pinned to the sidebar group list ŌĆö it is reached from the top-bar user-account dropdown (top-right avatar) under **My Profile**, and from the `/profile` route directly. The bell icon's "Channels" link in the Notifications module (Chapter 16) and the **My DPA Requests** entry in the user-account dropdown (Chapter 26) live alongside it as related self-service shortcuts. New hires never have to find it ŌĆö the password-must-change middleware redirects them to `/profile#security` on first login.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **Every authenticated user**, regardless of role, sees their own Profile portal ŌĆö there is no permission gate on `/profile`. Super_admin, ceo, hr_admin, dept_head, manager, finance_officer, auditor, it_support, employee ŌĆö they all land on the same page and see the same six tabs. The page renders for users without an attached `Employee` row too (legacy admin-only accounts created before the employee-portal expansion) ŌĆö they get the Account and Security sections, plus an empty state on every other tab.
    - **No role sees anyone else's profile** through this URL. There is no `/profile/{id}` form. To see another person's record, HR uses the directory in Chapter 3, the data subject uses the Act 843 portal in Chapter 26, or the auditor uses the masked register in Chapter 24.
- **Related modules:** Employees (Chapter 3) ŌĆö the data source; every write on this page lands on the same Employee row HR edits from the directory, and the salary visibility, soft-delete, and visibility-scope rules all carry through unchanged. Leave (Chapter 4) ŌĆö the My Leave tab pulls balances and the latest five requests via `LeaveService::balances()` so the employee sees what HR sees, scoped to themselves. Payroll Engine (Chapter 19) ŌĆö the My Pay tab lists the latest six `payments` rows, with the gross monthly salary card shown only when `employees.view_salary` is held (so admins and finance see their own number; an ordinary employee sees the payslip list without the salary card unless they hold the permission). Benefits (Chapter 23) ŌĆö the Benefits tab renders the active enrolment list from the same `benefit_enrolments` relation the HR-side profile uses. Notifications (Chapter 16) ŌĆö the channel-preferences screen at `/notifications/channels` is a sibling self-service surface; the Profile page links across to it from the user-account dropdown rather than embedding it inline. Identity (Chapter 25) ŌĆö the National ID field on the Profile tab is the same `national_id` column that the Ghana Card verifier reads; the green "verified" tick is roadmapped to land on the hero card once the NIA adapter ships. DPA & Privacy (Chapter 26) ŌĆö the in-app subject self-service queue at `/privacy/my` is reached from this page (the cross-link is a known nav gap recorded in Ch 26 ŌĆö "employees reach `/privacy` from the Profile portal"). Standards (Chapter 44) ŌĆö every Act 843 ┬¦38 data-subject right relies on the read surface this page provides.

## The screens

![Profile portal ŌĆö hero card with avatar + tenure strip, six-tab bar, Profile tab](../assets/screenshots/32_profile_portal/profile.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero card ŌĆö avatar (initials fallback with deterministic gradient when no photo, real image when uploaded), camera button on the bottom-right of the avatar for re-upload, name + position + department, role chip + employee-number chip + employment-status badge, and a right-side strip of three quick stats (Hire Date, Tenure in years, total leave balance left across all types). The hero gradient is the obsidian ŌåÆ midnight cobalt of the Sovereign Precision system with a 1-pixel gold hairline at its foot ŌĆö the single accent moment on the whole page. ┬Ę ŌØĘ TabBar ŌĆö Profile ┬Ę My Leave ┬Ę My Pay ┬Ę Benefits ┬Ę Documents ┬Ę Tickets ┬Ę Security. Each tab has a Material Symbols icon and switches the body content via a single reactive `activeTab` ref. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Profile body ŌĆö four cobalt-framed cards in a two-column grid: Account (name + email), Personal Details (phone, gender, DOB, national ID, address), Emergency Contact (name, phone, relationship), Bank Details (bank name, account number). A fifth full-width card below is Skills & Certifications ŌĆö a chip list of existing entries with one-click remove buttons, and a four-field add row (name, level, expiry, "+ Add").*

![My Leave tab ŌĆö balance rings, recent requests](../assets/screenshots/32_profile_portal/leave.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Balance grid ŌĆö one `ProgressRing` tile per leave type (Annual, Sick, Maternity, Paternity, Emergency, Study, Unpaid) with a coloured ring, a Material Symbols icon centred in the ring, the type label, a large tabular-nums "days left" figure, and a "used / total" footnote. Each ring is colour-keyed: cobalt for Annual and Maternity, amber for Sick, cyan for Paternity, rose for Emergency, emerald for Study, slate for Unpaid. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Recent Requests list ŌĆö the latest five `leave_requests` for this employee, each row showing the type label with its colour swatch, the start ŌåÆ end date range, and the status badge. "All requests ŌåÆ" link in the header jumps to the full Leave module from Chapter 4.*

![My Pay tab ŌĆö gross salary card (gated) and payslip list](../assets/screenshots/32_profile_portal/pay.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Gross Monthly Salary card ŌĆö large emerald-accented tile shown only when the `salary` key is present on the resource (i.e., the viewer holds `employees.view_salary`). Ordinary employees who don't hold the gate don't see this card at all ŌĆö the salary key is omitted server-side in `EmployeeResource`. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Recent Payslips list ŌĆö the latest six `payments` rows, each one showing the description (or "Payslip" when absent), the "Paid {date}" / "Created {date}" qualifier, the amount in GHS, and a status badge.*

![Benefits tab ŌĆö enrolment list](../assets/screenshots/32_profile_portal/benefits.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ "My Benefits" eyebrow + plan list ŌĆö each enrolment carries the plan name, the monospace code, the type and provider, the effective-from date, the monthly premium, and the enrolment status (active / paused / ended). "Open Benefits module ŌåÆ" link in the top-right jumps to the catalogue in Chapter 23. Empty state: a "verified_user" icon and the message "You're not enrolled in any benefit plans yet. HR can enrol you from the Benefits module."*

![Security tab ŌĆö change password + danger zone](../assets/screenshots/32_profile_portal/security.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Change Password card ŌĆö current password, new password, confirm new password. The form posts to `PATCH /profile/password` and on success clears the `password_must_change` flag on the user row, which lets the user navigate freely again. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Danger Zone ŌĆö rose-bordered card with an inline "Confirm with current password" input and a Delete Account button. The form deletes the User row (soft-deletes the Employee through the cascade), invalidates the session, and redirects to `/`. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Note that the Two-Factor Authentication setup is not embedded here ŌĆö enrolment lives on its own page at `/two-factor/enroll`, reached from the user-account dropdown. This is an intentional split because 2FA enrolment is challenge-and-confirm rather than a simple form save.*

> The five screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 2 (task W2.32). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### Hero card

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Camera button** (overlaid on the avatar, cobalt circle) | Opens the OS file picker, then POSTs the selected image to `POST /profile/avatar` as `multipart/form-data`. Validates `image`, `max:4096` KB, mime `jpg, jpeg, png, webp`. On success the new photo is hashed into `storage/app/public/avatars/` and the path is written to `employees.avatar_path`. The page reloads with the new `avatar_url` and the initials gradient disappears. | The signed-in user, but only when they have a linked Employee row. Admin-only legacy accounts see no camera button ŌĆö there's nowhere to attach the photo. | Self-service grooming. HR doesn't want to be a photo-upload service. The same endpoint is mirrored by `POST /employees/{employee}/avatar` for HR / dept-head uploads (Chapter 3), but the self-service one always operates on `$request->user()->employee` so an employee can't accidentally re-skin someone else. |
| **Name + role chip + employee-number chip + status badge** | Read-only display. Initials fall back to a deterministic gradient (hashed off the user's name) when no photo is uploaded ŌĆö six gradients in the disciplined cool palette, never random. The status badge uses the same component (`StatusBadge`) and colour mapping as Chapter 3. | Anyone viewing | Identity at a glance ŌĆö the same first paragraph of information HR sees on the employee detail page (Chapter 3) but from the employee's own viewpoint. |
| **Hire Date / Tenure / Leave Bal. strip** (right side of the hero) | Three read-only stat tiles. Hire date is `emp.hire_date` formatted `en-GH`; tenure is the `emp.tenure_years` accessor (computed off `hire_date` server-side); Leave Bal. sums `remaining_days` across every type returned by `LeaveService::balances($employee->id, now()->year)`. | Anyone viewing | The three figures the employee asks themselves first when opening their portal ŌĆö "when did I start, how long have I been here, how much leave do I have left?" ŌĆö answered above the fold without a click. |

> *Notes:* The hero gradient is intentionally the same obsidian ŌåÆ cobalt as the sidebar header so the page reads as part of the chrome rather than a marketing splash. The single gold hairline at its foot is the only gold moment on the page ŌĆö the design system reserves gold for governance accents and we don't want to over-spend it on a profile page.

### Profile tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Account ŌåÆ Full Name** | Editable text input, posts to `PATCH /profile` via `ProfileUpdateRequest`. Rule: `required, string, max:255`. Writes to `users.name`. | The signed-in user | Names change ŌĆö marriage, deed poll, legal correction. HR doesn't need to gatekeep this. |
| **Account ŌåÆ Email Address** | Editable email input, posts to the same endpoint. Rule: `required, lowercase, email, max:255, unique:users,email` (ignoring the current row). On save, if the email actually changed the `email_verified_at` column is nulled so the user has to re-verify. | Same | Login email is the canonical contact channel; the user must be able to keep it current themselves. The lowercasing rule means a typo in casing on the way in doesn't create a duplicate-key surprise on the way back out. |
| **Account ŌåÆ UNVERIFIED pill** | Read-only amber pill that appears next to the "Account" heading only when `mustVerifyEmail` is on **and** the user's `email_verified_at` is null. | Same | A visual reminder that the verification mail is sitting in the user's inbox ŌĆö not a blocking wall, just an honest status indication. |
| **Account ŌåÆ Save changes** | Submits the form via Inertia `useForm.patch`. Shows the cobalt shimmer-gradient button; disabled while `processing`. On success the page rerenders with the new name surfaced in the hero card and a green flash banner. | Same | Standard submit semantics, matching the Sovereign Precision button language used across Add Employee, Edit Employee, etc. |
| **Personal Details ŌåÆ Phone** | Text input, `tel` type. Rule: `nullable, string, max:20`. Saved to `employees.phone`. | The signed-in user **with a linked Employee row** | The phone number on the Employee row is what payroll, leave reminders, and incident SMS all dial ŌĆö the employee owns it. |
| **Personal Details ŌåÆ Gender** | Select with four values: `male, female, other, prefer_not_to_say` (the latter rendered as the placeholder option). Rule: `nullable, string, in:male,female,other,prefer_not_to_say`. Saved to `employees.gender`. | Same | Required by ISO 30414 ┬¦4.1 (workforce composition) for the analytics export in Chapter 13; offering "Prefer not to say" honours Act 843 ┬¦17 data-minimisation. |
| **Personal Details ŌåÆ Date of Birth** | Date input. Rule: `nullable, date, before:today`. Saved to `employees.date_of_birth`. | Same | DOB powers the age-and-retirement projections on the workforce analytics page and is needed for SSNIT statutory calculations. The `before:today` rule means a future-dated DOB is rejected with a clear error. |
| **Personal Details ŌåÆ National ID** | Text input. Rule: `nullable, string, max:64`. Saved to `employees.national_id`. The placeholder prompts the Ghana Card format `GHA-ŌĆ”`. | Same | This is the Ghana Card PIN ŌĆö the field the Identity Verification module from Chapter 25 reads on first verification, and the column the NIA adapter writes its `IdentityVerification` row against. The MVP accepts any 64-character string here; the Phase 1 adapter will validate the format synchronously on save. |
| **Personal Details ŌåÆ Address** | Text input. Rule: `nullable, string, max:255`. Saved to `employees.address`. | Same | Postal address for benefits cards, payroll exception correspondence, and statutory letters. |
| **Personal Details ŌåÆ Save personal info** | Posts the five-field block to `PATCH /profile/personal` via `useForm.patch`. Cobalt shimmer button, same as Account. On success: green flash and the form rerenders with the persisted values. | Same | Personal info is the most-edited block on the page ŌĆö moving house, new phone, new DOB on first proper data capture ŌĆö so it gets its own dedicated submit. |
| **Emergency Contact ŌåÆ Contact Name** | Text input. Rule: `nullable, string, max:255`. Saved to `employees.emergency_contact_name`. | Same | The named person Operations rings when an employee falls ill on site or is involved in a workplace incident. |
| **Emergency Contact ŌåÆ Phone** | Text input, `tel` type. Rule: `nullable, string, max:32`. | Same | Same purpose. |
| **Emergency Contact ŌåÆ Relationship** | Text input. Rule: `nullable, string, max:64`. Placeholder: "e.g. Spouse". | Same | Helps the on-call operator know whether they're ringing a spouse, parent, sibling, or friend before the conversation starts. |
| **Emergency Contact ŌåÆ Save emergency contact** | Posts to `PATCH /profile/emergency`. Rose-gradient submit (the colour mirrors the rose icon on the card header ŌĆö emergency = red across the whole product). | Same | A separate endpoint per concern keeps validation tight and lets the audit log say which block was touched. |
| **Bank Details ŌåÆ Bank Name** | Text input. Rule: `nullable, string, max:255`. Placeholder: "e.g. Ecobank Ghana". | Same | This is the bank label that prints on the payroll instruction file and on the disbursement receipt. |
| **Bank Details ŌåÆ Account Number** | Text input. Rule: `nullable, string, max:64`. Placeholder: "0000000000". | Same | The destination account for direct-deposit payroll. The card carries an italic line ("Used for direct deposits. Visible only to you and Finance.") so the employee knows where this travels. |
| **Bank Details ŌåÆ Save bank details** | Posts to `PATCH /profile/bank`. Emerald-gradient submit (the colour mirrors the emerald `account_balance` icon on the card header). | Same | A separate endpoint means a bank-detail change can be audit-logged distinctly from a name change ŌĆö important for fraud-investigation traceability, and the launchpad for the Phase 1 `BankChangeRequestService` approval loop that already lives in code. |
| **Skills & Certifications ŌåÆ Existing chip** | Read-only chip carrying the skill name, the level (beginner/intermediate/advanced/expert), the expiry date (if any), and an X button on the right. The chip uses a blue accent ŌĆö the only blue on the page ŌĆö to keep it visually distinct from the cobalt cards. | Same | Chips are scannable; an employee can tell at a glance whether their CompTIA Security+ is still in date. |
| **Skills & Certifications ŌåÆ Remove (X on a chip)** | Asks `confirm("Remove \"{name}\" from your skills?")` then DELETEs to `employees/{employee}/skills/{skill}`. | Same | A confirmation guard against an accidental click; the same flow HR uses from the directory in Chapter 3. |
| **Skills & Certifications ŌåÆ Add row (name, level, expiry, + Add)** | Posts to `POST /employees/{employee}/skills` via `useForm.post`. Validates against the `StoreSkillRequest`. The level select is populated from `EmployeeSkill::LEVELS` (`beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert`). | Same | The employee adds, the HR sees ŌĆö no approval loop in the MVP; the skill is real to the employee from the moment they save it. |
| **Skills & Certifications ŌåÆ "{n} on file" counter** | Read-only counter in the card header. | Same | A small dignity touch ŌĆö keeping count of one's own certifications. |

> *Notes:* Every `PATCH` endpoint on this tab calls `abort_unless($employee, 404, 'No employee record linked to this account.');` before validating, so a legacy admin user without an Employee row gets a clean 404 instead of a confusing "Field X is required" cascade. The Personal / Emergency / Bank cards all hide entirely (`v-if="hasEmployee"`) for those legacy accounts. Salary is **not** edited from this tab ŌĆö it is gated by `employees.view_salary` and is set through the dedicated compensation flow in Chapter 3; an employee changing their own pay would be a control failure. The Skills add form keeps the `level` default of `intermediate` because that is the modal value in the seed data; user research showed people overestimate themselves by one level when the default is `beginner`.

### My Leave tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Balance ring (per type)** | Read-only `ProgressRing` showing `used_days / total_days` with the remaining figure called out in tabular-nums. Colour-keyed per type (cobalt for Annual / Maternity, amber for Sick, cyan for Paternity, rose for Emergency, emerald for Study, slate for Unpaid). | The signed-in user | The same balance figure HR sees on the Leave History tab in Chapter 3, scoped to the employee themselves. The figure is computed server-side in `LeaveService::balances($employee->id, now()->year)` so the calculus matches the approvals desk. |
| **Recent Requests row** | Shows the type icon, the type label, the `start ŌåÆ end` date range, and a status badge (pending / approved / rejected / withdrawn). | Same | A glanceable history; the row is non-interactive in the MVP ŌĆö to act on a request the employee navigates to the Leave module from the header link. |
| **All requests ŌåÆ** (header link) | Jumps to the Leave index page in Chapter 4. | Same | Promotes the full module for the long-tail of leave operations (apply, withdraw, attach medical certificate). |
| **Empty state** (no leave history) | "No leave history ŌĆö Your leave requests will appear here once submitted." | Same | A reassurance to new joiners that the absence of rows is correct, not a bug. |

> *Notes:* The list shows the latest five requests, ordered `latest()` (i.e. by `created_at DESC`), which matches the Leave History tab on the HR-side detail page. Balance keys are passed through as a flat array (the `LeaveBalanceResource::collection(...)->resolve()` pattern matching `PrivacyController::myRequests` from the V2 audit) so the Vue page can iterate without having to peer into a paginator-shaped response.

### My Pay tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Gross Monthly Salary card** | Large emerald tile showing the GHS-formatted `emp.salary` figure. Only rendered when the key is present on the resource (`v-if="emp?.salary !== undefined && emp?.salary !== null"`). | Anyone whose viewer holds `employees.view_salary` ŌĆö which, for the self-view, is whoever HR has granted the permission to. By default that is super_admin, ceo, hr_admin, and finance_officer. Ordinary employees see no card here. | Salary visibility is enforced at the API layer (Chapter 3 ŌĆö `EmployeeResource` omits the key entirely for unauthorised viewers), and the front end honours that by simply not rendering the tile when the key is missing. There is no "blocked" placeholder ŌĆö the field is invisible to anyone not entitled to see it. |
| **Recent Payslips row** | One row per `payments` record, showing description, "Paid {date}" / "Created {date}", the amount + currency, and a status badge. | Anyone viewing their own portal | Six payments is enough to cover the last half-year for monthly payroll cycles ŌĆö the cut-off matches the HR-side Payroll tab in Chapter 3. |
| **Empty state** (no payslips) | "No payslips yet ŌĆö Payslips will appear here after payroll runs." | Same | Honest reassurance for first-payroll employees that nothing is broken. |

> *Notes:* This tab is the read-only consumer surface of the Payroll Engine in Chapter 19 ŌĆö every payment shown here is a row produced by `PayrollService::calculate()` and approved through the 2FA-gated approval lane. The portal never lets the employee edit, dispute, or otherwise interact with a payment from this surface; disputes flow through the Service Desk module in Chapter 11. The currency defaults to `GHS` because every payroll line is stored in cedi today; the multi-currency posture is a Phase 2 concern.

### Benefits tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Enrolment row** | One row per `benefit_enrolments` record. Shows the gold `verified_user` icon, the plan name, the monospace plan code, the type and provider, the effective-from date, the monthly premium in GHS, and the status (active / paused / ended). | Anyone viewing their own portal | Confirms what the employee is signed up for without making them open the Benefits module ŌĆö the most-asked question of HR is "Am I enrolled in the medical scheme?" and this row answers it. |
| **Open Benefits module ŌåÆ** (header link) | Jumps to `/benefits` from Chapter 23. | Same | Promotes the full module for dependants, e-card download, and claims submission. |
| **Empty state** | "You're not enrolled in any benefit plans yet. HR can enrol you from the Benefits module." | Same | A correct, unalarming reading for new joiners whose benefits-enrolment paperwork is still in HR's tray. |

> *Notes:* The list comes from `emp.benefit_enrolments` on the EmployeeResource ŌĆö the same relation rendered on the HR-side detail page in Chapter 3. The portal does not show dependants or claims in the MVP; those live in the Benefits module proper. The portal does **not** let the employee self-enrol in a plan from this page ŌĆö the operational flow is HR-led, matching Chapter 23. The gold accent on the icon is the one place this page borrows from the governance palette and is reserved for benefits as a category-of-trust signal.

### Documents tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Document row** | Cobalt-tinted icon well + filename + (uploaded-on date ┬Ę mime type). | Anyone viewing their own portal | The same document list HR sees on the detail page in Chapter 3, scoped to the employee themselves. |
| **Download** (per row) | Streams the file from `/storage/{file_path}` in a new tab. | Same | The same public-disk streaming as the HR side ŌĆö `storage:link` must have been run for this to work, which is a one-time deployment step. |
| **"{n} files" counter** | Read-only counter in the card header. | Same | A small dignity touch ŌĆö knowing how much of one's paperwork has been filed. |
| **Empty state** | "No documents yet ŌĆö Documents uploaded by HR will appear here." | Same | An honest reading: the document drawer is empty because HR has not yet attached anything, not because the page is broken. |

> *Notes:* Uploads from the employee side are **not** wired in the MVP ŌĆö the employee can read and download but not upload. The HR-side directory in Chapter 3 is the only upload surface. This is a deliberate posture for the MVP because HR wanted to be the gatekeeper of the personnel folder; the Phase 1 "employee upload with HR approval" loop will land here behind a `documents.self_upload` permission.

### Tickets tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Ticket row** | Shows the title, the created-on date, the status badge, and a "View" link. | Anyone viewing their own portal | A glance at what is open against the employee's name; the same five rows the HR-side detail page in Chapter 3 surfaces, scoped to the viewer. |
| **View** (per row) | Jumps to `/tickets/{id}` (the full Tickets module from Chapter 11). | Same | Promotes the full module for replies, attachments, and status changes. |
| **All tickets ŌåÆ** (header link) | Jumps to `/tickets`. | Same | Same affordance, mass-action variant. |
| **Empty state** | "No tickets ŌĆö Open a ticket from the Service Desk module if you need help." | Same | Directs the employee to the right surface for raising a new ticket, which is not this page. |

> *Notes:* The portal lists the latest five tickets that match the same RBAC scope as the HR-side detail page ŌĆö i.e., tickets the employee is the subject of or has been assigned to. New ticket creation does not happen on this page; the Service Desk module is the only creation surface in the MVP.

### Security tab

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Current Password** | Password input, `autocomplete="current-password"`. Rule: `required, current_password`. | The signed-in user | Standard re-authentication before a password change. Failure surfaces a clear "current password is incorrect" message ŌĆö the rule resolves through Laravel's built-in `current_password` validator. |
| **New Password** | Password input, `autocomplete="new-password"`. Rule: `required, string, min:8, confirmed`. | Same | Eight-character floor is the MVP minimum; a Phase 1 control will raise this to the institute's password policy (12+ characters, complexity classes). |
| **Confirm New Password** | Password input, `autocomplete="new-password"`. Rule: `confirmed` (i.e., must equal `password`). | Same | Confirmation guard against a typo locking the user out. |
| **Update password** | Posts the form to `PATCH /profile/password`. On success: `users.password` is updated via `Hash::make`, **and** the `password_must_change` flag is set to `false`. The form clears and a green flash banner appears. | Same | This is the same endpoint the password-must-change wall lands on ŌĆö once the user picks a real password, the flag clears and they can navigate the rest of the product. |
| **Danger Zone ŌåÆ Confirm with current password** | Password input. Rule: `required, current_password`. | Same | A second re-authentication before account deletion. |
| **Delete Account** | DELETEs to `/profile`. On confirmation: the User is `Auth::logout()`-ed, the session is invalidated, the User row is soft-deleted (which cascades to the Employee via model relationships), and the browser is redirected to `/`. | Same | The user-driven exit. An ordinary employee will almost never use this ŌĆö it exists for compliance reasons and because the Laravel Breeze template offers it; the Off-boarding module in Chapter 10 is the official institute path. |

> *Notes:* Two-Factor Authentication enrolment is **not** on this tab in the MVP ŌĆö the enrolment flow lives at `/two-factor/enroll` (a TOTP-secret + recovery-code flow rendered by `TwoFactorController::enroll`) and is reached from the user-account dropdown. This split is intentional because 2FA enrolment is a challenge-and-confirm flow rather than a simple form save, and the recovery codes are a once-only display that wants its own page. Phase 1 will surface "2FA: Enabled / Not enrolled" on this tab as a read-only status with an "Enrol ŌåÆ" link to the existing page, but the enrolment screen itself stays where it is.

## The password-must-change wall

A new hire created by HR on the Add Employee panel from Chapter 3 inherits a temporary password set by the HR officer. The user row is also stamped with `password_must_change = true` (the migration `2026_06_01_000001_add_password_must_change_to_users` added the column with a default of `false`; the `IssuePasswordResets` console command and the HR seed flow both flip it on for fresh users). On the next login, the `ForcePasswordChange` middleware intercepts every request and redirects the user to `/profile#security` with a flash error: *"Please set a new password before continuing."*

The middleware allows a tiny set of routes through so the user is not trapped in a redirect loop:

- `profile.edit`, `profile.update`, `profile.personal`, `profile.password` ŌĆö so the user can land on the page and submit the change.
- `logout`, `password.confirm` ŌĆö escape hatches for re-auth and exit.

Every other route (Dashboard, Employees, Leave, anything) is blocked with a redirect for HTML requests and a `403 Password change required before continuing.` for JSON / API requests. The flag clears as soon as the user submits a successful `PATCH /profile/password` ŌĆö the `updatePassword` controller method writes `password_must_change => false` alongside the new hash in the same update, so the user's very next click goes through unimpeded.

> This is the only modal-feeling guardrail in the entire product. It is deliberately not a JavaScript modal ŌĆö a middleware redirect is harder to bypass with browser tooling and works even on the JSON API surface.

## Channel preferences and DPA requests

Two adjacent self-service surfaces live alongside the Profile portal and are reached from the same user-account dropdown rather than being embedded inline:

- **Notification Channels (`/notifications/channels`, Chapter 16)** ŌĆö toggle email, in-app, WhatsApp, Slack, and Teams individually; provide a WhatsApp phone and explicit consent (Meta requires the pairing of consent + phone before template messages are allowed); provide a Slack user id for direct messaging. The `NotificationChannelController::update` endpoint stamps `whatsapp_consent_at` only when both consent and phone are present, and clears the timestamp the moment either is revoked ŌĆö a deliberate posture so the institute always has provable consent on file for every WhatsApp outbound. The channel preferences are stored on `users.notification_channels` (JSON) and read by `MessagingDispatcher` to decide which adapters fire for any given event.
- **My DPA Requests (`/privacy/my`, Chapter 26)** ŌĆö file an Access / Rectification / Erasure / Portability / Objection / Information request under Act 843 ┬¦38, see every request you have ever filed, download the fulfilled ZIP export when ready, and withdraw an open request. The page is reachable from the user-account dropdown, surfaces a six-card "education panel" restating every right in plain English, and shows a register of the subject's own requests with the statutory deadline countdown. The MVP does **not** have a sidebar entry for this surface ŌĆö that is a recorded nav gap in Chapter 26 ŌĆö and the Profile portal is the documented in-app entry point.

> Both surfaces are sibling self-service screens to the Profile portal rather than tabs inside it. The split keeps each page focused on a single concern and lets each carry its own RBAC / consent posture without polluting the others.

## The data behind it

Every write on this page goes to one of three tables ŌĆö `users`, `employees`, or `employee_skills` ŌĆö through dedicated `/profile/*` routes that always resolve the target row from `$request->user()->employee` rather than from a URL parameter. There is no `/profile/{employee}` route; an employee can never edit somebody else's record from this surface.

What every reader of the screen needs to keep in mind:

- **The Profile portal writes to the same row HR edits.** The personal, emergency, and bank fields on the Profile tab are the very same columns the HR-side Edit Employee panel in Chapter 3 writes to. The two surfaces are layered on the same table, gated by different controllers ŌĆö `ProfileController` for the employee, `EmployeeController` for HR ŌĆö but the data lands in the same place. A change saved from one surface is visible from the other on the very next render.
- **Avatar uploads land on the public disk.** Files write to `storage/app/public/avatars/` and are served from `/storage/avatars/{filename}`. The `php artisan storage:link` step must have been run once at deployment; if it hasn't been, the photo upload succeeds but the URL 404s ŌĆö a known gotcha called out in the deployment doc.
- **Salary visibility is enforced at the API layer, not the UI.** The My Pay tab's Gross Monthly Salary card renders only when the `salary` key is present on the resource ŌĆö and that key is omitted entirely for viewers who fail `EmployeePolicy::viewSalary`. The same gate is in force on the HR-side directory and detail page, so the posture is consistent across every salary surface in the product.
- **The portal degrades gracefully for legacy admin accounts.** The Profile portal renders for users who don't have a linked Employee row ŌĆö the seed data's `super_admin` account is one such ŌĆö but the Personal, Emergency, Bank, and Skills cards all hide (`v-if="hasEmployee"`), and the tabs that need an employee (My Leave, My Pay, Benefits, Documents, Tickets) render with empty states. The Account and Security tabs always work.
- **Password change clears the must-change flag in the same update.** The `updatePassword` controller method writes `password_must_change => false` alongside the new hash, so a user who lands on the security tab through the password-must-change wall is freed the moment they submit a successful change.

## How it talks to other modules

- **Employees (Chapter 3)** ŌĆö the data source. `ProfileController::edit` calls `EmployeeService::find($user->employee->id)` and passes the resulting `EmployeeResource` to the Vue page. Every write from this surface lands on the same `employees` row that HR edits from the directory in Chapter 3 ŌĆö the `UpdateEmployeeRequest::authorize()` three-tier rule (HR > department head > self) is the same one this surface implicitly relies on; the `/profile/*` routes are a thin convenience that always resolves to the self-edit branch.
- **Leave (Chapter 4)** ŌĆö the My Leave tab calls `LeaveService::balances($employee->id, now()->year)` for the ring tiles and reads `$employee->leaveRequests()->latest()->limit(5)` for the recent-requests list. The "All requests ŌåÆ" link routes to `/leave-requests`, the same page HR uses to manage the institute's leave queue.
- **Payroll Engine (Chapter 19)** ŌĆö the My Pay tab reads `$employee->payments()->latest()->limit(6)`. Every payment is a row produced by `PayrollService::calculate()` and approved through the 2FA-gated approval lane; the portal is the read-only consumer surface.
- **Benefits (Chapter 23)** ŌĆö the Benefits tab renders `emp.benefit_enrolments` from the EmployeeResource. The enrolment list is the same one HR sees on the detail page in Chapter 3; the portal does not surface dependants or claims (those live in the full Benefits module).
- **Notifications (Chapter 16)** ŌĆö the user-account dropdown links across to `/notifications/channels` for per-user channel preferences (email / in-app / WhatsApp / Slack / Teams). Channel preferences are a sibling self-service surface to the Profile portal rather than a tab inside it.
- **Identity (Chapter 25)** ŌĆö the National ID field on the Profile tab writes to the same `employees.national_id` column the Ghana Card verifier reads. The MVP accepts any 64-character string; Phase 1's NIA adapter will validate the format synchronously on save and write an `IdentityVerification` row asynchronously via `VerifyEmployeeIdentity`. The green "verified" tick on the hero card is roadmapped to land alongside the adapter.
- **DPA & Privacy (Chapter 26)** ŌĆö the in-app data-subject self-service queue at `/privacy/my` is reached from this page (the cross-link is the only in-app entry point ŌĆö see the recorded nav gap in Chapter 26). The page is the Act 843 ┬¦38 data subject's right-to-read surface for their own data; the DPO acts on it from `/admin/privacy`.
- **Audit Logs (Chapter 24)** ŌĆö every write on this page is event-sourced through the same Employee event listeners HR's writes are. A bank-detail change saved from this portal is indistinguishable in the audit chain from one saved from the HR-side edit panel, except for the `actor_id` (the employee themselves, not an HR officer).
- **Off-boarding (Chapter 10)** ŌĆö the Danger Zone account-deletion path soft-deletes the user and cascades to the Employee. The official institute exit path is the Off-boarding module, which clears settlements, ends benefit enrolments, runs the final payroll, and only then archives the employee. The Danger Zone is a Breeze-template inheritance, not the recommended path.

## Standards touchpoints

- **Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843) ┬¦38 ŌĆö rights of the data subject** ŌĆö the entire Profile portal is the Act 843 right-to-read surface for the employee's own personal data. Every field on the Profile tab is data the subject is entitled to see and (subject to the institute's contractual position) correct. The "My DPA Requests" page reachable from the user-account dropdown is the formal lifecycle endpoint for the same rights at scale (Access, Rectification, Erasure, Portability, Objection, Information) ŌĆö see Chapter 26 for the full Act 843 mechanism. See Chapter 44.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦17 ŌĆö lawful basis for processing personal data** ŌĆö the employee's voluntary self-edits of phone, address, gender, DOB, national ID, emergency contact, and bank details are processed under the contractual basis (their employment contract). The "Prefer not to say" option on the Gender select honours data-minimisation expectations under the same statute.
- **Data Protection Act ┬¦18 ŌĆö data quality** ŌĆö the self-service portal is the operational mechanism for satisfying ┬¦18; the data subject is the most reliable source of their own information and giving them a permanent self-service surface to update it is the cleanest way to keep the record current.
- **Ghana Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651)** ŌĆö the My Leave tab surfaces the entitlement and balance figures (Annual, Sick, Maternity) computed against Act 651's statutory floors; the Leave module in Chapter 4 owns the calculus.
- **WCAG 2.1 AA (form accessibility)** ŌĆö every form input on the page carries an explicit `aria-label`, every button has a visible focus state, the colour contrast across the cobalt-on-white system clears the 4.5:1 floor, and the tab order follows the visual reading order. The avatar upload button is keyboard-reachable (the underlying `<input type="file">` is hidden but focusable), the file input carries its own `aria-label`. The form-validation errors render as red text directly under the offending field rather than as a top-of-page summary ŌĆö a "near the error" pattern WCAG 3.3.1 recommends. See Chapter 44.
- **ISO 30414 ┬¦4.1 (workforce composition)** ŌĆö the self-edited Gender and Date of Birth fields are inputs into the workforce composition disclosures Chapter 13 produces. Honest self-disclosure here improves the quality of the analytics output downstream.

## What's planned next

Three additions are scheduled to land directly on this page: (1) **2FA status tile** on the Security tab ŌĆö a read-only "Two-factor authentication: Enabled / Not enrolled" line with a link to the existing enrolment page at `/two-factor/enroll`, plus a "Disable" button gated by the `two_factor_required` flag so role-mandated 2FA can't be removed by self-service; (2) **Ghana Card verification badge** on the hero card ŌĆö a green "Verified" tick rendered when the linked `identity_verifications` row is non-expired, sourced from the same NIA adapter scheduled in Chapter 25; (3) **Bank-change approval loop** on the Bank Details card ŌĆö the `BankChangeRequestService` scaffolding already exists in code but is not yet wired into the portal; once shipped, a self-service bank-detail edit will become a request that lands in Finance's approval queue rather than an immediate write, with HR notified and the change auditable. A fourth, lower-priority item is **employee-side document upload** on the Documents tab behind a new `documents.self_upload` permission, with an HR-side approval queue so the personnel folder stays curated.

<!-- ===== 33_settings.md ===== -->

# Chapter 33 ŌĆö Settings

> *In one paragraph.* Settings, in CIHRMS today, is not a single "system configuration" screen ŌĆö it is the umbrella under which six small admin surfaces live: three asset libraries that feed the Documents pipeline (Stamps, Letterheads, Watermarks), and three integration surfaces that govern how external systems talk to the institute (API Tokens, Webhooks, API Reference). The "Settings" link in the sidebar itself points at the My Profile page in Chapter 16 ŌĆö there is no central organization-profile/branding/locale page in the MVP because those values live in environment variables and seeders, not in the database. Everything else that feels like a "system setting" lives in its purpose-built module: notification channel toggles in Notifications (Ch 16), SMS provider config in Messaging Admin (Ch 15), retention defaults in DPA (Ch 26), SSO providers in their own admin (Ch 28), and integrations such as Paystack in the Integrations admin (Ch 28). This chapter documents the six screens that genuinely sit under the `/settings/...` URL prefix and the `Settings/` Vue namespace, plus the honest gaps around the configuration that should live here in Phase 1 but does not yet.

## Where to find it

- **Sidebar location:** **System** group, towards the bottom of the rail. The umbrella exposes six entries:
    - **API Tokens** (`vpn_key` icon, route `api-tokens.index`) ŌĆö gated on `api.token_manage`.
    - **Webhooks** (`webhook` icon, route `webhooks.index`) ŌĆö gated on `api.webhooks_manage`.
    - **API Docs** (`menu_book` icon, route `api.docs`) ŌĆö visible to every signed-in user; the page itself is the live developer reference.
    - **Settings** (`settings` icon, route `profile.edit`) ŌĆö this is the catch-all label; today it deep-links to My Profile (Ch 16) because there is no system-config screen yet.
    - **Audit Logs** (full-system roles only) ŌĆö covered in Chapter 24, listed here only because it lives in the same sidebar section.
    - The three asset libraries ŌĆö **Stamps**, **Letterheads**, **Watermarks** ŌĆö are not in the sidebar at all. They are reached from inside the Documents module (Ch 13) and the composer's asset picker, but they live at `/settings/stamps`, `/settings/letterheads`, `/settings/watermarks` and are documented here because they are administrative-grade, organisation-scoped, and they share the `Settings/` Vue folder.
- **Roles that see it:**
    - **super_admin** and **ceo** ŌĆö every entry, every action. CEO mirrors super_admin through the `Gate::before` fall-through (see Ch 24).
    - **hr_admin** ŌĆö everything except API Tokens and Webhooks unless explicitly granted; holds `document_assets.manage` (the flag that unlocks the **organization** scope in every asset library) by default in the seeded RBAC.
    - **dept_head / manager** ŌĆö can see the asset libraries (personal + their department) but the **organization** radio is disabled unless they hold `document_assets.manage`. They do not see API Tokens or Webhooks.
    - **employee / finance_officer / it_support / auditor** ŌĆö personal-scope asset libraries only; no API Tokens, no Webhooks. API Docs is visible because the page itself is internal documentation, not a privileged tool.
- **Related modules:** Documents (Ch 13) ŌĆö every stamp, letterhead, and watermark is consumed there; the composer's letterhead picker, the annotation toolbar's stamp picker, and the burn-time watermark layer all read from these libraries. Notifications (Ch 16) ŌĆö channel toggles for email/SMS/in-app live on the per-user notification preferences screen, not here. Messaging Admin (Ch 15) ŌĆö the SMS provider config (Hubtel/Twilio/Arkesel keys) is environment-driven and surfaces in the Messaging admin's "Provider" tab. DPA (Ch 26) ŌĆö retention defaults and erasure schedules sit on the privacy admin, not in Settings. SSO Providers (Ch 28) ŌĆö the OIDC/SAML credential vault is at `/sso-admin`, gated on `sso.manage`. Integrations (Ch 28) ŌĆö the Paystack public/secret key pair, Zoho refresh token, and other third-party credentials are in `/admin/integrations`, gated on `integrations.manage`.

## The screens

![API Tokens ŌĆö vault hero, KPI tiles, token register table](../assets/screenshots/33_settings/api_tokens.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Vault secret banner (only after a token is issued) ŌĆö cobalt-gold gradient card with the plaintext token on display once, a click-to-copy button that turns emerald on success, and a "this is the only time you'll see it" warning. The banner enters with a 300 ms slide-down animation and never re-renders on subsequent loads. ┬Ę ŌØĘ KPI band ŌĆö five tiles: Total issued (lifetime), Active (in use), Used 24h (hit in the last day), Revoked (manual), Expired (time-out). Each tile uses a different icon-tile colour: brand, success, cyan, magenta, warning. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Token register ŌĆö eight-column table: token name + audit note, issued-to (User name), scopes (each ability rendered as a colour-tinted chip ŌĆö read=cyan, write=cobalt, export=gold, manage=magenta, `*`=red), rate limit per minute (mono), last-used relative timestamp, expiry date, status pill (ACTIVE/REVOKED/EXPIRED with coloured dot), and a Revoke button only on usable tokens.*

![Webhooks ŌĆö secret hero, KPI tiles, partner endpoint list with health stripe](../assets/screenshots/33_settings/webhooks.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Signing-secret banner (only after a subscription is registered) ŌĆö same vault style as the token banner with "this secret cannot be retrieved again" copy and the HMAC verification recipe `sha256(timestamp + '.' + body) = X-CIHRMS-Signature`. ┬Ę ŌØĘ KPI band ŌĆö Subscriptions, Active, Paused, Failing (flagship rose tint with a pulsing number when the count is non-zero), Hit 24h. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Partner endpoint rows ŌĆö leading 4 px health stripe (emerald = healthy, amber = wobbling at 1ŌĆō2 failures, orange = degraded at 3ŌĆō9, rose = failing at 10+, slate = paused), partner name + truncated callback host, up to three event chips with a `+N` overflow counter, last-delivery relative time, failure count (rose number when non-zero), health pill, and trailing Pause/Resume + Delete buttons.*

![API Docs ŌĆö gradient hero with base URL, quick-start curl block, endpoint catalogue cards](../assets/screenshots/33_settings/api_docs.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Hero band ŌĆö cobalt gradient with the live `v1` chip, the base URL in a copy-on-click code block, and four KPIs on the right (Resources, Endpoints, Tokens issued, Webhooks). A thin gold ribbon sweeps across the top of the band every 3.6 s. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Quick-start row ŌĆö two cards side-by-side: a terminal-flavoured curl example with a Copy button, and a cyan-tinted Authentication note that links straight to API Tokens. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Endpoint catalogue ŌĆö three-column grid of group cards, each with a coloured left border, an icon, the resource name, and the required scope chip, then a divided list of routes with colour-coded verb pills (GET=teal, POST=emerald, PATCH/PUT=amber, DELETE=rose).*

![Stamp library ŌĆö upload form, scope tabs, four-column grid of PNG thumbnails](../assets/screenshots/33_settings/stamps.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Upload row ŌĆö four inputs across the top: Name, Scope dropdown (personal / department / organization ŌĆö Organization disabled without `document_assets.manage`, Department disabled when the user is not assigned to a department), PNG file picker (PNG only, Ōēż 1 MB), cobalt Upload PNG button. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Scope-tab strip ŌĆö three secondary chips for switching between personal / department / organization views; the active chip uses a 10 % secondary tint. ┬Ę ŌØĖ Stamp tile ŌĆö 64 px PNG preview on a white card, name, and a rose Remove link (the policy blocks deletion of anything the user did not upload unless they hold `document_assets.manage`).*

![Letterhead library ŌĆö upload form with header-height field, two-column tile grid](../assets/screenshots/33_settings/letterheads.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Upload row ŌĆö five inputs: Name, Scope, Header height in mm (20ŌĆō80, defaults to 36), PNG/JPEG file picker (up to 3 MB), Upload button. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Letterhead tile ŌĆö full banner preview, name, height label, and a green "default" pill on the seeded organisation letterhead. The Remove button is hidden on default rows because the policy refuses to delete them ŌĆö the composer must always have a fallback.*

![Watermark library ŌĆö text vs image toggle, colour picker, live preview](../assets/screenshots/33_settings/watermarks.png)

*Callouts: ŌØČ Upload row ŌĆö six inputs: Name, Scope, Type (Text / Image), then conditional inputs ŌĆö for text watermarks a text box and a native colour picker, for image watermarks a PNG picker spanning two columns. Opacity defaults to 0.18 and angle to ŌłÆ30┬░. ┬Ę ŌØĘ Watermark tile ŌĆö for image watermarks the PNG preview on a white card; for text watermarks a rotated text rendering using the saved colour, opacity, and angle so the burned-PDF appearance is visible at a glance.*

> The six screenshot files referenced above will be captured in Wave 1 (task W1.33). Until then the build will substitute a "missing image" placeholder ŌĆö that is expected and does not break the build.

## Every button, every action

### API Tokens (`/admin/api-tokens`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Issue token** (cobalt CTA, top right) | Opens the right-hand slide panel. Required fields are Name and at least one Scope; optional fields are Audit note (purpose), Rate limit per minute (1ŌĆō6000, defaults to 60), Expires in days (1ŌĆō3650, defaults to 365). On submit, calls `Sanctum::createToken` on the current user with the chosen abilities and writes an `api_token_metadata` row with purpose, rate limit, expiry, and issuer. | `api.token_manage` | One-step issuance for partner systems (GIFMIS, IPPD, Ghana Card). The metadata row carries the audit story the bare Sanctum table cannot ŌĆö who asked for it, why, and how fast it can hit the API. |
| **Token name** | Required, max 120 chars. | Same | Free-text label that appears in the table and in the audit log; "GIFMIS production integration" is more useful than "Token #34". |
| **Audit note (purpose)** | Optional textarea, max 500 chars. | Same | Surfaces in the audit log and in a tooltip on the token row. The intent is that every revoke decision has the original "why" one click away. |
| **Scopes** (multi-select grid) | At least one required. The seeded scopes are `employees:read`, `payroll:read`, `attendance:read`, `positions:read`, `statutory:export`, `identity:read`, `identity:write`, `webhooks:manage`, and `*`. The `*` wildcard is rendered in red with a "use sparingly" footnote. | Same | Sanctum abilities are checked per-endpoint; minimum-scope tokens limit the blast radius of a leak. |
| **Rate limit / min** | Optional integer 1ŌĆō6000, defaults to 60. | Same | Per-token throttling at the gateway; partners with batch jobs can be raised to 600/min, public-facing scripts kept at 60. |
| **Expires in days** | Optional integer 1ŌĆō3650, defaults to 365. | Same | Every token has a default sunset; long-lived integrations must be renewed annually, which forces the audit conversation on rotation. |
| **Issue token** (footer) | Posts to `POST /admin/api-tokens`. On success the page redirects back to the index with `flash_token` set to the plaintext; the secret banner reveals it once. | Same | Sanctum stores only the hash ŌĆö once the response is gone, the token cannot be retrieved. The single-reveal banner is the only place it is ever shown. |
| **Copy** (on the secret banner) | Writes the plaintext to the clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText`; the button turns emerald and reads "Copied" for 2.5 s. Silent fail if clipboard access is blocked. | Anyone seeing the banner | The token is long and base64-ish; manual copying is error-prone. |
| **Revoke** (per row, rose button) | Confirmation dialog ("Revoke this token? Any system using it will be disconnected immediately.") ŌåÆ DELETE `/admin/api-tokens/{id}`. Sets `revoked_at` and `revoked_by` on the metadata row, then calls `delete()` on the `PersonalAccessToken` itself. | `api.token_manage` | Compromise response ŌĆö instant cut-off with an audit trail of who pulled the plug. |
| **KPI tile band** | Read-only ŌĆö Total issued (from `tokens.meta.total`), Active (`is_usable` true), Used 24h (last-used within 86 400 000 ms), Revoked (`revoked_at` set), Expired (not usable and not revoked). | Anyone with view | The five questions an integrations lead asks on landing: "how many do we have, how many are live, how many actually get used, how many have we killed, how many have aged out?". |

> *Notes:* The plaintext is never persisted ŌĆö Sanctum hashes it before storing. The `flash_token` survives exactly one Inertia redirect cycle thanks to Laravel's session flash mechanism; refresh the page and the banner is gone. The metadata table carries `allowed_ip_cidrs` (validated on issue) and `issued_to_user_id` (so an admin can issue tokens *to* another service account) but the MVP UI does not surface either yet ŌĆö they are reachable via the API. `is_usable()` is the canonical "can this token still authenticate a request?" check; it folds expiry and revocation into a single predicate.

### Webhooks (`/admin/webhooks`)

| Button / field | What it does | Who can use it | Why it exists |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Register partner** (cobalt CTA) | Opens the right-hand slide panel. Required: Partner name, Callback URL, at least one Subscribed event. The default event selection is `*` (wildcard). | `api.webhooks_manage` | One-step partner onboarding; the secret-reveal flow mirrors the Token issue flow so admins meet a consistent "save now" experience. |
| **Partner name** | Required, max 120 chars. | Same | Used in the row header and in delivery logs. |
| **Callback URL** | Required, valid URL, max 1024 chars, must be HTTPS in production. | Same | HMAC-SHA256 signatures protect the body, but transport must still be encrypted; partner endpoints behind plain HTTP are rejected by the queue worker as a second line of defence. |
